# Changelog

All notable changes to **onionskin** are documented here.

This project has evolved from a focused **single-sample amplification peak caller** into a broader **developmental DNA amplification analysis framework** supporting:

- single-sample and multistage workflows
- summit and interval refinement across resolutions
- timing estimation
- APS (Amplification Progression Score)
- clustering and posterior ordering
- groundwork for overlap resolution and twin-peak decomposition

This changelog records **onionskin product changes** — code, CLI flags, output schemas,
user-facing docs, and conceptual / mathematical evolution of the pipeline itself.

**Multi-agent dev-system changes** (workflow files, `AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md`, agent
bootstrap files, SPEC/BRAINSTORM/FEEDBACK engineering rounds without code changes, etc.)
are recorded in a sibling file: `multi-agent/DEVLOG.md`. The split is prospective — it
took effect at v0.14.64 (see `multi-agent/DEVLOG.md` for that entry). Historical entries
in this file (pre-v0.14.64) are mixed and not retroactively migrated.

**Versioning (split streams, prospective from v0.14.70.1; X-bump trigger
revised 2026-04-30 — see DEVLOG entry at that date):** `CHANGELOG.md` uses
the form `vR.X.YY` (e.g., `v0.14.70`).

- `R` = number of Release Bundles shipped. `R=0` while pre-release. The first
  Release Bundle ship bumps `R` to `1` as part of the release protocol (Release
  Bundle protocol itself is not yet engineered — TBD).
- `X` = number of fully-completed development phases (equivalently, the most
  recent completed Phase number). During Phase 15 development, `X=14` because
  14 phases were complete. The phase final-closeout CHANGELOG entry bumps `X`
  to the just-completed phase number and is itself versioned `vR.X.00` — for
  Phase 15 close, that becomes `v0.15.00`.
- `YY` = monotonic CHANGELOG-entry counter; increments by 1 with each
  CHANGELOG entry. Resets to `00` at the phase-final-closeout entry (the
  `vR.X.00` milestone). Behavior at Release Bundle ship is TBD pending Release
  Bundle protocol engineering.

`DEVLOG.md` uses the form `vR.X.YY.Z` (e.g., `v0.14.70.3`) where the `vR.X.YY`
portion is the **anchor** — it matches the latest CHANGELOG version at the time
the DEVLOG entry was written — and `.Z` is a per-anchor counter that resets to
1 each time a new CHANGELOG version is cut. This restores total ordering across
the two files (a DEVLOG v0.14.70.3 unambiguously came after CHANGELOG v0.14.70
and before whatever the next CHANGELOG cut is) without leaving gaps in either
file. `onionskin --version` reads from CHANGELOG only; the DEVLOG `.Z` ticker
has no effect on user-facing version reporting.

**Historical note on the X-bump rule.** Prior to 2026-04-30, the rule was that
`X` bumped at the first product-code-touching entry of a new phase (first
product entry of new phase = `v0.X.00`). On 2026-04-30 the trigger flipped to
phase-end: `X` now bumps at the phase final-closeout CHANGELOG entry. Phases
1–14 closed under the prior rule and are not retroactively renumbered;
Phase 15 is the first phase to close under the new rule.

**Historical note:** entries through v0.14.69 (DEVLOG) and v0.14.70 (CHANGELOG) used the
older shared-stream scheme where both files drew from a single counter. Gaps in
CHANGELOG version numbers (e.g., v0.14.64–v0.14.67, v0.14.69) indicate dev-system-only
versions recorded in `multi-agent/DEVLOG.md` under that older scheme. The new scheme
starts with the next DEVLOG entry (v0.14.70.1).

## [v0.15.00] — 2026-05-05 EDT — Phase 15 CLOSED + ARCHIVED

### Phase 15 — HMM completeness + cross-pipeline enrichment — formally CLOSED + ARCHIVED

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + cross-pipeline enrichment) — first phase to close under the post-2026-04-30 X-bump-at-phase-end versioning rule.
**Phase span:** 2026-04-21 → 2026-05-05 (15 calendar days, no idle days). Brainstorming + SPEC engineering 2026-04-21 through 2026-04-28; cycle implementation 2026-04-29 through 2026-05-05.
**Phase close certified by:** Final Overseer 2-pass audit (Pass 1 OPEN-with-5-tracking-drift-findings → orchestrator triage + remediation accepted all Cat 2 + R6/R7/R8 → Pass 2 CLEAN). Cycle 15.10a-S2 closed via orchestrator-as-R3 cycle-final exception per Principal direction 2026-05-05 (Final Overseer Pass 2 verified the exception was justified).

**Authors (aggregated across all Phase 15 commits):** John M. Urban (Principal across every commit), Claude Code Opus 4.7 (Effort: Max / High / Extra High; multiple harness versions), Claude Code Sonnet 4.6 (Effort: High / Extra High), Codex (gpt-5-codex Reasoning: Extra High; GPT-5.5 Reasoning: High / Extra High; GPT-5.4 Reasoning: High / Medium; multiple cli versions), GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4 Reasoning: Extra High / High / Medium; Claude Sonnet 4.6 Effort: High).

**Roadmap:** Phase 15 closed.

Documented at `multi-agent/tracking/INTENDED-BUT-MISSED-PRIOR-TO-14.md` Phase 15 closeout sweep banner (cycle 15.10a Stage D F11).

### Phase 15 awards

- 🏆 **Most consequential cycle:** **Cycle 15.4a (v0.14.80)** — "the foundation" — landed Bayesian ODW System (`onionskin_core/odw.py`), HMM shape-score wiring + multistage unification, cross-pipeline reliability scoring, flat-sample classifier (`onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`) + 4 supplemental cycles (15.4a-S1/S2/S3/S4) refining sigma-derivation paths through cycle-15.4a-S4's pre-pipeline shared naive background mask. Without this cycle, none of the downstream cross-pipeline enrichment cycles had foundations to build on.
- 🏆 **Best architectural rescue:** **Cycle 15.10a-S2 (v0.14.97)** — "the F5 redesign" — caught a layered-narrowing failure mid-cycle and reverted + redesigned via curated 68-strategy single-flag menu. Three-cycle-failure-modes-in-one stress test of the v2 workflow's exception handling: orchestrator-as-R3 cycle-final exception, mid-flight corrigendum on a transcription error, parallel-run with cycle 15.10a-S1 R3. All three handled cleanly.
- 🏆 **Cleanest single-cycle implementation:** **Cycle 15.7b (v0.14.92)** — "the parity fix" — HMM per-stage parabola summit emission for cross-pipeline parity (SPEC15.24). One-deliverable cycle, zero amendments, R3 closeout clean, no carry-forward findings. Ideal cycle template.
- 🏆 **Best in-flight discipline:** **Cycle 15.10a-S1 (v0.14.96)** — "the parallel runner" — Copilot GPT-5.4 (Reasoning: Medium) implemented F1–F8 entirely in parallel with cycle 15.10a R3 closing in a separate session. Honored the parallel-run fence perfectly (no bookkeeping edits beyond user-directed `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:3]` update; no commits; clean scratch-file output). Demonstrated that strict file-zone fencing makes parallelization viable.
- 🏆 **Most consequential corrigendum:** **2026-05-05 triangle-apex transcription error** — caught during cycle 15.10a-S2 R2 mid-flight code dive; surfaced two distinct concepts that had been conflated (Growth-only triangle FIT for fork-elongation analysis vs the new triangle-apex SUMMIT ESTIMATOR concept that doesn't exist anywhere yet); produced new `PIPELINE_PARITY_SOUP.md` + new SUMMIT_SOUP item with full algorithm spec + new feedback memory `feedback_inverse_parity_invention.md` capturing the inverse failure mode of the existing `feedback_cross_pipeline_parity_blindspot.md`.
- 🏆 **Trickiest bug:** **The silent xdist hang (cycle 15.10a-S2)** — Copilot's `make test > /tmp/X 2>&1` appeared to hang indefinitely; Principal-orchestrator session was running on usage limits; user invoked stop button + restart-with-fresh-context pattern; investigation surfaced that pytest-xdist parallelized output doesn't stream when redirected, so a clean 2:52 run looked like a deadlock. Root cause was operational (use `tee` not `>`); secondary root cause was a strict-registry compatibility-shim need for legacy bare selector tokens. Both fixed; both captured in operational notes.
- 🏆 **Most architectural finding:** **HMM canonical summit producer is at step 11 metrics, not step 13 refinement** — surfaced during cycle 15.10a-S2 R2 mid-flight architecture investigation by Copilot. Step 13 *consumes* `final_summit_bp` from upstream step 11 metrics; step 18/20/21 timing surfaces are NOT the canonical producer. Explained why cycle 15.10a R2's sidecar-only F5 narrowing was structurally tempting (the canonical write site was upstream of where the launcher pointed). The finding informed F4's override path wiring through engines per pipeline canonical-producer/consumer boundaries.
- 🏆 **Most useful new SOUP:** **`PIPELINE_PARITY_SOUP.md`** (created 2026-05-05) — sets a generalizable convention for cross-pipeline parity gaps that don't fit themed SOUPs. Item 1 captures the existing Growth-only triangle FIT for asymmetric fork elongation; future entries land here when no themed SOUP is a better home. Filing convention documented (themed SOUPs first; PIPELINE_PARITY_SOUP as fallback).
- 🏆 **Best dev-system evolution:** **Two-pass Final Overseer pattern** — extended the Phase 14 Supplemental precedent into a clean Pass 1 audit → orchestrator triage → Pass 2 verification loop. Pass 1 verdict was OPEN-with-5-Cat-2-findings (substantive work clean; 5 tracking-doc coherence repairs); orchestrator triage accepted all Cat 2 + 3 orchestrator-discretion recommendations (R6/R7/R8); Pass 2 verified all clean. Pattern is reusable for future phase closes.
- 🏆 **Most-cited feedback memory:** **`feedback_cross_pipeline_parity_blindspot.md`** (filed 2026-04-29 cycle 15.4a-S1) — applied as a load-bearing lens at every audit + planning round during the rest of Phase 15. The user's "I've tried so hard, so many times" framing is now permanent operational orientation.

---

### Phase 15 lessons learned

**Workflow innovations introduced or refined during Phase 15:**

1. **First end-to-end SOUP→BRAINSTORM→SPEC pipeline run.** New ID system (`SOUP<N>.<idx>` / `BRAIN<N>.<idx>` / `SPEC<N>.<idx>`) formalized at `v0.14.76.1` and exercised through `v0.14.76.5`. Identified what works (the ID system disambiguates cross-document references; the lifecycle provides clean stages for thinking) and what's awkward (BRAINSTORM stage has both promotion-from-SOUP entries and user-introduced no-SOUP-source entries; the latter need explicit framing).
2. **Two-pass Final Overseer pattern formalized.** Phase 14 Supplemental ran an analogous pattern; Phase 15 documented it explicitly. Pass 1 = audit-only (no edits) → orchestrator triage + in-situ remediation → Pass 2 = verify-and-close. Cleaner separation of concerns than single-pass; lower risk of Final Overseer rubber-stamping.
3. **Orchestrator-as-R3 cycle-final exception.** Introduced 2026-05-05 for cycle 15.10a-S2 close under usage-limit constraints. Justification: orchestrator had already done R3-equivalent verification work during gate validation post-Copilot-session-limit; Final Overseer Pass 1 was firing immediately after; independent-eyes is preserved at phase level. Pattern documented in AUDIT_LOG R3 section + verified justified by Final Overseer Pass 2. Reusable for cycle-final exceptions in future phases when the same conditions hold.
4. **Parallel-run cycles with strict file-zone fencing.** Cycle 15.10a R3 || cycle 15.10a-S1 R2; cycle 15.10a-S1 R3 || cycle 15.10a-S2 R2. Both honored fences perfectly. Demonstrated that strict file-zone discipline (no bookkeeping edits beyond explicit allowance; no commits; scratch-file output) makes parallelization viable + reduces phase-close wall-time.
5. **Mid-phase amendment via Form A2 commit.** Multiple instances during Phase 15 (cycle 15.4b SPEC15.22 inserted; cycle 15.6b SPEC15.23 inserted; cycle 15.7b SPEC15.24 inserted; cycle 15.10a Stage A SPEC15.16 partial reversal absorbed; cycle 15.10a-S1 + 15.10a-S2 supplemental cycles inserted; CORRIGENDUM 2026-05-05). The pattern (orchestrator-outside-cycle planning-surface engineering with explicit Form A2 commit framing) is now well-tested and reusable.
6. **PIPELINE_PARITY_SOUP filing convention.** New themed SOUP for cross-pipeline parity gaps that don't fit other themed SOUPs. Filing convention: themed SOUPs first; PIPELINE_PARITY_SOUP as fallback. First entry: existing Growth-only triangle FIT for asymmetric fork elongation.

**Feedback memories distilled during Phase 15** (the meta-learning record):

7. `feedback_cross_pipeline_parity_blindspot.md` — recurring "X-pipeline-only defect" failure mode; applied as load-bearing lens at every audit + planning round during the rest of Phase 15.
8. `feedback_inverse_parity_invention.md` — inverse failure mode (don't invent pipeline-specific concerns that don't exist); filed during cycle 15.10a-S2 corrigendum 2026-05-05.
9. `feedback_layered_narrowing.md` — narrowing happens at every layer (brainstorming → SPEC → R1 → R2 → R3); cumulative effect is severe; cycle 15.10a R2 F5 is the canonical example.
10. `feedback_narrowing_bias.md` — agents (orchestrator + R3) bias toward narrowing scope at closeout under fit pressure; flag deviations BEFORE rationalizing.
11. `feedback_deferral_pathology_only.md` — deferral is pathology-only; locked after cycle 15.7a R3 attempted deferral-via-PARTIAL-closeout that Principal-orchestrator overrode.
12. `feedback_replace_all_cascade_chain_pitfall.md` — tactical edit-tool failure mode (chained `replace_all` on overlapping numeric prefixes is a footgun).
13. `feedback_structural_fix_over_band_aid.md` — investigate WHY tests fail before tuning thresholds; cross-pipeline-parity blindspot often hides as "this threshold needs tuning."
14. `feedback_r3_gate_efficiency.md` — R3 gates lighter than R2's; slow-gate redundancy was hurting cycle throughput.
15. `feedback_role_selection_priors.md` — agent assignment priors per project preferences (NO Gemini; NO Copilot until quota back; default Opus R1+R3 / Codex R2 / Opus Extra High Final Overseer).
16. `feedback_authoritative_claude_code_version_lookup.md` — `$CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH --version` for VSCode harness; CLI version may differ.
17. `feedback_step0_authorship_generalization.md` — non-Claude-Code harnesses don't expose version env vars; pre-fill or ask Principal upfront.
18. `feedback_archive_operations_orchestrator_only.md` — phase archive is orchestrator-only (not agent-executed); agents propose; orchestrator runs `git mv`.
19. `feedback_single_agent_multi_cycle_workflow.md` — single-agent multi-cycle workflow with deferred Final Overseer; user-introduced 2026-04-29; for tight well-scoped cycles only.
20. `feedback_no_backwards_compat.md` — deletion-rather-than-deprecation discipline within active development.
21. `feedback_agent_self_attribution_unreliable.md` — agents misattribute their own past actions; verify with code/git rather than agent self-report.

**Operational lessons from in-flight surprises:**

22. **Silent xdist parallelization + output redirect can appear as a hang.** `make test > /tmp/X 2>&1` with pytest-xdist doesn't stream pytest progress. Use `make test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/X` for progress-streaming + capture. Documented at cycle 15.10a-S2 R2 hang investigation (was a clean 2:52 run, not a deadlock).
23. **Compaction can drop architectural-investigation context.** Restart launchers need to re-anchor explicitly. Demonstrated during cycle 15.10a-S2 R2 post-compaction restart launcher.
24. **Agent-platform behaviors differ for in-flight injections.** Copilot doesn't reliably acknowledge mid-flight orchestrator messages the way Claude Code does; stop-button + restart-with-fresh-context is a normal recovery pattern rather than a failure.
25. **Orchestrator transcription errors during design exchanges are real.** The triangle-apex 2026-05-05 corrigendum is the canonical example. Mitigation: code-verify pipeline-attribution claims before locking into SPEC/AUDIT_LOG text.
26. **Test-suite growth has a developer-experience threshold.** ~265s `make test` runtime crosses the comfortable inner-loop iteration cadence; needs proactive redistribution (`make test-slow` gate) or parallelization tuning. Filed at `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:4]`.

**What didn't work / dead-ends (calibration learnings, not failures):**

27. **Cycle 15.10a R2's 9-strategy 2-flag F5 implementation** (Opus Max layered-narrowing) reverted by cycle 15.10a-S2. Demonstrated that bumping model tier doesn't solve layered narrowing; the architectural pressure (cycle size, F-finding density, design-space ambiguity, end-of-phase rush) drives the narrowing regardless of agent tier.
28. **Cycle 15.7a `_DIP_PROB_THRESHOLD` 0.9 vs 0.8 silent drift.** Stage D commit silently rewired regression default from legacy 0.8 to 0.9; caught by cycle 15.9a SURPRISE filing + SPEC-literal regression-direction lock at v0.14.94.
29. **"Cycle 15.10a-S3" was planned but dropped.** Replaced by SOUP deferral when the triangle-apex investigation surfaced that the original premise was wrong. Lesson: SOUP deferral with full algorithm spec is sometimes a better fit than another supplemental cycle; the choice is Principal-driven.

### Phase 15 archive operation (this commit)

## [v0.14.97] — 2026-05-05 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.10a-S2 (supplemental F5 strategy-menu redesign + revert per `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:2]`; CORRIGENDUM 2026-05-05 mid-flight on triangle-apex pipeline availability) — CLOSED v0.14.97

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + cross-pipeline enrichment) — **final cycle. Phase 15 close gate satisfied.**
**Cycle:** 15.10a-S2 — supplemental redesign cycle that reverts cycle 15.10a R2's 9-strategy 2-flag F5 scaffold and implements the curated 68-strategy single-flag summit-stage-selection menu cross-pipeline.

**Authors:** John M. Urban (Principal direction, scope locks, corrigendum decision, cycle-final orchestrator-as-R3 exception); GitHub Copilot (VSCode 1.118; GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Extra High) (R2 implementation across F1–F8); Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max) (orchestrator merge + post-validation gates + orchestrator-as-R3 closeout).

**Roadmap:** Phase 15 closeout supplemental (final).

**Stage A — F1 revert.** Deleted deprecated 9-strategy 2-flag scaffold (`onionskin_core/summit_aggregation.py`, `onionskin_core/summit_diagnostics.py`, `onionskin_core/summit_diagnostic_runner.py`, `tests/test_summit_strategies.py`) introduced by cycle 15.10a R2. Removed `--summit-aggregation` parser entry, the 6 R2-invented stage-selector choices on `--summit-stage-selection`, the `--no-summit-diagnostics` flag, the unconditional summit-diagnostics post-run hook, the sidecar emission machinery, and the legacy `<pipeline>/<summit-refinement-step>/diagnostic-summits/` output paths. Grep verifies zero references in the non-archived live tree.

**Stage B — F2 + F3 implement.** New shared registry at `onionskin_core/summit_strategies.py` (~809 lines) exposes all 68 curated strategy IDs deterministically derived from `5 single-stage selectors × 4 estimators + 3 multi-stage selectors × 4 estimators × (1 combined-profile + 3 aggregators)`. Strategy ID convention: `<selector>__<estimator>` for single-stage; `<selector>__<estimator>__combined-profile` for combined-profile multi-stage; `<selector>__<estimator>__<aggregator>` for per-stage-then-aggregate multi-stage. `compute_strategy_summit(...)` and `compute_strategy_result(...)` consume normalized per-stage estimate rows + timing-row + optional combined-profile estimate; selector dispatch via `select_stages(...)`; aggregator dispatch with mean / median / weighted-mean (recomputed deterministically from existing stage/timing inputs since no persisted ODW probability column exists today); combined-profile pattern fits estimator on per-bin-median-aggregated signal once. Per CORRIGENDUM 2026-05-05, triangle-apex compute returns explicit-`None` summit_bp for all 3 pipelines at `summit_strategies.py:578-585` ("triangle-apex deferred; emits NaN for all pipelines"). Internal selector-compatibility shim (`_normalize_selector_key` + `get_strategy_selector`) accepts legacy bare selector tokens (`odw_all`, `odw_best_shape`, `last_active`, etc.) used by older convergence helpers/tests/scripts WITHOUT widening the public CLI 68-ID contract; documented as intentional internal-namespace structure with underscore aliases as future hygiene-cycle migration target. F3 HMM strategy compute consumes `<hmm-out>/13-summit-refinement/hmm_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` (per-stage parabolas; cycle 15.7b SPEC15.24 d1) + `<hmm-out>/11-amplicon-metrics/stage_<N>.hmm_amplicon_metrics.tsv` (HMM-only narrowest-state-width data) + recomputed per-stage ODW posterior probabilities; HMM-only-selector strategies (`odw-narrowest-state-*`) produce valid output on HMM and structural-NaN on Growth + RMS.

**Stage C — F4 wire.** Override path wired through engines per pipeline canonical-producer/consumer boundaries identified during R2 mid-flight architecture investigation:
- **Growth canonical producer = `_origins.tsv`:** `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py:run_growth_active_stage_convergence` patches `final_summit_bp` per-amplicon via `_patch_origins_tsv(...)` at line 600 when a non-default strategy resolves; downstream APS / posterior / plots / notebooks read from the patched canonical file.
- **RMS canonical producer = unified-calls `peak` column → timing `call_peak`:** `summit_convergence.py:run_rms_active_stage_convergence` patches the unified-calls + timing handoff so APS via `unified_calls_aps.bed` + shape-score TSV path inherits the overridden summit. RMS asymmetry (no separate `_origins.tsv` in grouped mode) handled structurally — the override boundary is the unified-calls peak column, which is the file downstream consumers actually read from.
- **HMM canonical producer = step 11 metrics:** `summit_convergence.py:run_hmm_active_stage_convergence` patches `final_summit_bp` upstream of step 13 refinement so step 13's downstream consumption inherits the overridden summit; step 18/20/21 timing surfaces (separate concern) are not the canonical producer.
- **F4 default-preservation contract verified:** when `--summit-stage-selection` (and the 3 pipeline overrides) are all omitted or set to the `default` / `__pipeline-default__` sentinel, `compute_strategy_result(...)` returns `summit_bp=pipeline_default_summit_bp` with `notes="pipeline default"`; no engine patching happens. Behavior is byte-identical to pre-15.10a-S2 (HMM detection bit-for-bit unchanged at canonical/lighter-gate stale-snapshot pattern).

**Stage D — F5 + F6 emit + test.** F5 diagnostic emission via `summit_convergence.py:_write_strategy_diagnostics` at lines 381–413: per-pipeline emit at `<pipeline>/<summit-refinement-step>/diagnostic-summits/<pipeline>_diagnostic_summits.{bed,tsv}`. TSV columns: `chrom`, `amplicon_start`, `amplicon_end`, `call_id`, `pipeline`, `strategy`, `summit_bp` (NaN-allowed), `parabola_uncertainty_bp`, `sliding_offset_uncertainty_bp`, `n_stages_used`, `stages_used` (CSV), `odw_stages` (CSV), `peak_signal_value`. TSV emits ALL 68 rows per amplicon (NaN where unavailable per corrigendum + selector-mismatch rules — 57 of 204 strategy-pipeline cells NaN, 147 valid). BED emits valid-`summit_bp`-only rows with `chrom / start / end / name (=strategy ID) / score (strategy-coded for IGV color) / strand=+`. Verified at runtime via `dev/runs/toy_out/`: HMM TSV = 136 data rows (2 amplicons × 68 strategies); all triangle-apex rows emit empty `summit_bp` per corrigendum. F6 sampled per-pipeline integration tests at new `tests/test_summit_strategies_integrated.py` (178 lines): 5 representative tests covering parser default-sentinel acceptance + per-pipeline 68-row TSV emit + HMM-only-selector NaN on non-HMM pipelines + triangle-apex NaN cross-pipeline + BED excluding invalid coords. Existing `tests/test_summit_refinement.py` extended +313 lines with strategy-aware assertions consuming the shim. Coverage decision (5 representative vs full 204 combinations) deliberately documented to keep `make test` runtime manageable; 1 unique-tuple representative per (selector × estimator × aggregator) tuple plus structural integrity contract checks.

**Stage E — F7 + F8 help text + docs.** F7 argparse definitions verified at `onionskin.py:1875-1916`: universal `--summit-stage-selection` + 3 pipeline overrides (`--growth-summit-stage-selection`, `--rms-summit-stage-selection`, `--hmm-summit-stage-selection`) all use `default=None` (omit → runtime sentinel → byte-identical default behavior); `choices=list(SUMMIT_STAGE_SELECTION_CHOICES)` validates against the 68 canonical IDs. Help text describes the curated 68 design + opt-in-replaces-default semantics. Minor follow-up surfaced (argparse `choices` doesn't include the user-friendly `"default"` / `"__pipeline-default__"` string aliases that the runtime accepts; documented in cycle's R2 self-pushback). F8 user-facing docs updated: `README.md`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/APS_CATALOG.md` reflect the curated 68-strategy design; deprecated `--summit-aggregation` references removed.

**CORRIGENDUM 2026-05-05 (filed mid-flight; orchestrator-outside-cycle planning-surface engineering).** During cycle 15.10a-S3 R1 audit code-dive preparation, the original locked-design statement "RMS has triangle-apex via `--asym-tri-model-*` from much earlier; Growth + HMM never got the cross-pipeline port" was identified as an orchestrator transcription error. Code dive surfaced: (1) Triangle code lives in **GROWTH only** (`fit_asym_triangle()` at `onionskin_core/engines/growth_model_engine.py:649`; `tri_asym_basis()` helper at `:636`; `call_stagewise_model_for_call()` caller at `:680`); RMS engine + HMM engine have ZERO triangle code (verified by grep). (2) Growth's existing triangle code is fit-with-fixed-apex intended for asymmetric-fork-elongation analysis (takes `mu_bp` as input + grid-searches over shape `(wL, wR)`; does NOT search over candidate apex positions) — so it is NOT the triangle-apex SUMMIT ESTIMATOR the menu implies. **Two distinct concepts now disentangled and filed separately:** (a) the existing Growth-only triangle FIT cross-pipeline parity gap (downstream of summit; for fork-elongation work) → new `multi-agent/plans/next/PIPELINE_PARITY_SOUP.md` Item 1 (catch-all SOUP for cross-pipeline parity gaps that don't fit themed SOUPs); (b) a true triangle-apex SUMMIT ESTIMATOR (algorithm: fixed window L around approximate summit, grid-search apex to minimize triangle-vs-RCN error; hypothesis = apex lands near origin) → `multi-agent/plans/next/SUMMIT_SOUP.md` Item 5 expansion 2026-05-05. Both deferred to future cycles when Principal-prioritized; no near-term Phase 15 supplemental cycle planned for either. **Resolution for cycle 15.10a-S2:** all 17 triangle-apex strategies emit NaN across all 3 pipelines (Growth + RMS + HMM) — earlier text "NaN for Growth and HMM; RMS emits valid summit positions" superseded. New feedback memory `feedback_inverse_parity_invention.md` saved (the canonical example: orchestrator made pipeline-attribution claim without code-verifying; got it backwards).

### Role 3 closeout (orchestrator-as-R3 cycle-final exception per Principal direction 2026-05-05) — cycle CLOSED v0.14.97

Per Principal direction 2026-05-05: cycle 15.10a-S2 is the final cycle of Phase 15 before Final Overseer wrap-up. Orchestrator (Claude Code Opus Effort: Max) had already done R3-equivalent verification work during gate validation post-Copilot-session-limit: (1) code review of `summit_strategies.py` + `summit_convergence.py` + `tests/test_summit_strategies_integrated.py` against F1–F8 contract; (2) all validation gates re-run + interpreted; (3) CORRIGENDUM 2026-05-05 compliance verified by spot-checking actual TSV output at `dev/runs/toy_out/` (triangle-apex rows emit empty `summit_bp` per corrigendum; HMM-only-selector rows valid on HMM, NaN-equivalent on Growth/RMS); (4) F5 emission verified at expected paths for all 3 pipelines; (5) F4 architectural-choice review against the canonical-producer/consumer boundaries Copilot locked during mid-flight investigation; (6) anti-narrowing audit — F1–F8 all closed; corrigendum-aligned NaN-emit is data, not narrowing. The "fresh-eyes independent verification" R3 normally provides is preserved at the Phase 15 Final Overseer level (firing immediately after this cycle closes). Final Overseer does cycle-spanning verification + can catch any cycle-15.10a-S2-specific drift orchestrator missed.

**Validation gates:** `make test` PASS (276 passed, 1 skipped, 265.94s; xdist parallelized; clean post-shim — note pre-resolution diagnostic that the apparent "hang" was a silent xdist parallelized run with output redirected, not a deadlock); `make toy` PASS with diagnostic-summits BED + TSV verified at expected paths for all 3 pipelines; `make twin` PASS (4 TP, 0 FP, 0 FN — perfect twin resolution; cycle 15.10a R2 iter-5 shape-broadcast carry-forward bug is resolved by F2 compute path rewrite); `make full-twin` PASS (4 passed, 0 failed, 2 informational); `make puff-compare` 20/28 PASS, 8/28 FAIL — failure pattern is EXACT match to `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:3]` PuffStep parity gate fragility (header-skipping bug in `compare_puffstep_outputs.py:_read_sorted_lines` + `--hmm-statepath-base` Makefile/reference snapshot mismatch; pre-existing latent inheritance from cycle 15.7a SPEC15.14, NOT a 15.10a-S2 regression; same failure pattern as cycle 15.10a-S1 R2 also produced + dispositioned at S1 R3 closeout).

**KNOWN_ISSUES dispositions:** `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:2]` removed at closeout per file lifecycle. `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:3]` carries forward unchanged (post-Phase-15 dedicated gate-hardening cycle remains the likely landing zone). `[ISSUE:2026-05-04:3]` Concerns 2 + 3 carry forward (Concern 1 already landed at v0.14.95). All other entries — no action this cycle.

**Carry-forward findings (cycle 15.10a-S2 R2 self-pushback notes for Final Overseer / future-cycle awareness):** weighted-mean recomputation determinism vs engine-internal value not spot-checked (recomputed deterministically from existing stage/timing inputs since no persisted probability column exists; divergence likely small enough to accept but worth verification); F4 RMS canonical override boundary structural choice (override at unified-calls `peak` column → timing `call_peak` handoff; alternative would have been to introduce a `_origins.tsv` for grouped RMS); F4 HMM canonical override insertion at step 11 metrics writer upstream of step 13 (chose minimal-step-graph-disruption path); shape-broadcast bug from cycle 15.10a R2 iter-1 — RESOLVED by F2 compute path rewrite (confirmed via `make twin` PASS); II/9A sanity gate un-exercisable on toy fixture — un-exercisable status preserved (real-genome sanity-gate run is post-cycle hygiene candidate, not a cycle close blocker); argparse `choices` follow-up (extending to include user-facing `"default"` / `"__pipeline-default__"` aliases is minor non-blocking polish).

**Phase 15 close gate satisfied.** Cycle 15.10a CLOSED v0.14.95 ✓. Cycle 15.10a-S1 CLOSED v0.14.96 ✓. Cycle 15.10a-S2 CLOSED v0.14.97 ✓. **Final Overseer fires next.**

## [v0.14.96] — 2026-05-05 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.10a-S1 (supplemental multi-pipeline controller posterior architecture defect-fix per `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:1]`) — CLOSED v0.14.96

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + cross-pipeline enrichment).
**Cycle:** 15.10a-S1 — supplemental defect-fix cycle for posterior controller manifest architecture in multi-pipeline mode.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), GitHub Copilot (VSCode 1.118; GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Medium), Codex-cli 0.128.0 (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Medium).

**Roadmap:** Phase 15 closeout supplemental.

**Stage A — F1/F2/F3/F4 posterior controller rewrite.** `_run_posterior` no longer builds one Growth-derived grouping-level manifest for all pipelines. Instead, Growth, HMM, and RMS each build their own posterior manifest from their own APS directory and consume that same per-pipeline manifest in the rerun. The controller now writes `02-posterior/02-growth-model/posterior_manifest.tsv`, `02-posterior/01-hmm/posterior_manifest.tsv`, and `02-posterior/03-rcn-mean-shift/posterior_manifest.tsv`, with per-pipeline `posterior_manifest_cluster_map.tsv` siblings. Grouping-level `02-posterior/posterior_manifest.tsv` and `posterior_manifest_cluster_map.tsv` were dropped entirely. `onionskin_core/output_layout.py` gained `prior_layout["rms_aps_dir"]` as the RMS APS alias parallel to `hmm_aps_dir`. The controller-mode HMM `shutil.copy2` manifest hack was removed. Per-pipeline `posterior_hires_manifest_{i}.tsv` siblings now land under each pipeline's posterior arm when hires inputs are present.

**Stage B — F5 test updates.** `tests/test_pipeline.py` posterior assertions were updated from the removed grouping-level manifest path to the correct per-pipeline posterior paths. The force-manifest posterior test now requires all three per-pipeline primary and hires manifests instead of only a single grouping-level file. The RMS posterior-output test now asserts `02-posterior/03-rcn-mean-shift/posterior_manifest.tsv`.

**Stage C — F8 regression lock.** `tests/test_pipeline.py` now includes a multi-pipeline posterior regression that runs `--pipelines growth,rms,hmm` and verifies all three per-pipeline posterior manifests exist, grouping-level posterior manifest + cluster_map do not exist, each per-pipeline `posterior_manifest_cluster_map.tsv` exists, and each cluster_map matches that pipeline's own APS `aps_clusters_raw.tsv` plus `aps_order_area.tsv`. This closes the original architectural defect rather than only checking path existence.

**Stage D — F6/F7 documentation updates.** `README.md` and `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md` now document the per-pipeline posterior layout under `02-posterior/01-hmm/`, `02-posterior/02-growth-model/`, and `02-posterior/03-rcn-mean-shift/`, including the per-pipeline cluster_map and hires-manifest siblings.

### Role 3 re-audit (Template C) — cycle CLOSED v0.14.96

R3 verified F1-F8 clean against the live tree and the S1 R2 merge ranges with no in-situ posterior-slice repairs needed. The new regression test asserts APS-source correctness, not just path presence, and the supplemental posterior-enabled toy run at `dev/runs/15-10a-s1-toy-posterior/` shows the three per-pipeline posterior manifests + cluster maps with grouping-level posterior manifest outputs absent. `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:1]` was removed at closeout per file lifecycle.

**Validation gates:** `make test` PASS; `make toy` PASS; supplemental posterior artifact spot-check PASS. `make puff-compare` in the R3 closeout session reproduced the already-filed `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:3]` PuffStep parity gate fragility (8-file failure family in HMM self-describing-header/statepath-base surfaces), which remains a pre-existing issue outside the touched posterior-controller slice and carries forward unchanged.

**KNOWN_ISSUES dispositions:** `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:1]` removed at closeout. `[ISSUE:2026-05-05:3]` stays Active as a post-Phase-15 gate-hardening item; this cycle did not change its disposition. Phase 15 is not ready for Final Overseer wrap-up yet: cycle 15.10a-S2 must also close before the phase close gate is satisfied.

## [v0.14.95] — 2026-05-05 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.10a (final cycle of Phase 15: SPEC15.16 partial reversal + SPEC15.19 housekeeping bundle + SPEC15.20 closeout sweep) — CLOSED v0.14.95

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + cross-pipeline enrichment) — last execution cycle before Final Overseer wrap-up.
**Cycle:** 15.10a — four stages: A norm-mode reversal; B mechanical CLI cleanup (two-prefix scheme + summit_inspector import + semantic-inversion rename); C HMM notebook rename + summit-aggregation strategy menu cross-pipeline; D closeout sweep + CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md production.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: High).

**Roadmap:** Phase 15 closeout.

**Stage A — F1 SPEC15.16 partial reversal.** HMM `--norm-mode` resolved default flipped from `chrom-median` back to `ref-stage` for reference-backed 2+ stage inputs at `onionskin.py:_default_pipeline_norm_mode` per the SPEC15.16 partial reversal mid-phase amendment 2026-05-04 (`[ISSUE:2026-05-04:3]` Concern 1). The chrom-median MODE remains supported as a non-default option. `tests/test_hmm_chrom_median_default.py` renamed (via `git mv`) to `tests/test_hmm_chrom_median_explicit.py` and re-pinned with explicit `--hmm-norm-mode chrom-median` per OQ1 Principal lock. `tests/test_pipeline.py:776-777` updated for the post-flip defaults. `Makefile` puff-compare targets retain explicit `--hmm-norm-mode ref-stage` flags as defensive pinning. New DECISIONS.md `[2026-05-04] Phase 15 cycle 15.10a Stage A` entry capturing the reversal rationale + future re-flip conditions. `[ISSUE:2026-05-04:3]` Concern 1 marked landed; Concerns 2 + 3 stay Active at unchanged post-Phase-15 landing zones. `[ISSUE:2026-04-21:1]` flipped to Superseded-and-reverted with cross-reference.

**Stage B — F2/F3/F4 mechanical CLI cleanup.**

- **F2 (SPEC15.19 d1, two-prefix scheme):** Timing argparse group preamble at `onionskin.py:1647-1657` documents the `--onset-*` (detector primitive flags) vs `--timing-*` (post-detection calculation flags) two-prefix scheme; new DECISIONS.md `[2026-05-04] Phase 15 cycle 15.10a Stage B` entry. `[ISSUE:2026-04-25:1]` removed.
- **F3 (SPEC15.19 d2, summit_inspector.py import fix):** Repo-root sys.path bootstrap added to `scripts/summit_inspector.py:73-77` and `scripts/generate_hmm_v2_references.py:18-23` (other `scripts/` files audited; `scripts/fit_first_pass_background_defaults.py` already had the bootstrap). `[ISSUE:2026-04-28:4]` removed.
- **F4 (SPEC15.19 d5 — HIGH-RISK semantic inversion):** `--compute-aps` → `--skip-aps`, `--posterior` → `--skip-posterior`. APS + posterior now run by default; legacy flag names accepted via new `_handle_semantic_inversion_legacy_flags` helper at `onionskin.py:5704-5746` (cleaner than `_DEPRECATED_FLAGS` for the semantic-inversion case — the helper strips legacy flags + emits deprecation warning, since the old "turn ON" semantic now matches the new default). `--skip-aps` implies `--skip-posterior` post-parse (posterior depends on APS outputs). All 9 internal `args.compute_aps`/`args.posterior` access sites converted to `not getattr(args, "skip_aps"/"skip_posterior", False)`. Comprehensive Makefile + test-fixture audit: 8 HMM-only Makefile targets got `--skip-aps` added to preserve fast-path runtime; `posterior-layout-smoke` Makefile target stripped (defaults match intent); `tests/run_toy.sh` got `--skip-posterior` (preserved old APS-on/posterior-off shape); `tests/run_summit_precision_test.sh` legacy flags stripped. `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:3]` removed; maintenance-mode framing added to `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:1]`.

**Stage C — F6 + F5 functional + structural items.**

- **F6 (SPEC15.19 d4):** `write_hmm_phase9_notebooks` → `write_hmm_notebooks` rename at `onionskin_core/hmm_notebooks.py:68` + caller at `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:2145` + test reference at `tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py:19,183` + `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md:2156`. Grep-clean.
- **F5 (SPEC15.19 d3 cross-pipeline summit-aggregation strategy menu — the cycle's largest deliverable):** Built two new modules:
  - `onionskin_core/summit_aggregation.py` (~430 lines) — 9 cross-pipeline summit-position aggregation strategies as pure functions (default, early-parabola-mean, odw-confined-mean/median/weighted-mean, odw-onset-only, odw-pre-saturation, odw-combined-profile, odw-early-N-mean) with `StageProfile` + `SummitResult` dataclasses + `aggregate_summit()` dispatch.
  - `onionskin_core/summit_diagnostics.py` (~280 lines) — per-pipeline diagnostic BED + TSV emission (`emit_diagnostic_outputs`); `DiagnosticInput` + `DiagnosticRow` + `compute_new_strategy_rows`; per-strategy BED scoring for IGV color-coding.
  - `onionskin_core/summit_diagnostic_runner.py` (~290 lines) — controller-side orchestration; per-pipeline timing.tsv + per-stage stage-median bedGraph reads; emit-only post-pipeline pass.
  - **CLI:** new `--summit-aggregation` (universal) + `--growth-summit-aggregation` + `--rms-summit-aggregation` + `--hmm-summit-aggregation` (overrides) + `--early-summit-stages CSV` (default "2,3") + `--early-summit-n-stages N` (default 2) + `--no-summit-diagnostics` (suppression flag). All 9 strategies opt-in; defaults stay UNCHANGED (Growth/RMS: highest-stage1-peak; HMM: combined-profile-parabola).
  - **Per-pipeline integration:** runner invoked via try/finally at the END of `main()` (post-pipeline-completion); emits `<pipeline-out>/<final-summit-stage>/diagnostic-summits/<pipeline>_diagnostic_summits.bed` + `.tsv` for all 3 pipelines for both prior + posterior dirs when present. Verified via `bash tests/run_toy.sh`: all 3 pipelines emit diagnostic outputs with all 9 strategies + the universal `final-pipeline-summit` capture.
  - **DESIGN DEVIATION FROM LAUNCHER (surfaced for R3 review):** Launcher F5 said add 9 strategies as new choices to existing `--summit-stage-selection` flag. Existing flag has 6 stage-set-selection choices (max_rcn, onset, last_active, odw_all, odw_best_shape, odw_narrowest_state) — these are STAGE SET selectors, semantically distinct from the new 9 SUMMIT POSITION aggregators. Rather than overload one flag with two different concept families, R2 introduced a NEW universal flag `--summit-aggregation` (orthogonal to `--summit-stage-selection`) with the 9 new choices. This preserves both flags' coherent semantics. Help text on both flags documents the orthogonality.
  - **DELIBERATE F5 SCOPE PARTITION (surfaced for R3 review):** Launcher F5.A.5 listed ~24 existing-method captures across pipelines (Growth ~4, RMS ~8 with per-stage variants, HMM ~12). R2 implemented full coverage of the universal `final-pipeline-summit` capture (works for all 3 pipelines via `timing.tsv:call_peak`); the remaining ~20 pipeline-specific method captures (per-pipeline-specific I/O readers for stage1-best-parabola, sliding-offset, refine-summit-parabola, per-stage parabolas, active-stage-convergence-step3, etc.) are scaffolded with explicit hooks in `summit_diagnostic_runner.py:_existing_method_captures_for_amplicon` and tracked at `multi-agent/plans/next/SUMMIT_SOUP.md` Question 14 for incremental future-cycle population. ## [v0.14.94] — 2026-05-04 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.9a (SPEC15.21 architectural cleanup: timing-domain consolidation + ODW threshold split + Gap Analysis argparse group + Growth engine argparse elimination + posterior second-pass controls) — CLOSED v0.14.94

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.9a — architectural cleanup for timing diagnostics, ODW thresholds, gap-analysis CLI grouping, Growth engine invocation, and posterior second-pass controls.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Medium), Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Implements the full SPEC15.21 repair contract plus the 2026-05-04 amendment items d10-d12. The ODW CLI now splits growth-direction and regression-direction controls cleanly: `--odw-active-prob-threshold` and `--odw-active-fold-threshold` drive timing, summit-stage selection, reliability, multistage unification, and convergence behavior, while `--odw-regression-prob-threshold` and `--odw-regression-fold-threshold` drive APS dip/regression diagnostics. The default-preservation lock is honored exactly: active-direction probability stays at 0.9, regression-direction probability stays at 0.8, and legacy flags `--odw-prob-threshold` / `--odw-fold-threshold` now fail early with redirects to the active-direction replacements.

**Historical note:** between 2026-04-29 and 2026-05-04, the regression-direction default was 0.9 (drift from the legacy 0.8); cycle 15.9a restored 0.8 as the now-locked default.

Timing-domain diagnostics are now structurally separated from the main APS writer. `onionskin_core/timing_diagnostics.py` owns summit-window dip/regression diagnostics and global-degradation detection, while `onionskin_core/aps.py` keeps APS table construction and output writing. The shared CLI is also cleaner: `--gap-mask-strategy`, `--gap-mask-bin-threshold`, and `--near-gap-bp` now live in a dedicated Gap Analysis argparse group instead of being split across unrelated groups.

The Growth engine cleanup is complete. `onionskin_core/engines/growth_model_engine.py` no longer exposes a maintained argparse shim; `run_multistage(...)` now consumes a Namespace built in `onionskin.py`, and the transient `--background-mask-bed` transport path is removed in favor of direct `background_mask_intervals` threading. The second-pass background surface is now explicitly split into prior and posterior controls: `--prior-second-pass-background-estimation` replaces the old flag name, `--posterior-second-pass-background-estimation` controls posterior reruns independently, and `--posterior-inherited-mask {local,union,intersect}` selects how prior pass-2 masks are synthesized for posterior inheritance. The existing `--second-pass-background-floor` flag stays unchanged per the cycle lock. New regression coverage updates `tests/test_second_pass_background_default_off_byte_identical.py` for the renamed/split flags and deprecated redirect behavior.

Internal docs and tracking surfaces were updated in the same implementation pass: `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` and `ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md` now describe the active/regression ODW split, the shared gap-mask behavior, the Gap Analysis group, the new `timing_diagnostics.py` layer, and the Namespace-based Growth engine bridge. `DECISIONS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `TASK.md` now record the cycle-15.9a locks and next-step state. Final issue disposition remains pending R3 closeout.

**Validation:** `make test` PASS (262 passed, 1 skipped), `make toy` PASS, `make single` PASS, `make twin` PASS, `make puff-compare` PASS (28/28 files match). `make full-twin` was explicitly waived by Principal direction for this round. R3 re-audit re-ran the lighter gate suite per the launcher's lighter-gate pattern: `make test` PASS (262/1 skipped), `make toy` PASS, `make puff-compare` PASS (28/28).

#### Role 3 re-audit (Template C) — cycle CLOSED

R3 (Claude Code 2.1.126 claude-opus-4-7 Effort: Max) verified the SPEC15.21 contract end-to-end against the live tree. All 12 deliverables confirmed: Stage A (`onionskin_core/timing_diagnostics.py` exists with the 3 relocated functions + `_DIP_PROB_THRESHOLD` constant; `onionskin_core/aps.py:21` imports them; param renamed `dip_prob_threshold` → `regression_prob_threshold` for cleaner naming; pipeline-emitted columns unchanged). Stage A.5 — Cat 3 narrow scope verified grep-clean across `hmm_summit_refinement.py` + `hmm_ported_analyses.py`; engine-internal Cat 1 territory correctly out of scope. Stage B — all four ODW flags present (`onionskin.py:1787, 1798, 1809, 1820`) at OQ4-locked defaults (active 0.9 / regression 0.8 / both fold 1.25); 8 threading sites updated correctly; `--odw-prob-threshold` + `--odw-fold-threshold` deprecate via `_DEPRECATED_FLAGS:5704-5705`. Stage C — "Gap Analysis" argparse group at `onionskin.py:1724` with all three relocated flags. Stage D — `DECISIONS.md` `[2026-05-04]` cycle 15.9a lock entry present with all five Principal locks documented; `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:2]` and `[ISSUE:2026-05-04:1]` removed from `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` per file convention; `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:2]` Cat 3 narrow-scope-resolved postscript added by R3 (in-situ trivial-fix); `[ISSUE:2026-05-04:2]` retains correct future-phase landing zone. Stage E — `_run_argv()` + `_build_ms_argv()` removed; `run_multistage(args: argparse.Namespace)` at `growth_model_engine.py:1129` per OQ3 Namespace lock; `--background-mask-bed` flag removed; new `_make_growth_namespace` helper threads `background_mask_intervals` directly via Python references; AGENT_CONVENTIONS § Conventions item 12 elevated by R3 in-situ from "Future engine modules do not add argparse" with stale `_run_argv()` description to "No engine modules have argparse" with current state and history. Stage F — three new flags present (`onionskin.py:2322, 2333, 2343`); `--second-pass-background-floor:2354` retained per OQ5 lock; `_synthesize_posterior_inherited_masks` helper at `:5295` consumed by all three posterior helpers; deprecated-flag redirect at `:5706`.

## [v0.14.93] — 2026-05-04 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.8a (SPEC15.17 APS analysis-surface parity + master APS catalog + step-less plots/notebooks + HMM plot ports; SPEC15.18 `--peak-summary` extension to RMS + HMM + Growth) — cycle CLOSED; SPEC15.17 + SPEC15.18 DONE

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.8a — APS parity + plot-surface parity + step-less output cleanup + RMS/HMM/Growth peak-summary extension.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (Claude Sonnet 4.6 ; Effort: High), Codex 0.128.0 (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: High)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Implements the full 15.8a R1 repair contract across SPEC15.17 and SPEC15.18. Growth and RMS now use step-less `plots/` and `notebooks/` directories in `onionskin_core/output_layout.py`, HMM now has a step-less `plots/` key alongside its pre-existing `notebooks/` key, `scripts/summit_inspector.py` and pipeline tests were updated to the new layout, and the repo no longer carries live Python references to `14-plots`, `15-notebook`, `11-plots`, or `14-notebook`. Growth and RMS now also emit Tier-2 `00-pipeline-metadata/pipeline_info.json` sidecars with atomic writes, completing the 2026-05-03 DECISIONS.md carry-forward after HMM's sidecar landed in cycle 15.7a.

HMM's APS surface is no longer thinner on the trivial-port slice. `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py::run_hmm_aps()` now threads `timing_tsv` and `plots_dir`, writes `aps_amplicon_importance.tsv` via `build_amplicon_importance(...)`, emits scalar ordering tables via `build_scalar_orderings(...)`, and generates the portable APS plot subset (raw/z heatmaps, scalar-ordered heatmaps, focus plots) via `write_aps_plots(...)` with `Z_raw=None` / `Z_z=None`; the remaining HMM dendrogram gap is explicitly documented in the new `multi-agent/full_instructions/APS_CATALOG.md` and cross-referenced to `CROSS_PIPELINE_UNIFICATION_SOUP Item 3`. HMM also now gains the missing plot-surface ports after step 14 in `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`: summit plots, profile/genome-overview plots, shape-filter plots, QC plots, and new fork-width progression plots emitted from `onionskin_core/hmm_fork_travel.py`. RMS picks up QC-plot parity in `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, and the Makefile now exposes a new `summit-hmm` by-eye evaluation target using the existing HMM summit-training fixtures.

SPEC15.18 now extends `--peak-summary` beyond Growth. RMS and HMM both gained `peak-summary` output directories, peak-summary generation wired through the shared `onionskin_core/summaries.py::write_peak_summary_outputs(...)` helper, and pipeline-specific override flags in `onionskin.py` (`--rms-peak-summary`, `--rms-peak-quantile`, `--rms-peak-topk`, `--hmm-peak-summary`, `--hmm-peak-quantile`, `--hmm-peak-topk`). `max` mode now emits the required three variants (`*_max_projection.bedGraph`, `*.chrom_median_norm.bedGraph`, `*.smoothed.bedGraph`) for RMS and HMM, and `all` mode now fans out across max/topk/quantile runs. Validation for this round passed cleanly: focused pytest on the new RMS max-projection regression PASS, focused HMM chrom-median threading regression PASS, `make test` PASS (`257 passed, 1 skipped`), `make toy` PASS, `make full-twin` PASS, and `make puff-compare` PASS 28/28.

### Role 3 re-audit (Template C) — cycle CLOSED

R3 independently verified all Stage A–K deliverables as clean. No new blocking findings. One in-session Principal-directed broadening resolved the F16 naming inconsistency and wired `write_peak_summary_outputs()` into the Growth pipeline in the same pass.

**Stages verified clean:** A (step-less rename — zero residual step-numbered paths across all three pipelines, HMM `plots/` key confirmed), B (Growth + RMS `pipeline_info.json` sidecars — atomic write pattern confirmed), C (hmm_notebooks.py — verification-only, no code change needed), D (HMM APS parity — `run_hmm_aps()` signature, `build_amplicon_importance`, `build_scalar_orderings`, `write_aps_plots` with `Z_raw=None`/`Z_z=None` + TODO, `write_aps_score_tracks` pre-existing not duplicated, engine threading confirmed), E (HMM plot ports — all four try/except blocks confirmed, OQ1 inline comment confirmed, `_find_stage_bedgraphs()` HMM pattern additive-only confirmed, start/end synthesis conditional confirmed, RMS QC wired), G (fork/width progression — function exists, output dir correct, engine wiring confirmed), H (Makefile `summit-hmm` target — correct fixtures, `dev/runs/` output), I.1–I.5 (all 6 peak-summary CLI flags, `write_peak_summary_outputs()` max/topk/quantile/all modes, three max-projection variants, d4 TODO present), J (APS_CATALOG.md — all five required sections present).

Principal directed: (1) normalize RMS to step-less `peak-summary/`, (2) simultaneously wire `write_peak_summary_outputs()` into the Growth pipeline so Growth also emits a step-less `peak-summary/` directory via the shared helper, alongside its pre-existing `07-signal-tracks/` legacy output. Growth's `peak-summary/` is now the cross-pipeline-uniform surface; the legacy `07-signal-tracks/` redundancy is documented in `[ISSUE:2026-05-04:2]` for future unification. ## [v0.14.92] — 2026-05-04 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.7b (SPEC15.24 HMM per-stage parabola summit emission for cross-pipeline parity) — cycle CLOSED (SPEC15.24 DONE); `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:2]` Category 2 COMPLETE (9/9)

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.7b — HMM per-stage parabola summit emission + active-stage convergence parity.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-sonnet-4-6 ; Effort: High), Codex-cli 0.128.0 (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Medium)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Implements the full R1 repair contract for SPEC15.24. Stage A lands the mandatory rename sweep: `run_step13_summit_refinement` → `run_hmm_summit_refinement`, `_discover_step11_stage_files` → `_discover_amplicon_metrics_stage_files`, `_load_step11_rows` → `_load_amplicon_metrics_rows`, and `_load_step12_growth` → `_load_fork_travel_growth` in `onionskin_core/hmm_summit_refinement.py`, with the public call site updated in `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`; the same private helper rename was also propagated through `onionskin_core/hmm_fork_travel.py` so the tree no longer carries that stale step-numbered helper name. Stage B adds `run_hmm_per_stage_parabola_summit_estimates(step4_dir, step11_dir, out_dir)` to `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py`, emitting `13-summit-refinement/hmm_stage{N}_summit_estimates.tsv` from step-4 medians plus step-11 trajectory windows using HMM-local `_estimate_parabola_bp`; the engine now runs it immediately after HMM summit refinement. Stage C adds `run_hmm_active_stage_convergence(...)` to `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py` and wires it into HMM step 20 so HMM now writes additive `hmm_summits_step1.tsv` and `hmm_summits_step3.tsv` alongside the pre-existing combined-profile `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` (Q2 coexistence lock preserved; no change to the existing step-20 combined-profile `run_hmm_timing` block).

#### SPEC15.13 — SAPS implementation (state-path APS) HMM step 17

- **NEW module** `onionskin_core/hmm_saps.py` (213 lines) implementing per-locus state-path APS scoring. `compute_saps:132-212` reads per-sample HMM state-path bedGraphs from `<hmm-out>/06-HMM/indiv_samples/` + retained trajectories from `<hmm-out>/14-multistage-unification/all_trajectories.multistage_unification.tsv` (or `.summit_refinement.tsv` fallback). Per-locus scorer `_score_one_locus:84-129` computes `saps_area = sum((state - background_state).clip(0) × bp)`, `saps_width_bp = above-background bp`, `saps_summit_state = max state`, `saps_summit = summit_state + length-tiebreaker`, `saps_mean_state = bp-weighted mean`. Module is label-dictionary-aware via `state_label_map` parameter at `compute_saps:137`.
- **Function rename** per `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:2]` mandatory in-flight: `run_step17_hmm_saps` → `run_hmm_saps` at `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:556`. Thin compatibility shim retained at `:551-553` for stale-import safety. Engine call site updated at `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:1815-1819`.
- **CLI flag** `--saps-disable` at `onionskin.py:2751`; engine kwarg `saps_disable` at `hmm_engine.py:1350`; controller thread at `onionskin.py:4332`.
- **Outputs** at `<hmm-out>/17-saps/saps_sample_scores.tsv` + `<hmm-out>/17-saps/saps_locus_scores.tsv` per SPEC15.13 d1-d6 contract.
- **11-column state-path credibility extension** per `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_CREDIBILITY.md` § "Provisional locks — LOCKED 2026-05-04". `STATE_PATH_CREDIBILITY_COLUMNS` at `onionskin_core/reliability.py:44-56` (8 existing per-amplicon checks + 3 NEW width columns: `state_path_latest_stage_width_bp` + `state_path_width_credibility` soft-ramp + `state_path_growth_credibility_score_width_adjusted` multiplicative aggregate). New CLI flags `--state-path-width-trust-low-bp` (default 10000) + `--state-path-width-trust-high-bp` (default 100000) at `onionskin.py:4451-4454`; threaded to `hmm_multistage_unification.py:_state_path_checks:374-435` extension at lines 430-432. `attach_state_path_credibility(reliability_df, state_path_credibility_tsv)` at `reliability.py:237-276` LEFT JOINs onto `amplicon_reliability.tsv` for HMM rows; `reliability_score` byte-identical at v0.14.90 calibration (purely additive).
- **Tests** at `tests/test_hmm_saps.py` + `tests/test_hmm_state_path_aware_reliability.py`.

#### SPEC15.14 — `--hmm-statepath-base` flag + adaptive defaults + label-dictionary internal architecture + self-describing file headers + per-pipeline + run-level JSON sidecars

- **New flag** `--hmm-statepath-base {0, 1}` at `onionskin.py:2327` default `0`. Single value-flag form (re-scoped 2026-05-03 per Principal direction; cleaner than original `--hmm-1-based-statepath` boolean proposal). Help text at `:2337` documents the label-dictionary contract.
- **Adaptive defaults** for `--hmm-thresh-state` (`onionskin.py:2690`) + `--hmm-max-state-thresh` (`onionskin.py:2729`) via `argparse.SUPPRESS`; `_resolve_hmm_statepath_defaults:4437-4454` enforces the locked formula: base 0 → `thresh_state=0` + `max_state_thresh=-1`; base 1 → `thresh_state=1` + `max_state_thresh=0`. Validation rejects structurally-invalid combinations.
- **Internal HMM math 0-based** throughout: `onionskin_core/hmm_core.py:_viterbi:309` returns `path` (no `+ 1`); `:_posterior_path:382` returns `out` from `int(np.argmax(fb))` directly (no `+ 1`).
- **Label dictionary** at `onionskin_core/state_path_io.py:make_state_label_map` (identity dict for base 0; `+1`-shift dict for base 1). Threaded through controller at `onionskin.py:4239-4245` (multistage path) + `:5185-5189` (standalone-engine path); engine kwarg + step-6 emitter at `hmm_core.py:80-109` applies dictionary lookup at line 86 (`state = label_map.get(internal, internal)`) BEFORE writing each row. NO direct internal-state emission bypasses the dictionary.
- **Self-describing file headers** at every state-value-bearing emission per SPEC15.14 d9. `onionskin_core/state_path_io.py` provides `write_state_label_header`, `read_state_label_header`, `emission_params_by_label`, `emitted_state` translation helper. Header schema: `# statepath_base = ...`, `# background_state = ...`, `# state_count = ...`, `# emission_params = ...`, `# state_label_map = ...`, `# run_metadata_path = ...`, `# pipeline_metadata_path = ...`. State-path bedGraphs at `<hmm-out>/06-HMM/*.expdecay.states.bedGraph` carry canonical headers verified live.
- **Run-level Tier-1 JSON sidecar** at `<grouping>/00-run-metadata/run_info.json` per DECISIONS.md 2026-05-03 § Tier 1. `_write_run_info_sidecar` at `onionskin.py:4457` emits at startup with full schema: `manifest_path`, `version`, `chromosomes_run`, `out_dir`, `cli_args_resolved`, `pipelines_selected`, `pipelines_run`, `timestamp_start_iso8601`.
- **HMM Tier-2 JSON sidecar** at `<hmm-out>/00-pipeline-metadata/pipeline_info.json` per DECISIONS.md 2026-05-03 § Tier 2. `_write_hmm_pipeline_info` at `engines/hmm_engine.py:745-797` emits after HMM step-21 with schema: `pipeline_name = "hmm"`, `statepath_base`, `state_label_map`, `state_count`, `emission_params` (keyed by emitted state label, NOT internal index), `gap_mask_hash`, `chrom_renorm_stats`, `pass_metadata`, `pipeline_completed_at`, `cross_reference: run_metadata_path`. Growth + RMS Tier-2 sidecars staged to cycle 15.8a per DECISIONS.md staged rollout.
- **Downstream consumers** verified to skip `#` lines exhaustively across the live state-bedGraph reader surface: `hmm_core.py:41`, `hmm_saps.py:66`, `hmm_summits.py:38`, `signal_utils.py:141`, `hmm_metrics.py:88,101,117`, `hmm_engine.py:159,445`. No silent breakage on `#` headers.

#### SPEC15.16 — HMM `--norm-mode chrom-median` default switch (validation-driven flip) + dual-test validation strategy

- **Default flip** at `onionskin.py:_default_pipeline_norm_mode:3410-3411`: HMM 2+-stage with reference stage now resolves to `chrom-median` (was `ref-stage`). HMM 2+-stage WITHOUT reference stage still falls back to `ref-stage` (correct: ratio normalization requires a reference stage). The override flag `--hmm-norm-mode {inherit, chrom-median, ref-stage}` retains all three choices; only the resolved fallback flips.
- **Dual-test strategy locked** per SPEC15.16 d2 (a) + (b). Existing regression tests preserved via explicit `--hmm-norm-mode ref-stage` flag-threading where they depend on legacy behavior. New `tests/test_hmm_chrom_median_default.py` covers default-resolution + explicit-override behavior. `tests/test_pipeline.py:771-775` extended: `_resolve_hmm_norm_mode(..., n_stages=2 OR 5, has_reference_stage=True)` now asserts `"chrom-median"`. `tests/test_pipeline.py:1734-1738` extended: HMM 17-saps directory assertion now expects `saps_sample_scores.tsv` + `saps_locus_scores.tsv` (catches regression on SAPS implementation backsliding).
- **PuffStep gold-standard parity preserved** via `Makefile:125, 138, 151` `--hmm-norm-mode ref-stage` explicit flag on `puff-compare` targets (alongside the SPEC15.14 `--hmm-statepath-base 1`).
- **Behavioral validation** via existing integration suite: `make full-twin` PASS 4/0/2; `make twin` PASS (perfect twin peak resolution); `make full` PASS; `make full-no-split` PASS — no summit-accuracy regression observed under the new `chrom-median` HMM default. R2 did not retune any thresholds to make tests pass; the default flip lands cleanly per the structural-fix discipline.

The gate `if str(...).lower() == "on" and ... is not None:` at three sites (`hmm_engine.py:1648`, `onionskin.py:893`, `onionskin.py:4123`) ensures default-off skips the pass-2 logic block entirely.
2. **Growth `retain_above_hi=True` locked** at `onionskin.py:913, 981, 991`.
3. **HMM/RMS `retain_above_hi=False` locked** at `hmm_engine.py:1667` (HMM) + `onionskin.py:4142, 4238, 4248` (RMS).
4. **`_resolve_prior_mask` never writes to `args._first_pass_background_mask`** (pure resolver) — verified by `grep -n "args._first_pass_background_mask" onionskin.py` showing only READ sites.
5. **Posterior pass-2 stays OFF (SPEC15.15 d6 lock)** under default invocation; explicit user-visible toggle queued for SPEC15.21 d12 (cycle 15.9a) `--posterior-second-pass-background-estimation` flag.

R3-original verification findings (Stages A/B/C/E/F all CLEAN) remain valid and form the basis for the new Stage G work.

## [v0.14.90] — 2026-05-03 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.6a-S1 (SPEC15.9 d1/d3/d4 + SPEC15.12 entire priority) — APS sample-posterior + HMM-trajectory clustering defaults finalization + `--aps-aggregation-*` flag matrix replacing retired `--aps-weight-loci` + cross-pipeline IGV-track emission + HMM step-16 cross-pipeline parity repair + per-fork-preserved HMM trajectory feature engineering with z-score scaling default + `aps_amplicon_importance.tsv` schema gains chrom/start/end + standalone CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md DROPPED and deferred to cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 d9 + PHASE15_BACKGROUND.md lifecycle correction

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.6a-S1 (Stage D follow-up to cycle 15.6a closed v0.14.84; gated by cycle 15.6b determinism work closed v0.14.86; HARD prerequisite met). Two orchestrator-outside-cycle clarifications were absorbed during execution: F5 expansion (Option A/B/C → `--aps-aggregation-*` flag matrix) and F3 trajectory clustering redesign (all-levels-mean → per-fork-preserved feature engineering). A post-Stage-B/C orchestrator-outside-cycle SPEC engineering follow-up dropped the standalone CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md artifact + deferred user-facing rewrite to cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 d9 + corrected the PHASE15_BACKGROUND.md preamble lifecycle.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: High), Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Extra High)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Closes SPEC15.9 (d2 mechanism DONE at v0.14.84; d1/d3/d4 default-flip evaluation completed this cycle with no-flip rationale: 18-cell DS1 × DS2 × {Growth, RMS, HMM} × {on, off, post_sum} grid produced byte-identical truth-distance metrics across modes per `(dataset, pipeline)` lane; Q22 asymmetry blocks any flip; default stays `on`) and SPEC15.12 (Track 1 APS sample-posterior defaults finalized; Track 2 HMM trajectory clustering refactored to Principal-locked per-fork-preserved feature engineering; Track 3 SAPS scope LOCK preserved). Resolves `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]` (sample-level APS aggregation reliability awareness — replaced by `--aps-aggregation-*` flag matrix) and `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a-S1:1:A:1]` (HMM step-16 cross-pipeline parity repair) and `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:1]` (`--aps-weight-loci` retired entirely; help-text drift no longer exists).

**1. New `--aps-aggregation-*` flag matrix replaces retired `--aps-weight-loci` (cross-pipeline).** New CLI flag group at `onionskin.py:1969-2014`:
- `--aps-aggregation-mode {filter, weight, both, none}` default `weight`. Modes: `filter` drops loci with combined score < `--aps-aggregation-score-min` AND drops `gap_suspect=True` loci, then sums unweighted survivors; `weight` keeps all loci and multiplies per-locus signals (`area_excess`, `summit_excess`, `width_above_threshold_bp`) by the normalized score; `both` filters then weights; `none` disables score-aware aggregation (legacy / dev-comparison).
- `--aps-aggregation-score {reliability, importance, joint}` default `joint`. Sources: `reliability` uses `amplicon_reliability.tsv` per-amplicon trustworthiness; `importance` uses `aps_amplicon_importance.tsv` per-amplicon discriminating power; `joint` independently max-normalizes both and multiplies (`joint = (reliability/max_reliability) × (importance/max_importance)`, `joint ∈ [0, 1]`).
- `--aps-aggregation-score-min FLOAT` default `0.01` (underflow safety net for filter/both modes).
- `--aps-emit-unfiltered-aggregates` default off (emits `<aps-out>/onionskin_aps_unfiltered.tsv` side-car for spot-checking).
- `--aps-weight-loci` is RETIRED (no parser entry; DECISIONS.md 2026-05-03 entry locks the retirement and points downstream readers at the new flag matrix).

Wiring: `aps.py:1107-1191` defines `build_aps_aggregation_scores` + `_filter_for_aggregation` + `_weight_for_aggregation`; consumed at `aps.py:2062-2117` in `compute_and_write_aps`. Cross-pipeline parity verified: HMM step-16 calls the same helper at `hmm_ported_analyses.py:416-444`. End-to-end CLI threading: `onionskin.py:923-925` (controller path) and `onionskin.py:3962-3964` + `onionskin.py:4214-4216` (HMM engine path) → `engines/hmm_engine.py:1640-1643` → `run_step16_hmm_aps`. HMM falls back to reliability-only when `--aps-aggregation-score importance|joint` is selected this cycle (HMM importance scorer lands at cycle 15.8a SPEC15.17 d12-15).

**2. HMM step-16 cross-pipeline parity repair (F2.4; closes `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a-S1:1:A:1]`).** `run_step16_hmm_aps` previously hard-coded `shape_normalize=True` and `weight_loci=False` at the `build_aps_feature_matrix` call site (`hmm_ported_analyses.py:431-433` pre-fix), silently bypassing `--aps-shape-no-normalize`. The repair declares the function-level parameters (`hmm_ported_analyses.py:236` for `shape_normalize`; the aggregation flags at 237-240) and threads them into `build_aps_feature_matrix(...)` at line 470-472 (`shape_normalize=shape_normalize`, `aggregation_mode=aggregation_mode`). The `weight_loci` parameter is fully retired from HMM step-16. New regression test `test_hmm_step16_threads_shape_and_weight_flags` at `tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py:214-289` directly asserts that `shape_normalize=False` + `aggregation_mode="weight"` + `emit_unfiltered_aggregates=True` survive the parameter thread to the call site, and that `onionskin_aps_unfiltered.tsv` + `tracks/amplicon_reliability.bedGraph` land in HMM step-16 output.

**3. Cross-pipeline IGV-track emission (`<aps-out>/tracks/`).** New `write_aps_score_tracks(score_df, out_dir)` at `aps.py:1245-1273` writes 4-col bedGraph + 6-col BED for `amplicon_reliability` (cross-pipeline), `amplicon_importance` (Growth + RMS only this cycle; HMM at cycle 15.8a), and `amplicon_joint_reliability_and_importance` (Growth + RMS only this cycle). Called from `aps.py:2070` (controller path) and `hmm_ported_analyses.py:424` (HMM step-16). Goal: load amplicon-level reliability + importance + joint scores into IGV alongside amplicons themselves.

**4. `aps_amplicon_importance.tsv` schema gains `chrom`/`start`/`end` columns.** `aps.py:1083-1090` merges call-id coordinates into the importance dataframe before write; the ordered column list now includes `['importance_rank', 'call_id', 'chrom', 'start', 'end', 'onset_stage', 'lag_from_earliest', 'max_RCN', 'n_active_stages', 'activity_breadth', 'importance_score']`. Schema change makes the IGV-track extraction uniform across reliability + importance + joint.

**5. HMM trajectory clustering refactored to Principal-locked per-fork-preserved feature engineering (Track 2; F3 redesign).** `run_step19_hmm_clustering(...)` at `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:932-1044` replaces the previously-considered all-levels-mean design entirely. Algorithm:
- Compute global `max_layers` cross-amplicon (max nesting depth across all `(amplicon × stage)` combinations; line 959).
- Per `(amplicon, stage)` block: `max_layers × 3 + 1` features (`{stage}__layer_{L}__left_travel_bp`, `{stage}__layer_{L}__right_travel_bp`, `{stage}__layer_{L}__asymmetry_fraction` per layer, plus `{stage}__max_level_at_stage`).
- 0-fill missing layers within a stage; 0-fill missing stages entirely (sparse rows by design).
- Per-amplicon vector concatenates stage blocks: total length `n_stages × (max_layers × 3 + 1)`.
- Cluster amplicons (rows = `trajectory_id`) on the full feature matrix. NO PCA on trajectory features. NO column dropping / heuristic feature filtering. NO fallback to the dropped all-levels-mean design.

New CLI flags at `onionskin.py:2714-2750`:
- `--hmm-trajectory-clustering-k {auto, k2, k3, k4, ...}` default `auto`.
- `--hmm-trajectory-clustering-scaling {raw, zscore}` default `zscore` (the new feature design produces features that vary by orders of magnitude — `left_travel_bp` in thousands of bp vs `max_level_at_stage` as count of single digits; z-score normalization per feature column is essential for distance-based clustering to behave sensibly).
- `--hmm-trajectory-clustering-singleton-guard {on, off}` default `on`.

Engine boundary mirrors the parser default at `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py` (verified via `step19_scale=getattr(args, "hmm_trajectory_clustering_scaling", "zscore")` at `onionskin.py:4225`). New regression test `test_hmm_step19_accepts_cli_style_flags` at `tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py:292-378` asserts: feature matrix shape `(n_trajectories, n_stages × (max_layers × 3 + 1))`; `trajectory_id` index label; full column ordering; explicit 0-fill assertions for missing-layer + missing-stage cells; z-score columns produce unit-variance for varying features; cluster output schema; singleton-guard guarded vs unguarded behavior.

Output schema: `<hmm-out>/19-clustering/trajectory_feature_matrix.tsv` (raw) + `<hmm-out>/19-clustering/trajectory_feature_matrix_zscore.tsv` (when scaling=zscore) + `<hmm-out>/19-clustering/trajectory_clusters.tsv` (locked schema `trajectory_id, cluster, leaf_order`).

**6. Default-flip evaluation conclusions (no flips this cycle).** R2's Stage A grids consumed cycle 15.6b's strategy-selection determinism fix (v0.14.86) for stability-runs=3 verification. SPEC15.9 default-flip evaluation: 18-cell grid byte-identical across `on`/`off`/`post_sum` per `(dataset, pipeline)` lane → keep `--aps-area-excess-floor` default `on`. SPEC15.12 Track 1 default-finalization: 48-cell grid produced no single dominant replacement default family; strongest negative evidence noted (sample-aggregation-floor on/off no-op; `area_keep_floor_off` introduces stage-1 contamination warnings; `summit_elbow` not consistently better than `summit_keep`); DS1 RMS `area_keep_floor_on` vs `area_keep_floor_on_weighted` posterior order signature divergence verified as a real stable signal (not a determinism artifact) via posterior order signature hashes + posterior manifest cluster-map hash. SPEC15.12 Track 2 default-finalization: `auto` and `k2` behave as the same default family; `keep` without singleton-guard is pathological (e.g., dataset2 prior raw 85 clusters with 78 singleton clusters); `keep` with singleton-guard collapses to a sane default. All Stage A evidence at `dev/runs/15-6a-S1-eval/{spec15-9-defaults,spec15-12-track1,spec15-12-track2}/`.

**7. Standalone `multi-agent/full_instructions/CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` DROPPED + deferred to cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 d9.** The cycle-15.6a-S1 R2 attempt at the user-facing reference doc landed in development-decision-notes register rather than user-facing register. Per Principal direction 2026-05-03 post-Stage-B/C review (orchestrator-outside-cycle commit `5139782`), the standalone artifact is dropped from this cycle. Decision content for cycle 15.6a-S1 is documented across (a) `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md` cycle 15.6a-S1 entries (R1 audit + first orchestrator clarification F5 expansion + second orchestrator clarification F3 trajectory clustering redesign + R2 Stage B/C report + this R3 closeout); (b) `multi-agent/project_context/DECISIONS.md` 2026-05-03 sidecar convention entry (cross-references the new flag matrix). User-facing rewrite is deferred to cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 d9 with explicit guardrails (audience: external users; required content; anti-pattern list; Opus-or-equivalent authorship lock; location-TBD; validation gates). SPEC15.12 d4 updated in place to reflect the deferral and document the F7 attempt failure mode.

**8. PHASE15_BACKGROUND.md preamble lifecycle correction.** The original preamble framed the file as a "temporary scratchpad to be deleted when the design lands in a SPEC, AUDIT_LOG, or DECISIONS entry." Per Principal direction 2026-05-03 (orchestrator-outside-cycle commit `5139782`): tracked per-phase planning artifact that archives alongside SPEC / AUDIT_LOG / STRATEGY / FEEDBACK at phase close. Do NOT delete. Scope locked to background-region thinking only — NOT a per-phase synthesis surface; cycle narratives belong in AUDIT_LOG. Captured to prevent future agents from misreading "BACKGROUND" as a per-phase synthesis-index surface.

**9. Cross-pipeline parity at cycle close.** Per the F8 mandatory parity table in the AUDIT_LOG cycle 15.6a-S1 R2 implementation report (verified independently at re-audit closeout), every flag/default/schema-addition landed this cycle is either ✓ shared cross-pipeline OR explicitly justified as pipeline-specific (HMM trajectory clustering = HMM-only by SPEC15.12 d8; HMM importance + joint tracks = deferred to cycle 15.8a SPEC15.17). No silently-divergent surface landed.

**12. Out of scope this cycle (deferred per landing zones).** SAPS implementation (cycle 15.7a SPEC15.13); `--hmm-statepath-base` flag + label-dictionary internal architecture + self-describing file headers + per-pipeline JSON sidecar (cycle 15.7a SPEC15.14); within-prior second-pass machinery + posterior background-region inheritance from prior (cycle 15.7a SPEC15.15); HMM `--norm-mode chrom-median` default switch (cycle 15.7a SPEC15.16); HMM per-stage parabola summit emission (cycle 15.7b SPEC15.24); APS analysis-surface parity + master APS catalog + HMM importance + joint track emission + HMM-thinner-APS-path ports (cycle 15.8a SPEC15.17); `--peak-summary` extension (cycle 15.8a SPEC15.18); architectural cleanup + ODW threshold split + Gap-analysis group (cycle 15.9a SPEC15.21); SPEC body line-number annotation refresh + HANDOFF deep-section refresh + planning-doc-hygiene drift (cycle 15.10a SPEC15.19 + SPEC15.20); user-facing CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md production (cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 d9); cross-pipeline trajectory-tracking generalization for Growth/RMS (`multi-agent/plans/next/TRAJECTORY_SOUP.md`; future phase); reliability + importance scorer principled redesign + relocation (`[ISSUE:2026-05-03:1]`; verify post-cycle-15.8a).
