## [v0.14.89] — 2026-05-03 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.4a-S4 (SPEC15.6 supplemental defect-fix) — Pre-pipeline shared first-pass background mask + per-sample chrom-MAD bedGraph emission cross-pipeline + sigma-source value rename + new `chrom-background-mad` default: new CLI flags `--first-pass-background-bounds LO,HI|none` (default `0,1.75` biology priors), `--chrom-trim`, `--background-flank-bp`; new helper module `onionskin_core/first_pass_background_mask.py`; controller-owned mask computation in `_prepare_shared_gap_analysis` cross-pipeline; per-sample chrom-MAD bedGraphs at `<aps-out>/samples/` cross-pipeline; HMM step-16 gains `<hmm-out>/16-aps/samples/` per-sample RCN + chrom-MAD bedGraphs (Path B.2.A); sigma-source value `chrom-mad` → `whole-chrom-mad` rename + new `chrom-background-mad` value as default; F11 per-chromosome screen diagnostic; mask sidecar BED + metadata JSON

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.4a-S4 (mid-phase amendment 2026-05-01 evening; supplemental defect-fix to cycle 15.4a SPEC15.6 deliverables; consumes the dispatch architecture shipped by cycle 15.4a-S3 v0.14.88 and lands the structural background-mask portion of `[SURPRISE:15:15.4a-S2:1:A:1]`).

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Extra High), Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Closes the structural-background-mask portion of `[SURPRISE:15:15.4a-S2:1:A:1]` (Within-stage between-sample MAD as the per-locus flat-test sigma is a systematic-error design flaw). Cycle 15.4a-S3 (v0.14.88) shipped the dispatch architecture + half-formulation `chrom-mad` (placeholder whole-chrom MAD); cycle 15.4a-S4 (this entry) lands the controller-owned first-pass background mask machinery + per-sample chrom-MAD bedGraph emission cross-pipeline + the new principles-default `chrom-background-mad` sigma source. The carry-forward continues into cycle 15.7a (re-scoped SPEC15.15) for posterior background-region inheritance from prior. Per the SECOND orchestrator clarification 2026-05-03, the originally-planned `learn` mode + GMM/k-means machinery + guard rails were dropped after R2's Stage A.5 empirical sweep showed the GMM-derived valley algorithm produces artifact-tight cutoffs (σ-floor sanity check fired on 7 of 9 manifests); biology-grounded static defaults (`LO=0`, `HI=1.75`) replace the algorithmic approach.

**1. Controller-owned first-pass background mask (Stage B.1).** New helper module `onionskin_core/first_pass_background_mask.py` (386 lines) owns the cross-pipeline mask construction. `_prepare_shared_gap_analysis(...)` at `onionskin.py:257-342` computes the mask once per grouping by (a) reading the SPEC15.2 shared gap mask, (b) calling `compute_first_pass_background_mask_from_bedgraphs(bedgraph_paths, gap_masks, bounds, chrom_trim, background_flank_bp, bounds_source)` which loads per-sample chrom-median-normalized RCN tracks via `_compute_chrom_median_rcn_track(path)` (`first_pass_background_mask.py:116-127`), applies per-sample exclusion masks (`RCN < LO OR RCN > HI`, gap-flank halo within `--background-flank-bp` of any gap, first/last `--chrom-trim` of each chromosome), then reduces via cross-sample union-of-excluded-bins to produce the shared mask. The result is stored on `args._first_pass_background_mask` (`onionskin.py:339`) and threaded into Growth APS (`:942`), RMS APS (`:3916`), and HMM step-16 via `_run_hmm_engine` (`:4156`). All three posterior helpers (`_run_posterior` at `:4759`, `_run_rcn_posterior` at `:4666`, `_run_hmm_posterior` at `:4581`) re-invoke `_prepare_shared_gap_analysis` so posterior runs get a freshly-computed mask under the posterior layout.

**2. New CLI flags + alias contract (Stage B.2).** New argparse group "Pre-pipeline first-pass background mask" at `onionskin.py:2047-2086`:
- `--first-pass-background-bounds LO,HI|none` — accepts numeric `LO,HI` pair OR `none` alias only (per SECOND clarification scope-shrink). Default `0,1.75` (biology priors: no LO filter by default since most samples don't have under-replication; HI=1.75 excludes ≥2-fold-amplified bins). `none` resolves to `(0, inf)` disabling the RCN-bounds filter while keeping structural gap+flank+trim filters. Validation in `resolve_first_pass_background_bounds(...)` at `first_pass_background_mask.py:67-101` raises `ValueError` on malformed input or `lo<0 / hi<=0 / hi<lo`. Help text recommends `LO=0.25-0.5` for samples with biologically-meaningful under-replication (UR) domains.
- `--chrom-trim FRACTION` (default `0.001`, max `0.01` enforced at `first_pass_background_mask.py:245-246`).
- `--background-flank-bp BP` (default `50000`).

**3. Sigma-source value rename + addition + default flip (Stage B.6 + F13 rename ordering).** `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`:
- `DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCE = "chrom-background-mad"` (`:56`; flipped from cycle 15.4a-S3's `chrom-mad`).
- `VALID_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCES = ("within-stage-mad", "chrom-background-mad", "whole-chrom-mad", "contrib-spread", "fixed")` (`:58-62`; 5 values, up from cycle 15.4a-S3's 4).
- `chrom-mad` (the v0.14.88 value name) is RENAMED to `whole-chrom-mad`; **NO backward-compat alias preserved** per LD-1 (the v0.14.88 flag was <12-18 hours old; never used in the wild). `normalize_flat_sample_sigma_source("chrom-mad")` raises `ValueError` post-rename (regression-locked at `tests/test_flat_sample_sigma_source.py:148`). F13 verification: `grep -nE "[^-]chrom-mad" onionskin.py onionskin_core/*.py` returns ZERO hits.
- `chrom-background-mad` semantic: MAD restricted to the first-pass background mask. `whole-chrom-mad` semantic: MAD over whole chromosome (preserves cycle 15.4a-S3's `chrom-mad` semantic byte-for-byte). Mask gating at `flat_sample.py:503` — `mask = chrom_mad_mask if sigma_mode == "chrom-background-mad" else None`.

**4. Per-sample chrom-MAD bedGraph emission cross-pipeline + Path B.2.A (Stages B.4 + B.5).** New `write_sample_chrom_mad_tracks(sample_tracks, mask, metas, out_dir)` helper at `onionskin_core/aps.py:178-210` — computes per-sample per-chrom MAD via `_chrom_mad_values_from_track(df, chrom, mask=mask)`; writes constant-per-chrom bedGraph values (one chrom-MAD scalar replicated at every non-gap bin); gap rows zeroed via `_interval_overlap_mask(...)` at `:206`. Format per F10: NO NaN written; gap bins = numeric 0; non-gap bins = chrom-MAD scalar. Wired at:
- Growth/RMS APS path: `aps.py:1642-1643` calls `write_sample_rcn_tracks(...)` then `write_sample_chrom_mad_tracks(...)` into `<aps-out>/samples/`.
- **Path B.2.A — HMM step-16:** `hmm_ported_analyses.py:341-342` calls both helpers (HMM gains its own `<hmm-out>/16-aps/samples/` directory at cross-pipeline parity with Growth/RMS).

**5. F11 per-chromosome screen diagnostic + sidecar emission (Stages B.7 + F12).** `first_pass_background_mask_diagnostic_lines(mask, bed_path)` at `first_pass_background_mask.py:200-229` emits the F11 spec exactly:
- Header: `[first-pass-background-bounds] using LO=..., HI=... (source: ...) on N samples, NNN bins`.
- Per-chrom table with columns `chrom %trim %below-LO %above-HI %gap+flanks %total-masked n_bins`.
- Tail: `[first-pass-background-mask] kept N_kept of N_total bins (X.X%); shared mask written to <path>`.

Mask sidecar BED at `<run-out>/01-prior/00-gap-analysis/first_pass_background_mask.bed` and metadata JSON at `<run-out>/01-prior/00-gap-analysis/first_pass_background_mask.metadata.json` written via `write_first_pass_background_mask(...)` at `onionskin.py:317-321`. Metadata schema includes `lo`, `hi`, `bounds_source`, `chrom_trim`, `background_flank_bp`, `n_samples`, `total_bins`, `excluded_bins`, per-chrom diagnostics, and excluded intervals per chromosome.

**6. Calibration evidence (Stage B.9; reframed to evidence-only per SECOND clarification).** `dev/runs/15-4a-S4-calibration/sigma_source_comparison.tsv` extends cycle 15.4a-S3's calibration TSV with the new `chrom-background-mad` columns. Key empirical results:
- SAG40 stage-1 chr-II: `chrom_background_mad_sigma_log2 = 0.290` vs `whole_chrom_mad_sigma_log2 = 0.371` (~22% reduction; materially smaller).
- HMM `II:33175000-33795000`: `chrom_background_mad_P = 0.787` clears the tuned 0.7 gate (vs `whole_chrom_mad_P = 0.732`).
- HMM `II/9A` (`II:5975000-6515000`): `chrom_background_mad_P = 0.623` still below the tuned 0.7 gate. Documented and accepted per SECOND clarification: chrom-background-mad is preferred for being PRINCIPLED (excludes ≥2× amplification from MAD computation by construction), NOT for producing a measurably smaller sigma at every worked-example locus. The truly principled formulation lands at cycle 15.7a (re-scoped SPEC15.15) when the prior's HMM-derived above-background regions inform the chrom-background mask.

**7. Stage A artifacts retained as documentation per SECOND clarification.** `dev/runs/15-4a-S4-stage-A/static_default_sweep.tsv` + `static_default_summary.json` + `static_default_model_selection.tsv` + 9 plots (`plots/*.png`) + research script `scripts/fit_first_pass_background_defaults.py` retained as evidence that the GMM-derived empirical-default approach was tried and why it didn't work. Future-cycle agents revisiting first-pass background-mask design under different assumptions can consult this evidence.

**8. New `[ISSUE:2026-05-01:1]` filed during R2 Stage A.2.** Stage-1 active-stage anchor detection consumes per-bin within-stage MAD as noise estimate — the same systematic-error pattern v0.14.87/v0.14.88 fixed for the flat-sample test. Confirmed real on the live tree at `onionskin_core/timing.py:_median_mad_sigma:363-384` (consumed for ALL stages including stage 1 at `:488-504`). Per F4 deferral + Stage A.2 contract: file the issue THIS CYCLE; defer in-cycle fix to a follow-up cycle (or to cycle 15.7a SPEC15.15 broader timing/posterior re-scoping). The new `chrom-background-mad` bedGraphs from this cycle are exactly what the future fix will consume.

**9. New regression tests (Stage C).** Two new test files + extended existing ones:
- `tests/test_first_pass_background_mask.py` (NEW; 153 lines) — covers mask construction with numeric bounds + `none` alias + chrom_trim + background_flank_bp + sidecar emission + default LO=0 behavior. Per the SECOND clarification, the `learn`-mode test was dropped from the contract; the default-LO-zero test was added.
- `tests/test_per_sample_chrom_mad_emission.py` (NEW; 178 lines) — covers Growth/RMS/HMM per-sample chrom-MAD bedGraph emission cross-pipeline + Path B.2.A HMM `<aps-out>/samples/` parity + no-NaN gate + constant-per-chrom format + gap-bin handling.
- `tests/test_flat_sample_sigma_source.py` (extended; 5-mode parametrize) — `chrom-background-mad` as default; `chrom-mad` rejection regression-lock; `whole-chrom-mad` semantic preservation; new value acceptance.
- `tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py` (extended) — `chrom-background-mad` end-to-end through HMM-only posterior; first-pass mask sidecar + HMM `<aps-out>/samples/` outputs verified at posterior path.
- `tests/test_reliability.py` (extended) — pinned to `whole-chrom-mad` for legacy MAD-bedGraph regressions (post-rename).

**10. Validation gates at re-audit closeout (independently re-run by Role 1 in configured conda env `/Users/johnurban/software/conda/anaconda3/bin/python`).**
- `pytest tests/test_first_pass_background_mask.py tests/test_per_sample_chrom_mad_emission.py tests/test_flat_sample_sigma_source.py tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py tests/test_per_pipeline_flat_sample_state.py tests/test_reliability.py tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py -q` — PASS (41 passed, 1 skipped in 25.76s).
- `make test` — PASS (205 passed, 1 skipped in 90.34s).
- `make toy` — PASS.
- `make full-twin` — PASS (no regressions).
- `make puff-compare` — PASS (`OVERALL: PASS  (28 files match)`) — CRITICAL HMM-detection bit-for-bit gate held; the new mask machinery + new bedGraph emissions correctly do NOT touch HMM detection (steps 06-10), which was the structural intent.

**11. Carry-over dispositions at closeout.**
- `[SURPRISE:15:15.4a-S2:1:A:1]` (within-stage MAD systematic-error design flaw) — status field stays "Resolved (v0.14.88) for the dispatch-architecture portion"; appended cycle-15.4a-S4-completion postscript at v0.14.89 documenting the structural mask portion landed cleanly + carry-forward continues into cycle 15.7a for posterior background-region inheritance. Multi-cycle plan now 2 of 3 cycles complete.

## [v0.14.88] — 2026-05-02 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.4a-S3 (SPEC15.6 supplemental defect-fix) — Flat-sample sigma-source dispatch architecture: new `--flat-sample-sigma-source {within-stage-mad, chrom-mad, contrib-spread, fixed}` flag (default flipped to `chrom-mad` as a principles-default per user authorization); separate `--flat-sample-detector` (F2 cleanup; `hardthreshold` is detector-only, NOT a sigma-source value); `--flat-sample-rcn-sigma` / `--flat-sample-log2-sigma` for `fixed` mode; cross-pipeline gap-bin → NaN handling at chrom-MAD computation site (in-memory only; no NaN written to bedGraphs); `sigma_source` schema column added to `flat_samples.tsv`; placeholder `chrom-mad` semantic = naive whole-chrom MAD from chrom-median sample tracks (cycle 15.4a-S4 upgrades to mask-derived)

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.4a-S3 (mid-phase amendment 2026-05-01; supplemental defect-fix to cycle 15.4a SPEC15.6 deliverables per `[SURPRISE:15:15.4a-S2:1:A:1]`; scope narrowed 2026-05-01 evening per orchestrator corrections appendix to keep background-mask machinery for cycle 15.4a-S4).

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Extra High), Claude Code 2.1.126 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Closes the dispatch-architecture portion of `[SURPRISE:15:15.4a-S2:1:A:1]` (within-stage between-sample MAD as the per-locus flat-test sigma is a systematic-error design flaw — conflates true per-sample noise with inter-sample biological signal). The structural background-mask machinery + per-sample chrom-MAD bedGraph emission cross-pipeline lands in cycle 15.4a-S4; the within-prior second-pass + posterior background-region inheritance lands in re-scoped SPEC15.15 cycle 15.7a. Cycle 15.4a-S3 ships the half-formulation (placeholder naive whole-chrom MAD on chrom-median sample tracks) plus the pluggable architecture that authorizes future improvements without further argparse churn.

**1. Sigma-source dispatch architecture (F1 + F7 + F11; CR4 narrowed contract).** New `--flat-sample-sigma-source` argparse flag at `onionskin.py:2017-2029` with `choices=list(VALID_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCES)` and `default=DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCE`; help text explicitly notes that `hardthreshold` is a detector value, not a sigma source — set it via `--flat-sample-detector`. `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py:52` defines `DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCE = "chrom-mad"` (default-flip; see ¶6 below for the principles-default rationale); `:54-59` defines `VALID_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCES = ("within-stage-mad", "chrom-mad", "contrib-spread", "fixed")` (4 values; `hardthreshold` excluded). `normalize_flat_sample_sigma_source(sigma_source)` at `:71-91` validates and returns the canonical form, raising `ValueError` on unsupported input including `"hardthreshold"`. `compute_flat_sample_sidecar(...)` and `write_flat_sample_sidecar(...)` at `:309-616` accept a new `sigma_source` kwarg with dispatch into `_chrom_mad_sigmas_from_track` (chrom-mad path) / per-stage MAD bedGraph lookup (within-stage-mad path) / per-call summit_rcn spread (contrib-spread path) / explicit kwargs (fixed path). New `sigma_source` column added to `FLAT_SAMPLE_COLUMNS` at `:43` and emitted to `flat_samples.tsv`.

**2. F2 cleanup — separate `--flat-sample-detector` flag.** New `--flat-sample-detector` argparse flag at `onionskin.py:2030-2039` with `choices=list(VALID_ACTIVE_STAGE_DETECTORS)` (`hardthreshold | gaussian_overlap | bayesian_posterior`); when unset, inherits from `--odw-active-stage-detector`. Cleanly separates the detector choice (which test is run) from the sigma-source choice (how the noise sigma is estimated). Argparse `choices=` enforces the F2 distinction at parse time; the explicit help-text note in both flags makes the distinction visible in `-h` output.

**3. F3 fixed-mode sigma overrides.** New `--flat-sample-rcn-sigma` and `--flat-sample-log2-sigma` argparse flags at `onionskin.py:2040-2061` (both `type=float`, default `None`). Used when `--flat-sample-sigma-source fixed` and as a fallback when a dynamic source is unavailable. Threaded through Growth/RMS controllers (`onionskin.py:898-899`, `:3826-3827`), HMM step-16 controller (`:4065-4066`), and `write_flat_sample_sidecar` kwargs at `onionskin_core/aps.py:1771-1772` and `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:349-350`.

**4. Cross-pipeline plumbing (B.5).** Growth controller threads `flat_sample_sigma_source` / `flat_sample_detector` / `flat_sample_rcn_sigma` / `flat_sample_log2_sigma` to `compute_and_write_aps` at `onionskin.py:894-899`; RMS controller at `:3822-3827`; HMM step-16 (via `_run_hmm_engine`) at `:4061-4066`. Posterior helpers re-invoke pipeline controllers which read kwargs from `args`: `_run_posterior` at `:4607` (`("growth", "rcn-mean-shift", "hmm")`); `_run_rcn_posterior` at `:4449` (`("rcn-mean-shift", "hmm")`); `_run_hmm_posterior` at `:4535` (`("hmm",)`). `chrom-mad` reads from cross-pipeline-shared `compute_sample_rcn_tracks(manifest, norm_mode="chrom-median", ref_stage=ref_stage)` at `onionskin_core/aps.py:1615-1619` (Growth/RMS) and `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:276-280` (HMM) — NOT HMM step-5 `indiv_samples`. The Stage A.3 HMM-vs-Growth/RMS per-stage MAD-bedGraph divergence is bypassed by sourcing chrom-MAD from chrom-median tracks instead of per-stage MAD bedGraphs; the asymmetry remains documented as a future-cycle observation for cycle 15.4a-S4 / SPEC15.15 cycle 15.7a.

**5. CR3 / Stage B.NaN — gap-bin → NaN at chrom-MAD computation site.** `_chrom_mad_sigmas_from_track` at `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py:182-214` converts non-finite values (`:200`) and `RCN <= 0.0` (`:201`) to NaN, then masks via `finite_rcn = rcn[np.isfinite(rcn)]` (`:202`) before computing `np.median` and MAD. Stays in-memory only — no NaN appears in any bedGraph output (per user constraint). Growth/RMS chrom-median sample surfaces represent shared-gap bins as numeric zero on the live tree; the NaN-fill at the chrom-MAD computation site keeps those gap fills from inflating the sigma estimate without changing the on-disk bedGraph contract.

**6. Default-flip from `within-stage-mad` to `chrom-mad` — principles-default per user authorization.** At cycle 15.4a-S3 R1 audit handoff time, the corrections appendix CR2 specified `within-stage-mad` as the safe-fallback default. After the Stage A diagnostic, the user explicitly authorized flipping the default to `chrom-mad` as a principles-default: within-stage between-sample MAD treats inter-sample biological variance as noise (conflates signal with the very thing the flat-sample test is trying to find); per-sample chrom-MAD is the right-direction formulation. Cycle 15.4a-S3 ships the half-formulation (whole-chrom MAD from chrom-median sample tracks, with no background mask yet); cycle 15.4a-S4 lands the full mask-restricted formulation. Threshold re-tuning is explicitly deferred to cycle 15.4a-S4 — the probability/fold pair (`0.7` / `1.10`) remains locked to cycle-15.4a-S2's tuned values throughout this round. Empirical no-regression validated on the workspace fixture (see ¶8).

**7. New regression tests (Stage C).** Three test files updated:

- `tests/test_flat_sample_sigma_source.py` (new) — covers the four supported sigma-source modes (parametrized over `within-stage-mad`, `chrom-mad`, `contrib-spread`, `fixed`); locks the default to `chrom-mad` via `assert DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCE == "chrom-mad"`; verifies that detector-only aliases like `hardthreshold` are rejected at the sigma-source parser layer (`assert raises ValueError`).
- `tests/test_reliability.py` (extended) — pins legacy MAD-bedGraph tests to `sigma_source="within-stage-mad"` so pre-default-flip semantics remain explicit; adds `test_flat_sample_chrom_mad_ignores_zero_filled_gap_bins` proving zero-filled gap bins are ignored by the new whole-chrom MAD path.
- `tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py` (extended) — pins existing synthetic SAG40-like fixture to `sigma_source="within-stage-mad"` for both legacy and signal-override comparisons, preserving the historical interpretation of that regression.
- `tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py` (extended) — invokes the HMM-only posterior path with `--flat-sample-sigma-source fixed`, `--flat-sample-detector bayesian_posterior`, and explicit fixed sigmas, then asserts that `01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/flat_samples.tsv` records `sigma_source=fixed`. Narrow regression proving the new flat-sample kwargs make it through the posterior-helper path, not just the main controllers.

**8. Calibration + workspace-fixture parity evidence (B.6 + B.7).** `dev/runs/15-4a-S3-calibration/sigma_source_comparison.tsv` captures the two SAG40 chr-II anchor loci across Growth/RMS/HMM and reports tuned 0.7/1.10 probabilities for all four in-scope sigma sources. At HMM `II:33175000-33795000`, `within-stage-mad`/`chrom-mad`/`fixed` clear the tuned 0.7 gate; `contrib-spread` fails. At HMM `II:5975000-6515000` (II/9A), only `fixed` clears — the chrom-mad default flip is a principles-default (right structural direction), NOT a worked-example rescue at II/9A; the rescue at II/9A under the tuned gate awaits cycle 15.4a-S4's mask-restricted formulation. `dev/runs/15-4a-S3-fixture-check/{fixture_summary,pipeline_comparison}.tsv` verifies that SAG40 stays non-flat under both `within-stage-mad` and `chrom-mad` for Growth/RMS/HMM, and that chr-II `reliability_flag==pass` counts are unchanged: Growth `8 → 8`, RMS `13 → 13`, HMM `3 → 3`. Per user direction during R2, real-fixture validation was redirected from Google Drive paths to workspace-local manifests under `dev/datasets/full_genome/batch/`. HMM SAG40 nuance recorded for cycle 15.4a-S4: under chrom-mad `n_flat_loci` jumps `1 → 5` on the local HMM rerun, but `flat_sample_flag` stays `False` because `min_flat_fraction=1.0` and the fraction is 5/7=0.714.

**9. Cross-pipeline parity table (B.7 / CR4) — built inline at re-audit (GAP-1 remediation).** A 5-row × 3-column structural parity table covering `--flat-sample-sigma-source` honored, `--flat-sample-detector` honored (F2), `--flat-sample-rcn-sigma`/`--flat-sample-log2-sigma` honored for `fixed` mode (F3), posterior-helper threading, and gap-bin → NaN handling lives at `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md` cycle 15.4a-S3 Role 1 Re-Audit (Template C) entry. Every cell cites the specific file:line where the threading occurs. R2's implementation report had conflated B.6 calibration evidence with the B.7 structural parity table; R3 verified threading independently while building the table.

**10. Help-text polish at re-audit (contract item d).** `--flat-sample-sigma-source` help text at `onionskin.py:2022-2029` now reads "... fixed uses the explicit --flat-sample-rcn-sigma/--flat-sample-log2-sigma pair. Note: hardthreshold is a detector value, not a sigma source — set it via --flat-sample-detector. [aps + reliability]". `--flat-sample-detector` help text at `:2034-2039` enumerates `hardthreshold | gaussian_overlap | bayesian_posterior` explicitly. Argparse `choices=` already enforced the structural exclusion before the edit; the edit makes the F2/CR4 distinction explicit in `-h` output. Smoke-tested via `pytest tests/test_pipeline.py tests/test_flat_sample_sigma_source.py -q` — 123 passed clean.

**11. Validation gates at re-audit closeout (independently re-run by Role 1 in configured conda env `/Users/johnurban/software/conda/anaconda3/bin/python`).**
- `pytest tests/test_flat_sample_sigma_source.py tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py tests/test_per_pipeline_flat_sample_state.py tests/test_reliability.py tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py -q` — PASS (25 passed, 1 skipped in 12.16s).
- `pytest tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py -q` — PASS (1 passed in 1.10s).
- `make test` — PASS (190 passed, 1 skipped in 91.22s).
- `make full-twin` — PASS (4/0/2; 0 regressions).
- `make puff-compare` — PASS (`OVERALL: PASS  (28 files match)`) — CRITICAL HMM-detection bit-for-bit gate held; the new `chrom-mad` default does NOT touch HMM detection (steps 06-10).

**12. Carry-over dispositions at closeout.**
- `[SURPRISE:15:15.4a-S2:1:A:1]` (within-stage MAD systematic-error design flaw) flipped to **Resolved (v0.14.88)** for the dispatch-architecture portion, with carry-forward postscript noting that cycle 15.4a-S4 lands the full mask-restricted formulation and SPEC15.15 cycle 15.7a lands the posterior background-region inheritance.
- `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:6]` (HMM shape-filter window principled-cleanup) stays Active per orchestrator direction (out of cycle 15.4a-S3 scope; future-cycle work).
- `[ISSUE:2026-04-14:1]` (detector redesign) stays Active — out of scope per STRATEGY guardrail.

**13. Out of scope (deferred to other landing zones).**
- Pre-pipeline shared naive background mask + per-sample chrom-MAD bedGraph emission cross-pipeline — cycle 15.4a-S4 (queued).
- Within-prior second-pass background-region-anchored chrom-renorm + posterior background-region inheritance from prior — re-scoped SPEC15.15 cycle 15.7a.
- Detector redesign (`[ISSUE:2026-04-14:1]`) — future-phase via SOUP/BRAINSTORM.
- GC-aware chrom-median normalization — `multi-agent/plans/next/GECKO_SOUP.md`.
- Broader ODW transition test rework at `onionskin_core/timing.py:_median_mad_sigma` — narrowed F10; only stage-1 anchor case has same vulnerability per orchestrator corrections appendix; differential-expression-style stages-N>1 transition test is correctly using per-bin within-stage MAD for its question structure.
- HMM step-number leakage refactor (`[ISSUE:2026-04-30:2]`) — cycle 15.9a SPEC15.21 + future structural-unification phase.

**14. Files changed (cycle aggregate; R2 commit + R3 closeout commit).**
- `onionskin.py` — new `--flat-sample-sigma-source`, `--flat-sample-detector`, `--flat-sample-rcn-sigma`, `--flat-sample-log2-sigma` argparse flags; threading of `flat_sample_sigma_source` / `flat_sample_detector` / `flat_sample_rcn_sigma` / `flat_sample_log2_sigma` through Growth + RMS + HMM step-16 controllers; help-text polish at re-audit.
- `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` — `DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCE = "chrom-mad"`; `VALID_FLAT_SAMPLE_SIGMA_SOURCES = (within-stage-mad, chrom-mad, contrib-spread, fixed)`; `normalize_flat_sample_sigma_source` validator; `_chrom_mad_sigmas_from_track` helper with in-memory NaN handling; `sigma_source` column in `FLAT_SAMPLE_COLUMNS`; `compute_flat_sample_sidecar` + `write_flat_sample_sidecar` accept `sigma_source` + `chrom_mad_sample_tracks` kwargs.
- `onionskin_core/aps.py` — Growth/RMS APS path resolves chrom-median tracks for the `chrom-mad` sigma source; threads new kwargs to `write_flat_sample_sidecar`.
- `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py` — HMM step-16 path resolves chrom-median tracks for the `chrom-mad` sigma source; threads new kwargs to `write_flat_sample_sidecar`.
- `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py` — `step16_*` kwargs for the new flat-sample knobs.
- `tests/test_flat_sample_sigma_source.py` (new) — 4-mode sigma-source coverage + default-locks-to-chrom-mad + detector-alias rejection.
- `tests/test_reliability.py` — pinned to `within-stage-mad` for legacy MAD-bedGraph regressions; new `test_flat_sample_chrom_mad_ignores_zero_filled_gap_bins`.
- `tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py` — pinned to `within-stage-mad` for pre-default-flip semantics.

## [v0.14.87] — 2026-05-01 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.4a-S2 (SPEC15.6 supplemental defect-fix) — Flat-detector + amplicon reliability tuning to real ground-truth data + HMM-only `--posterior` unit fix: chrom-median flat-sample signal source; tuned flat-sample thresholds (0.7 / 1.10); standalone `_run_hmm_posterior` helper + per-pipeline `_flat_sample_auto_switch_<pipeline>` refactor; HMM step14 shape-score harmonized to last-stage profile + 100-bin context; `locus_contributions.tsv` flat_sample_flag backfill cross-pipeline

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.4a-S2 (mid-phase amendment 2026-04-30; supplemental defect-fix to cycle 15.4a SPEC15.6 deliverables; broadened 2026-04-30 to also absorb `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:4]` HMM-posterior unit findings 1+2+3+6).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), Codex-cli 0.126.0-alpha.8 (GPT-5.5 ; Reasoning: Extra High)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Closes both drivers of cycle 15.4a-S2: `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:3]` (flat-detector + amplicon reliability tuning to real ground-truth data) AND `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:4]` (HMM-only `--posterior` + `--compute-aps` silent no-op + flat-sample per-pipeline state divergence; findings 1, 2, 3, 6 absorbed). Folds in the adjacent `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:5]` `locus_contributions.tsv` `flat_sample_flag` backfill across all three pipelines. Honors the cycle's "tune, don't redesign" scope-boundary lock — the noise-source rework for the per-locus flat-test sigma (`[SURPRISE:15:15.4a-S2:1:A:1]` within-stage between-sample MAD as a systematic-error design flaw) is OUT OF SCOPE and lands in cycle 15.4a-S3 per user direction 2026-05-01.

**1. Driver 1 / Stage B.1 — Flat-detection-on-chrom-median signal path (SPEC15.6 deliverable 3 refinement).** New `--flat-sample-norm-mode {chrom-median, ref-stage}` argparse option at `onionskin.py:2031-2042` defaults to `chrom-median`. `compute_flat_sample_sidecar(...)` and `write_flat_sample_sidecar(...)` accept a new optional `signal_contrib_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None` kwarg at `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py:252,472`. When provided, the per-locus Bayesian flat test consumes `summit_rcn` from `signal_contrib_df` (chrom-median-normalized) instead of the default ref-stage-normalized `contrib_df`. At each call site (`onionskin_core/aps.py:compute_and_write_aps` for Growth + RMS APS; `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:run_step16_hmm_aps` for HMM APS), when `flat_sample_norm_mode == "chrom-median"` and the primary normalization is something else, the writer builds a separate chrom-median contrib_df via `compute_sample_rcn_tracks(manifest, norm_mode="chrom-median", ref_stage=ref_stage)` + `compute_aps_tables(...)` and threads it to `write_flat_sample_sidecar`. Decouples flat-sample classification from ref-stage anchor pollution (the chicken-and-egg root cause confirmed at Stage A.1).

**2. Driver 1 / Stage B.8 — Tuned flat-sample default thresholds.** `DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_PROB_THRESHOLD = 0.7` and `DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_FOLD_THRESHOLD = 1.10` at `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py:49-50`, decoupled from the broader ODW active-stage defaults (`prob_threshold=0.9` / `fold_threshold=1.25`). Explicit `--flat-sample-prob-threshold` / `--flat-sample-fold-threshold` CLI overrides preserved at `onionskin.py:2008-2030`. Stage A.6 trace showed several plausible threshold pairs catch the synthetic SAG40-residual fixture; the landed pair (0.7/1.10) is the narrowest one that catches the measured residual-bump under the live fallback-sigma path.

**3. Driver 2 / Stage B.4 — Standalone `_run_hmm_posterior` helper.** New `_run_hmm_posterior(prior_layout, args, chromosomes_override)` at `onionskin.py:4455-4520` (sister to `_run_rcn_posterior` and `_run_posterior`). Reads APS cluster files from `prior_layout["hmm_aps_dir"]` (Stage B.6's new key); applies per-pipeline auto-switch via `_apply_flat_sample_auto_switches(args, ("hmm",))`; calls `build_posterior_manifest`; runs `_prepare_shared_gap_analysis` + `_run_hmm_engine` on the posterior manifest with `include_hmm=True, include_growth_model=False, include_rcn_mean_shift=False`. Wired at the two HMM-only short-circuits at `onionskin.py:4818` (multistage HMM-only) and `:5090` (single/two-stage HMM-only) under `if getattr(args, "posterior", False)`. `--pipelines hmm --posterior --compute-aps` now produces a `02-posterior/01-hmm/` directory with HMM posterior outputs.

**4. Driver 2 / Stage B.5 — Per-pipeline `_flat_sample_auto_switch_<pipeline>` refactor + F11 RMS posterior auto-switch consumption.** `onionskin.py:97-133` introduces `_pipeline_state_suffix(pipeline_name)` (folds `rcn-mean-shift` to `rms`), `_flat_sample_auto_switch_attr(pipeline_name)` (`_flat_sample_auto_switch_growth`, `_rms`, `_hmm`), `_pipeline_norm_attr(pipeline_name)` (resolves to `growth_norm_mode` / `rms_norm_mode` / `hmm_norm_mode`), and `_apply_flat_sample_auto_switches(args, pipeline_names)` — a single helper that applies the zero-flat-sample posterior norm-mode switch per-pipeline. `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch(args, aps_dir, pipeline_name)` at `:136-189` writes to the per-pipeline attribute (compatibility shim `args._flat_sample_auto_switch` retained for older readers). `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode(args, requested_norm_mode, pipeline_name="growth")` at `:217-233` is now pipeline-aware. Per-pipeline consumption sites: `_run_rcn_posterior` calls `_apply_flat_sample_auto_switches(args, ("rcn-mean-shift", "hmm"))` — fixes new finding F11 (RMS posterior previously did not consume the auto-switch flag); `_run_hmm_posterior` calls `("hmm",)`; `_run_posterior` calls `("growth", "rcn-mean-shift", "hmm")`. Eliminates the prior shared-flag pattern's "any-pipeline-with-zero-flats wins globally" hazard.

**5. Driver 2 / Stage B.6 — `hmm_aps_dir` layout key + cross-pipeline cluster/order file emission parity.** New `hmm_aps_dir = grouping_dir/01-hmm/16-aps` key at `onionskin_core/output_layout.py:293`. Single-purpose addition; broader Growth-anchored layout refactor stays in `multi-agent/plans/next/CROSS_PIPELINE_UNIFICATION_SOUP.md` Item 6b. HMM step16 APS now emits `aps_clusters_raw.tsv` + `aps_clusters_zscore.tsv` + `aps_dendrogram_order_raw.tsv` + `aps_dendrogram_order_zscore.tsv` + the order-comparison files via `write_order_tables` at `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:399-420` — satisfying `build_posterior_manifest`'s `_POSTERIOR_REQUIRED` (`aps_clusters_raw.tsv`, `aps_order_area.tsv`) cross-pipeline contract. The legacy `aps_clusters.tsv` is preserved for backward compatibility.

**6. Stage B.7 — HMM step14 shape-score structural harmonization (per `[SURPRISE:15:15.4a-S2:1:A:3]`).** Picked up as in-cycle work after user pushback rejected the threshold-0 band-aid an earlier draft considered. `_shape_score_for_row` at `onionskin_core/hmm_multistage_unification.py:196-214` now consumes the latest-stage profile via `_latest_stage_context_profile(profiles, chrom, start, end, peak)` (iterates `sorted(profiles.keys(), reverse=True)` for the latest non-empty stage at `:165-178`) and `ctx_bins=100` (at `:163, 188-189`) — matching Growth/RMS's `shape_filter_calls` at `onionskin_core/refinement.py:473-526`. HMM `step14_shape_score_threshold` default restored to `50.0` at `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:1246` and `_effective_hmm_shape_score_threshold` at `onionskin.py:3260-3265` — cross-pipeline parity at the same calibrated threshold Growth/RMS use. User-eyes rerun on the user-supplied chr-II fixture: 9/12 chr-II step13 trajectories survive the keep gate at threshold 50 (was 3/12). Cross-pipeline context on the same fixture: Growth 10 final chr-II / 8 APS reliability pass; RMS 13 pass; original HMM 3 pass; new HMM 9 pass — squarely within plausible biological-real-data variance range. The 3 dropped HMM trajectories (`II:6675000-6740000` shape=1.55, `II:47345000-47430000` shape=5.86, `II:55640000-55670000` shape=26.53) all fall below the calibrated threshold AND fail state-path evidence (`state_path_growth_credibility_score=0.17`); they are HMM-detection false-positives, not by-eye-confirmed amplicons.

**7. Folded `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:5]` — `locus_contributions.tsv` per-(sample, prior_stage) flat_sample_flag backfill.** `attach_flat_sample_flags_to_contributions(contrib_df, flat_df)` at `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py:508-541` merges per-(sample_id, prior_stage) `flat_sample_flag` from `flat_samples.tsv` onto contribution rows. Called by Growth/RMS at `onionskin_core/aps.py:1765` and HMM at `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:343` after reliability attachment. Closes the prior stale-False bug where `locus_contributions.tsv` rows for samples flagged flat in `flat_samples.tsv` showed `flat_sample_flag=False`. Verified at re-audit on `dev/runs/toy_out/01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/locus_contributions.tsv` — stage-1 samples (flagged True in `flat_samples.tsv`) now show `flat_sample_flag=True` per row; stage-2 through stage-5 samples (flagged False) show False.

**8. In-situ catch — Active-mask alignment under ref-stage HMM normalization.** The user-eyes HMM rerun exposed `active_stages_mask must align to HMM stage rows` after step14 output expanded. Codex traced this to ref-stage HMM step4 excluding the reference stage while active masks were built against the full stage list. Fix at `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:1696`: select `_hmm_non_ref_stages if use_ratio_norm else groups` for the active-mask construction. Not in the audit's repair contract; in-situ pickup by the implementer.

**9. New regression tests.** Three new test files cover the cycle's surfaces:

- `tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py` — synthetic SAG40-like residual fixture verifying `signal_contrib_df` chrom-median path defeats the false-positive flat call (residual `summit_rcn=1.20` at one locus, baseline 1.0 at seven loci) AND that `attach_flat_sample_flags_to_contributions` correctly maps per-(sample, prior_stage) verdicts onto contribution rows.
- `tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py` — end-to-end `--pipelines hmm --posterior --compute-aps --aps-cluster-k 2 --hmm-norm-mode ref-stage` invocation against the 5kb toy fixture; asserts exit code 0, asserts prior `01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/aps_clusters_raw.tsv` + `aps_order_area.tsv` exist, asserts posterior `02-posterior/01-hmm/posterior_manifest.tsv` + `02-posterior/01-hmm/14-multistage-unification/` exist.
- `tests/test_per_pipeline_flat_sample_state.py` — per-pipeline auto-switch isolation: setting `_flat_sample_auto_switch_growth` and applying with `("growth", "rcn-mean-shift", "hmm")` overrides only `growth_norm_mode`; subsequent `_flat_sample_auto_switch_rms` and applying with `("rcn-mean-shift",)` overrides only `rms_norm_mode`; HMM stays on user's requested mode. Pipeline-aware `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode` returns `chrom-median` for `hmm` only when `_flat_sample_auto_switch_hmm` is set.

**10. Validation gates at re-audit closeout (independently re-run by Role 1).**
- `python -m pytest tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py tests/test_per_pipeline_flat_sample_state.py -q` — PASS (5 passed in 11.75s).
- Fresh `rm -rf dev/runs/puffstep-py && make puffstep-py` followed by `make puff-compare` — PASS 28/28 (CRITICAL gate; HMM detection bit-for-bit unchanged on freshly re-run post-R2 tree).
- `make test` — PASS (183 passed, 1 skipped in 91.87s).
- `make toy` — PASS.
- `make twin` — PASS (perfect twin peak resolution).
- `make full-twin` — PASS.
- User-eyes verification: `dev/runs/15-4a-S2-user-eyes-flagfix/` re-checked; SAG40 `flat_sample_flag=False` (was True), `n_reliable_loci=9 / n_flat_loci=4`; II/9A locus chrom-median signal `summit_rcn=1.20`; 12 chr-II step13 trajectories present in `all_trajectories.multistage_unification.tsv`; 9 chr-II rows with `filter_keep=1`.

- Cross-pipeline structural unification of three posterior helpers (`_run_posterior` + `_run_rcn_posterior` + new `_run_hmm_posterior`) — `multi-agent/plans/next/CROSS_PIPELINE_UNIFICATION_SOUP.md` Item 6.
- `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:4]` Findings 4, 5, 7 — CROSS_PIPELINE_UNIFICATION_SOUP Items 6a/6b + cycle 15.8a SPEC15.17 (deliverables 16-19).
- HMM step-number leakage refactor (`[ISSUE:2026-04-30:2]`) — cycle 15.9a SPEC15.21 + future structural-unification phase.

**13. Files changed.**
- `onionskin.py` — `_pipeline_state_suffix`, `_flat_sample_auto_switch_attr`, `_pipeline_norm_attr`, `_apply_flat_sample_auto_switches`, `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch` per-pipeline write, `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode` pipeline-aware, `_run_hmm_posterior` standalone helper, HMM-only short-circuit posterior wiring, `--flat-sample-norm-mode` flag, threading of `flat_sample_norm_mode` through Growth/RMS/HMM APS callers, `_effective_hmm_shape_score_threshold` default 50.
- `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` — `DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_PROB_THRESHOLD=0.7`, `DEFAULT_FLAT_SAMPLE_FOLD_THRESHOLD=1.10`, `signal_contrib_df` kwarg in `compute_flat_sample_sidecar` + `write_flat_sample_sidecar`, `attach_flat_sample_flags_to_contributions` helper, `flat_sample_ids` helper.
- `onionskin_core/aps.py` — `compute_and_write_aps` builds chrom-median contrib_df when `flat_sample_norm_mode="chrom-median"` and normalization differs; calls `attach_flat_sample_flags_to_contributions` for the locus_contributions backfill.
- `onionskin_core/hmm_multistage_unification.py` — `_latest_stage_context_profile` helper; `_shape_score_for_row` rewritten to use last-stage profile + 100-bin context (replaces prior cross-stage mean + no-context implementation).
- `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py` — `run_step16_hmm_aps` accepts `flat_sample_norm_mode`; builds chrom-median contrib_df + threads to `write_flat_sample_sidecar`; emits `aps_clusters_raw.tsv` + `aps_clusters_zscore.tsv` + `aps_dendrogram_order_raw.tsv` + `aps_dendrogram_order_zscore.tsv` + order-comparison files; calls `attach_flat_sample_flags_to_contributions`.
- `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py` — `step14_shape_score_threshold` default restored to `50.0`; `step16_flat_sample_norm_mode` engine kwarg; active-mask alignment fix at the active-stage convergence dispatch.
- `onionskin_core/output_layout.py` — new `hmm_aps_dir` layout key.
- `tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py` (new) — synthetic SAG40-like residual fixture + locus_contributions backfill unit test.
- `tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py` (new) — end-to-end HMM-only `--posterior --compute-aps` regression.
- `tests/test_per_pipeline_flat_sample_state.py` (new) — per-pipeline auto-switch isolation regression.
- `tests/test_hmm_multistage_unification.py` + `tests/test_pipeline.py` — updated to reflect harmonized HMM shape-score behavior + cross-pipeline default-threshold parity.

## [v0.14.86] — 2026-04-30 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.6b (SPEC15.23) — Strategy-selection determinism diagnostic + fix: HMM bare-`except Exception` narrowed; RMS worker-failure swallow re-raised; cross-pipeline shared-grid-invariant assertions; `os.listdir` sort-order locks; new `tests/test_determinism.py` regression test

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.6b (mid-phase amendment 2026-04-30; inserted between cycles 15.6a and 15.6a-S1 per `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` strategy-selection determinism observation surfaced at cycle 15.5a R3 closeout).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Xhigh)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Closes the strategy-selection determinism investigation (`[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]`) via Path 1: source likely identified, prophylactically hardened across all three pipelines, and guarded by a new regression test. Cycle 15.5a R3's observation that `selected_stages` differed between identical-input toy runs (II:3680000-4345000 → "4" vs "4,5") motivated a diagnostic-first cycle: Stage A.1 instrumented the bare-`except Exception` adjacency at `hmm_engine.py` (per `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:4]`) cheaper-test-first; Stages A.2 + A.3 ran the broader 5×-toy diagnostic + canonical-grid invariant audit. Stage A returned no swallowed exceptions across 5 runs, 58/58 identical TSVs across 5 runs, and 42/42 grid-invariant checks `same_grid: true` — empirically clean diagnostic surface. Stage B landed the prophylactic hardenings anyway because the silent-failure + implicit-order hazards on the determinism critical path are real on the live code path and any of them could plausibly have been the original cause. Stage C added `tests/test_determinism.py` to guard reintroduction.

**1. HMM active-stage summit refinement broad-catch narrowing (B-Branch i; SPEC15.23 deliverable 4 + `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:4]`).** `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:1665-1734` — the outer `try/except Exception as exc: print(...)` wrapping ~70 lines of step18→step20→step21 timing-promotion orchestration is replaced. New local `_safe_copy(src, dst)` helper wraps **only** the file-finalization `shutil.copy2(...)` calls, catching **only** `OSError`. All non-FS operations — `masks_from_timing(...)`, both `run_step18_hmm_timing(...)` calls, `csv.DictReader(...)` parsing, and the per-row `int(float(raw))` sliding-offset conversion — now propagate up to the engine's outer error path. The narrow inner `try/except (TypeError, ValueError): continue` on the per-row sliding-offset parse is preserved (intentional narrow handling). Step-21 timing-promotion failures + step-20 summit-promotion failures no longer get a one-line stderr warning while the run continues with stale step18 timing.

**2. RMS per-stage worker swallow → re-raise (B-Branch i'; Option B-i'-b — collect-then-raise).** `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` — the post-`as_completed` worker-result loop's `except Exception: stage_results[st] = (pd.DataFrame(), pd.DataFrame())` silent-empty-fallback is replaced by collecting failures in a `failures: List[Tuple[int, Exception]]` list and re-raising a single `RuntimeError(f"[rcn-mean-shift] per-stage worker failure(s) on stages {failed_stages}; first failure: {failures[0][1]!r}")` after all futures complete. A stage that previously substituted empty DataFrames on worker failure (silently changing `selected_stages` downstream) now fails the run loudly with a diagnostic message. The broad `try/except Exception` wrapping `run_rms_active_stage_convergence(...)` at the original line 730 is removed entirely; convergence failures propagate up.

**3. Cross-pipeline shared-grid-invariant assertions (B-Branch v; SPEC15.23 deliverable 4 + `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:5]`).** New `onionskin_core/common.py` helpers: `assert_shared_track_grid(sample_tracks, sample_ids, *, context)` validates `(chrom, start, end)` triples across all sample IDs vs the first-sample reference, raises `AssertionError` with diagnostic context on mismatch, and returns the validated reference DataFrame for caller reuse. `assert_shared_chrom_track_grid(...)` is the per-chromosome variant. Both helpers convert the silent-determinism-risk of "alphabetically-first sample is canonical bin grid" into a loud failure on any future divergence. Wired across all surfaces that depended on the implicit invariant: `aps.py:457` (`build_aps_shape_matrix`), `aps_pca.py` (`compute_amplicon_layer1_pca` + `compute_sample_layer2_pca_raw`), `engines/growth_model_engine.py` (5 sites: `apply_ref_stage_baseline`, `stage_median_track`, `compute_stage_medians_all`, `build_model_peak_profile`, `_run_argv` per-chrom growth-track loop), `engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` (`_per_stage_one` base + hires per-chrom loops), and `rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py` (mirror sites).

**4. Sort-order locks on determinism-relevant filesystem enumeration (B-Common).** `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py:295` (RMS active-stage convergence per-stage `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` enumeration that feeds `_selected_summit_from_stage_rows`): `os.listdir(...)` → `sorted(os.listdir(...))`. `onionskin_core/engines/growth_model_engine.py:1968` (post-`max_rcn_stage` fix-up over Growth stage-median bedGraphs): same swap. POSIX does not guarantee `os.listdir` order; explicit sort eliminates the implicit dependency.

**5. Cross-pipeline parity validation (SPEC15.23 deliverable 6).** R2 implementer extended Stage B beyond R1's named callouts (`aps.py:457` + `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:241`) to the same-surface audit misses in `aps_pca.py`, `growth_model_engine.py`, and `rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py` — disclosed as a deviation in the implementation report and verified in-scope at re-audit. The fix is symmetric across Growth + RMS + HMM wherever the diagnostic surface is shared. Bare-except narrowing applies to the two highest-priority sites (HMM block + RMS worker swallow) plus the RMS active-stage-convergence wrapper. Remaining `except Exception` blocks in the engines (`rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:278/408/514/787/806`, `growth_model_engine.py:483/1868/1883/2012`) are genuinely defensive numeric/string/file-write fallbacks with deterministic alternatives — correct judgment to leave.

**6. New regression test `tests/test_determinism.py` (Stage C; SPEC15.23 deliverable 5).** Module-scoped `toy_determinism_runs` fixture invokes the toy recipe (`onionskin.py --manifest tests/toy_dataset/manifest_5kb.tsv --chromosomes II ... --norm-mode chrom-median`) twice into separate tmp dirs; per-pipeline parametrized test case asserts `assert_frame_equal(check_exact=True)` over per-pipeline TSV globs covering timing, APS, summit_estimates, summits_step, and stage_summary surfaces. Loud per-file failure messages; explicit out-of-scope edges documented in the test docstring (cross-platform / cross-Python / plot / notebook reproducibility). Also flags files present in run2 but missing in run1 (extra-paths check). Per-pipeline parametrization gives clean per-pipeline failure signal per `feedback_cross_pipeline_parity_blindspot.md` discipline. Total cost ~60s for one full pass; acceptable per `[ISSUE:2026-04-21:2]` budget concerns.

**Behavioral changes at default invocation.**
- HMM active-stage summit refinement now fails the run loudly on parsing/orchestration errors (was: one-line stderr warning + continue with stale step18 timing).
- RMS per-stage worker failure now fails the run loudly with a `RuntimeError` naming the failed stages (was: silent substitution of empty DataFrames + downstream `selected_stages` distortion).
- Any future bedGraph-grid divergence across samples now fails APS / APS-PCA / Growth / RMS construction loudly with a diagnostic `AssertionError` (was: silently used alphabetically-first sample's grid as canonical, producing implicit-order-dependent shape matrices and per-stage results).
- File enumeration on determinism-relevant surfaces (Growth post-`max_rcn_stage` stage-median bedGraphs; RMS active-stage convergence per-stage summit-estimate TSVs) now uses `sorted(os.listdir(...))` (was: implicit POSIX-undefined order).

**Validation:** `pytest tests/test_determinism.py -q` PASS (3/3); `make test` PASS (176 passed, 1 skipped); explicit 2-run toy diff PASS (58 files, 0 diffs at `dev/runs/15-6b-validate-toydiff/`); `make twin` PASS; `make full-twin` PASS (`Passed: 4, Failed: 0, Informational: 2`); `make puff-compare` PASS (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`). **The CRITICAL HMM-detection determinism gate is satisfied** — bare-except narrowing + grid assertions + sort-order locks landed without disturbing HMM detection at steps 06-10 (which is upstream of summit refinement and APS), exactly as the SPEC predicted. Stage A diagnostic artifacts preserved at `dev/runs/15-6b-stageA1-run{1..5}.stderr.log` (5 runs × 0 swallowed exceptions), `dev/runs/15-6b-stageA2/targeted_tsv_digest_summary.tsv` (58/58 identical), and `dev/runs/15-6b-stageA3/grid_invariant_checks.json` (42/42 same-grid checks true).

**Workflow note (R1 + R2 + R3 cycle).** Role 1 initial audit (Claude Code 2.1.117, 2026-04-30, Template A) declared OPEN with a diagnostic-first Stage A→C contract (11 findings F1-F11) integrating two carry-over SURPRISE entries (`[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:4]` HMM bare-except → Stage A.1; `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:5]` alphabetical-first canonical grid → Stage A.3) and one carry-over KNOWN_ISSUES (`[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` strategy-selection determinism → Stage A.2 broader 5×-toy recipe). Role 2 implementation (GitHub Copilot GPT-5.4 Extra High, 2026-04-30, Template B) executed all three Stage A diagnostics + extended Stage B beyond R1's narrow callouts to same-surface audit misses + landed Stage C regression coverage. Role 1 R3 re-audit (Claude Code 2.1.117, 2026-04-30, Template C) verified the implementation against the live tree at HEAD `d507a3e`, spot-checked all Stage A artifacts, re-ran the validation gates independently, and disposed of the cycle's two SURPRISE carry-overs as Resolved (v0.14.86) plus the `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` KNOWN_ISSUES entry as Resolved (v0.14.86) via Path 1. Praise leaved for the shared-helper factoring of `assert_shared_track_grid` / `assert_shared_chrom_track_grid` into `common.py` (cleanest cross-pipeline parity surface vs the audit's per-site inline pattern), the in-situ same-surface audit-miss extension to `aps_pca.py` + `growth_model_engine.py` + `rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py` (honest scope-handling, not silent broadening), the per-stage `stage_sample_ids` scoping in `_per_stage_one` (quiet correctness win), Option B-i'-b adoption matching the audit's recommended path, and `_safe_copy`'s `OSError`-only catch matching the audit's narrow-scope recommendation.

**R3 disposition of R2-reported `make test` failures.** R2's implementation report stated `make test` was failing on three `tests/test_pipeline.py` help-text assertions (`test_summit_inspector_help_mentions_rms_pipeline_alias`, `test_help_runs`, `test_help_step_mentions_per_group`). R3 re-ran the targeted slice + the full suite on the identical tree and observed all three tests passing cleanly + `make test` PASS at 176/177 + 1 skipped. The R2-reported failures were not reproducible on the live tree at HEAD `d507a3e`; most likely cause was an environmental issue in R2's test environment (Matplotlib/fontconfig cache permission glitches have hit prior cycles 14S.5a + 15.4a) or a stale `.pyc`. The cycle's actual test surface is clean at closeout.

**SURPRISE_LOG dispositions.** `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:4]` (HMM bare-except) → Resolved (v0.14.86). `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:5]` (alphabetical-first canonical grid) → Resolved (v0.14.86). No new SURPRISE entries filed during this cycle's R1 / R2 / R3 rounds.

**KNOWN_ISSUES dispositions.** `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` (strategy-selection determinism) → Resolved (v0.14.86) via Path 1. The original observation was not directly reproduced in 5+ identical-input toy runs at the post-cycle-15.6a HEAD; the determinism critical-path silent-failure + implicit-order hazards that were prophylactically hardened ARE real on the code path and any of them could plausibly have been the original cause. The new `tests/test_determinism.py` regression test prevents reintroduction across all three pipelines.

**Out-of-scope items (correctly deferred — no work this cycle).**
- HMM step-number leakage refactor (`[ISSUE:2026-04-30:2]`) — landing zone is cycle 15.9a SPEC15.21 sub-deliverable + future structural-unification phase per `multi-agent/plans/next/CROSS_PIPELINE_UNIFICATION_SOUP.md`.
- HMM per-stage parabola summit emission (SPEC15.24) — landing zone is cycle 15.7b (separately authorized 2026-04-30 mid-phase amendment).
- Cycle 15.6a-S1 Stage D quantitative defaults work (SPEC15.9 d3-d4 + SPEC15.12 + `CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` + `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]` Option A/B/C) — gated on this cycle's closeout (now satisfied) but explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for cycle 15.6b itself.
- Cycle 15.4a-S2 flat-detector + amplicon reliability tuning to ground-truth data (`[ISSUE:2026-04-30:3]`) — gated on this cycle's closeout (now satisfied), separately authorized 2026-04-30 mid-phase amendment.
- Remaining defensive `except Exception` blocks in `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` (lines 278/408/514/787/806) and `growth_model_engine.py` (lines 483/1868/1883/2012) — verified at re-audit as genuinely defensive numeric/string/file-write fallbacks with deterministic alternatives, not silent-orchestration swallows. Tightening them further is hygiene work, not determinism-critical-path work, and is appropriately deferred to a future cleanup pass.

**Phase summary.** 9 cycles closed (15.1a → v0.14.77, 15.2a → v0.14.78, 15.3a → v0.14.79, 15.4a → v0.14.80, 15.4a-S1 → v0.14.81, 15.4b → v0.14.82, 15.5a → v0.14.83, 15.6a → v0.14.84, **15.6b → v0.14.86**). v0.14.85 was the README freshness pass (separate work item, not a cycle deliverable). 7 cycles remaining post-amendments: 15.4a-S2, 15.6a-S1, 15.7a, 15.7b, 15.8a, 15.9a, 15.10a. Both 15.4a-S2 (flat-detector tuning) and 15.6a-S1 (Stage D quantitative defaults) were gated on this cycle and are now unblocked; orchestrator picks ordering.

**Files changed:** `onionskin_core/common.py`, `onionskin_core/aps.py`, `onionskin_core/aps_pca.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/growth_model_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py`, `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py`, `tests/test_determinism.py` (new), `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SURPRISE_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/DECISIONS.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

## [v0.14.85] — 2026-04-30 EDT

### README freshness pass (Pass 1: current-state-only) — drop Phase 9 framing for Phase 15+ era; correct output layout block; expand APS / Timing / HMM / Reliability sections; add 13 missing modules to project structure; expand validation Makefile targets; triage Future directions

**Phase:** Phase 15 (mid-execution).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max).

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

User-facing documentation freshness pass. Triggered by Codex audit of `README.md` against the live codebase + post-Phase-15 expected state. The audit identified the README as globally version-stale (still framed as Phase 9, v0.9.19+) with 10+ specific staleness categories. All findings confirmed against live tree at HEAD `3450cac` (post-cycle-15.6b R2 implementation). Pass 1 lands the current-state-accurate refresh; Pass 2 (final post-Phase-15 closeout pass) lives in cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 closeout sweep when the queued Phase 15 work has landed (SAPS implementation, chrom-median norm-mode default flip, ODW threshold split, APS_CATALOG.md, CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md, etc.).

**1. Header + framing.** Replaced *"Current capabilities (Phase 9, v0.9.19+)"* with *"Current capabilities (Phase 15+ era; v0.14.84+)"* + brief note that the project has evolved well beyond original Phase 9 single-sample focus. Status section updated to reflect Phase 15 mid-execution.

**2. Output directory layout block.** Full rewrite per `onionskin_core/output_layout.py` at HEAD. HMM step layout corrected post-cycle-15.4b reorder + cycle 15.5a additions: `15-gap-analysis → 16-aps → 17-saps (placeholder) → 18-timing → 19-clustering → 20-active-stage-summit → 21-timing-updated` (was incorrectly shown as `15-aps → 16-saps → 17-timing → 18-clustering → 19-gap-analysis`). Growth layout corrected: 14 numbered subdirs (`01-growth-track` through `14-plots / 15-notebook`) instead of the old 7-subdir pattern. RMS layout adds `15-timing/`, `15b-active-stage-summit/`, `15c-timing-updated/`. Shared pre-pipeline `01-prior/00-gap-analysis/` documented. Note added that step layout is subject to phase-mid drift; live tree is source of truth.

**3. Detection + refinement section.** Added cross-pipeline summit-stage selection menu (`--summit-stage-selection` 6 strategies including HMM-only `odw_narrowest_state`); ODW-confined refinement; HMM sliding-offset sub-bin port; cross-pipeline summit↔timing 4-step convergence (cycle 15.5a SPEC15.7+SPEC15.8). Added RMS pipeline subsection.

**4. Timing section — Bayesian ODW framework.** Documented 3 detector modes (`hardthreshold` / `gaussian_overlap` / `bayesian_posterior` default); 4-metric framework (`onset_stage` / `last_active_stage` / `max_rcn_stage` / `regression_stage`); ODW boundary metadata columns; `--odw-active-stage-detector` / `--odw-prob-threshold` / `--odw-fold-threshold` flags; variance-aware sigma resolution from per-stage MAD bedGraphs; cross-pipeline timing reach (Growth + RMS + HMM all emit timing post-SPEC15.4); summit↔timing 4-step convergence pattern. (Was previously documented as just *"onset-stage estimation; max-stage / max-RCN reporting; tunable onset thresholds."*)

**5. Reliability + flat-sample detection — new subsection.** Documented cross-pipeline reliability scorer (`onionskin_core/reliability.py`); `amplicon_reliability.tsv` emission; 5 evidence axes; `keep_override` escape hatch; `gap_suspect` hard-exclude axis; per-locus Bayesian-ODW-consistent flat-sample test (`onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`); posterior stage-1 anchoring; auto-switch-to-chrom-median controller; new `--flat-sample-*` and `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` flags. (Was completely absent from the README.)

**6. APS section — full rewrite.** New column schema documented (`summit_rcn`, `mean_rcn`, `area_excess`, `width_above_threshold_bp`, `log10area`, `asymmetry_ratio` — replacing legacy `summit_excess` / `mean_excess` / `peak_rcn` retired in cycle 15.6a SPEC15.10). Floor modes: `--aps-area-excess-floor on|off|post_sum`. Sample-aggregation floor: `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor on|off`. 7+ feature modes (`shape_pca`, `log2shape_pca`, `sample_shape_pca_raw`, `sample_shape_pca_reduced`, `amp_metrics`, `sample_metrics`, `metrics`) plus existing scalar/shape modes. 3-layer deterministic PCA via new `onionskin_core/aps_pca.py`. PCA controls (`--aps-num-amp-pc` / `--aps-num-sample-pc` / `--aps-pca-matrix` / `--aps-final-pc`). Amplicon importance ranking output (`aps_amplicon_importance.tsv`) on Growth + RMS; HMM parity queued for cycle 15.8a (SPEC15.17 expansion). Per-locus reliability weighting via `--aps-weight-loci` documented as currently active (cycle 15.4a Stage J wired `reliability_score` as `weight_col`). Per-pipeline `_amplicons_recommended_to_keep.bed` + `_amplicons_recommended_for_exclusion*.bed` advisory BEDs documented cross-pipeline (post-cycle-15.4b). Forward cross-reference to `APS_CATALOG.md` (post-cycle-15.8a).

**7. HMM state-path pipeline section.** Replaced Phase-9-ported framing with current state. Step layout corrected. Gap-analysis, MAD bedGraphs, shape filter, multistage unification, sliding-offset port, 4-step convergence, gap-suspect filter all documented. SAPS noted as currently a reserved placeholder. `--norm-mode chrom-median` validation-driven default switch flagged as queued Phase 15 work (SPEC15.16, cycle 15.7a) — not yet flipped.

**8. Project structure / module inventory.** Added 13 missing modules: `aps_pca.py`, `flat_sample.py`, `gap_analysis.py`, `hmm_core.py`, `hmm_summits.py`, `odw.py`, `rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py`, `reliability.py`, `signal_transforms.py`, `signal_utils.py`, `summit_convergence.py`, `summit_metrics.py`, `summit_strategies.py`. Reorganized list by functional grouping: cross-pipeline analysis surface / pipeline orchestration / HMM-specific / detection-refinement primitives / plots-notebooks / I/O-utility. Removed step-numbered identifiers from module descriptions (per `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:2]` naming convention).

**9. Installation deps.** Added `matplotlib` (heavily used), `scikit-learn` (PCA + hierarchical clustering). Noted `nbformat` for notebook generation. Mentioned optional `geckocorrection` external repo (gitignored at top level, parallel to PuffStep handling) reserved for future-phase GC-aware chrom-median work via `multi-agent/plans/next/GECKO_SOUP.md`.

**10. Validation Makefile targets.** Added missing targets: `make full-no-split`, `make full-twin`, `make shape-score-bic`, `make puff-compare` (CRITICAL gate explicitly called out), `make test-single`, `make summit-smoke`. Noted PuffStep parity 28/28 as the critical gate for any HMM-detection-touching change. Updated *"summit covers growth + HMM"* to *"growth + HMM + RMS"* per current Makefile.

**11. Current limitations — false claim removed.** Deleted *"APS per-locus reliability weighting remains scaffolded but not implemented; `--aps-weight-loci` is currently an experimental no-op"* — this was already false post-cycle-15.4a Stage J (which wired `reliability_score` as `weight_col` when present). Replacement limitations reflect actual current gaps (twin-peak ≥3, joint DNA+RNA, hires summit utility, SAPS placeholder status, HMM analysis-surface parity in flight, normalization-strategy choices queued).

**12. Future directions — triaged.** Restructured into "Coming in Phase 15 (mid-execution)" + "Beyond Phase 15." Phase 15 queued work explicitly listed: SAPS implementation, 0-based statepath default flip, two-pass renormalization, chrom-median norm-mode default flip evaluation, master APS catalog, HMM-thinner-APS-path explicit ports, HMM per-stage parabola summit emission, strategy-selection determinism diagnostic + fix, APS clustering defaults + reliability-filtered aggregate decision, ODW threshold split. Beyond Phase 15: twin-peak ≥3 amplicons, RNA-seq integration, Bayesian-weighted reliability framework (BRAINSTORM), GC-aware chrom-median (GECKO_SOUP), cross-pipeline structural unification (CROSS_PIPELINE_UNIFICATION_SOUP), summit methodology phase (SUMMIT_SOUP).

**13. Handoff and AI-agent docs section.** Added explicit pointer to `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md` (the pipeline contract reference). Mentioned tracking-tier reservoirs (KNOWN_ISSUES, BRAINSTORM, DECISIONS, SURPRISE_LOG) and `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md` for the full convention set.

**Pass 2 deferred.** Final post-Phase-15-closeout README refresh (final HMM defaults, SAPS status, APS/cluster defaults, ODW flag names post-SPEC15.21 split, links to APS_CATALOG.md / CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md, Phase 15 highlights summary) lives in cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 closeout sweep — already in scope for that cycle's tracking-file cleanup pass + user-facing doc cross-alignment. No SPEC amendment needed.

**Validation:** No code change; documentation-only. No test runs needed. Codex's prior README audit confirmed in full (zero false positives across all 10+ findings).

**Files changed:** `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

## [v0.14.84] — 2026-04-30 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.6a (SPEC15.10 + SPEC15.11 DONE; SPEC15.9 PARTIAL — d2 mechanism only) — APS column rename + raw-RCN migration; composite multi-feature APS clustering modes + 3-layer PCA + harness extension; post-sum-floor variant for `--aps-area-excess-floor`

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.6a (6 of 10 in `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`; bundles SPEC15.9 + SPEC15.10 + SPEC15.11 + SPEC15.12, but the orchestrator-locked Stage-A-C-only scope landed SPEC15.10 + SPEC15.11 + SPEC15.9 d2 mechanism here. SPEC15.9 d1/d3/d4 default-flip evaluation + SPEC15.12 entire priority + `CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` + the `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]` reliability-filtered aggregate decision are deferred to cycle 15.6a-S1, which itself is gated by separately-authorized cycle 15.6b strategy-selection-determinism work.).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Codex-cli 0.126.0-alpha.8 (GPT-5.5 ; Reasoning: Extra High), Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Lands the cross-pipeline APS schema migration from excess-facing fields back to raw-RCN-facing fields (`peak_rcn` → `summit_rcn`; `mean_excess` → `mean_rcn`; new per-locus columns `log10area`, `asymmetry_ratio`, `shape_score_raw`); the post-sum-floor variant of `--aps-area-excess-floor` (`on|off|post_sum`); a new `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor on|off` flag that moves the floor semantics to sample-aggregation time; and a family of seven new `--aps-feature` modes (`shape_pca`, `log2shape_pca`, `sample_shape_pca_raw`, `sample_shape_pca_reduced`, `amp_metrics`, `sample_metrics`, `metrics`) backed by a new 3-layer deterministic PCA module (`onionskin_core/aps_pca.py`). The HMM-side feature dispatch (formerly a duplicated `_FEATURE_COL` dict) is collapsed into a shared `build_aps_feature_matrix()` resolver consumed by Growth, RMS, and HMM identically.

**Default-flip flag (per SPEC15.10 d4 — read carefully).** `--aps-feature summit` now reads raw `summit_rcn` (windowed at the refined origin position when available; max-RCN bin midpoint as fallback) instead of the floored `summit_excess`. APS contributions per locus emit `summit_rcn` + `mean_rcn` raw values; `summit_excess`, `mean_excess`, and `peak_rcn` are no longer per-locus columns. Pin to ≤v0.14.83 if the legacy `summit_excess` schema is required for downstream reproduction; this change is a deliberate v0.14.84 schema migration with no deprecation flag.

**1. SPEC15.10 — APS column rename + raw-RCN migration (DONE).** `_locus_metrics()` at `onionskin_core/aps.py:242-303` returns `{area_excess, mean_rcn, width_above_threshold_bp, summit_rcn, log10area, asymmetry_ratio}`; the empty-locus return at lines 255-262 mirrors the new schema with diploid baseline (`summit_rcn=1.0`, `mean_rcn=1.0`). `_FEATURE_COL` at `onionskin_core/aps.py:694-701` resolves `summit` and `log2summit` to `summit_rcn`. Cross-pipeline parity: HMM's `run_step16_hmm_aps` consumes the shared `build_aps_feature_matrix()` resolver instead of a duplicated `_FEATURE_COL` dict — the cleanest possible structural response to the recurring cross-pipeline-divergence pattern. `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor on|off` flag added at `onionskin.py:1894-1905`; default `on` preserves legacy nonnegative `APS_summit` / `APS_sum_of_means` semantics, `off` retains signed `(RCN - 1)` aggregation.

**2. SPEC15.11 — Composite multi-feature APS clustering modes + 3-layer PCA + harness extension (DONE).** New module `onionskin_core/aps_pca.py` provides `compute_amplicon_layer1_pca` (per-amplicon shape PCA), `compute_sample_layer2_pca_raw` and `compute_sample_layer2_pca_reduced` (sample-level shape PCA, two variants 2a/2b), and `apply_layer3_pca` (matrix-level PCA). The module includes a `_orient_scores()` helper that fixes each loading's max-magnitude term to positive sign, eliminating PCA sign-flip non-determinism. Iteration order is genomic-position-sorted via `genomic_sort_key` to keep determinism robust. New `--aps-feature` modes wired at `APS_FEATURE_CHOICES`: `shape_pca`, `log2shape_pca`, `sample_shape_pca_raw`, `sample_shape_pca_reduced`, `amp_metrics`, `sample_metrics`, `metrics`. New flags wired at `onionskin.py:1906-1932`: `--aps-num-amp-pc` (default 2), `--aps-num-sample-pc` (default 2), `--aps-pca-matrix` (off), `--aps-final-pc` (default 2). All flags cascade through the engine boundary into HMM-side step 16 APS. Layer-1/2/3 PCA artifacts are persisted to `aps_matrix_layer1_pca.tsv`, `aps_matrix_layer2_pca.tsv`, `aps_matrix_layer3_pca.tsv`, and `*_pre_layer3.tsv`. `scripts/aps_cluster_experiments.py` extended with `--pipelines growth,rms,hmm` (default: all three; replaces growth-only hardcoding), the new feature/PCA flags, and a `--stability-runs N` cluster-stability summary mode.

**3. SPEC15.9 d2 — Post-sum-floor variant for `--aps-area-excess-floor` (DONE; d1/d3/d4 evaluation deferred).** `--aps-area-excess-floor` choices extended from `["on", "off"]` to `["on", "off", "post_sum"]`. Internally normalized to `per_bin`/`off`/`post_sum`. `per_bin` preserves legacy floor-on (each bin contributes `max(RCN - 1, 0)` before summing). `off` preserves legacy floor-off (sum signed `(RCN - 1)` contributions). `post_sum` is the new mechanism: sum signed bin contributions across the amplicon, then clamp the final amplicon total at zero (per BRAIN15.14 user concern #1: per-bin flooring systematically biases APS high when RCN oscillates around 1; the post-sum variant allows bin-level negatives to offset positives during the sum and floors only the per-amplicon total). For shape matrices, `post_sum` is interpreted as signed-per-bin (off behavior) because a post-sum clamp is an amplicon-total operation with no meaningful per-bin analogue. Default-flip evaluation (`per_bin` vs `off` vs `post_sum` on live datasets, with summit-evaluation regression checks) is deferred to cycle 15.6a-S1 alongside SPEC15.12 quantitative defaults; the determinism gate from `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` is load-bearing for a credible default flip.

**4. SPEC15.12 deferred to cycle 15.6a-S1.** No `multi-agent/full_instructions/CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` written this cycle; no quantitative defaults locked; no reliability-filtered aggregate Option A/B/C decision. The orchestrator-locked Stage-A-C-only scope explicitly excluded these per the C2 escalation in the cycle 15.6a R1 audit. Cycle 15.6a-S1 fires after cycle 15.6b's strategy-selection-determinism work closes.

**5. DECISIONS.md `[2026-04-30] Phase 15 cycle 15.6a SPEC15.11 morphology feature definitions`.** Locks `asymmetry_ratio = abs(summit_bp - midpoint) / (locus_width / 2)` clipped to `[0, 1]`; retains `width_above_threshold_bp` as the canonical per-locus column (with `--aps-feature width` mode as user-facing alias); emits `log10area = log10(max(area_excess, 1.0))` per-locus; attaches `shape_score_raw` opportunistically via id + coordinate aliases (NaN where missing). Out of scope for the cycle's DECISIONS lock: SPEC15.9 default-flip and SPEC15.12 quantitative clustering defaults — deferred to cycle 15.6a-S1.

**6. PIPELINE_SPEC.md updated** with the new APS schema, floor modes, sample aggregation floor, feature-mode catalog (including all seven new modes), and PCA outputs.

**7. New regression test suite (12 tests).** `test_locus_metrics_emits_summit_rcn_and_mean_rcn`, `test_aps_sample_aggregation_floor_on_off`, `test_locus_metrics_per_bin_floor_matches_legacy`, `test_locus_metrics_off_matches_legacy`, `test_locus_metrics_post_sum_floor_oscillating_locus` (asserts `per_bin > post_sum > off`), `test_locus_metrics_post_sum_floor_clamps_negative_total_to_zero`, `test_aps_layer1_pca_per_amplicon_pcs`, `test_aps_layer2_pca_raw_vs_reduced`, `test_aps_layer3_pca_matrix_projection`, `test_aps_metrics_mode_emits_all_features`, plus updates to the existing `test_aps_area_excess_floor_switches_per_bin_contributions` for the renamed schema.

**Behavioral changes at default invocation.**
- Per-locus APS contributions emit `summit_rcn` + `mean_rcn` raw values; `summit_excess`, `mean_excess`, `peak_rcn` are no longer columns.
- Per-locus contributions gain new columns: `log10area`, `asymmetry_ratio`, `shape_score_raw` (NaN where upstream calls TSV does not expose it).
- Sample-level `aps_df` gains `APS_log10_area = log10(max(APS_area, 1.0))`.
- `--aps-feature summit` (and `log2summit`) now read raw `summit_rcn`, not `summit_excess`. Preserved with `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor on` (default) for the sample-aggregation pathway: `APS_summit` continues to sum `max(summit_rcn - 1, 0)`. To get signed `(RCN - 1)` aggregation: `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor off`.
- New flags: `--aps-area-excess-floor post_sum` (third option in addition to `on`/`off`); `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor on|off`; `--aps-num-amp-pc N`, `--aps-num-sample-pc N`, `--aps-pca-matrix`, `--aps-final-pc N`.
- New feature modes: `shape_pca`, `log2shape_pca`, `sample_shape_pca_raw`, `sample_shape_pca_reduced`, `amp_metrics`, `sample_metrics`, `metrics` — all available cross-pipeline (Growth/RMS/HMM).
- HMM step-16 APS now consumes the shared `build_aps_feature_matrix()` resolver, so all new feature modes work identically on HMM as on Growth/RMS.
- New per-pipeline output files (when relevant feature mode is chosen): `aps_matrix_layer1_pca.tsv`, `aps_matrix_layer2_pca.tsv`, `aps_matrix_layer3_pca.tsv`, `*_pre_layer3.tsv`.

**Validation:** `make test` PASS (165 passed, 1 skipped — +12 new tests for APS schema migration + post-sum-floor + sample-aggregation-floor + Layer-1/2/3 PCA + metrics mode); `make toy` PASS (clean re-run; verified all three pipeline output dirs `02-growth-model/13-aps/`, `03-rcn-mean-shift/12-aps/`, `01-hmm/16-aps/` emit the new schema with `summit_rcn`, `mean_rcn`, `log10area`, `asymmetry_ratio`, `shape_score_raw` per F7 cross-pipeline parity guard); `make twin` PASS; `make full-twin` PASS; `make puff-compare` PASS (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`). **The CRITICAL HMM-detection determinism gate is satisfied** — APS schema/feature work has zero blast radius on steps 06-10 of the HMM pipeline. R1 re-audit independently re-confirmed the targeted APS / HMM-ported / reliability test slices (11/0/0 + 14/0/1 skipped) and `make puff-compare` (28/28).

**Workflow note (R1 + R2 + R3 cycle).** The cycle ran three rounds. Role 1 initial audit (`Claude Code 2.1.117`, 2026-04-30) declared OPEN-greenfield with 23 findings F1.1-F1.3 + F2.1-F2.4 + F3.1-F3.6 + F4.1-F4.5 + F5/F6/F7 plus two carry-over verifications (C1 — HMM APS step-21 timing-promotion ordering harmless; C2 — `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` strategy-selection determinism, escalated as recommendation-c "dedicated cycle 15.6b mid-phase amendment"). The audit also created `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SURPRISE_LOG.md` as a new per-phase planning surface for fresh-eyes peripheral-vision observations and seeded eight inaugural entries `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:1-8]` from the audit's residue. Role 2 implementation (`Codex-cli 0.126.0-alpha.8 GPT-5.5 Extra High`, 2026-04-30) shipped Stages A-C across one consolidated commit per the v2 batching rule; the round honored the orchestrator-locked Stage-A-C-only scope cleanly with explicit cross-references to deferred Stage D work in cycle 15.6a-S1 (and added one R2-authored surprise `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:2:B:1]`). Role 1 R3 re-audit (`Claude Code 2.1.117`, 2026-04-30) closed the cycle: praise for the `_orient_scores()` PCA sign-orientation guard (clever determinism guard the audit did not request), the broader-than-minimum `build_aps_feature_matrix` shared resolver (cleanest structural response to the cross-pipeline divergence pattern), the BEFORE-coding DECISIONS.md schema lock, and the explicit Stage-D-not-touched scope discipline. No remediation findings; nine SURPRISE_LOG entries dispositioned with explicit landing zones.

**Out-of-scope items (correctly deferred — no work this cycle).**
- SPEC15.9 d1 + d3 + d4 (default-flip evaluation + shape-floor implications + validation criteria) → cycle 15.6a-S1 after cycle 15.6b closes.
- SPEC15.12 entire priority (clustering defaults Track 1 + Track 2 + `multi-agent/full_instructions/CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` + Track 3 SAPS lock cross-reference) → cycle 15.6a-S1 after cycle 15.6b closes.
- `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]` (sample-level APS aggregation reliability-filter integration; Option A/B/C decision) → cycle 15.6a-S1 alongside SPEC15.12 d3.
- `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` (strategy-selection determinism diagnostic + fix) → cycle 15.6b (separately authorized; not emitted from cycle 15.6a closeout).
- The cross-pipeline structural unification meta-observation (`[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:8]`, High weight) → orchestrator triage for adjudication; possible dispositions are Logged-only (existing per-cycle ports suffice), Promoted to Phase 16+ BRAINSTORM, or Promoted to an `AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md` / `DEVPLAN-*` plan.
- SPEC15.20 closeout sweep items (six SURPRISE entries' fallback landing zone) → cycle 15.10a.
