## [v0.14.83] — 2026-04-30 ~16:00 EST

### Phase 15 cycle 15.5a (SPEC15.7 + SPEC15.8) — Cross-pipeline summit refinement (per-amplicon stage-selection menu + ODW-confined refinement + HMM sliding-offset port) + cross-pipeline summit↔timing one-pass 4-step convergence

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.5a (5 of 10 in `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`; bundles SPEC15.7 + SPEC15.8 because both share the summit-refinement module + active-stage logic, and SPEC15.8 explicitly consumes refined summits from SPEC15.7).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), Codex (codex-cli 0.126.0-alpha.8, GPT-5.5, Reasoning: Extra High)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Lands the cross-pipeline summit-refinement strategy menu, the one-pass 4-step summit↔timing convergence pattern, and the HMM sliding-offset port — promoting BRAIN15.29 / IBM-C6C from brainstorm to all three pipelines (HMM + Growth + RMS). Default invocation now runs the 4-step convergence (`step1: initial summit → step2: initial timing → step3: active-stage-confined summit re-refinement → step4: final timing using refined summits`), with `--summit-stage-selection odw_best_shape` as the universal default and per-pipeline overrides for the strategy choice. Step-numbered audit files preserve the pre-convergence intermediates; canonical timing/summit paths promote to the step-4 / step-3 outputs.

**1. New cross-pipeline `--summit-stage-selection` argparse group + per-pipeline overrides + opt-out (SPEC15.7 dlv 1, dlv 9 / SPEC15.8 dlv 7).** New flags in `onionskin.py:build_parser()`: universal `--summit-stage-selection {max_rcn,onset,last_active,odw_all,odw_best_shape,odw_narrowest_state}` (default `odw_best_shape`) + per-pipeline overrides `--hmm-summit-stage-selection`, `--growth-summit-stage-selection`, `--rms-summit-stage-selection` + opt-out `--no-active-stage-summit-refinement` + HMM sliding-offset knob `--hmm-sliding-offset-step` (int; default 100 bp). Strategy 6 (`odw_narrowest_state`) is HMM-only — Growth + RMS reject it at parse time with a clear error message. Help-text follows the Universal-in-spirit pattern (BRAIN15.16 cross-cutting list extended with **NEW: Summit Refinement**).

**2. Active-stages-mask infrastructure on shared refinement helpers (SPEC15.7 dlv 2 / SPEC15.8 dlv 3).** Extended `onionskin_core/refinement.py:refine_summit_parabola` and `refine_origin_sliding_offset` with optional `active_stages_mask` parameters; legacy all-stage behavior preserved when mask is `None`. Same extension on HMM-local `_estimate_parabola_bp` + `_compute_summit_estimates` + `run_step13_summit_refinement` (per-trajectory mask dict). Growth + RMS call sites pre-filter `smoothed_profiles` + `active_w` to ODW-active stages when a mask is set.

**3. HMM sliding-offset port + new `sliding_offset_bp` column on HMM summit estimates (SPEC15.7 dlv 3).** HMM now invokes `refine_origin_sliding_offset` from `_compute_summit_estimates`, reading per-sample bedGraphs from `01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/sample_tracks.tsv` (cycle-15.2a SPEC15.3 dlv 1) when present, with graceful fallback to aggregate stage bedGraphs when the per-sample manifest is absent. Cross-pipeline parity: Growth + RMS already emit `sliding_offset_bp`; HMM gains the same column.

**4. Cross-pipeline `max_rcn_stage` semantic parity via shared helper (SPEC15.7 dlv 4 — Option A).** New shared module `onionskin_core/summit_metrics.py` factors the windowed-argmax-of-peak-fold computation out of HMM's `hmm_ported_analyses.py:_max_rcn_stage` into `compute_max_rcn_stage()` plus three supporting helpers (`peak_fold_in_region`, `read_bedgraph_rows`, `load_per_stage_bedgraphs`). HMM's `_max_rcn_stage` is now a thin wrapper preserving the trajectory-based call site and the empty-input `""` return semantic. Growth at `growth_model_engine.py:1925-1986` (post-`write_stage_median_within_calls`) and RMS at `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:624-663` (post-`as_completed`) use post-write fix-up passes that recompute `max_rcn_stage` per row using the shared helper — the right structural choice because the per-stage RCN bedGraphs aren't on disk at the original write site (Growth writes them ~150 lines later; RMS writes them per-stage in parallel, so any stage's per-stage TSV is written before ALL other stages' chrom-norm bedGraphs exist). All three pipelines now produce mathematically identical `max_rcn_stage` semantics: argmax across stages of per-stage peak-fold over a window centered on the summit, half-width clamped to `[1 kb, min(width // 10, 50 kb)]`. Retires the prior buggy semantics (Growth: highest stage NUMBER present; RMS: trivially `int(stage)` per row).

**5. Six-strategy stage-selection machinery (SPEC15.7 dlv 1 strategy implementations).** New module `onionskin_core/summit_strategies.py` implements `select_stages(timing_row, all_stages, strategy)` plus `validate_strategy_for_pipeline()` and `resolve_pipeline_strategy()` helpers. Six strategies: `max_rcn` (reads `max_rcn_stage`/`max_stage` from timing.tsv), `onset` (reads `onset_stage`), `last_active` (reads `last_active_stage`), `odw_all` (reads `odw_start_stage` → `odw_end_stage`), `odw_best_shape` (default; reads ODW + per-stage shape-confidence; falls back to `odw_all` when shape evidence is absent), and HMM-only `odw_narrowest_state` (reads HMM summit-state interval data). Within-ODW non-monotonicity tolerance verified for strategies 4-5.

**6. Cross-pipeline 4-step summit↔timing convergence orchestration (SPEC15.8 dlv 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 9).** New module `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py` provides `run_growth_active_stage_convergence()` + `run_rms_active_stage_convergence()` + `_write_updated_timing()`. Per-pipeline new step directories (added to `output_layout.py`): Growth `09b-active-stage-summit/` + `10b-timing-updated/`; RMS `15b-active-stage-summit/` + `15c-timing-updated/`; HMM `20-active-stage-summit/` + `21-timing-updated/`. Each pipeline emits four step-numbered TSV pairs: `*_summits_step1.tsv` (initial summit estimates), `*_timing_step2.tsv` (initial timing), `*_summits_step3.tsv` (active-stage-confined re-refined summits using strategy-selected stages), `*_timing_step4.tsv` (final timing recomputed from step-3 summits). Stage-summary regeneration recomputes `median_peak_fold` against a window centered on the refined summit (BRAIN15.29 IBM-C6C "non-trivial refactor"). Canonical promotion: step-4 timing → canonical timing path; HMM step-3 summit estimates → canonical `18-timing/hmm_summit_estimates.tsv`. Step-numbered audit files preserved for diagnostic comparison. `--no-active-stage-summit-refinement` opt-out runs steps 1+2 only (legacy single-pass behavior).

**7. RMS step-3 active-stage refinement reads per-stage parabola TSVs.** `summit_convergence.run_rms_active_stage_convergence:286-314` globs RMS per-stage `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` files in the summit-refinement step directory, parses the stage number from each filename (defends against lexicographic glob ordering when ≥10 stages exist), concats into one DataFrame, spatial-matches each timing-row's call to the per-stage rows by `(chrom, start < call_end, end > call_start)` overlap, filters to strategy-selected stages, and takes the median of `parabola_summit_bp` (with `peak_raw_bp` fallback if no parabola data, `unified_calls.peak`/`call_peak` fallback if no per-stage rows match). Real-data verification on toy data: RMS call `II:3680000-4345000` shifts step1=4097843 → step3=4096689 (median of selected-stages-4-and-5 parabola summits [4095535, 4097843] = 4096689).

**8. Diagnostic notebooks comparing step-1 vs step-3 summits (SPEC15.8 dlv 8).** Growth + RMS notebook generators now write `summit_convergence.ipynb`. The HMM atlas notebook includes a step-1 vs step-3 summit-position comparison panel when convergence files exist.

**9. Strategy evaluation harness + 8 regression tests (SPEC15.7 dlv 5, 6 / SPEC15.8 dlv 9 + F19 contracts).** New `scripts/summit_strategy_evaluation.py` enumerates the 6 strategies × ODW probability/fold grid (36 cells) and writes a per-strategy / per-knob-combo summary report with truth BED counts and nearest-truth summit-distance metrics when real run outputs exist. Outputs go to `dev/runs/<run_name>/` per the CLAUDE.md dev rule. The harness automatically constrains `pipelines = "hmm"` when `strategy == "odw_narrowest_state"` (HMM-only strategy). Dry-run path validated. Full 36-cell benchmark deferred to a stand-alone `dev/runs/` task post-closeout — the default-strategy lock at `odw_best_shape` was made on design-contract grounds, so this is a benchmark report rather than a contract gate. New `tests/test_summit_refinement.py` covers 8 contracts: active-stages-mask correctness on `refine_summit_parabola` + `refine_origin_sliding_offset`, HMM sliding-offset + `max_rcn_stage` emission, ODW-all non-monotonicity tolerance, output-layout step-directory keys, Growth step-1/2/3/4 file emission, **RMS step-3 active-stage shift** (`test_rms_active_stage_convergence_shifts_step3_summit` — proves the no-op-bug regression is fixed), and **HMM mask-shift** (`test_hmm_active_stage_mask_shifts_summit` — asymmetric peak heights so masking out the dominant stage shifts at least one of `parabola_summit_state_bp` / `parabola_active_window_bp` / `sliding_offset_bp`). Updated `tests/test_timing_odw.py` for the RMS timing stage-summary schema required by convergence.

**Behavioral changes at default invocation.**
- 4-step convergence runs by default; canonical Growth/RMS/HMM timing outputs are promoted to step-4 timing; HMM canonical summit estimates are promoted to step-3 active-stage estimates; step-numbered audit files preserved for diagnostic comparison.
- Default summit-stage strategy is `odw_best_shape` (with all-ODW fallback when per-stage shape scores are absent).
- New cross-pipeline columns: `sliding_offset_bp` on HMM summit estimates (was Growth + RMS only); `max_rcn_stage` semantically corrected on Growth `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv` and RMS `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` (now mathematically identical to HMM's per-trajectory windowed RCN argmax via the shared `summit_metrics.compute_max_rcn_stage` helper).
- New per-pipeline directories: Growth `09b-active-stage-summit/` + `10b-timing-updated/`; RMS `15b-active-stage-summit/` + `15c-timing-updated/`; HMM `20-active-stage-summit/` + `21-timing-updated/`.
- New flags: `--summit-stage-selection`, `--{hmm,growth,rms}-summit-stage-selection`, `--no-active-stage-summit-refinement`, `--hmm-sliding-offset-step`.

**Validation:** `make test` PASS (`163 passed, 1 skipped in 54.19s` — +8 tests for the new convergence + mask + shift contracts, +2 of which are the R2-J shift regression tests), `make toy` PASS (clean re-run; cross-pipeline 4-step outputs verified; RMS step-3 shifts on real toy data; Growth + RMS + HMM `max_rcn_stage` all consistent at `5` on this 5-stage toy data via the shared helper), `make twin` PASS (perfect twin-peak resolution unaffected), `make full-twin` PASS (`Passed: 4, Failed: 0, Informational: 2`), `make puff-compare` PASS (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`). **The CRITICAL PuffStep parity gate is satisfied** — HMM detection (steps 06-10) is upstream of summit refinement and unaffected by both the 4-step convergence and the shared-helper refactor of `_max_rcn_stage`.

**Workflow note (R1 + R2 + R2-J + R3 cycle).** The cycle ran four rounds: Role 1 initial audit (`Claude Code 2.1.117`, 2026-04-29) declared OPEN-greenfield with 19 findings F1-F19 + a 9-stage commit-split spine; Role 2 implementation (`Codex 0.126.0-alpha.8 GPT-5.5 Extra High`, 2026-04-30) shipped Stages A-I across nine commits; Role 1 Round 2 re-audit (`Claude Code 2.1.117`, 2026-04-30) declared OPEN-remediation with three findings F-R2-1 (substantive — RMS step-3 was a no-op), F-R2-2 (cross-pipeline `max_rcn_stage` parity with user-decision Option A vs Option B), F-R2-3 (missing shift regression tests on RMS + HMM); Role 2 R2-J narrow round (`Claude Code 2.1.117`, 2026-04-30) addressed all three findings (Stage A: RMS step-3 fix; Stage B: Option A landed via shared helper; Stage C: 2 new shift tests); Role 1 Round 3 re-audit (`Claude Code 2.1.117`, 2026-04-30) closed the cycle. The R2-J implementer's three flagged in-situ departures (R3 named wrong path for Growth's per-stage RCN bedGraph; inline replacement at original line numbers wasn't structurally possible because per-stage bedGraphs aren't on disk yet at those points; HMM mask-shift test fixture took 2 iterations to land cleanly because symmetric peak heights left the parabola vertex unchanged under masking) were verified honest at re-audit and the right structural calls.

**Out-of-scope items (correctly deferred).**
- HMM APS ordering vs canonical timing promotion (DECISIONS.md `[2026-04-30]` caveat 1) — deferred to cycle 15.6a R1 audit per user direction. Currently harmless because HMM APS does not consume `hmm_timing.tsv`.
- Full 36-cell benchmark on full-chromosome truth fixtures (caveat 3) — deferred to a stand-alone `dev/runs/` task post-closeout. The harness is operational; the default-strategy lock at `odw_best_shape` was made on design-contract grounds.
- SUMMIT_SOUP-deferred parity work (cross-pipeline summit-algorithm parity completion; HMM per-stage parabola summit emission) — explicitly OUT OF SCOPE per SPEC15.7 + the user's brainstorm-stage direction. Captured at `multi-agent/plans/next/SUMMIT_SOUP.md` Item 5 expansion + Open brainstorm questions 6-12. The R2-J round expanded SUMMIT_SOUP with Open brainstorm questions 11-12 + Item 5 expansion (2026-04-30) covering cross-pipeline `step3_n_stages_used` parity discussion + Phase 15 scope-expansion question (committed separately as `b4fd100`); parked pending user direction at orchestrator triage.

**Files changed:** `onionskin.py`, `onionskin_core/summit_strategies.py` (new), `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py` (new), `onionskin_core/summit_metrics.py` (new), `onionskin_core/refinement.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_summit_refinement.py`, `onionskin_core/output_layout.py`, `onionskin_core/timing.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_notebooks.py`, `onionskin_core/notebooks.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/growth_model_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py`, `scripts/summit_strategy_evaluation.py` (new), `tests/test_summit_refinement.py`, `tests/test_timing_odw.py`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/plans/next/SUMMIT_SOUP.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/DECISIONS.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

## [v0.14.82] — 2026-04-29 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.4b (SPEC15.22) — Gap-analysis architectural cleanup: HMM step relocation + cross-pipeline `gap_suspect` filter contract + cross-pipeline `_finalize_amplicon_recommendations` port

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.4b (mid-phase amendment per `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:5]` part-a; partial reconciliation of the parallel keep/exclude frameworks — full reconciliation deferred to a future phase per `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:5]` part-b).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max).

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Closes the architectural defect surfaced at cycle 15.4a-S1 closeout: HMM gap-analysis lived at step 19 (LAST in the HMM dispatch order), so APS / SAPS / timing / clustering all ran on call sets the gap analysis had not yet annotated. The fix relocates HMM gap-analysis to step 15 (immediately after multistage unification at step 14), promotes gap-analysis from annotation-only to a participating filter via a new `gap_suspect` column on `_calls.gap_annotated.tsv` cross-pipeline, threads `gap_suspect` through the reliability scorer's flag-decision logic as a hard-exclude axis (subject to the `keep_override` escape hatch from `--keep-amplicons-bed` / `--known-reliable-amplicons`), and ports the long-growth-only Priority 4.9 `_finalize_amplicon_recommendations()` advisory-BED logic to RMS + HMM so all three pipelines now emit `*_amplicons_recommended_to_keep.bed` + `*_amplicons_recommended_for_exclusion*.bed` from their gap-analysis directories.

**1. HMM step relocation + renumber.** `output_layout.build_hmm_steps()` updated: step15 = `15-gap-analysis` (NEW SLOT — was previously the LAST step at `19-gap-analysis`), step16 = `16-aps` (was 15), step17 = `17-saps` (was 16), step18 = `18-timing` (was 17), step19 = `19-clustering` (was 18). HMM engine dispatch reordered so gap-analysis fires after `step14_hmm_multistage_unification` and before `run_step16_hmm_aps`. New `step15_*` gap-analysis kwargs (`step15_near_gap_bp`, `step15_gap_mask_strategy`, `step15_gap_mask_bin_threshold`, `step15_gap_bedgraphs`, `step15_precomputed_mask_bed`) plumbed through the engine signature; existing `step15_*`/`step17_*` APS+timing kwargs renamed to `step16_*`/`step18_*`. Old post-engine HMM gap-analysis call retired from `onionskin.py` controller (now done inside the engine at step15). Help-text `[hmm: NN-...]` annotations swept across `onionskin.py`. `readme.py`, `flat_sample.py` docstring, and `timing.py:_mad_track_dirs()` updated for the new HMM APS path (`16-aps`). PIPELINE_SPEC HMM step table + per-step output catalog + mermaid diagrams + module docstrings (`output_layout.py`, `engines/hmm_engine.py`) updated.

**2. New `gap_suspect` column on `_calls.gap_annotated.tsv`, cross-pipeline.** `onionskin_core.gap_analysis.derive_gap_suspect()` factors out the gap-portion of Priority 4.9's rescue logic into a reusable helper. `annotate_calls_with_gap_distance()` gains an optional `width_growth_lookup` callable kwarg + emits the new `gap_suspect` boolean column + a new `*_amplicons_recommended_for_exclusion_gap.bed` cross-pipeline (HMM step15 + Growth step12 + RMS step09). The threshold constants `_WIDTH_RESCUE_MIN_SLOPE_KB = 0.0` and `_WIDTH_RESCUE_MIN_R2 = 0.40` agree with `_finalize_amplicon_recommendations()` so the new column and the legacy growth-only `_amplicons_recommended_*.bed` outputs make consistent decisions on the same data. **v1 caveat:** per-pipeline `width_growth_lookup` is not yet wired (a v1.x follow-up); without rescue, any `high_density` gap-concern call → `gap_suspect=True` (more conservative; flags more for filtering — the right direction biologically).

**3. Reliability scorer integrates `gap_suspect` as a hard-exclude axis.** `compute_reliability_sidecar()` gains a `gap_suspect_call_ids: Optional[Set[str]]` kwarg; new `gap_suspect` column added to `RELIABILITY_COLUMNS`. Flag-decision precedence is now: (1) `keep_override` → pass; else (2) `gap_suspect` → filter; else (3) `reliability_score >= class_threshold` → pass else filter. `gap_suspect` is hard-exclude subject to `keep_override` (same semantics as today's reliability filter). New `read_gap_suspect_call_ids()` helper reads `gap_suspect=True` call_ids from a pipeline's `_calls.gap_annotated.tsv` (handles `call_id` / `trajectory_id` / coordinate-key fallback). Threaded `gap_annotated_tsv` kwarg through `compute_and_write_aps()` + `run_step16_hmm_aps()`; wired call sites in growth (Phase-2 post-processing), RMS (controller post-detection), HMM (engine step16 dispatch). **Behavioral effect:** any `high_density` gap-concern call cross-pipeline is now filtered from APS matrix + sample-level APS aggregation unless `--keep-amplicons-bed` / `--known-reliable-amplicons` supplies a keep override.

**4. `_finalize_amplicon_recommendations()` ported cross-pipeline.** Function generalized to accept full-path overrides (`calls_tsv_override`, `gap_tsv_override`, `timing_tsv_override`, `origins_tsv_override`) + `out_basename_override` + `pipeline_label`. Same Priority-4.9 integration logic now serves growth (existing v0.4.13 path preserved verbatim), RMS (NEW: wired in `_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller` post-`compute_and_write_aps`), HMM (NEW: wired post-engine in `_run_hmm_engine`). Column-name normalization at function entry (HMM's `amplicon_start`/`amplicon_end` → `start`/`end` aliases) so the legacy growth-style downstream loop body works for HMM trajectory schema. HMM `origins_tsv` passed as None — HMM has no growth-style `_origins.tsv` (analog at `12-fork-travel/width_progression_metrics.tsv` not yet adapted into rescue logic; a v1.x follow-up). Per-pipeline log labels distinguish growth/rms/hmm output messages. **Behavioral effect:** all three pipelines now emit `*_amplicons_recommended_to_keep.bed` + `*_amplicons_recommended_for_exclusion.bed` from their gap-analysis directories (cross-pipeline parity with the long-growth-only Priority 4.9 BEDs that have shipped since v0.4.13).

**5. Notebook + spec sweep completion.** Final-Overseer audit caught five doc/notebook surfaces that the Stage-A renumber sweep had missed: (a) `hmm_notebooks.py` Atlas + Parameter + Fork Travel notebook templates wrote stale `'15-aps'` / `'18-clustering'` / `'17-timing'` paths into shipped `.ipynb` files (runtime regression — `FileNotFoundError` on user notebook execution; not caught by `make` targets because notebooks are written but not executed by tests); (b) `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` HMM step section header re-ordering + per-step output catalog rows for `16-saps/`, `17-timing/`, `18-clustering/` not touched by the original sweep; (c) `output_layout.py` module docstring + `_INDEX_MD_TEMPLATE` (the latter ships verbatim into user-facing `00-INDEX.md` in every grouping dir) referenced `19-gap-analysis/` as the LAST HMM step; (d) `engines/hmm_engine.py` module docstring layout block carried the pre-renumber sequence; (e) `flat_sample.py` two stale `step15-aps` prose mentions. Post-Final-Overseer remediation round (this entry) repairs all five surfaces; freshly regenerated toy notebooks now ship correct paths.

**6. New regression tests.** `tests/test_reliability.py::test_gap_suspect_filter_excludes_call_from_reliability` proves the new gap_suspect → filter contract. `::test_keep_amplicons_bed_overrides_gap_suspect_filter` proves the `--keep-amplicons-bed` / `--known-reliable-amplicons` escape hatch wins over `gap_suspect`. `::test_derive_gap_suspect_rescue_logic` covers the four-way rescue-logic decision matrix (summit_dense always suspect / high_density without rescue suspect / high_density with rescue passes / everything else not suspect). Two existing pipeline tests updated for the HMM step renumber: `test_two_stage_hmm_can_force_chrom_median_no_ratio` (step paths) + `test_force_manifest_remaps_pri0` (HMM APS path).

**Behavioral changes at default invocation.** HMM `01-hmm/` step layout now reads: `14-multistage-unification → 15-gap-analysis (NEW SLOT) → 16-aps → 17-saps → 18-timing → 19-clustering`. Old `19-gap-analysis` slot retired. Cross-pipeline `*_amplicons_recommended_for_exclusion_gap.bed` emitted by all three pipelines when ≥1 call has `gap_suspect=True`. New `gap_suspect` column on `_calls.gap_annotated.tsv` AND `amplicon_reliability.tsv` cross-pipeline (additive — no existing column renamed). Cross-pipeline `*_amplicons_recommended_{for_exclusion,to_keep}.bed` advisory BEDs now emitted by HMM + RMS + Growth (was growth-only since v0.4.13). HMM is more conservative about gap-suspect calls than growth on equivalent data because the HMM width-growth rescue plumbing isn't wired yet (v1.x follow-up).

**Validation:** `make test` PASS (`155 passed, 1 skipped` — +3 tests for the new gap_suspect contract), `make toy` PASS (clean notebook regeneration with correct step paths), `make twin` PASS (perfect twin-peak resolution unaffected), `make full-twin` PASS (`Passed: 4, Failed: 0, Informational: 2`), `make puff-compare` PASS (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`). **The CRITICAL PuffStep parity gate is satisfied** — HMM detection (steps 06-10) is upstream of the renumber and was unaffected.

**Workflow note (single-agent multi-cycle mode + Final-Overseer remediation).** Cycle 15.4b ran in the user-introduced single-agent multi-cycle workflow (R1+R2 in one solo session; per-cycle self-assessment + self-pushback notes in lieu of R3 closeout) with closeout deferred to an independent fresh-chat Final Overseer. Final Overseer ran the five validation gates independently, verified the runtime contract held cleanly, but caught five doc/notebook surfaces the Stage-A R1 blast-radius table had not enumerated. Post-Final-Overseer remediation round (returned to the original solo session per orchestrator direction; Final Overseer recommended skip-reaudit on the doc/notebook fix as mechanical with no design judgment) repaired all five surfaces and re-ran the three relevant gates (`make test`, `make toy`, `make puff-compare`).

**Surprises surfaced during cycle (planning misses worth recording).** The cascade-rename bug during Stage A (chained `replace_all` on overlapping numeric prefixes `step15:` → `step16:` → `step17:` → `step18:` collapsed all log lines to the final value) is captured in the session memory file `feedback_replace_all_cascade_chain_pitfall.md` for future agents. The two-layer audit gap (R1 missed naming five surfaces; R2 only swept what R1 named; only Final-Overseer's independent live-tree pass caught them) reinforces `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:7]` (systemic cross-pipeline-parity audit gap) — applies the same pattern to documentation surfaces, not just code surfaces.

**Files changed:** `onionskin_core/output_layout.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `onionskin_core/gap_analysis.py`, `onionskin_core/reliability.py`, `onionskin_core/aps.py`, `onionskin_core/timing.py`, `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_notebooks.py`, `onionskin.py`, `tests/test_reliability.py`, `tests/test_pipeline.py`, `tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

## [v0.14.81] — 2026-04-29 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.4a-S1 (post-closeout follow-up to cycle 15.4a) — Path B: HMM gains MAD-derived sigmas in stage-to-stage AND per-locus ODW tests, on par with Growth + RMS

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.4a-S1 (supplemental follow-up to cycle 15.4a; addresses R3-O4).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max).

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Closes the R3-O4 statistical-fidelity gap surfaced at cycle 15.4a closeout (v0.14.80): the new Bayesian per-locus flat-sample test in `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` was using a peak_rcn-spread heuristic for sigmas while the stage-to-stage ODW transition test in `onionskin_core/timing.py` was already consuming per-stage MAD bedGraphs (cycle 15.3a). User chose Path B (broader fix) over Path A (narrow): not just consume MAD where available, but also wire HMM step15-aps to *emit* MAD bedGraphs so HMM's stage-to-stage ODW timing test ALSO benefits from variance-aware sigmas — closing a previously-unflagged gap from cycle 15.3a where HMM's timing fell back to heuristic because HMM step15 had only ever called `compute_aps_tables()` (lightweight metric path), not `compute_and_write_aps()` (full APS with MAD emission).

**1. HMM step15-aps now emits per-stage median + MAD bedGraphs.** `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:run_step15_hmm_aps()` calls `aps.write_stage_median_tracks()` after `compute_aps_tables()`, writing `stageN.RCN.bedGraph` + `stageN.log2RCN.bedGraph` + `stageN.MAD_RCN.bedGraph` + `stageN.MAD_log2RCN.bedGraph` to `01-hmm/15-aps/genome_stage_medians/`. Output is additive — no existing schema rename, no existing-file changes. PuffStep gold-standard parity confirmed unchanged (`make puff-compare` PASS 28/28).

**2. `timing.py:_mad_track_dirs()` includes HMM step15-aps.** Adds `base/15-aps/genome_stage_medians` alongside the long-standing growth (`13-aps`) and RMS (`12-aps`) candidates. Same shared timing module serves all three pipeline arms; per-pipeline branching avoided. **Behavioral effect:** HMM's stage-to-stage ODW transition test now consumes MAD-derived sigmas instead of the heuristic fallback, on par with the Bayesian-ODW-consistency commitment. Could shift `last_active_stage` / `amplicon_class` assignments on real HMM data in a direction that is biologically more faithful to the variance-aware framework.

**3. `flat_sample.py` consumes MAD bedGraphs at the per-locus level.** New `mad_dir` kwarg on `compute_flat_sample_sidecar()` and `write_flat_sample_sidecar()`. Per-row sigma resolution now caches per-stage MAD bedGraphs and reads MAD at the locus interval (median MAD over `[start, end)` × 1.4826), with fallback chain: per-locus MAD → `rcn_sigma`/`log2_sigma` overrides if passed → peak_rcn-spread heuristic → module-level `_DEFAULT_*_SIGMA` constants. New helpers `_load_locus_mad_sigma`, `_median_mad_at_locus`, `_median_mad_from_cached` mirror the `timing.py:_median_mad_sigma` pattern. Threaded through `aps.py:compute_and_write_aps` and `hmm_ported_analyses.py:run_step15_hmm_aps`. When `mad_dir` is None or bedGraphs are absent, behavior matches v0.14.80 exactly.

**4. New regression tests.** `tests/test_reliability.py::test_flat_sample_consumes_mad_bedgraphs_when_available` proves the MAD path produces different verdicts than the heuristic fallback (synthetic MAD bedGraphs with skewed-narrow vs skewed-wide spread → tight-MAD-sample tests "not flat" while wide-MAD-sample tests "flat" at the same peak_rcn). `tests/test_reliability.py::test_flat_sample_falls_back_when_mad_dir_missing` verifies behavior is identical to v0.14.80 when no `mad_dir` is provided OR the directory does not exist.

**Behavioral changes at default invocation.** HMM `01-hmm/15-aps/` now contains a `genome_stage_medians/` subdirectory with the four per-stage bedGraph files (RCN + log2RCN + MAD_RCN + MAD_log2RCN). HMM stage-to-stage ODW timing test transitions from heuristic-fallback sigmas to MAD-derived sigmas — may produce slightly different `last_active_stage` / `amplicon_class` assignments compared to v0.14.80 on real datasets. HMM per-locus flat-sample test similarly transitions to MAD-derived sigmas — may produce different `flat_sample_flag` assignments at the (sample, locus) level. Posterior stage-1 anchoring downstream of these changes inherits the new flat-sample verdicts. PuffStep parity for HMM detection (steps 06-10) is unchanged; the behavioral shift is at the timing/classification/flat-sample layer downstream of HMM detection.

**Validation:** `make test` PASS (`152 passed, 1 skipped` — +2 tests for the new MAD path), `make toy` PASS, `make twin` PASS (perfect twin-peak resolution), `make full-twin` PASS (`Passed: 4, Failed: 0, Informational: 2`), `make puff-compare` PASS (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`).

**Surprises surfaced during cycle (planning misses worth recording).** Two related KNOWN_ISSUES entries written this cycle:
- `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:3]`: `--compute-aps` and `--posterior` should become `--skip-aps` / `--skip-posterior`. Code-dive verified that `--compute-aps` only suppresses growth APS — RMS APS is gated on its own preconditions and HMM step15-aps is gated on `len(non_ref_stages) >= 1`, neither honors `--compute-aps`. The flag is more vestigial than its name suggests.
- `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:4]`: posterior-regression tracking — posterior should perform as well or better than prior across tests. Currently no version-to-version A/B regression test exists for the timing/posterior layer; PuffStep covers detection only.

**Files changed:** `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `onionskin_core/timing.py`, `onionskin_core/aps.py`, `tests/test_reliability.py`, `multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

## [v0.14.80] — 2026-04-29 ~02:00 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.4a — HMM shape-filter + multistage unification + cross-pipeline reliability (SPEC15.5 + SPEC15.6): new HMM multistage-unification step + Bayesian flat-sample contract + posterior stage-1 anchoring

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.4a (4 of 10 in `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), Codex-cli 0.126.0-alpha.8 (GPT-5.5 ; Reasoning: Extra High), GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Xhigh)

**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Fourth execution cycle of Phase 15. This cycle lands the HMM-side multistage-unification surface from SPEC15.5, the shared pre-APS reliability contract from SPEC15.6, and the Round 4 redesign that replaces the original APS-area preamp gate with Bayesian per-locus flat-sample detection plus posterior stage-1 anchoring. HMM now emits a dedicated `14-multistage-unification/` step with shape-score and state-path credibility evidence; APS across HMM, growth, and RMS now emits `amplicon_reliability.tsv` plus a new per-(sample, prior_stage) `flat_samples.tsv`; posterior manifests now reserve stage 1 for flat/pre-amplification samples when they exist. This closes IBM-2026-04-14:1's exit condition that posterior stage 1 contain only pre-amplification samples.

**1. New HMM multistage-unification step + 4-mode decision surface.** Cycle 15.4a inserts `01-hmm/14-multistage-unification/` and wires the HMM shape-score sink, cross-stage shape meta-analysis, state-path growth-credibility scoring, and the new `--multistage-unification-mode {triangle-only,state-path-only,both-required,either-suffices}` flag (default `either-suffices`). The HMM late-step layout now reads `15-aps`, `16-saps`, `17-timing`, `18-clustering`, `19-gap-analysis`. HMM progression metrics remain under `12-fork-travel/width_progression_metrics.tsv` by design, rather than opening another numbered step just for the TSV sink.

**2. Shared reliability scorer for all APS-producing pipelines.** New `onionskin_core/reliability.py` computes one row per amplicon/call_id with five evidence-axis sub-scores (`rcn_increase_score`, `width_increase_score`, `area_increase_score`, `bic_increase_score`, `parabola_height_increase_score`), a composite `reliability_score`, `reliability_flag`, `flat_sample_flag`, and `keep_override`. The scorer is deliberately conservative: low absolute RCN is not a penalty by itself, but inconsistent developmental behavior is. Class-aware thresholds keep `Founder` / prolonged classes harder to exclude, and `--keep-amplicons-bed` remains the user-controlled escape hatch.

**3. Round 4 flat-sample redesign: Bayesian per-locus detector, new module, retired flag.** New `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` owns flat-sample detection as a reliability consumer rather than an APS-owned threshold rule. The per-locus question "is this sample showing growth at this locus?" now reuses the shared ODW detector primitive against the chrom-median baseline (`previous_rcn=1.0`, `previous_log2=0.0`) and rolls up to a sample-level flat verdict through `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` (default 1.0). The old `--aps-preamp-threshold` / `auto` gate is removed completely. New CLI flags replace it: `--flat-sample-prob-threshold`, `--flat-sample-fold-threshold`, `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction`, and `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage`. APS call sites now run a two-pass order: reliability sidecar first, flat-sample sidecar second, then backfill the per-call `flat_sample_flag` column.

**4. Posterior stage-1 anchoring is now in-cycle behavior, not deferred.** `build_posterior_manifest()` accepts `flat_sample_ids` and anchors those samples to `posterior_stage=1` before clustering the remaining samples into `2..k+1`. A new `anchor_pinned` column in `posterior_manifest_cluster_map.tsv` distinguishes anchored rows from algorithmically clustered rows. Growth and RMS inherit this through the shared APS/posterior machinery; HMM consumes the same posterior manifest. This is the biological payoff for the flat-sample system: when true flat samples exist, stage 1 is now explicitly the pre-amplification anchor.

**5. Zero-flat safeguard and reliability-flag synonym.** After each APS run, the controller reads `flat_samples.tsv`, prints the detected flat-sample count, and when that count is zero auto-switches any posterior analysis that would otherwise use `ref-stage` normalization to `chrom-median`. `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` opts out. The reliability keep-override flag now accepts both `--keep-amplicons-bed` and `--known-reliable-amplicons` as synonyms targeting the same setting.

**6. Regression coverage and required parity reruns.** New regression tests cover Bayesian per-locus flat detection, relaxed `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction`, zero-flat auto-switch trigger + override behavior, posterior anchoring, and the legacy no-flat stage-numbering path. The two posterior toy-pipeline tests were updated to accept either contiguous `[1, 2]` (no flat anchors) or `[1, 2, 3]` (flat anchors present) under `--aps-cluster-k 2`, which is the correct biology-aware expectation after the Round 4 contract change.

**Behavioral changes at default invocation.** APS no longer uses the retired `--aps-preamp-threshold` surface. Instead, HMM, growth, and RMS all default to the Bayesian per-locus flat-sample detector inheriting the ODW thresholds. `flat_samples.tsv` now records per-(sample, prior_stage) rows with `n_reliable_loci`, `n_flat_loci`, and `flat_fraction`; the old threshold-oriented schema is gone. Posterior stage numbering may now shift from `1..k` to `1..k+1` when flat samples exist because stage 1 is reserved for the anchor and clustered stages move to `2..k+1`. When zero flat samples are detected, any posterior analysis still pointed at `ref-stage` is automatically redirected to `chrom-median` unless the user explicitly passes `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage`.

**Validation:** `make test` PASS (`150 passed, 1 skipped`), `make toy` PASS, `make twin` PASS, `make full-twin` PASS, and `make puff-compare` PASS (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`) on the post-follow-up tree. This satisfies the cycle-closeout parity requirement after the Round 4 follow-up.

**Known follow-up (deferred from this cycle, surfaced for transparency).** The Bayesian per-locus flat-sample test in `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py:_resolve_default_sigma` derives RCN/log2 sigmas from a peak_rcn-spread heuristic vs the chrom-median baseline rather than from per-stage MAD bedGraphs. SPEC15.4 / cycle 15.3a explicitly plumbed `stageN.MAD_RCN.bedGraph` and `stageN.MAD_log2RCN.bedGraph` through `onionskin_core/timing.py:_median_mad_sigma` for the stage-to-stage ODW transition test; the per-locus flat-sample test reuses the same `transition_probability` primitive but does not yet consume that MAD source. The fallback ships as a conservative module-level constant (`_DEFAULT_RCN_SIGMA = 0.1`, `_DEFAULT_LOG2_SIGMA = 0.15`) plus a per-call peak_rcn-spread estimate. This is acceptable for closing v0.14.80 (the contract was "Bayesian per-locus test with default thresholds inheriting from `--odw-active-{prob,fold}-threshold`"; the SPEC did not lock the sigma source) but creates a statistical-fidelity gap inside the broader "Bayesian-ODW-consistent" commitment because the stage-to-stage path now consumes higher-fidelity sigmas than the per-locus path. The MAD-derived per-locus sigma plumbing will land in a small post-closeout follow-up cycle (cycle 15.4a-S1 or equivalent — orchestrator picks the slot) before any production calibration runs depend on it. The DS1 chr II calibration regression test currently skips when the calibration fixture is absent, so the locked acceptance criterion ("the 4 calibration amplicons must come out weight ≈ 1.0") is not yet biologically gated against the heuristic sigma either way.

**Files changed:** `onionskin.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_multistage_unification.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_fork_travel.py`, `onionskin_core/reliability.py`, `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`, `onionskin_core/posterior.py`, `onionskin_core/aps.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`, `tests/test_reliability.py`, `tests/test_pipeline.py`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

## [v0.14.79] — 2026-04-29 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.3a — Bayesian ODW System (SPEC15.4): new `--odw-*` argparse group + 3 detector formulations + `last_active_stage` rename + 4-metric framework + RMS-timing wiring

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.3a (3 of 9 in `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), Codex-cli 0.126.0-alpha.8 (GPT-5.5 ; Reasoning: Extra High).
**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Third execution cycle of Phase 15. Replaces the legacy 1.25× hard-threshold rule (formerly at `onionskin_core/timing.py:374` `_ACTIVE_FOLD_THRESHOLD = 1.25`) with a parameterized variance-aware Bayesian ODW (Origin Detection Window) framework exposed via a new dedicated `--odw-*` argparse group. Three detector formulations (`hardthreshold` / `gaussian_overlap` / `bayesian_posterior`); the new default is `bayesian_posterior`. Stage 1 is now detectable against background using the same primitive + thresholds. Renames `latest_activity_stage` → `last_active_stage` cross-pipeline. Wires RMS-timing as a new 15-timing/ output so all three pipelines (HMM, growth, RMS) emit the same ODW + `amplicon_class` columns. Lays the cross-pipeline statistical foundation that SPEC15.5 / 15.6 / 15.7 / 15.8 consume.

**1. New `--odw-*` argparse group (Origin Detection Window).** Three flags at `onionskin.py:1298-1340`:

| Flag | Default | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--odw-active-stage-detector` | `bayesian_posterior` | choice | Detector for ODW active-stage boundaries: `hardthreshold` (legacy 1.25× rule), `gaussian_overlap` (MAD-derived RCN uncertainty), or `bayesian_posterior` (log2-fold posterior, default). |
| `--odw-prob-threshold` | `0.9` | float | Probability threshold for `gaussian_overlap` and `bayesian_posterior`. Ignored by `hardthreshold`. |
| `--odw-fold-threshold` | `1.25` | float | Fold-change threshold for `hardthreshold` and `bayesian_posterior`. Ignored by `gaussian_overlap`. |

Group header enumerates the downstream surfaces affected: timing analyses (`onset_stage`, `last_active_stage`, `activity_breadth`, `amplicon_class`), summit refinement (SPEC15.7), reliability scoring (SPEC15.6), multistage unification (SPEC15.5), summit↔timing convergence (SPEC15.8), and optional APS integration (SPEC15.11). Cross-pipeline step references (`[growth: 10-timing; rms: 15-timing; hmm: 16-timing]`).

**2. New `onionskin_core/odw.py` module.** Centralizes the three detector formulations + dispatch + stage-1 background test. Exposes `transition_probability()`, `transition_is_active()`, and `detect_active_stages()`. Each detector handles direction-reversal cleanly via a `direction` parameter (`"growth"` for ODW active-stage detection, `"regression"` for APS dip detection — see point 6 below).

- `hardthreshold`: deterministic 1.25× ratio test (legacy compatibility; uses only `--odw-fold-threshold`).
- `gaussian_overlap`: `norm.cdf((med_{s+1} - med_s) / sqrt(sigma_s² + sigma_{s+1}²))` where `sigma = MAD × 1.4826`. Matches the existing `aps.py:1022` primitive at default thresholds (uses only `--odw-prob-threshold`).
- `bayesian_posterior` (DEFAULT): log-ratio Normal model `1 - norm.cdf((log(fold_threshold) - observed_log_ratio_mean) / observed_log_ratio_sigma)` using MAD-derived log2 sigma (uses both knobs).

**3. `latest_activity_stage` → `last_active_stage` rename cross-pipeline.** Single canonical name; no synonyms. Eleven occurrences in `onionskin_core/timing.py` + downstream classifier consumers updated. Test asserts the rename is enforced (`assert "latest_activity_stage" not in df.columns`).

**4. Four-metric framework formalized.** All four canonical metrics now emit:

- `onset_stage` — ODW START (existing).
- `last_active_stage` — ODW END (renamed from `latest_activity_stage`; detector configurable; non-monotonic-OK within ODW).
- `max_rcn_stage` — already existed at `aps.py:1016` (`peak_rcn_stage` retired as a noun per Universal rule 2).
- `regression_stage` — already existed at `aps.py:1017`; now consumes the same `--odw-active-stage-detector` with internal direction-reversal.

**5. New ODW boundary metadata columns.** `timing.py:541-543`:

- `odw_start_stage` (alias of `onset_stage`).
- `odw_end_stage` (alias of `last_active_stage`).
- `odw_duration_stages` = `odw_end_stage - odw_start_stage + 1`.

**6. APS regression-stage direction-reversal.** `onionskin_core/aps.py:1037-1051` calls `transition_probability(direction="regression", ...)` from the same shared `odw.py` primitive. Single detector flag drives both growth and regression directions. **Documented limitation:** the regression gate threshold preserves the legacy `_DIP_PROB_THRESHOLD = 0.8` constant rather than consuming `--odw-prob-threshold`. This is a deliberate backward-compat preservation for APS confirmed-dip detection. Tracking entry `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:2]` added to `multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md` for SPEC15.20 user-adjudication.

**7. Stage-1 background detection.** `odw.py:239-262` + `timing.py:504-519`. Stage 1 is now detectable against background (chrom-median-normalized RCN=1.0 baseline) using the same primitive + same thresholds as stages 2+. Per JQ32 LOCKED rule. Test `test_stage_1_background_growth_promotes_onset` asserts onset_stage is upgraded to 1 when stage-1-vs-background growth is detected.

**8. RMS-timing wiring (deliverable 9; cross-pipeline reach).** New `run_rms_timing()` at `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:107-198` mirrors the HMM `compute_timing_from_stage_summary` pattern. Builds `rms_stage_summary.tsv` from final unified RMS amplicon intervals + stage chrom-normalized tracks. Emits `rms_stage_summary.tsv` + `rms_timing.tsv` to a new RMS step directory `03-rcn-mean-shift/15-timing/` (`output_layout.py:166`). After this lands, all three pipelines (HMM, growth, RMS) emit the same ODW + `amplicon_class` columns — satisfies SPEC15.4 deliverable 10 (cross-pipeline reach) and unblocks SPEC15.6 / 15.7 / 15.8 dependencies.

**9. MAD bedGraph consumption for variance-aware sigma.** `timing.py:477-496` loads per-stage `MAD_RCN` and `MAD_log2RCN` bedGraphs (already shipped at v0.5.47) for each call's window. Falls back to `_fallback_sigma()` per-stage when MAD bedGraphs are missing. Sigma derivation: `sigma = MAD × 1.4826` (Gaussian-equivalent scale factor).

**10. Elongation-interference comments in code.** `timing.py:501-503` and `aps.py:1035-1036` document the underlying biological motivation (replication forks shifting parabola center in late stages → ODW boundary detection necessary).

**11. Tests added.** New `tests/test_timing_odw.py` (143 lines, 4 tests):

- `test_timing_schema_uses_last_active_stage_and_odw_boundaries` — schema rename + ODW alias columns.
- `test_stage_1_background_growth_promotes_onset` — stage-1 background detection.
- `test_three_detectors_dispatch_correctly` — each detector mode produces expected output on synthetic profiles.
- `test_rms_timing_emits_amplicon_class_and_odw_columns` — RMS-timing wiring + cross-pipeline column parity.

`tests/test_pipeline.py` also gains a help-string assertion for the new `[growth: 10-timing; rms: 15-timing; hmm: 16-timing]` cross-pipeline timing-step references.

**Documentation.** `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md` documents the three detectors + stage-1-vs-background test + the four-metric framework + the three ODW alias columns. `multi-agent/full_instructions/ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md` introduces ODW as the shared cross-pipeline detector framework with boundary-alias listing.

**Behavioral change call-out.** This is a **behavior-changing cycle at default invocation**:

- The default ODW active-stage detector flips from legacy `hardthreshold` (1.25× hard rule) to `bayesian_posterior` (`--odw-prob-threshold 0.9`, `--odw-fold-threshold 1.25`).
- Default invocations may produce different `last_active_stage`, `odw_end_stage`, `activity_breadth`, `n_active_stages`, and `amplicon_class` values relative to pre-cycle baselines.
- APS regression-stage detection threshold remains at the legacy 0.8 (preserved per pushback decision; tracked at `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:2]`).
- Stage 1 may now be tagged as `onset_stage=1` for amplicons that were previously onset-classified at stage 2+.

**For users needing legacy timing compatibility, pass:**

```
--odw-active-stage-detector hardthreshold --odw-fold-threshold 1.25
```

Backward-compat verified: at these flag values, growth and HMM timing outputs are byte-identical to pre-cycle baselines (after compensating for the `latest_activity_stage` → `last_active_stage` rename + the new ODW alias columns; "legacy projection" comparison passes).

**Validation:** `python -m pytest tests/test_timing_odw.py tests/test_pipeline.py` 109/109 PASS (4 new ODW tests + 105 prior pipeline tests). `make test` PASS. `make toy`, `make single`, `make twin` PASS. Fresh full-genome HMM run at `dev/runs/puffstep-py-full-15-3a/` regenerated under HEAD; `make puff-compare RUN=dev/runs/puffstep-py-full-15-3a/01-prior/01-hmm` PASS 28/28 across HMM steps 06-10 (SPEC15.4 doesn't touch HMM core, but the gold-standard regression gate is cheap insurance). Backward-compat anchor: `--odw-active-stage-detector hardthreshold --odw-fold-threshold 1.25` reproduces pre-cycle timing outputs byte-identical via legacy projection.

**Files changed:** `onionskin.py`, `onionskin_core/odw.py` (new, 277 lines), `onionskin_core/timing.py`, `onionskin_core/aps.py`, `onionskin_core/output_layout.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `tests/test_timing_odw.py` (new, 143 lines), `tests/test_pipeline.py`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SPEC.md` (priority status table), `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`, `multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

## [v0.14.78] — 2026-04-29 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.2a — Foundational pipeline architecture (SPEC15.2 + SPEC15.3): shared pre-pipeline gap mask + HMM parallel child pipeline (`indiv_samples/`) + step renumber (15-saps / 16-timing / 17-clustering / 18-gap-analysis) + step-14 APS bug fix

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.2a (2 of 9 in `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max), Codex (codex-cli 0.126.0-alpha.8, GPT-5.5 ; Reasoning: Extra High).
**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

Second execution cycle of Phase 15. Closes `[ISSUE:2026-04-19:1]` (HMM step-14 APS reading raw manifest bedGraphs instead of HMM-normalized per-sample tracks). Lifts the gap-mask + missingness diagnostic to a controller-level pre-pipeline step shared across all three pipelines (default to multi-file-intersection mask via `build_shared_gap_mask_from_bedgraphs()`). Renumbers HMM late steps to insert a SAPS placeholder at step 15 and an HMM gap-analysis output at step 18. Adds per-sample `indiv_samples/` outputs across HMM steps 03-11, unblocking SPEC15.6 (reliability scoring), SPEC15.7 (per-sample summit refinement), and SPEC15.13 (SAPS implementation).

**1. Shared pre-pipeline gap mask + missingness diagnostic.** New controller-level pre-pipeline step lifts gap-mask construction and missingness reporting out of growth into a once-per-grouping step that runs after bin-size detection and before any pipeline launches. Outputs at `<out_dir>/01-prior/00-gap-analysis/<out_prefix>_shared_gap_mask.bed` (chromosome-intersected gap mask across input bedGraphs) and `<out_prefix>_missingness.log` (chrom-level summary). Implemented as `_prepare_shared_gap_analysis()` in `onionskin.py` (called from 11 prior/posterior/rerun dispatch sites). The growth-private `_report_missingness()` is removed; growth, RMS, and HMM all now consume the shared diagnostic.

**2. `annotate_calls_with_gap_distance()` defaults to shared multi-file mask.** `onionskin_core/gap_analysis.py:678` adds a `strategy` parameter (default `"shared"`) plus a `precomputed_mask_bed` short-circuit and a `gap_mask_bin_threshold` knob. `strategy="shared"` calls `build_shared_gap_mask_from_bedgraphs()`; `strategy="single"` preserves the pre-Phase-15 single-file-mask behavior for compatibility. The same annotator now recognizes HMM trajectory column conventions (`amplicon_start`/`amplicon_end`, `combined_left`/`combined_right`) so all three pipelines can share a single annotator. Growth and RMS gap-distance values may shift slightly versus older runs — this is the intended correctness improvement; runs that need pre-Phase-15 behavior should pass `--gap-mask-strategy single`.

**3. HMM gains gap-distance annotation at step 18.** New step-18 directory `01-hmm/18-gap-analysis/` (output_layout `step18`) emits HMM gap-annotated trajectory outputs in the same format as growth (`12-gap-analysis/`) and RMS (`09-gap-analysis/`), reading from `13-summit-refinement/all_trajectories.summit_refinement.tsv`. HMM previously emitted no gap-distance annotation. Step-18 outputs include `*.gap_annotated.tsv`, `*.gap_analysis.tsv`, `*.gap_report.md`, and `candidate_amplicons_recommended_for_exclusion.bed`.

**4. HMM chrom-median path consumes the controller-shared mask.** `_step3_remove_gap_bins()` in `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py` now reads the controller-resolved `shared_mask_bed_path` when present (loaded via `load_gap_mask_bed()`), with a defensive fallback to local `build_shared_gap_mask_from_bedgraphs()` when the path is absent. Ref-stage zero-bin removal stays separate (no regression in ref-stage mode).

**5. New CLI flags.**

- `--gap-mask-strategy {shared,single}` (default `shared`): pipeline-wide control for `annotate_calls_with_gap_distance()` strategy.
- `--gap-mask-bin-threshold FLOAT` (default `1.0`): minimum-fraction threshold for the multi-file intersection in shared mask construction.

Help text describes the new pre-pipeline step + the 3-pipeline reach.

**6. HMM parallel child pipeline (`indiv_samples/` across steps 03-11).** Every HMM step from 03 (`03-removeZeroBins/`) through 10 (`10-summitBins/`) — including 04 chrom-renorm, 05 median-smoothed RCN, 06 HMM, 07 collapsed HMM, 08 above-background, and 09 summit-states — now emits per-sample outputs under each step's `indiv_samples/` subdirectory alongside the existing joint outputs. Step 11 (`11-amplicon-metrics/`) similarly extends. A new `sample_tracks.tsv` manifest at `05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/sample_tracks.tsv` (3-column: sample_id, stage, path) gives downstream consumers a stable lookup. Implementation uses a runtime `_indiv_dir(step_dir)` helper in `hmm_engine.py` instead of expanding the `output_layout.py` dict — keeps the layout contract clean and supports future per-sample emission needs at other steps cheaply.

**7. HMM step renumber.** Step layout in `onionskin_core/output_layout.py:281-302`:

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `15-timing/` | `16-timing/` |
| `16-clustering/` | `17-clustering/` |
| (none) | `15-saps/` (placeholder; SAPS implementation lands in SPEC15.13) |
| (none) | `18-gap-analysis/` (new HMM gap-annotation output per item 3 above) |

Function renames in `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py`: `run_step15_hmm_timing` → `run_step16_hmm_timing`; `run_step16_hmm_clustering` → `run_step17_hmm_clustering`; new `run_step15_hmm_saps` placeholder. Engine call sites at `hmm_engine.py:1548-1581` updated. Notebook generator paths in `hmm_notebooks.py:84, 111, 122` updated. `scripts/summit_inspector.py:156, 351, 359` updated to point at `step16` (the timing step's new dict key). Test references in `tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py` updated. Comprehensive grep across the repo confirms zero stale `15-timing` / `16-clustering` / `run_step15_hmm_timing` / `run_step16_hmm_clustering` references.

**8. HMM step-14 APS bug fix (`[ISSUE:2026-04-19:1]`).** This is a **behavioral change**. Pre-fix: `run_step14_hmm_aps()` called `compute_sample_rcn_tracks(manifest_path, ...)` which read RAW manifest bedGraphs and re-normalized them via its own chrom-median + ref-stage logic, bypassing HMM's mednorm → stage-medians → zero-bin/gap-bin removal → chrom-renorm → median-smoothing chain. Post-fix: a new `compute_sample_rcn_tracks_from_track_manifest()` helper at `onionskin_core/aps.py:92` reads pre-normalized RCN values from the per-sample bedGraphs at `05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/`, treating column 4 as already-normalized RCN. `run_step14_hmm_aps()` accepts a new `sample_tracks_manifest` parameter; when provided (default in the engine call site), the new helper is used. Defensive raw-manifest fallback preserved when the per-sample manifest is missing. **HMM step-14 APS values WILL differ from pre-fix baselines** — this is the intended correctness improvement. Comparison studies against pre-fix HMM APS results need to be regenerated. The growth-pipeline `compute_sample_rcn_tracks()` call site is untouched (growth legitimately reads raw paths through its own normalization layer).

**9. Tests added.**

- `tests/test_gap_analysis.py` (new, 102 lines): shared-mask artifact correctness + precomputed-mask consumption.
- `tests/test_pipeline.py`: integration assertions for the shared `00-gap-analysis/` artifact + HMM step-18 gap-annotated output.
- `tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py`: timing/clustering tests updated for the renumber + new function names.
- Targeted suite (`pytest tests/test_gap_analysis.py tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py tests/test_pipeline.py -q`) PASSES 108/108.

**Documentation.** `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md` updated for the shared pre-pipeline step + new flag surface + HMM step-18 gap-analysis. `multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md` adds `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:1]` (Phase 15 closeout-tracking entry verifying the shared-mask migration is reflected in docs/baselines at SPEC15.20).

**Validation:** `python -m pytest tests/test_gap_analysis.py tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py tests/test_pipeline.py -q` 108/108 PASS. `make test` 136/136 PASS. `make toy`, `make single`, `make twin` PASS. Fresh full-genome HMM run at `dev/runs/puffstep-py-full-15-2a/` regenerated under current code; `make puff-compare RUN=dev/runs/puffstep-py-full-15-2a/01-prior/01-hmm` PASS 28/28 across the gold-standard core HMM steps (06-HMM, 07-collapsedHMM, 08-aboveBackground, 09-summitStates, 10-summitBins). Bare `make puff-compare` continues to fail due to the pre-existing chromosome-set mismatch in the Makefile target (carried over from cycle 15.1a as a follow-up validation-target cleanup item).

**Files changed:** `onionskin.py`, `onionskin_core/aps.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/growth_model_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/gap_analysis.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_notebooks.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `onionskin_core/output_layout.py`, `scripts/summit_inspector.py`, `tests/eval_hmm_trim_halfwidth.py`, `tests/test_gap_analysis.py` (new), `tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `tests/test_pipeline.py`, `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SPEC.md` (priority status table), `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`, `multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md`, `multi-agent/workflows/spec_plan_three_role_audit_loop-v2.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

## [v0.14.77] — 2026-04-29 EDT

### Phase 15 cycle 15.1a — HMM CLI correctness (SPEC15.1): `--hmm-mu-scale` correctness fix, 5-tier sigma resolution, restored + added PuffStep synonyms, new `--hmm-sqrt-sigmas` flag

**Phase:** Phase 15 (HMM completeness + enrichment with cross-pipeline generalization).
**Cycle:** 15.1a (1 of 9 in `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`).
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.112 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Extra High), Codex GPT-5.5 (Reasoning: High).
**Roadmap:** Phase 15.

First execution cycle of Phase 15. Closes `[ISSUE:2026-04-26:1]` (the `--hmm-mu-scale` correctness bug introduced during PuffStep → onionskin translation), restores `--hmm-emodel` as a live PuffStep alias (its v0.14.68 retirement was unauthorized), adds the four missing PuffStep flag synonyms identified during Phase 15 SPEC engineering, codifies the standing rule that no PuffStep HMM synonym may be retired, and introduces a new locked sigma-resolution algorithm that preserves the calibrated gold-standard `(means, sigmas)` pair as the default while making `--hmm-mu-scale` actually behave like its PuffStep counterpart.

**1. `--hmm-mu-scale` correctness fix.** PuffStep's `--mu_scale` derives sigmas as `sigma = mean * mu_scale` per state (PuffStep `puffStep_core/hmm_fxns.py:391-411`). onionskin's port erroneously rescaled the emission MEANS instead. The fix removes the buggy `scaled_means = [m * mu_scale ...]` line from `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:_step6_hmm` and moves sigma resolution to a new controller-side function `_resolve_hmm_emissions()` in `onionskin.py`. `mu_scale` is removed entirely from the engine signature (`_step6_hmm` and `run_hmm`).

**2. Five-tier sigma resolution algorithm (locked design decision, captured in SPEC15.1 deliverables 2a-2c).** When `_resolve_hmm_emissions(args)` is invoked, sigmas are resolved by checking the following conditions in order:

| Order | Condition | Sigmas |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `--hmm-emission-sigmas` (or `--hmm-sigma`) set explicitly | use the user-provided values |
| 2 | `--hmm-mu-scale` set explicitly | `sigma = mean * mu_scale` per state |
| 3 | `--hmm-sqrt-sigmas` set | `sigma = sqrt(mean)` per state |
| 4 | None of the above set AND means at default | gold-standard `[0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 24]` |
| 5 | None of the above set AND means changed from default | `sigma = mean * 0.25` + informational stderr message |

Detection of "user set the flag" uses `argparse.SUPPRESS` defaults plus `hasattr(args, ...)` checks. The gold-standard sigma vector is preserved as a paired contract with the gold-standard means `[1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128]` (calibrated empirically against `PuffStep/utils/puffStepPipeline.parallel.sh`). Tier 5's `mu_scale = 0.25` fallback corresponds to the dominant `mean / 4` ratio in the gold-standard sigma vector at states 1–5; the gold-standard's wider ratios at states 6–8 are deliberately not generalized for tier 5 because automating the increasing-ratio shape is brainstorm-grade future work (deferred to a future HMM tuning phase).

**3. New flag `--hmm-sqrt-sigmas`.** `action="store_true"`. Mutually exclusive with `--hmm-emission-sigmas`/`--hmm-sigma` and `--hmm-mu-scale` (enforced via argparse `add_mutually_exclusive_group()` plus a controller-side defense-in-depth check in `_resolve_hmm_emissions`). Help text describes the `sigma = sqrt(mean)` derivation as PuffStep's historical fallback, abandoned because variance was too wide at low states (preventing state switching) — included for compatibility but not recommended for primary use with the calibrated gold-standard means.

**4. Restored `--hmm-emodel` and added 4 missing PuffStep synonyms.** All routed through `dest=` to their native onionskin destinations:

- `--hmm-emodel` (restored) → `hmm_emission_model`. Removed from `_DEPRECATED_FLAGS` in `onionskin.py`; `tests/fixture_deprecated_flags.sh` cleaned; deprecated-flag test sites in `tests/test_pipeline.py` updated to assert the alias is now live.
- `--hmm-mu` and `--hmm-discreteEmat` → `hmm_emission_means`.
- `--hmm-sigma` → `hmm_emission_sigmas`.
- `--hmm-path` → `hmm_decode_path`.
- `--hmm-constrainEmit` → `hmm_constrain_emit`.

camelCase suffixes preserve PuffStep's exact spelling per the standing rule below.

**5. Standing rule codified in HMM argparse group header.** The HMM group at `onionskin.py:build_parser()` now states: "PuffStep flag synonyms are accepted alongside the onionskin-native names. No PuffStep flag synonyms are to be retired; the `--hmm-` prefix is invisible for synonym-comparison purposes." Cross-referenced as a case study in `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md § Scope authority`, documenting the unauthorized v0.14.68 `--hmm-emodel` retirement as the cautionary precedent.

**6. `SUPPORTED_EMODELS` engine boundary tightened.** `onionskin_core/hmm_core.py:SUPPORTED_EMODELS` reduced from `{normal, exponential, poisson, geometric, gamma, discrete}` to `{normal, exponential, poisson, geometric, gamma}` to match the actual implemented backends in `_log_emit_seq` and the parser-advertised `--hmm-emission-model` choices. `discrete` was a vestigial entry with no `_log_emit_seq` branch; the parser, validator, and backend are now aligned.

**7. Help-text accuracy.** All HMM emission-flag help texts rewritten to describe the corrected semantics: `--hmm-mu-scale` no longer claims to "rescale sigmas by the same factor", `--hmm-emission-sigmas` no longer claims `mu-scale 0.5 sets sigma = 0.5 * mean for every state` as if it always did (now it actually does, per the 5-tier algorithm), `--hmm-emission-model` updated for the restored `--hmm-emodel` alias. All four sigma-mode flags cross-reference the priority and mutual-exclusivity rules.

**8. Tests added.** `tests/test_pipeline.py` gains:

- `test_hmm_puffstep_synonyms_parse_to_native_dests` — all 5 restored/added synonyms parse to their native `dest=`.
- `test_hmm_emission_resolution_uses_gold_standard_default_sigmas` — tier 4: default invocation produces gold-standard means + sigmas.
- `test_hmm_mu_scale_derives_sigmas_without_scaling_means` — tier 2: `--hmm-mu-scale 0.5` produces `sigma = mean * 0.5`, means unchanged. Asserts `mu_scale` is no longer in `run_hmm` signature.
- `test_hmm_changed_means_without_sigma_mode_use_quarter_scale` — tier 5: changed means without sigma mode produces `sigma = mean * 0.25` + stderr message.
- `test_hmm_sqrt_sigmas_mode_and_mutual_exclusion` — tier 3 derivation + argparse mutex error on conflicting flags.

**Documentation.** `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md` mirrors the corrected sigma precedence and restored aliases. `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md § Scope authority` adds a 3-sentence case study citing the unauthorized `--hmm-emodel` retirement as the documented cautionary precedent for the standing rule.

**Validation:** `python -m pytest tests/test_pipeline.py` 105/105 PASS. `make puff-compare RUN=dev/runs/puffstep-py-full-15-1a/01-prior/01-hmm` PASS 28/28 (gold-standard regression gate). Bare `make puff-parity` / `make puff-compare` fail due to a pre-existing chromosome-set mismatch in the Makefile target (full-genome reference vs core-chromosome run); this is not a SPEC15.1 regression and is logged as a follow-up validation-target cleanup item.

**Files changed:** `onionskin.py`, `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`, `onionskin_core/hmm_core.py`, `tests/test_pipeline.py`, `tests/fixture_deprecated_flags.sh`, `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md`, `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SPEC.md` (priority status table + SPEC15.1 deliverables 2a-2c), `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

---

## [v0.14.76] — 2026-04-27 EDT

### CLI quality-of-life: deprecated-flag pre-parse gate now collects-all-and-reports-all + `validate_onionskin_flags.py` gains `--rewrite-to` / `--rewrite-in-place` rewrite modes

Two related user-facing CLI improvements bundled into one cycle. No
phase number — small standalone product cycle. `make test` 130/130
PASS (128 → 130 with two new tests).

**1. Pre-parse gate (`onionskin.py:_check_deprecated_flags`) collects
all deprecated flags before reporting.** Previously the gate exited
on the FIRST deprecated flag encountered, forcing users to fix one
flag at a time across multiple invocations. Now it collects every
deprecated flag in one pass and reports them all in a single error
message. Behavior is preserved exactly when only ONE deprecated flag
is used (existing per-flag tests like
`test_deprecated_per_stage_norm_mode_flag_exits_with_redirect` see
the same single-flag message they always have). When TWO OR MORE
deprecated flags are used together, the new format kicks in:

```
Error: 3 deprecated flags were used:
  --method → use --growth-fit-method instead
  --per-stage-norm-mode → use --rms-norm-mode instead
  --hmm-emodel → use --hmm-emission-model instead

Hint: scripts/validate_onionskin_flags.py supports --rewrite-to FILE / --rewrite-in-place to fix a file.
```

Deduped (a flag passed twice is reported once); ordered by
first-appearance in argv.

**2. `scripts/validate_onionskin_flags.py` gains rewrite modes.** The
script now supports three modes:

- **DEFAULT (report-only):** unchanged from v0.14.73's 14-S24
  introduction + v0.14.75's substring-match fix. Scans one or more
  files, prints `path:line: flag → redirect` for each occurrence,
  exits 0 (clean) / 1 (findings) / 2 (file error). Suitable as a CI
  gate; this is what `make validate-flags` runs.
- **`--rewrite-to OUTFILE`** (mutually exclusive with
  `--rewrite-in-place`; requires exactly one input FILE; OUTFILE may
  be `-` for stdout): rewrites each deprecated flag token to its
  canonical replacement using the same token-boundary regex as the
  report mode (so `--growth-peak-search` inside
  `--growth-peak-search-halfwidth` is correctly rejected as a
  prefix-only match). Writes the rewritten content to OUTFILE; the
  original input file is left untouched. Reports the rewrite count
  and any multi-token replacement warnings to stderr at the end.
- **`--rewrite-in-place`** (mutually exclusive with `--rewrite-to`;
  requires exactly one input FILE): same as `--rewrite-to`, but
  modifies the input file directly. Add `--backup` to write
  `<file>.bak` first.

Multi-token-redirect warning fires for the three deprecated flags
whose canonical form has an explicit value:
`--shape-filter-multistage` → `--growth-shape-filter on`,
`--strict-bic` → `--shape-score-strict-bic on`,
`--no-aps-singleton-guard` → `--aps-singleton-guard off`. If the
original input was passing its own value alongside one of those
flags, the rewritten output will contain BOTH the auto-injected value
AND the original value (syntactically broken); the warning surfaces
so the user can review manually.

**Why bundle the two changes:** they target the same user
experience — getting a script with multiple deprecated flags into
working shape. Pre-parse gate now gives the user the full list at
once; the rewriter then lets them apply all the fixes mechanically.
The existing single-flag pre-parse-gate tests are unaffected by the
collect-all change (single-flag message preserved exactly).

**Tests added (128 → 130):**
- `test_validate_flags_rewrite_to_stdout` — exercises the new
  `--rewrite-to -` mode against `tests/fixture_deprecated_flags.sh`,
  asserts (a) exit 0, (b) deprecated flag tokens absent from
  rewritten stdout, (c) canonical replacements present, (d) non-flag
  content (the fixture's leading comment) preserved intact, (e)
  stderr summary names the rewritten flags.
- `test_deprecated_multiple_flags_collected_and_reported` —
  exercises the multi-flag pre-parse gate path with three deprecated
  flags (`--method`, `--per-stage-norm-mode`, `--hmm-emodel`),
  asserts (a) exit 1, (b) "3 deprecated flags were used" header,
  (c) all three canonical replacements appear, (d) hint pointing at
  the rewriter is present.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.112 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: High)

**Roadmap:** Small standalone product CLI quality-of-life cycle —
no phase number. Both changes extend deliverables that originally
landed in Phase 14 Supplemental: the pre-parse gate dates back to
Phase 14 proper (v0.14.46) but its multi-flag-collection behavior
is new at v0.14.76; the validator script is the 14-S24 deliverable
(introduced at v0.14.73, fixed for substring false-positives at
v0.14.75) and the rewrite modes are the v0.14.76 addition.

## [v0.14.75] — 2026-04-26 ~17:55 EDT

### Cycle Post-wrap-up remediation CLOSED: Findings A + B + C + D from Phase 14 Supplemental Final Overseer wrap-up audits — validator regex hardening + Input/Output group step-mention + INTENDED-BUT-MISSED stale-archived-path repair + KNOWN_ISSUES `--hmm-mu-scale` cross-reference

Phase 14 Supplemental post-wrap-up remediation cycle (cycle index 8;
sole cycle of its type — translates the actionable findings from the
two Final Overseer wrap-up audits at AUDIT_LOG lines 7233–7630 into
concrete repairs). Cycle decomposed into three rounds executed across
separate sessions: Role 1 Post-Wrap-Up Triage (Template E) →
Role 2 Post-Wrap-Up Implementation (Template F) → Role 1 Final
Closeout After Wrap-Up Remediation (Template G).

**Finding A — Validator substring-match false-positive bug fixed.**
`scripts/validate_onionskin_flags.py` previously used `if flag in line:`
substring matching, which flagged `--growth-peak-search-halfwidth` and
`--rms-peak-search-halfwidth` (live flags landed in cycle 14S.1a /
v0.14.68) as if they contained the deprecated `--growth-peak-search`
and `--rms-peak-search`. Replaced with token-boundary regex matching:
new `_compile_flag_patterns()` helper builds a per-flag dict of
pre-compiled `re.Pattern` objects each of the form
`re.compile(rf"(?<![A-Za-z0-9_\-]){re.escape(flag)}(?![A-Za-z0-9_\-])")`.
Compilation is cached once per script invocation. The negative
lookbehind/lookahead on `[A-Za-z0-9_\-]` correctly rejects strict-prefix
matches. Added new
`tests/test_pipeline.py::test_validate_flags_no_substring_false_positive`
exercising five lines that include the deprecated-prefix-as-substring
pattern in five different surrounding contexts (quoted in a tuple,
comment prose, argparse-flag-style assignment, `=`-style argument with
a value); test asserts `result.returncode == 0` and adds two explicit
contract assertions that `--growth-peak-search ` and `--rms-peak-search `
(with trailing space) must not appear in the validator's `flag → redirect`
output. Test count: 127 → 128.

**Finding B — Input/Output parser group step-mention added.** The
`Input / Output` argument group at `onionskin.py:728` previously had
no description and no step-mention tag, leaving it the only parser
group of 11 missing the `[applies to all pipelines]` /
`[<pipeline>: <NN>-<step-name>]` step-mention coverage required by
AGENT_CONVENTIONS § CLI Flag Naming and Structure Conventions item 11
(added in cycle 14S.5a). Added second positional
`"Input/output flags. [applies to all pipelines]"` to the
`add_argument_group` call. The 14-S8 SPEC contract (CLOSED v0.14.72)
required every flag's help string to end with a step mention;
Input/Output was the residual gap. `--help` output now renders
`Input / Output: Input/output flags. [applies to all pipelines]`
immediately after the `--version` block.

**Finding C — `INTENDED-BUT-MISSED-PRIOR-TO-14.md` stale archived/
path references repaired.** The 14S.5a archive operation was reverted
as out-of-scope (per AUDIT_LOG cycle 14S.5a's surgical-revert section),
leaving four stale references in
`multi-agent/tracking/INTENDED-BUT-MISSED-PRIOR-TO-14.md` that pointed
at non-existent `multi-agent/plans/archived/20260426-PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-*.md`
paths. Re-pointed all stale references to the live
`multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-*.md` paths with the
parenthetical "(planned for archive after Final Overseer per workflow
v2 § Step 8)" treatment. Two audit-listed sites (lines 5–8 + 92–94)
plus two in-situ supplemental sites (line 377 IBM-C8A "Already captured"
+ lines 618–619 APPENDIX C "Phase 14 and its Supplemental" bullet) —
the supplemental fixes apply the audit's same replacement pattern to
two sites the audit's "Required repair" line-range enumeration missed
but the audit's own "Cross-check after edit" implicitly required.
Both grep cross-checks (`archived/20260426-` and
`archived feedback file`) now return 0 hits.

**Finding D — `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` cross-reference for `--hmm-mu-scale`
correctness bug added.** Phase 15 BRAINSTORM has a substantive ~45-line
entry (`multi-agent/plans/next/PHASE15_BRAINSTORM.md` § "CORRECTNESS
BUG: --hmm-mu-scale translation from PuffStep is wrong + full HMM
translation re-audit required" at lines ~668–712) documenting that
`--hmm-mu-scale` was incorrectly translated from PuffStep — onionskin
scales emission means by the factor when PuffStep used it to derive
sigmas (`sigma = mean * mu_scale`) instead. The bug is user-visible
TODAY for anyone passing `--hmm-mu-scale 0.5` on the v0.14.74 CLI.
Added new `[ISSUE:2026-04-26:1]` entry in
`multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md` with all standard structural
fields (Status, Urgency, Why it matters, Current understanding,
Suggested user-side workaround until Phase 15 fix lands, Likely
landing zone, Exit condition) and an explicit cross-reference to the
Phase 15 BRAINSTORM section. Suggested workaround: do not pass
`--hmm-mu-scale`; set sigmas explicitly via `--hmm-emission-sigmas`
if non-default sigmas are needed. The substantive design home stays
in PHASE15_BRAINSTORM (untouched in this cycle) — KNOWN_ISSUES carries
only the user-visible cross-reference.

**Cycle-wide validation: all 5 steps PASS.** `make test` 128/128 in
53.61s (independently re-executed at closeout); `--help` renders the
new IO group description; `validate_onionskin_flags.py onionskin.py`
exit 1 with 50 hits, ALL genuine self-references inside
`_DEPRECATED_FLAGS` body and intentional help-text mentions in
`_build_ms_argv()` (the protected internal argv adapter per the 14-S10
carve-out) — the 2 false positives at lines 2016/2231 (the live
`--growth-peak-search-halfwidth` and `--rms-peak-search-halfwidth`
argparse declarations) are GONE. `archived/20260426-` grep on

hits (audit required ≥3). Bonus: `archived feedback file` grep on
INTENDED-BUT-MISSED → 0 hits.

**Out-of-scope confirmed not touched:** ROADMAP.md, the four agent
files, AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md ROADMAP-related sections, workflow v2

the archive-after-Final-Overseer rule into workflow v2 § Step 8 plus three
git-discipline principles — shipped as DEVLOG v0.14.73.1 (commit `6ee855a`)
between R2 and this re-audit closeout.
