## [v0.14.73] — 2026-04-26 ~EDT

### Cycle 14S.4a CLOSED: 14-S11 (CLI flag ordering) + 14-S24 (validate-flags script)

Phase 14 Supplemental cycle 14S.4a (index 6). Two structural polish + tooling priorities.

**14-S11 — CLI flag ordering within parser groups**

Mechanically reordered `add_argument` blocks in `onionskin.py:build_parser()` so flags
within each pipeline group appear in pipeline execution step order. Pure block reorder;
flag counts and behavior unchanged.

- **HMM group (37 flags):** `--hmm-norm-mode` [01+04] and `--hmm-pseudo` [04] moved
  to positions 2–3 (were at positions 34–35); `--hmm-aps-smooth-halfwidth` [14-aps]
  moved to position 35 (was position 3). Remaining step-05, step-06, step-08,
  step-13 blocks already in order.
- **Growth group (24 flags):** Corrected step-01/02 split (trend/smooth were at
  positions 16–17; z-thresh/halfwidths/peak-search/scan were at positions 14–15 and
  18–19 behind step-09/10 flags); shape-filter block moved from positions 21–23 to
  10–12; stage-weight-mode/refine-summit flags moved from positions 4–6 to 18–20.
  Full canonical order: 01 → 02 → 03 → 06 → 07 → 09 → 10.
- **RMS group (12 flags):** `--rms-norm-mode` [02-chrom-norm] moved from position 7
  to position 1 (was displaced behind six step-03 detection flags).

**14-S24 — Deprecated-flag validator**

New developer tooling for catching deprecated onionskin CLI flags in scripts and
pipeline files.

- `scripts/validate_onionskin_flags.py` — standalone script that imports
  `_DEPRECATED_FLAGS` from `onionskin`, scans one or more text files, and prints
  `file:line: flag → redirect` for each hit. Exit 0 (clean), 1 (deprecated found),
  2 (file error).
- `make validate-flags [file ...]` — Makefile target wrapping the script; included in
  `.PHONY`; help-text entry added.
- `README.md` — developer-tooling section updated with description + example.
- `multi-agent/full_instructions/ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md` — developer-tooling
  section updated with script + make target mention.
- `tests/fixture_deprecated_flags.sh` — test fixture with three deprecated flags:
  `--growth-window`, `--rms-window`, `--hmm-emodel`.
- `tests/test_pipeline.py` — two new tests: `test_validate_flags_detects_deprecated`
  (exit 1 + all fixture flags in stdout) and `test_validate_flags_clean_file` (exit 0
  on a clean file).

**Re-auditor corrections (applied during re-audit):**

- `--hmm-bin-size` help text updated (pre-Role-2): corrected step tag from
  `[hmm: 06-HMM]` to `[hmm: pre-run + 06-HMM]`; full help string rewritten to
  clarify this is a QC-only sanity check, NOT an override, with abort-on-mismatch
  semantics.
- `--hmm-pseudo` help text and step tag corrected: `[hmm: 01-mednorm]` →
  `[hmm: 04-chrom-renorm]`; help body corrected to say step 4 and note ref-stage-only
  computational effect. Block moved from position 3 → 3 (swap with `--hmm-norm-mode`
  to respect step order).

**Tests:** `make test` — 127/127 passed.

**Audit trail:** R1 initial audit (Sonnet, Effort: Max) → R2 implementation (Codex
GPT-5.5, Reasoning: Medium) → R1 re-audit + closeout (Sonnet, Effort: Max — all
deliverables verified, two in-cycle corrections applied, `--hmm-mu-scale` correctness
bug documented for Phase 15). Full round-by-round detail in
`multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-AUDIT_LOG.md` cycle 14S.4a section.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.112 (claude-sonnet-4-6 ; Effort: Max), Codex GPT-5.5 (Reasoning: Medium)

## [v0.14.72] — 2026-04-25 ~23:30 EDT

### Cycle 14S.3b CLOSED: 14-S8 full cross-group help-string quality pass

Phase 14 Supplemental cycle 14S.3b (index 5). Single substantive priority — 14-S8: full help-string quality pass across every parser group in `onionskin.py:build_parser()`. Two-part improvement: (1) step-mention coverage `[pipeline: NN-step-name]` for every flag (or carried at group-header level when all flags share a step), and (2) content-quality rewrite for any help string that was terse, didn't explain its default, or didn't say when/why a user would change it. Plus the targeted clarifications added in Q28/Q29: plain-language for RCN-profile-operating flags, "RCN signal" explicitness, peak-as-amplicon clarification, and summit-as-proxy-for-origin clarification.

Pure help-string + group-description + test surface. No runtime behavior changes; no PIPELINE_SPEC.md edits (per SPEC § 14-S8 directive).

**Per parser group:**

- **Universal group (`uni`):** Added group description explaining the cross-pipeline-with-pipeline-overrides convention. `--norm-mode` and `--ref-stage` rewritten with multi-pipeline step tags. `--peak-summary` / `--peak-quantile` / `--peak-topk` rewritten with explicit "RCN signal" language, "mountain-shaped amplicon — not a ChIP-seq narrow peak" clarification, and `[growth: 07-signal-tracks; rms: planned; hmm: planned]` step tags. Six infrastructure flags (`--threads`, `--rng-seed`, `--min-seq-length`, `--min-bin-count-per-seq`, `--verbose`, `--debug`) tagged `[applies to all pipelines]`. `--bootstrap-summits` got formal step tag `[growth: 09-summit-refinement; rms: 07-summit-refinement; hmm: planned]`.

- **Shape scoring group (`shp`):** Added group description noting Universal-in-spirit pattern + Phase 15 deferred HMM wiring. `--shape-score-threshold` rewritten with dBIC explanation + step tag. `--shape-score-strict-bic` step tag appended. Both flags tagged `[growth: 03-shape-filter; rms: 06-shape-filter; hmm: planned (Phase 15)]`.

- **Overlap / Dedup group (`ovr`):** Group description expanded with multi-pipeline step tag. All six `--overlap-*` flags rewritten with default values, when-to-change guidance, and `[growth: 11-overlapResolution; rms: post-detection]` step tags.

- **Timing group (`tim`):** Four onset-detection flags rewritten (`--onset-rcn-threshold`, `--onset-span-rcn-threshold`, `--onset-method`, `--onset-z-threshold`) with default explanations and when-to-change guidance. Group-header step tag retained.

- **Summit Refinement group (`ref`):** Group description appended with summit-as-proxy clarification (per § 14-S28).

- **Asymmetric Triangle Model group (`atm`):** No changes (already met the bar from 14-S3).

- **APS group (`aps`):** Three terse flags rewritten with substantive content (`--aps-scale` choice descriptions, `--aps-cluster-method` algorithm/limits, `--aps-width-threshold` RCN-feature semantics).

- **HMM engine group (`hmm`):** ~30 step-mention tags added across the modeling/decoding/transition/emission flags + step-8 (above-background) flags + step-13 (summit-refinement) flags. `--hmm-smooth-halfwidth` step-tag spelling corrected from `medianSmoothedRCN` → `medianSmoothed-RCN` (matches actual directory name in `output_layout.py`). `--hmm-pseudo` rewritten from terse "Pseudocount for RCN normalization step." to substantive default + when-to-change explanation. HMM shape-score placeholder flags tagged `[hmm: planned (Phase 15)]`.

- **Growth model group (`ms`):** Group description expanded. Four stage-1 detection flags rewritten (`--growth-z-thresh`, `--growth-halfwidths`, `--growth-trend`, `--growth-smooth`). Peak-as-amplicon clarification added to `--growth-peak-search-halfwidth`. Supplemental in-situ step tags on `--growth-norm-mode`, `--growth-rcn-smooth-halfwidth`, `--growth-shape-filter`, `--growth-shape-score-threshold`, `--growth-shape-score-strict-bic`.

- **RCN Mean Shift (RMS) group (`ss`):** Four stage-1 detection flags rewritten (`--rms-z-thresh`, `--rms-halfwidths`, `--rms-trend`, `--rms-smooth`). Peak-as-amplicon clarification added to `--rms-peak-search-halfwidth`. Three step tags reformatted to formal step-name format (`[rms: shape-filter]` → `[rms: 06-shape-filter]`; `[rms: summit-refinement]` → `[rms: 07-summit-refinement]`). `--rms-summit-policy` and `--rms-early-summit-stages` step tags appended. Supplemental in-situ tags on `--rms-norm-mode` and `--rms-scan-halfwidth`.

**Implementation-time fixes (in-situ):**

- Escaped new literal percent signs as `%%` in five new help strings so `argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter` does not treat them as printf placeholders (`onionskin.py:1021, 1033, 1102, 1115, 1350`).
- Made the new help-regression test line-wrap-tolerant by normalizing argparse's hyphenated line-wrap output (`"- "` → `"-"`) in a `compact` variant so long step tags such as `06-shape-filter` and `07-summit-refinement` are detectable regardless of where argparse line-breaks them in the rendered help.

**Tests:**

- New test `test_help_step_mentions_per_group()` in `tests/test_pipeline.py` (lines 168–187) asserts at least one step-mention string per parser group is present in `python onionskin.py -h` output. Uses both `flat` (whitespace-collapsed) and `compact` (hyphen-line-wrap-normalized) variants depending on whether the asserted string contains a hyphen that argparse may wrap on.
- All other existing help-regression tests continue to pass after rewrites — no test asserts a deleted pre-S8 substring.

**Validation:**

- `python onionskin.py -h`: 1223 lines of clean output (no argparse `%` errors; up from ~860 lines pre-S8).
- `python -m pytest -q tests/test_pipeline.py -k "help"`: 5 passed.
- `make test` (full suite): 125 passed (124 baseline + 1 new test).
- Final `rg` for residual pre-S8 substrings across `onionskin.py` and `tests/`: zero matches.

**Audit trail:** R1 initial audit (Opus, Effort: Extra High — 14 findings A-N + ~25 flag rewrites + ~30 step-mention tags + 3 group descriptions + 1 new test specified) → R2 implementation (Codex CLI 0.125.0-alpha.3 GPT-5.5 Reasoning: Extra High — all 11 ordered repair groups + 11 supplemental in-situ repairs including the argparse `%%` fix and line-wrap-tolerant test normalization) → R1 re-audit (Opus, Effort: Extra High — all findings PASS, all supplementals verified, full test suite green). Full round-by-round detail in `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-AUDIT_LOG.md` cycle 14S.3b section.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.112 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Extra High), Codex CLI 0.125.0-alpha.3 (GPT-5.5 ; Reasoning: Extra High)

**Roadmap:** Phase 14 Supplemental — Phase 3 help-string polish (14-S8 full cross-group quality pass).

## [v0.14.71] — 2026-04-25 ~19:00 EDT

### Cycle 14S.3a CLOSED: Help-string quality pass — six targeted priorities (14-S6, 14-S7, 14-S9, 14-S13, 14-S19, 14-S20)

Phase 14 Supplemental cycle 14S.3a (index 4). All six help-text priorities implemented
and re-audited against live code. Cycle also absorbed the deferred 14S.2a R3 scope:
three 14S.2a audit deliverables verified; no semantic conflicts between these rewrites
and the 14-S15 PuffStep findings or 14-S25 `-rcn-` audit recommendations.

**14-S6 — `--aps-rank-by` help expansion:**
- Expanded from terse `"APS scalar used for rank outputs"` (5 words) to full explanation:
  `area` semantics (ranks calls by total APS area excess; drives rank columns in APS
  output TSVs); that it is the default and only active choice; that `summit`, `width`,
  `shape` are planned for a future release. Step mention `[hmm: 14-aps; growth: 13-aps;
  rms: 12-aps]` prepended (consistent with all APS flags). Pre-existing
  `[ISSUE:2026-04-22:1]` KNOWN_ISSUES entry for future choices already present; no
  duplicate added.

**14-S7 — Placeholder flag help narrowed to the 2 live HMM placeholders:**
- `--hmm-shape-score-threshold` and `--hmm-shape-score-strict-bic` help reworded
  to: `"Inheritance placeholder — parser surface only; no current behavioral effect.
  HMM shape-score wiring is scheduled for Phase 15 HMM completeness work (HMM lacks
  the per-amplicon shape-filter sink that RMS and growth have). When wired, will
  inherit from --shape-score-[threshold|strict-bic] per the Universal/override
  pattern."` This is honest about the wiring gap, names the reason, and points to
  the Phase 15 work.
- 4 other SPEC-listed flags (`--rms-shape-score-threshold`, `--rms-shape-score-strict-bic`,
  `--rms-bootstrap-summits`, `--growth-bootstrap-summits`) are **not** placeholders
  post-v0.14.70: the RMS shape-score pair was wired in 14-S26 v0.14.70; both bootstrap
  flags are working overrides with live implementations. These flags unchanged.
- SPEC 14-S7 placeholder table updated at closeout: column header updated; 4 no-longer-
  placeholder flags annotated with post-v0.14.70 live state; flag names updated to
  reflect 14-S28 renames (`--rms-bootstrap-summits`, `--growth-bootstrap-summits`).

**14-S9 — HMM help improvements:**
- `--hmm-decode-path`: recommendation language added — `` `viterbi` (default, recommended):
  finds the single most-probable state path; strongly preferred at typical amplification
  detection scales — faster and equivalent accuracy in practice. Matches PuffStep.
  `posterior`: assigns each bin to the state with the highest posterior probability
  (soft decoding, may produce more fragmented paths); available for research use.``
- `--hmm-training`: practical-decision sentence appended: `"In practice, viterbi training
  with --hmm-iters 1 (no re-estimation) is the fast common case; iterative re-estimation
  is rarely needed when --hmm-emission-means is calibrated."`
- 7 PuffStep-specific per-flag cross-references (`"Port of PuffStep ..."`) moved to the
  front of their respective HMM help strings for grep stability; content unchanged.
  Group-level note (`"PuffStep flag synonyms are accepted alongside the onionskin-native
  names."`) retained.

**14-S13 — Timing group step-mention pass:**
- Timing group description updated from `"Parameters for onset and timing estimation."`
  to `"Parameters for onset and timing estimation. [growth: 10-timing; rms: planned;
  hmm: planned]"`.
- New `multi-agent/KNOWN_ISSUES.md` entry `[ISSUE:2026-04-25:1]`: documents the mixed
  `--onset-*` / `--timing-*` / `--near-gap-bp` prefix situation in the Timing group
  (4 `--onset-*` flags, 2 `--timing-*` flags, 1 standalone). Deferred naming cleanup
  from Q12; exit condition is future-phase prefix harmonization.

**14-S19 — `--pipelines` two-block help + corrected `all` semantics:**
- Rewrote `--pipelines` help: factual contradiction removed (`Default: all (growth +
  rcn-mean-shift)` and `HMM is opt-in: include 'hmm' explicitly` were both wrong —
  `_resolve_requested_effective_pipelines()` at line 2482 expands `all` to
  `{growth, rcn-mean-shift, hmm}` unconditionally). New help is two-block: quick-start
  line (`Default: all = growth + rms + hmm`) + Context paragraph explaining each
  pipeline's name, scope, and normalization mode requirements.
- `tests/test_pipeline.py`: three help regression assertions added: `"Default: all =
  growth + rms + hmm"` present; `"multiple stages with or without a reference stage"`
  present; `"HMM is opt-in"` absent.

**14-S20 — APS group Universal-in-spirit re-framing + `--dedup-dist` pipeline scope:**
- APS group description expanded from title-only to include: `"Universal-in-spirit: APS
  flags below apply to all three pipelines' APS computations. APS is computed on the
  per-pipeline summit/amplicon set as the final stage of each pipeline (Growth step 13,
  RMS step 12, HMM step 14). The flags drive feature construction, clustering, and rank
  computation identically across pipelines."` Step mention `[hmm: 14-aps; growth:
  13-aps; rms: 12-aps]` also prepended to the group description.
- All 12 APS flags that lacked the step mention now carry `[hmm: 14-aps; growth: 13-aps;
  rms: 12-aps]` at the front of their help string. (`--aps-area-excess-floor` already
  had it from 14-S22 v0.14.70.) Total `grep -c` count in `--help` output = 14 (13 flags
  + 1 group description).
- `--dedup-dist` help rewritten: `"Applies to the Growth and RMS pipelines (both share
  the dedup_calls_by_peak_proximity implementation in onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py).
  HMM uses its own merging chain controlled by --hmm-merge1 / --hmm-min-width /
  --hmm-merge2 (step 8); --dedup-dist does not apply to HMM. Set 0 to disable for
  Growth+RMS."` Step note `[growth: post-detection; rms: post-detection; hmm: not
  applicable]` appended.
- `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-FEEDBACK.md`: `## 14-S20 APS audit —
  universal reach confirmed` section appended confirming all APS flags reach all 3
  pipelines; no PHASE15_BRAINSTORM cross-pipeline dedup note added (RMS+Growth already
  share `dedup_calls_by_peak_proximity()`; HMM uses its own step-8 merging by design).

## [v0.14.70] — 2026-04-25 ~00:30 EDT

### Cycle 14S.1b CLOSED: Summit Refinement Universal promotion + comprehensive summit/origin terminology harmonization across all pipelines + APS area-excess-floor toggle + HMM step-14 APS smoothing split + RMS shape-score wiring confirmed

Phase 14 Supplemental cycle 14S.1b (priorities 14-S27 + 14-S28 paired, 14-S22, 14-S23, 14-S26).

This is the cycle's legitimate v0.14.70 closeout. A prior v0.14.70 attempt was retracted on 2026-04-24 after the user identified a load-bearing scope error in the original Role 1 audit (14-S28's column-rename scope was read as growth-engine-only, but the phase-decided scope per Q38 + DECISIONS.md `[2026-04-23]` is all public output columns across all pipelines). The cycle was reopened with an Opus-grade thorough re-audit covering 16 findings; Role 2 implemented the expanded scope (14 ordered steps + 4 supplemental in-situ repairs); Role 1 then re-audited live code and verified clean closeout. See `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-AUDIT_LOG.md` cycle 14S.1b section for full round-by-round detail.

**14-S27 — Summit Refinement Universal promotion:**
- New "Summit Refinement" parser group between Timing and Asymmetric Triangle Model. Group description carries the band-aid help text explaining mixed semantics of `final_summit_low_bp`/`final_summit_high_bp` and points to PHASE16_BRAINSTORM.md for the planned Summit Methodology pass.
- 3 Universal flags (`--stage-weight-mode`, `--refine-summit-halfwidth`, `--refine-summit-smooth`) with multi-pipeline step mentions.
- 3 growth-specific overrides (`default=None`): `--growth-stage-weight-mode`, `--growth-refine-summit-halfwidth`, `--growth-refine-summit-smooth`.
- `_build_ms_argv()` updated to use `_resolve_override_value()` for each pair.
- `_DEPRECATED_FLAGS`: removed previously-redirecting `--stage-weight-mode → --growth-stage-weight-mode` entry; updated `--refine-window-kb` and `--refine-smooth-kb` redirect targets; added `--growth-refine-halfwidth → --growth-refine-summit-halfwidth` and `--growth-refine-smooth → --growth-refine-summit-smooth`.
- `KNOWN_ISSUES.md [ISSUE:2026-04-24:2]` added for RMS summit parabola pre-smoothing investigation per Q36.

**14-S28 — Summit/origin terminology harmonization (comprehensive scope across all pipelines):**

The phase-decided scope is "ALL public output columns standardize on summit." Internal-boundary rule applies only to internal `onionskin_core/` function/variable names. Per-pipeline:

- **Bootstrap flag renames (Universal + per-pipeline overrides):** `--bootstrap-origins → --bootstrap-summits`, `--growth-bootstrap-origins → --growth-bootstrap-summits`, `--rms-bootstrap-origins → --rms-bootstrap-summits`. Universal `--bootstrap-summits` help carries the inline summit-as-proxy-for-origin clarification. Helper functions renamed; growth-engine internal `_run_argv()` parser also accepts `--bootstrap-summits`.

- **Growth `_origins.tsv` populated schema (12 column renames):**
  - Position/CI columns (band-aid scheme): `final_origin_bp → final_summit_bp`; `origin_ci_low_bp → final_summit_low_bp`; `origin_ci_high_bp → final_summit_high_bp`; `origin_ci_width_bp → final_summit_width_bp`; `origin_boot_sd_bp → argmax_mean_bootstrap_sd_bp` (honest renaming — the SD is always argmax-based).
  - Median variants: `final_origin_bp_median → final_summit_bp_median`; `origin_ci_low_bp_median → final_summit_low_bp_median`; `origin_ci_high_bp_median → final_summit_high_bp_median`.
  - Confidence/score columns: `origin_conf_logBF → summit_conf_logBF`; `origin_conf_width_inv → summit_conf_width_inv`; `origin_conf_neglog10_width → summit_conf_neglog10_width`; `origin_bed_score → summit_bed_score`.
- **Growth `_origins.tsv` empty-fallback schema:** `origin_bp`, `origin_bin_start`, `origin_bin_end`, `origin_conf_logBF`, `origin_conf_from_ci_inv`, `origin_conf_from_ci_log` all renamed to `summit_*` equivalents (and `origin_bp → final_summit_bp`).
- **Growth progression empty-fallback schema:** `origin_bp` (per-stage) → `summit_bp`.
- **Growth per-resolution columns:** `origin_{key}*` → `summit_{key}*` (e.g., `origin_base → summit_base`, `origin_hires_1000 → summit_hires_1000`, plus `_ci_low`/`_ci_high`/`_boot_sd_bp` siblings).
- **`ci_precision_metrics()` return-dict keys:** `origin_conf_width_inv` and `origin_conf_neglog10_width` renamed.

- **RMS `_origins.tsv` schema (5 column renames, band-aid scheme):**
  - `origin_bp → final_summit_bp`; `origin_conf_logBF → summit_conf_logBF`; `origin_ci_lo → final_summit_low_bp`; `origin_ci_hi → final_summit_high_bp`; `origin_ci_width_bp → final_summit_width_bp`. Long-form names align with growth schema.
  - Upstream DataFrame column also renamed: `df_calls["origin_conf_logBF"] → df_calls["summit_conf_logBF"]`.

- **RMS `_metrics.tsv` schema (2 column renames):**
  - `summit_bp_final → final_summit_bp` (cross-pipeline name harmonization with growth `_origins.tsv:final_summit_bp`); `origin_ci_width_bp → final_summit_width_bp`.

- **HMM `_metrics.tsv` schema (1 column rename):** `origin_bp → final_summit_bp`. Internal dataclass field name `origin_bp` retained per internal-boundary rule.

- **HMM summit refinement output (1 column rename):** `origin_stddev_bp → summit_stddev_bp` (3 emit sites). Parse site at `hmm_summit_refinement.py:95` reads upstream `final_summit_bp`.

- **HMM fork travel output (1 column rename):** `origin_bp → final_summit_bp` (3 emit sites + 1 nested-domain plot read site at line 787, supplemental in-situ repair). Parse site at `hmm_fork_travel.py:143` reads upstream `final_summit_bp`.

- **Consumer updates:** `onionskin_core/notebooks.py`, `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`, `onionskin_core/aps.py`, `onionskin_core/profile_plots.py`, `tests/eval_summit_precision*.py`, `tests/test_eval_summit_precision_v2.py`. Alias dicts simplified to `{amplicon_id: call_id}` only (RMS now emits long-form names directly).

- **`scripts/summit_inspector.py` repair:** This script was broken against the post-S28 column scheme (was missed entirely by the original cycle's R2 round). All read sites updated to use post-rename column names: `final_summit_bp`, `final_summit_low_bp`, `final_summit_high_bp`, `final_summit_bp_median`. Includes 5 supplemental in-situ refs beyond the audit's enumerated line list.

- **`scripts/rcn_summit_diagnostics.py`** updated to prefer `final_summit_bp` with `origin_bp` fallback for backward compatibility with older JSON test reports (supplemental in-situ repair).

- **User-visible runtime strings updated:** `rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py:555` "bootstrapping summit CI"; `growth_model_engine.py:1585` "summit refinement"; `hmm_notebooks.py:94` embedded plot xlabel "Distance from summit (bp)".

- **Internal-boundary items intentionally retained:** Internal `onionskin_core/` function names (`refine_origin_for_call`, `refine_origin_sliding_offset`); intermediate dict keys returned by these functions; dataclass field names in `hmm_metrics.py`, `hmm_summit_refinement.py`, `hmm_fork_travel.py`; growth-engine private `_run_argv()` parser's flag tokens and help strings; local Python variable names; test-report JSON keys (internal contract within the report ecosystem).

- **Documentation:** `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md` and `multi-agent/full_instructions/ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md` updated comprehensively for the new column schema across all pipelines + new flags.

## [v0.14.62] — 2026-04-23 ~14:30 EDT

### Phase 14 Supplemental: Q39 resolved — band-aid path approved; new Summit Methodology brainstorm created

User answered Round 9 (Q39) after a targeted code investigation that surfaced a
semantic ambiguity in the current `_origins.tsv` CI columns. Resolution:

**Band-aid path approved for Phase 14 Supplemental 14-S28:**

- Column rename with honest labels (no "ci" or "bootstrap" where they would be
  inaccurate).
- Parser-group description block in the new Summit Refinement group explaining the
  mixed semantics to users directly from `onionskin -h`.
- Defer the structural fix (bootstrap the parabola directly) to a dedicated future
  Summit Methodology phase.

**Final 14-S28 column rename table:**

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `final_origin_bp` | `final_summit_bp` |
| `origin_ci_low_bp` | `final_summit_low_bp` |
| `origin_ci_high_bp` | `final_summit_high_bp` |
| `origin_ci_width_bp` | `final_summit_width_bp` |
| `origin_boot_sd_bp` | `argmax_mean_bootstrap_sd_bp` |

`argmax_mean_bootstrap_sd_bp` is honestly named — it is always argmax-based regardless of
which estimator won, so the name now discloses that fact instead of implying it is the
SD of `final_summit_bp`.

**Code-investigation findings reported in FEEDBACK (JQ1/JQ2/JQ3):**

1. PIPELINE_SPEC.md and ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md already document that
   `final_origin_bp` uses parabola_mean with argmax fallback. User-facing documentation
   exists but is buried.
2. `parabola_mean_bp` exists as a separate column; identical in value to
   `final_origin_bp` when parabola wins; `summit_estimator_used` column discloses the
   winner.
3. Bootstrap CIs in the current `origin_ci_low/high_bp` columns have mixed semantics —
   `min/max` across per-stage parabola vertices when parabola wins, bootstrap CI on
   argmax_mean when argmax fallback wins. `origin_boot_sd_bp` is always argmax-based.
   We do NOT bootstrap the parabola today. Fix deferred to Summit Methodology phase.

**Also confirmed during Round 9:** onionskin has at least five summit-estimator families
across pipelines (argmax, parabola, sliding-offset, RMS selector policies, HMM
state-path) — not just two. This reinforced the case for a dedicated Summit Methodology
phase rather than a piecemeal fix.

**New file created:** `multi-agent/plans/next/PHASE-TBD_SUMMIT_BRAINSTORM.md`.

Scope bundle (parked for a future dedicated phase; likely Phase 16 pending archival of
the existing obsolete `PHASE16_BRAINSTORM.md`):

1. Bootstrap the parabola estimator directly (resolves Q39/JQ3 CI semantics).
2. Dynamic origin-detection window (IBM-C5 / KNOWN_ISSUES [2026-04-18:3]).
3. RMS summit parabola pre-smoothing investigation (from Q36).
4. HMM `peak_rcn_stage` + sliding-offset sub-bin refinement (IBM-C4).
5. Cross-pipeline summit estimator audit and unification.
6. Optional: `_origins.tsv` schema rationalization pass (post-Items 1–5).

Filename uses `PHASE-TBD_` prefix because the existing
`multi-agent/plans/next/PHASE16_BRAINSTORM.md` scaffold was confirmed obsolete — its
body describes per-stage completeness work that was consumed by Phase 13. User will
archive it as `multi-agent/plans/archived/20260423-PERSTAGE_BRAINSTORM.md` with a note.
Once that archival happens, this file is expected to be renamed to
`PHASE16_BRAINSTORM.md`.

**KNOWN_ISSUES tracked list updated:**

Items parked per user direction. What stays in the `KNOWN_ISSUES items to create during
Role 2 implementation` appendix in FEEDBACK:
1. `--onset-*` vs `--timing-*` prefix harmonization (Q12 → 14-S13).
2. Test `--growth-shape-filter on` on live datasets (Q34 → 14-S30).

What MOVED to `PHASE-TBD_SUMMIT_BRAINSTORM.md` instead of KNOWN_ISSUES:
- RMS summit parabola pre-smoothing investigation (Q36).
- Parabola bootstrap + mixed CI semantics (Q39/JQ3).
- Plus the other 3 Summit Methodology bundle items.

**PHASE16_BRAINSTORM.md status confirmation (for user's archival operation):**

Contents confirmed obsolete. The file self-describes as "Phase 13 is the finishing up
Per-Stage completeness to make it capable of producing its own analyses downstream of
amplicon calling, including summit detection, summit refinement, APS, posterior
ordering/grouping/manifest, timing, fork tracking, fork age, etc." Its only priority is
"13.1 — Per-stage completeness." That entire scope landed in Phase 13 (8-step layout,
APS, clustering, posterior manifests, plots, gap analysis, amplicon metrics, per-stage
QC). Safe to archive with a consumed-by-prior-phase note.

**Files changed this round:**

- `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-SPEC.md` — 14-S28 final column rename table +
  band-aid help-text block + scope boundary; 14-S27 implementation scope now references
  the band-aid help text block for the new Summit Refinement group description.
- `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-FEEDBACK.md` — KNOWN_ISSUES tracked list
  pruned; new "Summit Methodology items parked" section added pointing to the new
  brainstorm.
- `multi-agent/plans/next/PHASE-TBD_SUMMIT_BRAINSTORM.md` — **NEW** file with the 6-item
  bundle, design principles, cross-phase interactions, and starter open questions.

No code changes in this round. `AUDIT_HISTORY.md` updated.

  expansion noted: `notebooks.py`, `summit_plots.py`, test fixtures, downstream

  scope-boundary rule).

**Round 9 (Q39 only):**

User asked in Q38 what tag distinguishes this summit estimate from other per-method
summit columns. My answer: **"final" IS already the method tag** — `final_origin_bp`
means "the canonical/winner summit estimate" per ROADMAP Priority 5.9.7, distinguished
from `argmax_*_bp`, `parabola_*_bp`, `sliding_offset_*_bp` which carry method-specific
tags. Proposed renames preserve "final" as tag. Alternative offered: prepend `bootstrap`
to the CI/SD columns (`final_summit_bootstrap_ci_low_bp`) for explicitness at the cost
of length. Q39 is user's final choice.

   (Q34 → 14-S30 defer).

## [v0.14.60] — 2026-04-23 ~13:00 EDT

### Phase 14 Supplemental: Integrate Q31–Q35 answers + code investigations; Round 8 (Q36–Q38)

User-provided answers to OPEN QUESTIONS Q31–Q35 (Round 7) are now integrated into
`multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-SPEC.md`. Code investigations for each user
sub-question produced concrete findings that tightened several priorities:

**Resolved (SPEC now READY):**

- **14-S27** — 3-flag Universal promotion (not 4) under new "Summit Refinement" parser
  group between Timing and Asymmetric Triangle Model. `--growth-rcn-smooth-halfwidth`
  stays growth-specific per code investigation (HMM has its own RCN smoothing at step-5
  and step-14; forcing universal promotion would double-smooth).
- **14-S28** — terminology harmonization clarified with explicit treatment of
  `--bootstrap-origins` (code investigation confirmed "origin" here is the same summit
  concept; recommend rename per 14-S28 scope).
- **14-S29** — auditor pushback accepted per Q33: only `--growth-peak-search` and
  `--rms-peak-search` get the `-halfwidth` suffix. `--growth-trend`, `--rms-trend`,
  `--growth-smooth`, `--rms-smooth` stay as-is (full-width windows, not halfwidths).
- **14-S30** — `--growth-shape-filter` flip from `action="store_true"` to
  `choices=["on","off"]` with `default="off"` (user softened from the original
  `default="on"` proposal to minimize regression risk). Code investigation confirmed
  filter operates on log2-RCN signal, not growth-track signal. Added KNOWN_ISSUE
  scheduling default-flip test for a future phase.

**Round 8 questions added (Q36–Q38):**

- **Q36** — Confirm `--growth-rcn-smooth-halfwidth` stays growth-specific after my
  double-smoothing investigation.
- **Q37** — Rename `--bootstrap-origins` → `--bootstrap-summits` per 14-S28 now that
  user has the bootstrap explanation.
- **Q38** — Rename output columns `final_origin_bp`, `origin_ci_*_bp`,
  `origin_boot_sd_bp` too (option 1, large blast radius), or CLI flags only (option 2,
  recommended for this phase)?

**Code-investigation findings reported in FEEDBACK:**

1. `--growth-rcn-smooth-halfwidth` is dual-use (summit parabola + APS) and
   pipeline-specific to growth's architecture. HMM has its own RCN smoothing at
   step-5 (`--hmm-smooth-halfwidth`) and step-14 APS (`--hmm-aps-smooth-halfwidth`
   from 14-S23). RMS does not pre-smooth RCN before parabola. Promoting to Universal
   would cause double-smoothing for HMM.
2. `stage_weight_mode` is primarily (near-exclusively) summit-refinement in live code.
   The help text mentions timing but the timing path
   (`likelihood_confidence()`) weights by replicate count always and does not consult
   this flag.
3. `--bootstrap-origins` bootstraps over replicates to produce CIs on detected peak
   position. Same conflation of origin/summit as elsewhere.
4. `--growth-shape-filter` operates on log2-RCN profiles (`stage2_score` →
   `dBIC_flat_vs_tri`), not the growth evidence track.

**Files changed this round:**

- `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-SPEC.md` — 14-S27 (3 flags not 4; new
  Summit Refinement group; `rcn-smooth-halfwidth` stays growth); 14-S28 (bootstrap
  investigation note + output-column Q38 framing); 14-S29 (concrete scope after
  auditor pushback accepted); 14-S30 (default=off + RCN-signal code note + KNOWN_ISSUE
  scheduling).
- `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SUPPLEMENTAL-FEEDBACK.md` — OPEN QUESTIONS ROUND 8
  (Q36–Q38) in tight format per user direction.

No code changes in this round. `AUDIT_HISTORY.md` updated.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.104 (claude-opus-4-7)

**Roadmap:** Phase 14 Supplemental — integrate Round 7 answers; produce Round 8 final
questions.

has concrete scope: standalone script + Makefile target. Four new priorities added:
**14-S27** (Universal promotion of summit-refinement flags), **14-S28** (summit/origin
terminology harmonization), **14-S29** (detection-flag halfwidth review), **14-S30**
(`--growth-shape-filter` default flip). User's Q28 answer raised substantive new
questions that are captured as **Round 7 open questions (Q31–Q35)** in FEEDBACK.

**SPEC changes (summary):**

- **14-S24** — concrete scope: `scripts/validate_onionskin_flags.py` + `make validate-flags`
  + docs mention + fixture test. No `onionskin.py` dispatch wrapper.
- **14-S12** — survey outcomes finalized. "Promote next supplemental" items promoted INTO
  this supplemental (user: no next supplemental is coming; Phase 15 is HMM dev).
  `--growth-stage-weight-mode`, `--growth-refine-halfwidth`, `--growth-refine-smooth`,
  `--growth-rcn-smooth-halfwidth` → 14-S27. Detection flags review → 14-S29.
  `--growth-shape-filter` default/semantics → 14-S30.

  others no-rename; convention codified in 14-S16 closeout doc sweep). Help-string
  direction from Q29 absorbed (RCN-explicit language; simpler plain language; peak =
  amplicon clarification).

  clarification, summit-as-origin-proxy clarification, and RCN-explicit wording.
- **NEW 14-S27** — promote `--stage-weight-mode`, `--refine-summit-halfwidth`,
  `--refine-summit-smooth`, `--rcn-smooth-halfwidth` to Universal with growth overrides.
  Blocked on Q31 (parser group placement) and Q32 (final name confirmation).
- **NEW 14-S28** — summit/origin terminology harmonization, public-facing only. Live code
  investigation confirmed usage is interchangeable. Internal `refine_origin_*` function
  names stay per the 14.2 scope-boundary rule.
- **NEW 14-S29** — detection-flag halfwidth review with auditor pushback. User proposed
  `--growth-trend-halfwidth`, `--growth-smooth-halfwidth`, `--growth-peak-search-halfwidth`;

## [v0.14.58] — 2026-04-23 ~11:00 EDT

### Convention refinement: session-boundary rule tightened + orientation-pointer step added

Follow-up to v0.14.57 after the agent over-triggered the new rule mid-SPEC-engineering
round. The user correctly flagged this as evidence the rule was too trigger-happy.

**Changes to `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md` § Session-boundary recommendations:**

1. **Trigger list tightened.** Split into *triggering boundaries* (fire on) and
   *qualifying conditions* (make an already-valid trigger more urgent; do NOT trigger
   alone). Long conversation length was demoted from trigger to qualifier. Vague "closes
   a significant unit of work" language removed.

2. **Explicit guard added.** Agents must NOT surface a recommendation while an active
   workflow cycle is mid-flight: audit → implement → re-audit loop in progress, a
   SPEC-engineering round with unanswered open questions, a user-named "in progress"
   task, or a recently-declined recommendation (unless a clearly different triggering
   boundary is subsequently hit). The guard's intent is explicitly stated:
   "never recommend a break during the middle of a workflow cycle, because doing so is
   the exact failure mode the rule is designed to prevent."

3. **Orientation pointer added as step 9 of the prep-pass checklist.** When the user
   accepts the recommendation, the agent now also produces a compact orientation pointer
   — a one-shot block the user can paste into the first prompt of the new chat. The
   pointer names the authoritative files to read (HANDOFF, TASK, AGENT_CONVENTIONS), the
   last version, active work, pending open questions, blockers, and the concrete first
   action. This is NOT a chat summary (long, immediately stale); it is a warm-start
   prompt.

4. **Confirmation wording updated.** Final go-ahead now includes pasting the orientation
   pointer so the user can copy it directly. Two-step pattern is now formalized as
   `Yes → prep pass + orientation pointer → go-ahead`.

**Motivation for the tightening:** The v0.14.57 rule treated "After writing a CHANGELOG
entry that closes a significant unit of work" as a trigger, and long-conversation
length as a co-equal trigger. I interpreted the former loosely (a convention addition is
not the same as closing a priority or phase) and the latter without respecting active
workflow state, causing the agent to recommend a break mid-SPEC-engineering round. The
user correctly noted the actual natural boundary for this chat remains the Phase 14
Supplemental closeout, not the current cycle. The tightened rule prevents this specific
failure pattern.

No code changes in this round. `AUDIT_HISTORY.md` updated.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.104 (claude-opus-4-7)

**Roadmap:** Convention refinement — session-boundary discipline; independent of Phase 14
Supplemental work

---

## [v0.14.57] — 2026-04-23 ~10:30 EDT

### Convention addition: session-boundary recommendations (AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md)

Added a new rule to `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md` directly after the mandatory
session-end HANDOFF.md rule. The new rule formalizes **when to recommend** starting a new
chat and **how to prepare the handoff for a cold start** when the user accepts.

**Background.** Long chats accumulate context-window cost and are subject to silent
auto-compression that can drop detail later needed. The onionskin project is explicitly
designed for session-based work (HANDOFF.md, TASK.md, CHANGELOG, and the tiered CLAUDE.md
reading list all exist so a cold-start agent can orient in ~10 file reads). Previously
agents had no obligation to help the user use that system. The new rule makes session-break
recommendations an explicit convention.

**What the rule says (summary):**

- **When to surface:** at natural task boundaries — phase closeout, priority closeout,
  after a major recon/audit, after a significant CHANGELOG entry, after >1 hour idle
  return, or when the conversation has grown very long (~40+ turns or multiple large-file
  reads).
- **How to surface:** single-sentence recommendation (example wording provided in the
  rule). Do NOT re-surface in the same session if already declined. Do NOT force the
  break.
- **Two-step user-confirmation pattern:** user says "Yes" → agent performs a
  larger-than-normal handoff-prep pass → agent confirms "safe to leave" → user ends chat.
- **Larger-than-normal handoff-prep pass** itemized: verify HANDOFF.md, TASK.md, CHANGELOG,
  AUDIT_HISTORY, DECISIONS.md, user-facing docs, no orphaned state, and a cold-start
  readability test on HANDOFF.md + TASK.md.
- **Decline path:** if user declines or does not acknowledge, agent continues normally
  and does not re-surface until a new boundary is hit.

**Why as an AGENT_CONVENTIONS rule rather than propagated to all 4 agent files.** This
rule applies uniformly to all agents and does not require agent-specific behavior. Per
AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md § Bidirectional consistency: "If AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md is updated → it
takes effect for all agents automatically (all bootstrap files point here); no
propagation needed unless agent-specific behavior differs." No propagation needed.

No code changes in this round. `AUDIT_HISTORY.md` updated.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.104 (claude-opus-4-7)

**Roadmap:** General convention addition — session-boundary discipline; independent of
Phase 14 Supplemental work

## [v0.14.50] — 2026-04-22 ~17:30 EDT

### Scope authority rule added to AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md and all four agent instruction files

Added a new `## Scope authority` section to `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md` codifying that
the user is the sole authority on phase and priority scope. Propagated a summary block to all
four agent instruction files (`CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `GEMINI.md`,
`.github/copilot-instructions.md`).

Key points encoded:

- Narrowing a phase or priority scope without explicit user approval is prohibited. Agents that
  believe an item is risky or complex must raise an open question, not silently descope.
- Agents must not move intended phase work to `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` without user approval.
  `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` is for work the user has agreed to defer, not a descoping mechanism.
- Broadening is encouraged. Agents are expected to surface adjacent work that fits the spirit
  of the phase and recommend including it; the user is almost always open to broadening.
- Priorities in the audit-implement-reaudit loop must be substantive enough to justify their
  token cost. Thin single-item priorities should be consolidated.

Motivation: over several phases, agents repeatedly narrowed scope and moved intended work to
`KNOWN_ISSUES.md` without user approval, leaving large portions of intended phase work
permanently stranded and forcing later re-work. This rule encodes the user's standing preference
and is intended to prevent that pattern going forward.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.104 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Cross-cutting agent conventions — scope authority

## [v0.14.48] — 2026-04-22 16:05 EDT

### Phase 14 Supplemental: Role 2 implementation round for 14-S1, 14-S2, and 14-S3

- Implemented the full supplemental CLI rename / Universal-promotion pass in `onionskin.py`:
  - 14-S1: renamed 6 growth-specific top-level flags to their `--growth-*` spellings

    parser group and added `--growth-peak-*` overrides

    `--asym-tri-model-*` flags plus matching growth-only overrides

  `_resolve_override_value()` paths for the S2/S3 Universal-plus-growth-override surfaces.
- Added 9 deprecated-flag redirects in `_DEPRECATED_FLAGS` so the removed spellings now fail
  before argparse with explicit replacements.

## [v0.14.41] — 2026-04-22 ~13:45 EDT

### Phase 14 audit loop: Role 2 implemented Priority 14.11 (`AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md` CLI flag rule)

- Added the missing `CLI Flag Naming and Structure Conventions` section to
  `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md`.
- Wrote the new rule from the live parser structure in `onionskin.py` rather than from the
  oversimplified priority summary:
  shared concepts belong in `Universal`, pipeline-specific custom behavior uses
  `--<pipeline>-<concept>` overrides, and override inheritance is documented as an
  "unset/omitted inherits" contract rather than a literal `default None` requirement.
- Grounded the rule with the live owning references requested by the audit:
  `build_parser()` for parser grouping, `_resolve_override_value()` for inheritance behavior, and
  `_DEPRECATED_FLAGS` / `_check_deprecated_flags()` for pre-parse alias removal.
- Included the live internal-boundary rule that `onionskin_core/` parameter names are internal
  APIs and do not need to mirror CLI renames; CLI normalization and type conversion stay in
  `onionskin.py`.
- Supplemental audit found no adjacent inconsistency that required parser or test edits, so the
  round stayed documentation-only.
- Validation run:
  - re-read the inserted `CLI Flag Naming and Structure Conventions` section in
    `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md` and confirmed it explicitly covers Universal vs
    pipeline-specific overrides, unset/omitted inheritance, `--<pipeline>-<concept>` naming,
    pre-parse alias removal, and the `onionskin_core/` boundary

  still has no `CLI Flag Naming and Structure Conventions` section, nor any equivalent rule block
  that captures the Phase 14 parser structure.
- The audit verified that the live code already embodies the intended rule:
  - a real `Universal` parser group owns shared flags
  - pipeline-specific `--hmm-*`, `--growth-*`, and `--rms-*` overrides exist in their owning
    groups
  - `_resolve_override_value()` is the owning inheritance pattern
  - `_DEPRECATED_FLAGS` and `_check_deprecated_flags()` enforce pre-parse alias removal
- The audit also found one nuance missing from the priority summary: the conventions rule must
  describe inheritance as an "unset/omitted inherits" contract rather than requiring literal
  `default None` everywhere, because the live parser currently uses both `argparse.SUPPRESS` and
  `None` depending on whether an override is active now or only reserved for a future phase.

  next Role 2 documentation/conventions round.

## [v0.14.21] — 2026-04-21 22:37 EDT

### Phase 14 audit loop: Role 2 implemented Priority 14.3 norm-mode inheritance behavior

- Implemented the audited Priority 14.3 behavior change in `onionskin.py`:
  - `--norm-mode` now defaults to `None` in `build_parser()`
  - `_default_pipeline_norm_mode()` now provides built-in pipeline defaults only
  - `_resolve_pipeline_norm_mode()` now implements the three 14.3 contract cases directly:
    concrete per-pipeline override wins; `inherit` follows the explicit global mode when set or
    the built-in default when unset; no pipeline-specific override follows the explicit global mode
    when set or the built-in default when unset
  - `_resolve_controller_norm_mode()` no longer reintroduces the old hard-coded shared
    `chrom-median` fallback for active pipeline resolution
- Updated the APS post-processing boundary in `onionskin.py:_phase2_postprocess()` so an
  intentionally unset global norm mode is not silently treated as `chrom-median` until after the
  resolved lane-specific override has had a chance to apply; the local fallback to
  `chrom-median` now happens only at the final APS call boundary when no resolved override is
  available.
- Rewrote the norm-mode tests in `tests/test_pipeline.py` so they now lock in the 14.3 semantics:
  - unset global mode validates built-in per-pipeline defaults
  - explicit global `norm_mode="ref-stage"` is inherited when no pipeline-specific override is set
  - explicit per-pipeline overrides still take precedence over the global value
- Supplemental same-surface audit while implementing found one nearby cleanup that needed to move
  with the resolver change:
  `_default_pipeline_norm_mode()` still encoded the old shared-fallback coupling via
  `shared_norm_mode`; that coupling was removed so the helper now represents built-in defaults only.
- Appended a Role 2 implementation report to `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SPEC.md` and updated
  `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md` and `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md` for the next
  re-audit step.
- Validation run:
  - `python onionskin.py -h`
  - `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_pipeline.py -k "norm_mode or stage_aware_pipeline_norm or inherit_override" -q`
    → `6 passed, 39 deselected`
  - read-only probes:
    - `build_parser().get_default("norm_mode")` → `None`
    - `_resolve_growth_norm_mode(SimpleNamespace(), n_stages=3, has_reference_stage=True)` → `chrom-median`
    - `_resolve_hmm_norm_mode(SimpleNamespace(norm_mode="ref-stage"), n_stages=1, has_reference_stage=True)` → `ref-stage`
  - `make test`
    → FAIL on an unrelated existing RCN profile-plot permission issue in
      `tests/test_pipeline.py::test_multistage_rcn_mean_shift_only_runs_standalone_controller`
      (`[rcn-mean-shift] profile plots warning: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted`)

  `shape_score_raw` values for a live call.

### Phase 14 audit loop: Role 1 initial audit opens Priority 14.2

- Re-audited Priority 14.1 against live code to confirm the prior closure (v0.14.14) holds.
  Section order, group names, flag placements, and focused test all verified correct — 14.1
  remains CLOSED.
- Performed initial audit of `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SPEC.md` Priority 14.2
  (`Flag renames + args.* call-site updates`) against the live codebase.
- Verified that no renames have been implemented: all 19 old flag names remain in
  `onionskin.py:build_parser()`, all old `args.*` attribute names are active at call sites,
  both SUPPRESS hidden aliases (`--rcn-summit-policy`, `--rcn-early-summit-stages`) are still
  present, `--strict-bic` still uses `action="store_true"` instead of `choices=["on","off"]`,
  and override inheritance for the new pipeline-specific flags from 14.1 is not yet wired.
- Found two SPEC gaps and added them to the Priority 14.2 implementation contract:
  - **Finding 14.2-A (CRITICAL):** `_resolve_pipeline_norm_mode()` at `onionskin.py:2047`
    dynamically constructs the attribute name as `{pipeline.replace('-','_')}_norm_mode`.
    After renaming `dest="rcn_mean_shift_norm_mode"` to `dest="rms_norm_mode"`, the dynamic
    lookup will silently fail — the resolver will look for `rcn_mean_shift_norm_mode` but the
    attribute will be `rms_norm_mode`. Fix: change `_resolve_rcn_mean_shift_norm_mode()` to
    internally pass `"rms"` as the pipeline key to `_resolve_pipeline_norm_mode` (function
    name stays unchanged per scope boundary).
  - **Finding 14.2-B (SPEC GAP):** `--per-stage-norm-mode` is currently registered as a second
    name in the same `add_argument` call as `--rcn-mean-shift-norm-mode` and is not mentioned in
    the 14.2 rename plan. Correct treatment: keep it as a SUPPRESS alias pointing to
    `rms_norm_mode` in this priority; Priority 14.4 adds the deprecation catch.
- Verified all affected `tests/` surfaces: `test_pipeline.py` (multiple `--rcn-mean-shift-norm-mode`
  invocations and `rcn_mean_shift_norm_mode` attr), `test_strict_bic.sh` (`--strict-bic`),
  `optimize_single_params.py` (6 old RMS key names), `scan_growth_methods_ds1.sh`,
  `run_twin_peak_test.sh`, `run_full_chrII_test.sh`. No old flag names found in `scripts/`.
- Appended full audit findings and complete Role 2 implementation instructions to Priority 14.2
  in `multi-agent/plans/PHASE14_SPEC.md`.
- Updated `AUDIT_HISTORY.md`, `multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md`, and
  `multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`.
- Tests not run: audit-only round; no code changes.

## [v0.13.80] — 2026-04-21 ~00:10 EDT

### Role 1 Final Closeout — Phase 13 Priority 13.10 and Full Phase Closure

Performed the Role 1 Final Closeout audit (Template G) after Copilot's Priority 13.10
post-wrap-up implementation (v0.13.79). Verified all four FIND-R3 findings against live code.
All findings are resolved. Phase 13 is fully closed.

**FIND-R3-1 (standalone block skips posterior):** Resolved. `onionskin.py:3311–3312` now calls
`_run_rcn_posterior()` when `--posterior` is set in the standalone rcn-ms block.

**FIND-R3-2 (hardcoded APS dir):** Resolved via different mechanism — `_reconstruct_prior_layout()`
is absent from the live tree. `build_output_layout()` includes `"rcn_aps_dir"` (confirmed at
`output_layout.py:270`). `_run_rcn_posterior()` reads `prior_layout["rcn_aps_dir"]` correctly.

**FIND-R3-3 (standalone path):** Resolved. `_run_rcn_posterior()` calls
`_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller()`, not `run_multistage()`.

**FIND-R3-3 (combined path, was deferred):** Resolved as undocumented scope expansion.
`_run_posterior()` (lines 3093–3106) now calls `_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller()` when
`_pipelines_include(args, "rcn-mean-shift")`. This was listed as deferred to `KNOWN_ISSUES.md`;
Copilot fixed it and (inaccurately) framed it as pre-existing. Fix is correct, all tests pass.

**FIND-R3-4 (test masking):** Resolved. `test_posterior_rcn_ms_standalone` covers the
standalone path with thorough assertions including a negative check (no growth posterior dir).

Non-blocking observation appended to `## Final Closeout` in PHASE13_SPEC.md:
`_check_prior_complete()` at `onionskin.py:2674` still hardcodes `02-growth-model/13-aps/` for
its APS validation check. Not a blocking bug — only the combined block calls this function, and
the standalone block has no prior-reuse logic. Future work if prior-reuse is added to standalone.

Appended final sign-off to `## Final Closeout` in `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md`.
Phase 13 SPEC is now fully closed. Updated HANDOFF.md and TASK.md to reflect phase closure.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.98 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 per-stage restructuring / name migration

## [v0.13.79] — 2026-04-20 ~23:58 EDT

### Role 2 Post-Wrap-Up Implementation — Priority 13.10 Standalone RCN Posterior Support

Implemented the active post-wrap-up repair subset from `## Post-Wrap-Up Triage` in
`multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md` for standalone `--pipelines rcn-mean-shift --posterior`
support.

- Added `_run_rcn_posterior(prior_layout, args, chromosomes_override=None)` in `onionskin.py` to
  build posterior manifests from `03-rcn-mean-shift/12-aps/` and re-run only the RCN posterior
  arm under `02-posterior/03-rcn-mean-shift/`.
- Fixed the standalone multistage+rcn block in `main()` so it no longer returns before posterior
  processing. It now runs the prior standalone RCN controller, writes run metadata on success,
  and dispatches `_run_rcn_posterior(...)` when `--posterior` is enabled.
- Added regression test `tests/test_pipeline.py::test_posterior_rcn_ms_standalone`, which proves
  that `--pipelines rcn-mean-shift --posterior` creates posterior RCN outputs, posterior APS
  outputs, and a 2-stage posterior manifest without creating a growth posterior arm.

Implementation divergence from the triage note, verified against live code:

- The referenced `_reconstruct_prior_layout()` helper no longer exists in the current tree; the
  live prior-layout reuse surface is `_check_prior_complete()`, and `build_output_layout()`
  already reconstructs `rcn_aps_dir`, so no separate Step 1 patch was needed.
- The deferred combined growth+rcn posterior gap also does not exist in the current tree:
  `_run_posterior()` already calls `_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller(...)` at the end when the RCN
  pipeline is active. Because that issue was not present, no new `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` entry was
  added.

Validation:

- `PYTHONPATH=. pytest -q tests/test_pipeline.py::test_posterior_rcn_ms_standalone` PASS
- `make test` PASS (`72 passed in 143.37s`)
- `make toy` PASS
- `make single` PASS

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 per-stage restructuring / name migration

## [v0.13.78] — 2026-04-20 ~23:55 EDT

### Role 1 Post-Wrap-Up Triage — Phase 13 Posterior rcn-mean-shift Bugs

Completed Role 1 Post-Wrap-Up Triage (Template E) of the four cross-cutting posterior bugs
identified by Gemini's Final Overseer audit (v0.13.77). Verified all four findings against
live code, made active-vs-deferred triage decisions, and wrote concrete implementation
instructions for Copilot in `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md` `## Post-Wrap-Up Triage`.

Findings verified:
- **FIND-R3-1:** Confirmed at `onionskin.py:3215` — standalone rcn-ms block returns without
  any `--posterior` check. Growth+rcn combined block at line 3136/3193 handles posterior;
  standalone path does not.
- **FIND-R3-2:** Confirmed at `onionskin.py:2674` — `_reconstruct_prior_layout()` hardcodes
  `aps_dir = os.path.join(prior_dir, "02-growth-model", "13-aps")`. `output_layout.py`
  already defines `"rcn_aps_dir"` for `03-rcn-mean-shift/12-aps/` but it is never set in
  `_reconstruct_prior_layout()` and never used in `_run_posterior()`.
- **FIND-R3-3:** Confirmed at `onionskin.py:2989` — `_run_posterior()` calls
  `run_multistage()` (growth engine) unconditionally with no rcn-ms branch. No call to
  `run_rcn_mean_shift` or `_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller` anywhere in the function body.
- **FIND-R3-4:** Confirmed at `tests/test_pipeline.py:1166` — test uses default
  `--pipelines all`, exercises growth+rcn combined block, never reaches standalone rcn-ms
  posterior path.

Triage decisions:
- FIND-R3-1, FIND-R3-2, FIND-R3-3 (standalone path), FIND-R3-4: **Active repair — Priority 13.10.**
- FIND-R3-3 (combined growth+rcn posterior path — `_run_posterior()` not calling RCN engine):
  **Deferred to KNOWN_ISSUES** — requires its own design and is lower urgency.

Priority 13.10 implementation instructions written to spec covering:
1. Fix `_reconstruct_prior_layout()` to also set `"rcn_aps_dir"`.
2. Add `_run_rcn_posterior()` that uses `"rcn_aps_dir"` and calls `_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller()`.
3. Fix standalone rcn-ms block to call `_run_rcn_posterior()` when `--posterior`.
4. Add new test `test_posterior_rcn_ms_standalone` for `--pipelines rcn-mean-shift --posterior`.
5. Add `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` entry for the combined-posterior RCN gap.

No code changes this session — documentation and planning only.

Performed the final Role 3 wrap-up audit of `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md` against the
codebase. While the primary restructuring and renaming to `rcn-mean-shift` is complete and
functional for default runs, four cross-cutting logic bugs were identified that break
standalone `rcn-mean-shift` runs with the `--posterior` flag enabled.

- **FIND-R3-1:** Standalone `rcn-mean-shift` returns early from `main()`, skipping `--posterior`.
- **FIND-R3-2:** Posterior APS source hardcoded to `02-growth-model/13-aps/`, missing standalone
  `03-rcn-mean-shift/12-aps/` outputs.
- **FIND-R3-3:** `_run_posterior()` unconditionally runs the growth-model engine.
- **FIND-R3-4:** Regression test `test_posterior_rcn_mean_shift_output_exists` masks these failures
  by relying on default `--pipelines all`.

Appended the audit report to `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md` and updated
`multi-agent/AUDIT_HISTORY.md` per conventions. Phase 13 remains nominally closed for primary
objectives but is now blocked on a triage decision for these regressions.

Validation: no code changes in this documentation pass; findings verified via read-only
codebase inspection and a temporary test modification (reverted).

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Gemini CLI 0.38.2 (gemini-2.0-flash-thinking-exp-01-21)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 per-stage restructuring / name migration

## [v0.13.72] — 2026-04-20 ~22:00 EDT

### Documented two structural gaps in new `## Even More Loose Ends` section of PHASE13_SPEC.md

Added a new section to `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md` (before the Final Overseer heading)
documenting two Phase 13 structural gaps that were planned but never completed, with full
blast-radius analysis and concrete repair instructions.

- **Loose End A — `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` at wrong level:** The RCN mean-shift batch engine
  lives at `onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` while both peer batch engines
  (`growth_model_engine.py`, `hmm_engine.py`) live in `onionskin_core/engines/`. The Phase 13
  spec planned a rename-in-place and never addressed peer placement. Fix: `git mv` to
  `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`; update `onionskin.py:23–28` (split import
  to pull `run_rcn_mean_shift` from `engines.rcn_mean_shift_engine` and the three helper
  functions directly from `rcn_mean_shift_helpers`); update `tests/test_pipeline.py:20–21`;
  fix relative import inside the moved file (`from . → from ..`); update
  `PIPELINE_SPEC.md:1622`.
- **Loose End B — `engines/per_stage_mean_shift_engine.py` never renamed:** Phase 13 Priority
  13.1 item 3 explicitly planned `git mv engines/per_stage_mean_shift_engine.py
  engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`. Instead Copilot created a wildcard shim; the shim was
  later deleted; the original file was preserved with its stale name. After Loose End A occupies
  `engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, the rename target becomes
  `engines/rcn_mean_shift_singlefile_engine.py`. Call sites: `detection.py:14` and
  `tests/test_pipeline.py:19`. Stale doc references: `PIPELINE_SPEC.md:1621` and
  `PHASE14/15/16_SPEC-BRAINSTORM.md` (all three contain a stale "aligned future naming
  direction" note still citing `per_stage_mean_shift_engine.py`).

Implementation order: Loose End A steps first (batch engine move + import updates), then Loose
End B (single-file CLI rename + call sites + doc updates). Validation: `make test` PASS; confirm
`engines/` contains all four files: `growth_model_engine.py`, `hmm_engine.py`,
`rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, `rcn_mean_shift_singlefile_engine.py`.

No code changes in this pass — documentation only.

## [v0.13.69] — 2026-04-20 ~20:15 EDT

### Role 1 Second-Pass Audit — Deferred Loose Ends After Phase 13 Closeout — OPEN (2 precision gaps added)

Performed a second-pass audit of the Deferred Loose Ends section after Copilot's initial audit (v0.13.68). Verified all 3 of Copilot's findings against live code — all confirmed correct. Identified 2 precision gaps within Copilot's FIND-R1-2 and FIND-R1-3 repair instructions that pose implementer risk.

- **GAP-A (MEDIUM, within FIND-R1-2):** `tests/test_pipeline.py` contains two distinct per-stage imports: line 19 (`from onionskin_core.engines import per_stage_mean_shift_engine as legacy_per_stage_engine`) targets the engines/ CLI module and must NOT be changed; line 20 (`from onionskin_core.per_stage_mean_shift_engine import run_per_stage_mean_shift`) is the top-level helper import that is the actual target of FIND-R1-2 step 3. Copilot's instruction does not distinguish these, creating an implementer risk of incorrectly modifying or removing line 19 during a text-search rename.
- **GAP-B (HIGH, within FIND-R1-3):** `onionskin.py:41-43` is a multi-line import block from `onionskin_core.timing` that includes `compute_timing_from_stage_summary`, `annotate_calls_with_gap_distance`, `_REJECT_CONCERNS`, and `_REVIEW_CONCERNS`. Copilot's FIND-R1-3 step 2 only mentions updating `annotate_calls_with_gap_distance`, but `_REJECT_CONCERNS` and `_REVIEW_CONCERNS` are also gap-analysis constants listed in FIND-R1-3 step 1's move target. After the move, the import at lines 41-43 must be split: `from onionskin_core.timing import compute_timing_from_stage_summary` and `from onionskin_core.gap_analysis import annotate_calls_with_gap_distance, _REJECT_CONCERNS, _REVIEW_CONCERNS`.

Both gaps documented as additional repair instructions appended to FIND-R1-2 and FIND-R1-3 in `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md`.

Validation note: no runtime code changed in this audit pass, so no tests were run.

## [v0.13.57] — 2026-04-20 ~13:30 EDT

Role 1 initial audit of Priority 13.6 (Plots). **OPEN — 5 blocking findings.**

Audited: `onionskin_core/profile_plots.py`, `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`,
`onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, `onionskin.py`, `tests/test_pipeline.py`,
and live toy run outputs.

APS plot routing (`aps_plots_dir` param in Priority 13.5 APS call) — already correctly
implemented at `onionskin.py:2430`. No action needed for that sub-item.

Blocking findings for profile/summit plots:

- **FIND-R1-1**: `profile_plots._find_stage_bedgraphs()` globs `stage*.RCN.bedGraph`;
  rcn-mean-shift writes `{prefix}_stage{N}_chrom_norm.bedGraph` — pattern does not match;
  profile plots would silently produce nothing.
- **FIND-R1-2**: `profile_plots._find_sample_bedgraphs()` globs `*.RCN.bedGraph`;
  rcn-mean-shift `indiv_samples/` has `*_chrom_norm.bedGraph` — no match; per-sample
  background lines absent from all profiles.
- **FIND-R1-3**: Unified calls TSV and per-stage dedup calls TSV have no `call_id` column;
  `profile_plots.py` calls `row['call_id']` unconditionally — would raise `KeyError`.
- **FIND-R1-4**: `summit_plots._find_rcn_bedgraphs()` globs `*.RCN.bedGraph` — same
  mismatch. Summit plots will gracefully skip (accepted; gated on Priority 13.3), but
  bedGraph discovery must be fixed for when Priority 13.3 is resolved.
- **FIND-R1-5**: Neither `write_profile_plots()` nor `write_summit_plots()` is called in
  `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`. No test assertions for `11-plots/profiles/` or
  `11-plots/genome_overview/`.

## [v0.13.53] — 2026-04-20 ~10:45 EDT

### Role 2 Implementation — Priority 13.5 OPEN (Per-sample Normalization + APS + Clustering)

Implemented the audited Priority 13.5 repair set against the live RCN and HMM surfaces.

**What changed:**

- Added the routed RCN step-02b per-sample chrom-normalized bedGraph pass in `onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, writing one `02-chrom-norm/indiv_samples/{prefix}_{sample_id}_stage{st}_chrom_norm.bedGraph` file per sample.
- Added a clean 4-column APS BED alongside the existing classified unified BED: `08-multistage-unification/{prefix}_unified_calls_aps.bed`, with column 4 exactly `chrom:start-end` so `aps.py::parse_loci_bed()` can use it directly.
- Wired the shared APS machinery into `_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller()` in `onionskin.py`. After gap analysis, the controller now calls `compute_and_write_aps()` with `args.manifest`, routes APS outputs into `03-rcn-mean-shift/12-aps/`, and routes APS plots into `03-rcn-mean-shift/11-plots/aps/`.
- Added the HMM APS gate in `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`: step 14 now runs only when at least one non-reference stage is present, and skips cleanly for reference-only inputs.
- Corrected the Priority 13.5 spec contract plus `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` to match reality: APS clustering outputs live inside `12-aps/`; `13-clustering/` remains pre-created but intentionally unused; the live APS filenames are `onionskin_aps.tsv`, `onionskin_aps_locus_contributions.tsv`, `aps_matrix_*.tsv`, `aps_clusters_*.tsv`, and `aps_order_*.tsv`.

**Additional low-risk same-surface fixes:**

- Updated `tests/test_pipeline.py` to assert the new RCN per-sample bedGraphs, clean APS BED, and APS outputs, and to update the single-stage HMM expectation so reference-only HMM runs no longer require `14-aps/onionskin_aps.tsv`.
- Updated the grouped-layout documentation in `onionskin_core/output_layout.py` so `03-rcn-mean-shift/12-aps/` is no longer described as merely reserved.

**Validation:**

- `make toy` PASS
- Verified live toy outputs:
  - `toy_out/01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/02-chrom-norm/indiv_samples/` contains 15 `*_chrom_norm.bedGraph` files

  - `toy_out/01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/12-aps/aps_clusters_raw.tsv`

- `make test` PASS (`70 passed`)

## [v0.13.52] — 2026-04-20 ~10:00 EDT

Role 1 initial audit of Priority 13.5 — Per-sample normalization + APS + clustering.
Section OPEN. 7 findings documented; implementation instructions written into
PHASE13_SPEC.md. AUDIT_HISTORY.md updated.

**Findings summary:**
- FIND-R1-1 CRITICAL: `02-chrom-norm/indiv_samples/` empty — per-sample normalization pass
  not implemented. Engine receives `sample_tracks[sid][chrom]` DataFrames with `log2` column
  (already chrom-median normalized via `add_norm_log2`); need to write per-sample bedGraphs.
- FIND-R1-2 HIGH: `12-aps/` empty — no APS call in engine or controller for RCN pipeline.
  `compute_and_write_aps` imported at `onionskin.py:46` but never called in RCN path.
- FIND-R1-3 HIGH (spec error): `13-clustering/` target state is wrong. `compute_and_write_aps`
  writes ALL outputs (including clustering: `aps_clusters_raw.tsv`, `aps_order_area.tsv`, etc.)
  to `12-aps/`. No separate clustering step. Spec corrected.
- FIND-R1-4 HIGH (architectural): `compute_and_write_aps(manifest_path, ...)` needs a file
  path; engine has DataFrames only. Solution: per-sample bedGraphs written by engine from
  in-memory data; APS called from controller using `args.manifest`.
- FIND-R1-5 HIGH: unified BED call_id format incompatible with `parse_loci_bed()`. Existing
  BED has name `{chrom}:{start}-{end}|{classification}|{trail}` — breaks APS summit lookup.
  Fix: engine also writes clean 4-column `{prefix}_unified_calls_aps.bed`.
- FIND-R1-6 HIGH: `run_step14_hmm_aps()` called unconditionally at `hmm_engine.py:1238`.
  Spec requires ≥1 non-reference stage gating.
- FIND-R1-7 INFO: HMM step 16 (`run_step16_hmm_clustering`) is trajectory clustering (fork-
  travel feature vectors), not APS-based sample clustering — different concept, no change.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.98 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 13.5 — Per-sample normalization + APS + clustering

## [v0.13.50] — 2026-04-20 ~02:05 EDT

### Role 2 Implementation — Priority 13.4 OPEN (Gap Analysis + Amplicon Metrics)

Implemented the audited Priority 13.4 repair plan against the live RCN pipeline.

**What changed:**

- Wired RCN gap analysis at the correct controller layer in `onionskin.py::_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller()`. After the standalone RCN lane emits `08-multistage-unification/{out_prefix}_unified_stage_calls.tsv`, the controller now runs `annotate_calls_with_gap_distance()` from `onionskin_core/timing.py` using `samples[0].path` as the gap-mask bedGraph and writes step-09 outputs into `03-rcn-mean-shift/09-gap-analysis/`.
- Added `onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py::_write_rcn_amplicon_metrics()` and wired it immediately after unified call emission so step 10 now writes `03-rcn-mean-shift/10-amplicon-metrics/{out_prefix}_amplicon_metrics.tsv`.
- The step-10 table now filters to final `amplicon` rows and projects post-unification fields: `chrom`, `start`, `end`, `width_bp`, `summit_bp`, `best_shape_score`, `best_stage1_score_z`, plus stage-support provenance when available.
- Updated `tests/test_pipeline.py` so the standalone multistage RCN integration test asserts the new step-09 gap outputs and step-10 amplicon metrics output exist.
- Corrected the Priority 13.4 spec text and synced `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` so the live contract now points to `timing.py`, the real `_unified_calls.*` filenames, and the actual first-pass step-10 schema.

**Validation:**

- `make toy` PASS
- Verified live toy outputs:
  - `toy_out/01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/09-gap-analysis/onionskin_42_unified_calls.gap_annotated.tsv`
  - `toy_out/01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/09-gap-analysis/onionskin_42_unified_calls.gap_analysis.tsv`
  - `toy_out/01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/10-amplicon-metrics/onionskin_42_amplicon_metrics.tsv`
- Verified the amplicon-metrics header contains `chrom`, `start`, `end`, `width_bp`, `summit_bp`, and `best_shape_score`
- `make test` PASS

**Note:** The growth-lane keep-BED finalizer was intentionally not forced into the standalone RCN lane. `_finalize_amplicon_recommendations()` depends on timing/origins artifacts that the RCN lane does not emit, so the correct repair in this round was to fix the spec contract rather than fabricate a partial keep-BED path.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 Priority 13.4 implementation

## [v0.13.49] — 2026-04-20 ~01:15 EDT

### Role 1 Initial Audit — Priority 13.4 OPEN (Gap Analysis + Amplicon Metrics)

Performed a deep initial audit of Priority 13.4 against the live codebase at v0.13.48.

**State confirmed:** Both `09-gap-analysis/` and `10-amplicon-metrics/` directories are pre-created
by `output_layout.py` but are empty. Neither gap analysis nor amplicon metrics are implemented for
the RCN pipeline.

**7 findings:**

- **FIND-R1-1 CRITICAL:** `onionskin_core/gap_analysis.py` does not exist. The spec incorrectly
  names this as the source. Gap analysis lives in `onionskin_core/timing.py::annotate_calls_with_gap_distance()`.
- **FIND-R1-2 HIGH:** `09-gap-analysis/` never populated — no call to gap analysis exists in
  `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` or `onionskin.py` for the RCN pipeline.
- **FIND-R1-3 HIGH:** `10-amplicon-metrics/` never populated — HMM's `hmm_metrics.py::compute_amplicon_metrics_for_stage()` is HMM-specific and cannot be reused; a new function operating on the unified calls DataFrame must be written.
- **FIND-R1-4 MEDIUM:** Spec says to implement gap analysis in `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`, but the
  engine receives pre-loaded DataFrames; gap mask needs `samples[0].path`. Correct location:
  `onionskin.py::_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller()`, matching growth pipeline pattern.
- **FIND-R1-5 LOW:** Output filenames in the spec don't match what `annotate_calls_with_gap_distance()` actually produces (naming derivation issue; also "keep" BED requires a separate finalization function not in scope).
- **FIND-R1-6 MEDIUM:** `peak_rcn` column in spec's `10-amplicon-metrics/` schema does not exist in the unified calls TSV. Replace with `best_shape_score` (from `best_shape_score_raw`).
- **FIND-R1-7 LOW:** No test assertions for steps 09 and 10 for the RCN pipeline.

Full findings and 4-step implementation instructions (including concrete code snippets) appended to
Priority 13.4 in `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md`. `AUDIT_HISTORY.md` updated.

**Loose Ends item 6 added:** Gap analysis code currently lives in `onionskin_core/timing.py`
alongside unrelated timing code. Item 6 tracks extracting `GapMask`, `build_gap_mask_from_bedgraph`,
`annotate_calls_with_gap_distance`, and related helpers into a new `onionskin_core/gap_analysis.py`,
updating all import sites. Marked as a Phase 13 closeout blocker alongside items 4 and 5.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.98 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 Priority 13.4 initial audit

## [v0.13.47] — 2026-04-19 ~23:25 EDT

### Role 2 Runtime Update — RMS Unified Default Now Emits the Winning Contributor's Parabola Summit

- Implemented the simple RMS runtime summit improvement requested after the fresh ds2 diagnostic
  review.
- The change lands in `onionskin_core/per_stage_mean_shift_engine.py`, inside the shared unified
  selector helper used to emit the final step-08 peak:
  - the same winning contributor row is still chosen by the existing first-pass detection score
  - when that winning row already carries `peak_parabola`, the emitted unified `peak` now uses that
    parabola-refined summit instead of the raw peak
  - raw provenance is preserved in `peak_default_stage1_best`
  - the default refined counterpart is now exposed in `peak_default_stage1_best_parabola`
  - `summit_estimator_used` now reports `stage1_best_parabola` when the default refined summit was
    used, falling back to `stage1_best_peak` only when no parabola summit is available
- Updated the RMS summit evaluator normalization logic so runtime-emitted
  `stage1_best_parabola` summaries are interpreted correctly, and extended unit coverage for the new
  selector behavior.
- File-structure clarification:
  - `onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` is the routed Phase 13 pipeline implementation
  - `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` remains the legacy/reference engine-facing
    module path used by `load_reference_rcn_mean_shift()` and currently re-exports the older
    engine implementation in `onionskin_core/engines/per_stage_mean_shift_engine.py`
  - neither needed a matching logic edit for this change because the runtime selector behavior
    lives in the shared `onionskin_core/per_stage_mean_shift_engine.py` helper module

  - `pytest -q tests/test_rcn_mean_shift_selector.py tests/test_eval_summit_precision_v2.py tests/test_rcn_final_classification.py` PASS
  - `pytest -q tests/test_pipeline.py::test_multistage_rcn_mean_shift_can_emit_early_parabola_selector_candidate` PASS
  - `make test` PASS
  - `make toy` PASS
  - `SUMMIT_CHROMOSOMES=II bash tests/run_summit_precision_test.sh smoke` PASS
- Fresh smoke interpretation:
  - canonical ds2 hires RCN summit result improved from the earlier fresh `1/10`, `+4537 bp`
    runtime-selected point to `2/10`, `+3126 bp`
  - this confirms the simple default-parabola switch helps, but it does not finish summit
    optimization; ds2 still trails the better replayed parabola policies materially

**Phase 13 Loose Ends additions**: 
- Item 4 describes the shims problem in full: the broken 3-hop indirection chain for run_multistage, the empty engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py, the defeated lazy-load rationale, the broken common.py:50 path — and lists five concrete cleanup steps (a–e).

## [v0.13.45] — 2026-04-19 ~22:40 EDT

### Role 2 Fix + Reassessment — Summit Harness Fresh-Prior Repair and Fresh RMS II/9A Recheck

- Fixed the stale-artifact path in `tests/run_summit_precision_test.sh` by clearing each
  per-label output directory before launching the pipeline.
- Root cause: representative summit runs such as `ds1_hires`, `ds2_hires`, `ds1_5000bp`, and
  `ds2_5000bp` use `--posterior`, and `onionskin.py` intentionally skips the prior rerun when a
  valid prior already exists. That meant `summary_rcn.json` could be refreshed while
  `01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/08-multistage-unification/*_unified_stage_calls.tsv` remained stale.
- Verified that the refreshed representative RMS unified TSVs are now newly regenerated and carry
  the post-v0.13.41 schema, including `summit_estimator_used`, `final_classification`, and
  `bed_score`.
- Recomputed fresh representative `summary_rcn.json` outputs directly from those regenerated RMS
  TSVs and reran the dedicated diagnostic artifact into
  `dev/runs/phase13_rms_z2p5_check_20260419_fresh/summit_diagnostics/`.
- Fresh representative RMS II/9A runtime-primary results:
  - `ds1_hires`: `score=9/10`, `signed_orc_dist=-463 bp`, `summit_estimator_used=stage1_best_peak`
  - `ds1_5000bp`: `score=10/10`, `signed_orc_dist=-463 bp`, `summit_estimator_used=stage1_best_peak`
  - `ds2_hires`: `score=1/10`, `signed_orc_dist=+4537 bp`, `summit_estimator_used=stage1_best_peak`
  - `ds2_5000bp`: `score=2/10`, `signed_orc_dist=+4537 bp`, `summit_estimator_used=stage1_best_peak`
- Fresh diagnostic interpretation:
  - the old apparent summit regression was partly an artifact-refresh bug, because the evaluator is
    now scoring fresh runtime-emitted selector provenance instead of stale fallback behavior
  - however, the corrected summit-side result is still not good news for the current runtime RMS
    selector on dataset2: `stage1_best_peak` remains a weak II/9A choice there even on fresh artifacts
  - replayed parabola policies still outperform the runtime-selected point on the representative
    ds2 surface, so the remaining summit-side issue is selector quality rather than artifact staleness
- Validation / analysis:
  - `SUMMIT_CHROMOSOMES=II bash tests/run_summit_precision_test.sh full` (fresh prior artifact rewrite verified from regenerated representative RMS TSV timestamps/schema)
  - direct `tests/eval_summit_precision_v2.py` reruns on the four representative RMS TSVs
  - `bash tests/run_rcn_summit_diagnostic.sh existing phase13_rms_z2p5_check_20260419_fresh`
