## [v0.13.44] — 2026-04-19 ~20:55 EDT

### Role 2 Analysis + Tooling — Full chr-II By-Eye RMS/HMM Comparison and Summit Artifact Audit

- Added a dedicated full-chr-II by-eye interval comparison wrapper at
  `tests/run_full_chrII_byeye_compare.sh`.
- The new wrapper scores the correct interval-emitting outputs against
  `tests/full_chrom_training_data/amplicons.by-eye.bed` for both RMS and HMM:
  - RMS resolved BED
  - RMS unresolved BED
  - HMM final interval BED (`all_trajectories.summit_refinement.bed`)
- Ran the wrapper into `dev/runs/full_chrII/byeye_compare_20260419/`.
- Direct full-chr-II by-eye RMS-vs-HMM interval results:
  - dataset1:
    - RMS resolved vs HMM final: `summit_f1 0.6957 vs 0.7619`,
      `overlap50_f1 0.7826 vs 0.7619`, `jaccard 0.3859 vs 0.1119`
    - RMS unresolved vs resolved: lower `summit_f1` (`0.5714` vs `0.6957`) but
      slightly higher `overlap50_f1` (`0.8182` vs `0.7826`)
  - dataset2:
    - RMS resolved vs HMM final: `summit_f1 0.6667 vs 0.7563`,
      `overlap50_f1 0.7500 vs 0.7563`, `jaccard 0.3771 vs 0.2128`
    - RMS unresolved vs resolved: lower `summit_f1` (`0.5455` vs `0.6667`) but
      slightly higher `overlap50_f1` (`0.7784` vs `0.7500`)
- Interpretation of the corrected interval-side surface:
  - yes, the current RMS by-eye amplicon behavior is much stronger than the old
    lightweight summit-run proxy had implied
  - this is consistent with the step-08 partition dropping collapsed repeats
    from the amplicon output set and the lower `2.5` threshold recovering
    additional amplicon loci
  - however, not every metric moves in the same direction: the resolved outputs
    improve summit hit accounting while the unresolved outputs retain slightly
    better overlap50 F1 in both datasets.
- Audited the fresh summit-regression claim and found that the current
  `dev/runs/summit_precision/*/summary_rcn.json` results are not trustworthy as
  evidence about the new RMS implementation.
- Root cause of the summit confusion:
  - refreshed `summary_rcn.json` files are dated `2026-04-19 20:22`, but their
    `.rcn_calls_path` TSVs under `01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/08-multistage-unification/`
    are still dated `2026-04-17`
  - those stale TSVs still have the old 7-column schema and lack the new
    runtime selector / final-classification metadata
  - `tests/eval_summit_precision_v2.py` therefore falls back to its runtime
    default estimator resolution logic, which prefers `hires_500_mean` when no
    runtime selector label is present
  - so the apparent II/9A change reflects fresh evaluation over stale RMS
    artifacts plus newer evaluator fallback behavior, not a validated summit
    regression caused by the step-08 / threshold changes themselves
- Updated the prior Phase 13 comparison report to state that the current
  summit-side conclusion is provisional/invalid until the stale-artifact path in
  `dev/runs/summit_precision/` is resolved.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 RMS real-data validation / by-eye interval comparison + summit artifact audit

## [v0.13.41] — 2026-04-19 ~19:52 EDT

### Role 2 Implementation — Phase 13 Side Quest Step-08 Final Classification

- Implemented the Phase 13 RMS side-quest step-08 repair in
  `onionskin_core/per_stage_mean_shift_engine.py` and
  `onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`.
- Step 08 no longer unifies passing stage-local calls and shape-filter rejects
  on independent tracks. The final RMS partition is now built from one shared
  interval universe over both contributor types, followed by an explicit
  per-stage state classifier (`1` accepted, `0` reject, `-1` absent).
- Final unified RMS outputs are now non-overlapping by construction:
  - `{out_prefix}_unified_stage_calls.tsv/.bed` contains final `amplicon` and
    `low-confidence-amplicon` intervals.

    `collapsed-repeat` intervals.

  `final_classification`, `stage_state_trail`,
  `rescued_from_shape_reject`, `best_shape_score_raw`,
  `support_stage_count`, `reject_stage_count`, and
  `contributor_stage_count`.
- Preserved the existing RMS summit-selector provenance on unified amplicon
  outputs (`peak`, `peak_default_stage1_best`, `peak_early_parabola_mean`,
  `summit_selector_policy`, `summit_estimator_used`, and selector-support
  fields) so the current summit-evaluation tooling continues to work.
- Added a dedicated final step-08 BED writer for RMS unified outputs instead of
  mutating the generic `_calls_to_bed_domains()` helper. Final BED names now
  encode `{chrom}:{start}-{end}|{final_classification}|{stage_state_trail}` and
  the BED score field carries the raw unmodified `best_shape_score_raw` value.
- Added direct regression coverage in `tests/test_rcn_final_classification.py`
  for rescued intervals, pure collapsed repeats, low-confidence reversion, and
  explicit non-overlap of the final partition.
- Extended `tests/test_pipeline.py` so the RMS selector smoke surface also
  asserts the new unified step-08 metadata columns.

## [v0.13.40] — 2026-04-19 ~02:00 EDT

### Role 2 Planning Sync — Raw Triangle Score Approved for BED Score Field

- Confirmed the shape-score algebra in
  `onionskin_core/refinement.py:dBIC_flat_vs_tri`: for the default non-strict
  mode used by the current shape filter, the score is `BIC(flat) -
  BIC(triangle) = n * log(SSE_flat / SSE_tri)`. More triangular amplicons drive
  `SSE_tri` lower relative to `SSE_flat`, so the score becomes more strongly
  positive.
- Updated `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SIDE_QUEST.md` so the proposed step-08 BED
  score field now carries the raw unmodified `best_shape_score_raw` value for
  both unified amplicons and unified collapsed repeats. The prior clipping /
  integer-scaling language was removed.
- Clarified in the plan that `best_stage1_score_z` is not a decision-driving
  field for this side quest. If it is already naturally available in the final
  unified TSV, it may remain as ancillary upstream provenance, but no new logic
  should be added solely to preserve it.
- Appended a resolution note to
  `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SIDE_QUEST.FEEDBACK.md` so the old collapsed-repeat
  BED-score gap is closed by the user-approved raw triangle-score decision.
- Validation:
  - docs/planning only; no tests run

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 side quest planning — RMS threshold + step-08 final classification

## [v0.13.39] — 2026-04-19 ~01:50 EDT

### Role 2 Planning Sync — BED Score Decision Clarification for Phase 13 Side Quest

- Verified the live meaning of `stage1_score_z` in
  `onionskin_core/detection.py`: it is the stage-1 detection-strength score from
  the multiscale mean-shift scan, computed as the robust-z value of the smoothed
  residual signal at the selected local maximum. It is not a triangle/BIC shape
  score.
- Verified the live BIC-based shape score path in `onionskin_core/refinement.py`:
  `shape_score_raw = dBIC_flat_vs_tri = BIC(flat) - BIC(triangle)`, with positive
  values favoring the triangle model.
- Updated `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SIDE_QUEST.md` so the proposed step-08 BED
  score source is now the triangle BIC shape score (`best_shape_score_raw`) for
  both unified amplicons and unified collapsed repeats, rather than the upstream
  detection score `best_stage1_score_z`.
- The plan now explicitly keeps `best_stage1_score_z` as diagnostic/provenance
  metadata only and defines `best_shape_score_raw` as the maximum contributor
  `shape_score_raw` within the final cluster.
- Because BED6 scores are integer-constrained, the updated plan states that the
  raw triangle BIC score remains in the TSV while the BED score field uses the
  clipped integer encoding `min(max(int(round(best_shape_score_raw)), 0), 1000)`.
- Validation:
  - docs/planning only; no tests run

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 side quest planning — RMS threshold + step-08 final classification

## [v0.13.37] — 2026-04-18 14:22 EDT

### Role 2 Implementation + Analysis — Priority 13.3 Median Rescore and By-Eye Comparison

- Extended `scripts/rcn_summit_diagnostics.py` so the diagnostic lane now emits two explicit
  robust aggregate comparison rows instead of hiding them inside the generic aggregate pool:
  `aggregate_parabola_median` and `aggregate_all_stage_median`.
- Added focused unit coverage in `tests/test_rcn_summit_diagnostics.py` for the new diagnostic
  policy rows.
- Re-scored the existing chr-II RMS summit summaries without rerunning the large parameter
  surface; wrote the new artifact to
  `dev/runs/phase13_rcn_median_rescore_20260418/summit_diagnostics/`.
- Result from the four-label RMS diagnostic set:
  - explicit `aggregate_parabola_median` is respectable but still worse than the current
    runtime-selected point (`ii9a_mean_score 7.25` vs `8.0`; `ii9a_ds2_mean_score 6.0` vs `7.0`)
  - naive `aggregate_all_stage_median` is much worse (`ii9a_mean_score 3.75`; ds2 mean `1.5`)
  - therefore stage-agnostic robust medians do not look like the right next lever for Priority
    13.3.
- Ran a lightweight by-eye chr-II amplicon comparison against
  `tests/full_chrom_training_data/amplicons.by-eye.bed` using existing prior outputs from the
  summit-run labels; wrote the comparison artifact to
  `dev/runs/phase13_byeye_amplicon_compare_20260418/`.
- By-eye result: HMM outperformed RMS on both ds1 and ds2 representative surfaces in interval
  overlap and Jaccard, with the largest gap on ds2 (`overlap50_f1 0.7563` for HMM vs `0.5` for
  RMS). This suggests the current RMS weakness is not just summit placement.
- Validation:
  - `pytest -q tests/test_rcn_summit_diagnostics.py` PASS

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13, Priority 13.3 — Summit refinement (parabola estimator) + II/9A diagnostic

- Renamed the evaluator's selector-specific helper names from `rcn_*` to `rms_*` where they

## [v0.13.32] — 2026-04-18 06:48 EDT

### Role 2 Implementation — Priority 13.3 (Round 6)

- Extended the dedicated rcn summit diagnostic lane to run an explicit selector experiment rather
  than only a generic policy leaderboard.
- `scripts/rcn_summit_diagnostics.py` now writes:
  - `selector_experiment.tsv`
  - `selector_recommendation.json`
  - a new **Selector Experiment** section in the markdown report
  - a new **Selector Recommendation** section in the markdown report
- The selector experiment now compares the current runtime selector against the two most relevant
  stage-local parabola policies:
  - `runtime_selected_point`
  - `stage_best_parabola_any`
  - `stage_best_parabola_early`
- Added a transparent recommendation heuristic for the first policy to test:
  - keep the empirical top policy as the default winner
  - but prefer the biology-constrained early-stage parabola policy when it matches the empirical
    winner on ds2 and stays within a bounded overall-score / ORC-distance gap
- Current selector experiment results from the prior chr-II rcn runs:
  - `runtime_selected_point`: mean II/9A score `6.0`, mean ds2 II/9A score `3.5`
  - `stage_best_parabola_any`: mean II/9A score `8.5`, mean ds2 II/9A score `7.0`
  - `stage_best_parabola_early`: mean II/9A score `7.75`, mean ds2 II/9A score `7.0`
- Current recommendation emitted by the artifact:
  - empirical top policy: `stage_best_parabola_any`
  - recommended first policy to test: `stage_best_parabola_early`
  - rule: `biology-constrained-close-call`
- Interpretation: the diagnostic surface now supports testing a narrow selector-policy change
  before any broad parameter sweep.

  full 14-step directory family, including step-level layout keys and reserved future dirs.

  canonical runtime status strings, and the standalone `_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller()` path.

## [v0.13.17] — 2026-04-17 ~20:45 EDT

### Role 1 Pre-Implementation Audit — Priority 13.1 (Round 1)

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13, Priority 13.1 — Rename + 14-step layout + unified controller

Role 1 initial audit of `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md` § Priority 13.1 against live
codebase. All 18 scope items verified; 7 findings appended with repair instructions.
`AUDIT_HISTORY.md` updated.

**Findings:**

- **FIND-R1-1 (CRITICAL):** Spec item 2 merge instruction incorrect. `run_single_stage()` is
  a growth-model single-stage helper (`onionskin.py:3098, 3281` write to
  `02-growth-model/02-calls/`), not a per-stage controller function. Must not be merged into
  `run_rcn_mean_shift()`. Corrected: rename only `run_per_stage_mean_shift()` →
  `run_rcn_mean_shift()`; keep `run_single_stage()` separate. Complete 4-function import
  replacement block written into audit.

- **FIND-R1-2 (HIGH):** `_per_stage_one()` private helper not addressed in spec item 2. This
  function takes `out_dir: str` and routes all 8 file writes internally. Full routing table
  (stage-medians, detection, summits, shape-filter, summit-refinement, deduplication) and
  updated signature added as explicit repair instructions. Pre-dedup/post-dedup split write
  pattern specified. Unified call writes in `run_rcn_mean_shift()` routed to
  `multistage-unification/`. `os.makedirs(out_dir)` removal noted.

- **FIND-R1-3 (HIGH):** Spec item 5 import block shows only `run_rcn_mean_shift` but live
  `onionskin.py:24–29` imports 4 functions (`run_single_stage`, `run_per_stage_mean_shift`,
  `apply_shape_filter_tsv`, `apply_peak_proximity_dedup_tsv`). Dropping the others causes
  `NameError`. Covered by FIND-R1-1 repair.

- **FIND-R1-4 (HIGH):** `build_rcn_mean_shift_steps` missing from `onionskin.py` output_layout
  import block (`onionskin.py:33–38`). Controller call to `build_rcn_mean_shift_steps()` will
  fail at runtime without this import. Corrected import block written into audit.

- **FIND-R1-5 (MEDIUM):** `os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)` at
  `per_stage_mean_shift_engine.py:884` not called out for removal. Removal instruction added.

- **FIND-R1-6 (MEDIUM):** PIPELINE_SPEC.md sweep (item 9) needs explicit guard against
  renaming biological "per-stage" terms (61 total occurrences, majority analytical not pipeline
  names). Confirmed pipeline-name lines requiring update: 20, 21, 27, 33, 37, 146–150, 153,
  658. Guard instruction added.

- **FIND-R1-7 (LOW):** `output_layout.py` module docstring (line 32) contains
  `03-per-stage-mean-shift/` — not covered by item 1's `_INDEX_MD_TEMPLATE` scope. Update
  instruction added to item 1.

**Confirmed accurate (no action):** Items 3–4, 5 (all targets except import gaps), 6–7, 11–13,
15–18. Makefile has zero per-stage references. notebooks.py has zero pipeline-name references.
AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions.md, CLAUDE.md are clean. All line numbers verified against live
code.

### PHASE13_SPEC.md Round 4 response — FIND-R4-1 through FIND-R4-3 resolved + additional self-audit gaps; spec ready

- Verified all three Round 4 audit findings against live code before editing.
- **FIND-R4-1 (test surface):** Fully confirmed and resolved.
  - Fixed the wrong import module: spec said `onionskin_core`; live file imports `_resolve_per_stage_norm_mode` from `onionskin`.
  - Expanded Priority 13.1 item 6 to name every rename-sensitive test assertion in `tests/test_pipeline.py`: help group label, `eligible_pipelines` tuples, `_resolve_requested_effective_pipelines` result, `_pipelines_include("per-stage")` call, runtime `per-stage=chrom-median`/`per-stage=ref-stage` status strings, error string, and the two `per_stage_dir = prior / "03-per-stage-mean-shift"` path variables.
  - Added explicit decision on legacy-alias tests: keep existing `--pipelines per-stage` invocations as alias-coverage tests; add new canonical-name tests.
  - Expanded item 18 to explicitly name the `eval_summit_precision_v2.py` module-level usage string (line 21).
- **FIND-R4-2 (plot contract):** Fully confirmed and resolved.
  - Fixed Priority 13.6 target state: replaced `{prefix}_stage{st}_profile.png` and `{prefix}_genome_overview.png` with the actual helper outputs `profiles/profile_{call_id}` and `genome_overview/chrom_{chrom}`; renamed `summit_fit/` → `summit_fits/`.
  - Fixed the `write_summit_plots()` implementation snippet: now passes `plots_dir=rcn_ms_steps["plots"]` (the helper adds `summit_fits/` internally; passing a nested subdir would double-nest).
  - Fixed the `calls_tsv` source: N≥2 → unified calls from step-08; N=1 → per-stage post-dedup calls from step-05.
  - Updated validation to match actual helper output paths.
- **FIND-R4-3 (notebook gating):** Fully confirmed and resolved.
  - Fixed Priority 13.7 validation: single-stage run cannot emit a gap notebook because gap analysis is downstream of `08-multistage-unification/`, which is only populated when N≥2. Corrected to: gap notebook absent for N=1; APS notebook may be present if ≥1 non-ref stage; `14-notebook/` empty only when neither prerequisite is met.
- **Additional self-audit gaps resolved (beyond Round 4 findings):**
  - `_default_pipeline_norm_mode()`: added explicit note that its `if pipeline == "per-stage"` branches must change to `"rcn-mean-shift"`. Without this, `_resolve_rcn_mean_shift_norm_mode()` would silently return wrong defaults.
  - `_pipelines_include()` function body: added explicit note that the branch must accept both `"rcn-mean-shift"` and `"per-stage"` (legacy) in `--pipelines`, with a specified return expression.
  - `_resolve_requested_effective_pipelines()`, `_resolve_controller_norm_mode()`, `_validate_pipeline_norm_contract()`: all now explicitly listed in item 5.
  - Two HMM help-text lines (`"now shared with the multistage/per-stage lanes."`) and the `_pipelines_include()` docstring: now explicitly called out.

## [v0.13.14] — 2026-04-17 ~10:47 EDT
### Copilot Round 4 audit of `PHASE13_SPEC.md` — three final contract-accuracy findings remain

- Performed a fourth read-only audit of `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md`, focused on whether
  Claude's v0.13.13 fixes fully aligned the spec with the live test surface and helper-module
  contracts.
- Appended the Round 4 findings to the bottom of `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_BRAINSTORM.md`.
- Audit conclusion: the spec is **very close but still not ready to move on**. No missing
  Priority or dependency-order problem was found, but three contract-accuracy issues remain:
  1. **FIND-R4-1 (High):** Priority 13.1 still under-specifies the live rename-sensitive test
     surface. The `tests/test_pipeline.py` checklist misses several assertions that will change
     under the public rename, and one explicit import reference in the spec names the wrong module
     (`onionskin_core` instead of `onionskin`). The `tests/eval_summit_precision*.py` sweep also
     still omits the live `--pipeline ... per-stage` usage/help surface.
  2. **FIND-R4-2 (Medium):** Priority 13.6 still does not match the real output contract of the
     plot helpers it plans to reuse. `profile_plots.py` and `summit_plots.py` currently write into
     `profiles/`, `genome_overview/chrom_{chrom}.png`, and `summit_fits/`, while the spec still
     describes different target/validation paths and includes one contradictory `calls_tsv`
     example.

     gating rules by claiming a single-stage run should emit a gap notebook even though gap
     analysis is defined downstream of step-08 multistage unification.
- After these three fixes, Copilot would consider the spec ready to move on to the next phase of
  the audit/implement loop.

## [v0.13.13] — 2026-04-17 ~19:00 EDT
### PHASE13_SPEC.md Round 3 response — 3 Copilot findings + 2 own-audit gaps resolved; spec complete

Claude verified each of Copilot's Round 3 findings against the live codebase before applying changes.
All three were confirmed. Two additional gaps surfaced during Claude's own comprehensive sweep.

**FIND-R3-1 (High — public rename surfaces underspecified):** Confirmed `--per-stage-norm-mode`,
`_resolve_per_stage_norm_mode()`, the argparse group label, `--pipelines` help text, runtime status
strings, and error/warning strings are all live surfaces not previously named in the spec. **Fixed:**
Priority 13.1 item 5 now explicitly lists: `--per-stage-norm-mode` → `--rcn-mean-shift-norm-mode`
(legacy alias kept), argparse `dest` rename, `_resolve_per_stage_norm_mode()` rename, group label,
help text, and runtime/error string updates. Item 6 (tests) updated to cover the import,
`"--per-stage-norm-mode" in out` assertion, and `_resolve_per_stage_norm_mode()` call sites
(lines 185–219, 334). Item 9 (PIPELINE_SPEC.md Appendix A) updated to include the flag rename.

**FIND-R3-2 (Medium — `plots.py` doesn't exist):** Confirmed. The repo uses split modules:
`profile_plots.py` → `write_profile_plots()`, `summit_plots.py` → `write_summit_plots()`,
`aps_plots.py` → `write_aps_plots()` (called from inside `aps.py`, not from engine). **Fixed:**
Priority 13.6 current-state section states no `plots.py` exists and names the real modules with
concrete parameter signatures. Additional discovery: APS plots are routed via the `aps_plots_dir`
parameter in `aps.py`; this coordination note added to Priority 13.5 so implementer knows to pass
`aps_plots_dir=rcn_ms_steps["plots"] + "/aps/"` at APS compute time.

**FIND-R3-3 (Medium — notebook contract diverges from family convention):** Confirmed. Growth and
HMM notebooks use stable unprefixed filenames (`aps_exploration.ipynb`) and `(notebook_dir: str)`-
only signatures. **Fixed:** Priority 13.7 target state changed from `{prefix}_aps_exploration.ipynb`
→ `aps_exploration.ipynb`. Function signatures updated to `write_rcn_mean_shift_aps_notebook(
notebook_dir: str)` pattern. Added `write_rcn_mean_shift_notebooks(notebook_dir)` wrapper matching
`write_notebooks()` / `write_hmm_phase9_notebooks()` family pattern.

**Own-audit FIND-C2 (summit_inspector.py missing surfaces):** Lines 1202 (`--inspect-pipelines`
help text) and line 363 (stale skip reason) were not in the spec. **Fixed:** Item 7 now covers
all four per-stage surfaces in `summit_inspector.py` including those two.

**Own-audit FIND-C3 (`generated_run_docs_audit_prompt.md`):** Lines 76/149 of the active audit
prompt say "per-stage side-pipeline outputs". **Fixed:** Added to item 13 scope.

## [v0.13.12] — 2026-04-17 ~18:15 EDT
### Copilot Round 3 final sign-off audit of `PHASE13_SPEC.md` — three final conformance findings remain

- Performed a third read-only audit of `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md`, this time focused on
  final implementation-readiness against live codebase conventions rather than brainstorm parity.
- Appended the Round 3 findings to the bottom of `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_BRAINSTORM.md`.
- Audit conclusion: the spec is **close but not yet ready for implementation sign-off**. The
  priority order still looks sound and no new architectural gaps were found, but three final
  conformance issues remain:
  1. **FIND-R3-1 (High):** public-facing rename scope is still underspecified beyond
     `--pipelines per-stage`; the spec does not yet explicitly decide what happens to
     `--per-stage-norm-mode`, help-section titles, runtime/status strings, and the matching
     doc/test surfaces.
  2. **FIND-R3-2 (Medium):** Priority 13.6 still points at a non-existent
     `onionskin_core/plots.py` instead of the real split plotting modules
     (`profile_plots.py`, `summit_plots.py`, `aps_plots.py`, etc.).
  3. **FIND-R3-3 (Medium):** Priority 13.7 proposes prefixed notebook filenames and a notebook
     output contract that diverges from the existing growth/HMM notebook-family convention of
     stable, unprefixed filenames written into the notebook directory.
- No new dependency-order problems were found; after these three fixes, Copilot would consider the
  spec ready for implementation.

## [v0.13.11] — 2026-04-17 ~17:30 EDT
### PHASE13_SPEC.md Round 2 response — 5 findings addressed, Priority 13.7 (notebooks) added, renumbered to 9 Priorities

Claude evaluated each of Copilot's Round 2 findings against the live codebase before applying
any changes. One finding was a false positive; all others were confirmed and resolved.

**FIND-R2-1 (14-step count consistency):** Copilot flagged "stale 14-step" wording in a
spec that had been reduced to 13 steps. Root cause analysis: rather than a blind find-replace,
the correct fix was to restore step 14 as `14-notebook/` (which also resolves the user advisory
about missing notebook outputs). Layout is back to 14 steps; all "13-step" spec language is gone.

**FIND-R2-2 (additional rename surfaces):** Confirmed `autodetect.py` (3 `eligible_pipelines`
call sites), `detection.py` (import + call of `load_reference_per_stage_mean_shift`), and
`__init__.py` (export of `build_rcn_mean_shift_steps` missing) are real gaps. **Added as items
15–17 in Priority 13.1 scope.** `readme.py` was a false positive — thorough grep found zero
per-stage references there.

**FIND-R2-3 (test/tooling sweep):** Confirmed 4 additional files with functional per-stage
references: `tests/run_toy_single.sh` (lines 56, 59), `tests/eval_summit_precision.py` (line 33
+ lines 158/169/171), `eval_summit_precision_v1.py`, `eval_summit_precision_v2.py`, and
`tests/UTILITY_SCOPE.md` (line 43 heading). **Added as item 18 in Priority 13.1 scope.**

**FIND-R2-4 (posterior artifact contract):** Confirmed `posterior_manifest_cluster_map.tsv`
written at `posterior.py:147` and `posterior_hires_manifest_{i}.tsv` at `onionskin.py:2813`.
**Priority 13.9 (was 13.8) target state and validation updated** to explicitly document both
sidecars; implementation notes confirm these are already emitted by shared machinery.

**User advisory — notebooks:** `14-notebook/` step added to layout (step 14, reserved in 13.1).
**New Priority 13.7 added:** implements APS exploration and gap analysis notebooks for
rcn-mean-shift — counterparts to `write_aps_exploration_notebook()` and
`write_gap_analysis_notebook()` in `notebooks.py`. Growth-specific notebooks (growth curves,
timing, fork progression) explicitly excluded as not applicable to rcn-mean-shift.

**Priority renumbering:** Old 13.7 (QC) → 13.8. Old 13.8 (Posterior) → 13.9. Phase now has
9 Priorities total.

## [v0.13.09] — 2026-04-17 ~16:00 EDT
### PHASE13_SPEC.md revised after Copilot audit — three findings addressed plus conformance review

Copilot audited `PHASE13_SPEC.md` (v0.13.08) and found three material issues. Claude evaluated
each finding against the live code, confirmed all three, and identified one additional conformance
gap from reading `_run_posterior()` directly.

**FIND-S1 (High — posterior placement):** The spec placed posterior outputs as `14-posterior/`
inside the prior `03-rcn-mean-shift/` step tree. The correct pipeline-family pattern (verified
against `_run_posterior()` in `onionskin.py`) is: posterior outputs live under the top-level
`02-posterior/03-rcn-mean-shift/` grouping, using the same 13-step layout rooted under
`02-posterior/`. The posterior manifest is written to `02-posterior/posterior_manifest.tsv`
(grouping_dir), matching growth and HMM. **Fixed:** removed step 14 from the prior tree,
updated layout table to 13 steps, rewrote Priority 13.8 with the correct target state,
directory diagram, and implementation scope.

**FIND-S2 (Medium — APS per-sample normalization contract):** "Apply `_chrom_median_norm()`
(or equivalent)" was imprecise. **Fixed:** the contract is now explicit — load each replicate
sample's raw bedGraph independently; apply `_chrom_median_norm(bins, raw_vals)` directly
(no aggregation, no fold-change vs reference); write to `indiv_samples/`. Phrase "or equivalent"
removed.

**FIND-S3 (Medium — amplicon metrics input source):** Overview said "downstream of unified
calls" (correct) but implementation said "after shape filtering step 06" (wrong). **Fixed:**
`10-amplicon-metrics/` is fed from unified calls (step 08 output), with per-stage attributes
joined from in-memory shape-filter data. One row per unified amplicon. TSV schema updated.

**FIND-C1 (Claude-identified — `_run_posterior()` current state):** Code audit confirmed that
`_run_posterior()` already creates `02-posterior/03-per-stage-mean-shift/` dirs but never calls
the per-stage/rcn-mean-shift engine on `post_layout`. Priority 13.8 now explicitly specifies
extending `_run_posterior()` with an rcn-mean-shift branch gated on `_pipelines_include(args,
"rcn-mean-shift")`.

**Files changed:** `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_SPEC.md`  
**Tests run:** None (planning deliverable)

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 13 — Per-stage pipeline restructuring and expansion

  (3 triage rounds, fully resolved).

  to `11-plots/`, APS to `12-aps/`, clustering to `13-clustering/`, posterior to `14-posterior/`.
- **8 Priorities (13.1–13.8):** doc/guidance cleanup merged into Priority 13.1 (no dependency
  reason to keep separate), collapsing the original 9-Priority brainstorm scope to 8.
- **Priority 13.1** (largest): `per-stage` → `rcn-mean-shift` rename everywhere + 14-step layout
  + unified `run_rcn_mean_shift()` controller + doc/guidance/inspector sweep. Scope covers
  `output_layout.py` (`build_rcn_mean_shift_steps()`), `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` (renamed +
  merged), `engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` (renamed), `common.py`, `onionskin.py` (controller
  renamed, steps dict passed), `tests/test_pipeline.py`, `summit_inspector.py`, `README.md`,
  `PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `_INDEX_MD_TEMPLATE`, `Makefile`, `notebooks.py`, agent instruction files,
  stale historical reference annotation.

  `04-chromMedRatioNorm-RCN` → `04-chrom-renorm`, `*_unionBedGraphStats.txt` →
  `*_positional-bin-stats.txt`.
- **Priority 13.3:** Summit refinement — parabola estimator wired to `07-summit-refinement/`;
  II/9A + II/2B diagnostic.
- **Priority 13.4:** Gap analysis (`09-gap-analysis/`) + amplicon metrics (`10-amplicon-metrics/`).
- **Priority 13.5:** Per-sample normalization (`02-chrom-norm/indiv_samples/`) + APS (`12-aps/`)
  + clustering (`13-clustering/`). Includes mandatory HMM gating rule audit checkpoint.
- **Priority 13.6:** Plots (`11-plots/`) — profile, APS, summit-fit, genome-overview.
- **Priority 13.7:** Per-stage QC / parameter tuning — ground truth comparison using
  `tests/full_chrom_training_data/amplicons.by-eye.bed` and PuffStep HMM BED.
- **Priority 13.8:** Posterior (`14-posterior/`) — requires 13.5; mandatory blocker audit first.
- **`hires-{bin}/` note:** created at runtime by engine, not pre-created by `ensure_layout_dirs()`.
- **`02-chrom-norm/indiv_samples/hires-{bin}/` explicitly NOT needed:** hires is aggregate-only
  for summit refinement; APS uses per-sample base-resolution tracks.

  and filled — `Makefile` and `notebooks.py` were not named in Priority 13.1 rename sweep

## [v0.13.06] — 2026-04-17
### Phase 13 brainstorm Round 3 triage complete
- `multi-agent/plans/PHASE13_BRAINSTORM.md` updated with Claude's final triage after Copilot
  and Gemini addenda, resolving all outstanding questions and expanding the Phase 13 scope.
- **Pipeline rename confirmed:** `per-stage` → `rcn-mean-shift` (internal and public-facing).
  Directory `03-per-stage-mean-shift/` → `03-rcn-mean-shift/`; CLI token `--pipelines per-stage`
  → `--pipelines rcn-mean-shift` (with legacy alias); engine, layout keys, log tags, tests,
  inspector, README, PIPELINE_SPEC all renamed.

  `01-hmm/` → `02-growth-model/` → `03-rcn-mean-shift/`. Gemini found stale pre-Phase-12 text
  in historical docs, not a code defect. Stale references will be annotated in doc cleanup pass.

  even if initially empty. Prevents renumbering as pipeline gets meatier.

  handling 1-to-N stages with the same 13-step routing logic.

  pass at step 02; stage-level tracks remain for calling; `indiv_samples/` subdir feeds
  APS/clustering/posterior.

  calls TSV + BED; `03-detection/` holds pre-dedup TSV + BED.
- **Doc/guidance/inspector cleanup** promoted to explicit named Priority (13.7) — not an implied
  side effect.
- **Phase 13 now has 9 Priorities:** rename+layout, HMM prettification, summit refinement, gap
  analysis+metrics, per-sample norm+APS+clustering, plots, doc cleanup, QC/parameter tuning,
  posterior.

## [v0.12.23] — 2026-04-16 ~14:21 EDT

Role 1 post-wrap-up triage of Phase 12 Final Overseer findings.

Reviewed FIND-W1, FIND-W2, FIND-W3 against live code and triaged into actionable vs. non-actionable:

- **FIND-W1 (active repair):** Confirmed real bug in `onionskin_core/readme.py` lines 51–53.
  `_write_output_readme()` passes relative paths from `out_dir` (e.g. `02-posterior/posterior_manifest.tsv`),
  but the three posterior-manifest boolean checks compare against bare filenames, so all three
  always return `False` on Phase-12-layout posterior runs. Fix: replace equality/startswith checks
  with `Path(path).name` basename matching. `Path` already imported.

- **FIND-W2 (not actionable — false positive):** Verified that the "stale" content Codex
  identified at HANDOFF.md lines 346–369 is the intentional historical Last Action log (running
  log by design) and lines 676–749 are labeled `## Historical Snapshot (v0.12.02 session close)`
  (explicitly historical). The `## Current State` and `## Next Action` sections ARE current.
  No repair needed.

## [v0.12.18] — 2026-04-16 ~23:30 EDT

Role 1 feasibility audit for Priority 12.4 (HMM smoothed RCN reuse).
Investigation only — no code changes. **NOT FEASIBLE** conclusion.

Compatibility matrix written into `multi-agent/plans/PHASE12_SPEC.md`.
Four dimensions assessed:

1. **Smoothing algorithm** — `--hmm-smooth-halfwidth` drives `local_median_smooth_bedgraph()`
   (median); `--growth-rcn-smooth-bins` drives `smooth_mean()` (mean). Same units and defaults
   (halfwidth=3 → 7-bin window), different algorithms.
2. **Zero-bin / missing-bin semantics** — HMM step-3 removes zero bins from per-stage group
   median bedGraphs before normalization. Growth applies inline per-sample zero-masking on the
   Y matrix (≥90% zero/non-finite threshold). Not equivalent.
3. **Producer shape vs consumer need (hard blocker)** — HMM steps 2-5 collapse per-sample
   tracks to per-stage group aggregates at step-2 (`_step2_unionstats`); step-5 outputs are
   one smoothed RCN bedGraph per non-reference stage. Growth `load_tracks_from_manifest()`
   requires one bedGraph per individual sample. Fundamental shape mismatch — no substitution
   is possible without restructuring both pipelines.
4. **KNOWN_ISSUES step-14 ordering** — no ordering conflict introduced by Priority 12.3
   HMM-first order; step-14 normalization issue is orthogonal.

Long-horizon path added to `BRAINSTORM.md` as a future architecture idea (unified per-sample
smoothed RCN intermediates shared between pipelines). Priority 12.4 closed.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 12.4 — CLOSED (infeasible)

---

## [v0.12.15] — 2026-04-16 ~22:30 EDT

Role 1 initial audit of Priority 12.3 (Pipeline execution order HMM → Growth → Per-Stage).
Status: OPEN. 7 findings written into PHASE12_SPEC.md. Nothing has been implemented yet.

Key architectural confirmation: `_run_hmm_engine()` has no dependency on any growth output
(`growth_model_dir`, `calls_dir`, etc.); it is guarded by `_pipelines_include(args, "hmm")`
so inserting it early is always a no-op when HMM is not requested.

All 7 findings are in `onionskin.py` only. No test, script, or other file changes expected.

FIND-1 (main multistage+growth branch, lines 2979–3056): HMM at line 3049 runs after
per-stage, after `_run_posterior()`, and AFTER posterior HMM. Move to before `run_multistage`
at line 3011.
FIND-2 (multistage+per-stage only branch, lines 3058–3076): per-stage controller called with
`run_hmm_after=True` (default) → HMM runs after per-stage. Add `_run_hmm_engine()` call
before the `return _run_standalone_per_stage_controller(...)` and pass `run_hmm_after=False`.
FIND-3 (single-stage branch, lines 3078–3119): HMM at line 3112 after growth+phase2. Move to
before `run_single_stage()` at line 3088.
FIND-4 (single-file manifest path, lines 3121–3163): HMM at line 3156 after growth+phase2.
Move to before `run_singlefile_caller()` at line 3132.
FIND-5 (--onionskin single-file, lines 3222–3260): HMM at line 3254 after growth+phase2.
Move to before `run_singlefile_caller()` at line 3234.
FIND-6 (--onionskin 1/2-stage, lines 3262–3301): HMM at line 3295 after growth+phase2.
Move to before `run_single_stage()` at line 3272.
FIND-7 (`_run_posterior()`, lines 2774–2882): HMM block (manifest copy + engine) at
lines 2867–2881 runs after growth+phase2+QC. HMM manifest is a copy of `posterior_manifest`
which is fully built before either pipeline runs — move block before `run_multistage()`.

7-step implementation order with exact move instructions + post-implementation grep check
documented in PHASE12_SPEC.md. Validation: `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`, `make twin`.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

## [v0.12.10] — 2026-04-16 ~20:00 EDT

Role 1 initial audit of Priority 12.2 (Growth pipeline 15-step layout). Status: OPEN.

**FIND-1 (`output_layout.py` — 5 sub-findings):** `build_growth_steps()` absent; old dir
names still present in `build_output_layout()` (`01-stage_median_within_calls`, `02-summary_bedgraphs`,
`03-summits`, `04-summit_refinement`, `05-aps`, `06-plots`, `07-notebook`); `ensure_layout_dirs()`
missing all 15 new step dirs; `_INDEX_MD_TEMPLATE` still shows root files; `organize_outputs()`
still exists (to be retired).

**FIND-2 (`growth_model_engine.py` — architecture-critical):** Engine is argv-based
(`run_multistage(argv)` → `mod.main(list(argv))`); cannot accept dict parameter without
interface change. 7 flat-prefix writes need step-dir flags; 3 hardcoded subdir names need
renaming. Required solution documented: add 7 `--dir-*` argv flags to engine argparse
(`--dir-growth-track`, `--dir-calls`, `--dir-origins`, `--dir-progression`,
`--dir-stage-medians`, `--dir-summits`, `--dir-summit-refinement`) and update
`_build_ms_argv()` to pass them from `growth_steps`.

**FIND-3 (`onionskin.py` — 10 sub-findings):** Missing `build_growth_steps` import; ~26
`_layout_growth_model_{path,prefix}()` call sites to retire; `_apply_multistage_shape_filter()`
flat-prefix signature; `_finalize_amplicon_recommendations()` target dir; `--resolve-overlaps`
argparse + guard to remove; `_write_run_meta()` update; `_check_prior_complete()` hardcoded
`"05-aps"` path; posterior manifest location (`growth_model_dir` → `grouping_dir`); QC summary
location; `_build_ms_argv()` additions.

**FIND-4 through FIND-11:** test and script consumers — `tests/test_pipeline.py` (7 assertion
paths to step dirs), `tests/test_aps.py` (`--resolve-overlaps` removal + `05-aps` → `13-aps`),
`tests/eval_summit_precision*.py` (all 3 files — `_discover_summit_bed()` path ascent logic),
`scripts/summit_inspector.py` (`04-origins`, `13-aps`, `14-plots`), `scripts/aps_cluster_report.py`
(5 stale dir names), `onionskin_core/notebooks.py` (hardcoded `"05-aps"` + root-level glob),
`onionskin_core/readme.py` (minor comment), implementation order documented (6 steps).

## [v0.12.05] — 2026-04-16 ~16:30 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 12 spec — post-Copilot-audit corrections

### Phase 12 spec corrections after Copilot implementation-readiness audit

Copilot (GPT-5.4) performed an implementation-readiness audit of `PHASE12_SPEC.md` and
identified four concrete contract issues. All four accepted and corrected.

**Finding 1 — HMM step-3/step-4 path mismatch in `build_hmm_steps()` example:**
Verified against live `hmm_engine.py`. Corrected `03-zeroBinMasking` → `03-removeZeroBins`
and `04-rcn` → `04-chromMedRatioNorm-RCN`. Also added missing `"notebooks"` key that is
present in the live dict. Removed the "verify before committing" qualifier since the spec
now contains the verified live names.

**Finding 2 — `_layout_growth_model_path/prefix` helper retirement not explicit enough:**
Code audit confirmed these helpers are called from ~15 sites spanning every output family.
The original spec said "redirect to `02-calls/`" which would have re-created the flat-prefix
model inside a step-numbered tree. Corrected to: retire both helpers entirely. Spec now
enumerates the per-family step-dir mapping explicitly and also covers `_write_run_meta()`,
which persists the prefix for posterior reuse. Both functions to be deleted.

**Finding 3 — Shape-filter output ownership ambiguity:**
Code audit confirmed: `_apply_multistage_shape_filter()` in `onionskin.py` (lines 290–329)
is the sole writer of `*_shape_scores_multistage.tsv` and `*_amplicons_actively_rejected*.bed`.
The engine has never written these. Removed "Step 03 — shape filter" from the engine refactor
section; added explicit ownership note. The `onionskin.py` item now specifies reading from
`growth_steps["calls"]` and writing to `growth_steps["shape-filter"]`.

**Finding 4 — Posterior per-stage scope overstatement:**
Code audit confirmed: `_run_posterior()` (lines 2735–2840) runs growth + HMM only — no
posterior per-stage lane exists and the CLI rejects `--pipelines per-stage --posterior`.
Priority 12.3 now has a separate posterior target-order block (HMM → growth only), and
branch 6 explicitly states that adding posterior per-stage is out of scope.

Claude's response appended to `multi-agent/plans/PHASE12_BRAINSTORM.md`.
Validation: documentation/spec audit only; no code changes; no tests run.

## [v0.12.03] — 2026-04-16 ~15:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 12 spec complete — pre-implementation

### Phase 12 spec written (`multi-agent/plans/PHASE12_SPEC.md`)

Triaged the Copilot and Gemini addendums appended to `multi-agent/plans/PHASE12_BRAINSTORM.md`
(v0.12.01–v0.12.02) and produced the final `PHASE12_SPEC.md` with four dependency-ordered
Priorities, then performed a full self-audit of the spec against the brainstorm.

**Triage of Copilot and Gemini addendums (appended to `PHASE12_BRAINSTORM.md`):**

All six Copilot items accepted and integrated:
- [CP-1] Three additional hidden consumers added to scope: `tests/eval_summit_precision*.py`
  (origins path discovery breaks when `_origins.tsv` moves to `04-origins/`),
  `tests/test_aps.py` (dual breakage: APS path rename `05-aps` → `13-aps` AND
  `--resolve-overlaps` flag removal), and `onionskin_core/notebooks.py` (discovery globs
  break silently when root-level `*_calls.tsv` etc. move into step dirs).
- [CP-2] `organize_outputs()` retirement made an explicit spec deliverable (retire, not bridge).
- [CP-3] Posterior manifest files (`posterior_manifest.tsv`, `posterior_hires_manifest_*.tsv`)
  given a formal exception: moved up to the grouping dir level (`02-posterior/`) rather than
  inside the growth pipeline container — only permitted exception to "no root files in growth arm."
- [CP-4] Posterior branch now explicitly enumerated in execution-order reorder (not assumed).
- [CP-5] Exact-path vs wildcard consumer triage framework added as implementation guide.
- [CP-6] BRAINSTORM-C audit deliverable refined to a structured 4-dimension compatibility matrix.

Gemini items G-1 (engine file renames), G-3 (pre-wiring indiv_samples/ paths), and G-4
(0-based statepath flag) rejected: G-1 was already done in Phase 11 (stale Gemini context);
G-3 adds dead code for unimplemented feature; G-4 is out of scope for Phase 12.
Gemini G-2 (--resolve-overlaps CLI purge) partially accepted — subsumed by CP-1 since the
concrete new finding (test_aps.py line 28) was already accepted under that item.

**`PHASE12_SPEC.md` — four Priorities in dependency order:**

- **Priority 12.1** (BRAINSTORM-F): HMM steps dict migration to `output_layout.py`. Pure
  refactor; establishes `output_layout.py` as canonical path authority for both pipelines.
  Adds `build_hmm_steps(hmm_out_dir)`, removes internal construction from `hmm_engine.py`,
  passes dict from `onionskin.py`. HMM step paths and behavior unchanged. External script audit
  pass for hardcoded HMM step dir names.

- **Priority 12.2** (BRAINSTORM-A + BRAINSTORM-D + all Copilot items): Full growth pipeline
  15-step layout. Engine refactored to write steps 01–05 directly to numbered step dirs via
  `growth_steps` dict (constructed in `output_layout.py`, passed from controller). New
  `build_growth_steps(growth_model_dir)` added. All existing subdirs renumbered (01 → 06
  through 07 → 15). `organize_outputs()` deleted. `--resolve-overlaps` removed from argparse,
  all guards removed, overlap resolution mandatory. Posterior manifests moved to grouping dir.
  All exact-path consumers updated atomically: `test_pipeline.py`, `test_aps.py` (dual fix),
  `eval_summit_precision*.py`, `summit_inspector.py`, `aps_cluster_report.py`, `notebooks.py`,
  plus audit pass on lower-priority scripts.

- **Priority 12.3** (BRAINSTORM-B + Copilot CP-4): Pipeline execution order reordered to
  HMM → Growth → Per-Stage across all prior and posterior branches in `onionskin.py`.
  All 6 affected branches enumerated, including `_run_posterior()` explicitly.

- **Priority 12.4** (BRAINSTORM-C + Copilot CP-6): HMM smoothed RCN reuse feasibility audit.
  No code changes. Deliverable: structured 4-dimension compatibility matrix covering smoothing
  parameters, zero-bin semantics, producer/consumer shape mismatch, and KNOWN_ISSUES interaction.

**Self-audit of spec vs. brainstorm:** All brainstorm items accounted for. One implementation
ambiguity flagged: shape-filter outputs appear in both the engine refactor section (step 03)
and `_apply_multistage_shape_filter()` in `onionskin.py` — both point to the same target dir
(`03-shape-filter/`); implementer should read the actual code to determine which module
currently writes those files. No brainstorm items missing from spec.

## [v0.12.00] — 2026-04-15 ~22:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Sonnet 4.6

**Roadmap:** Phase 12 brainstorm and design

### Phase 12 brainstorm complete — growth pipeline restructuring design

Created `multi-agent/plans/PHASE12_BRAINSTORM.md` via a deep-dive codebase audit plus search
of `BRAINSTORM.md` and `KNOWN_ISSUES.md`. Documents three Phase 12 priorities and their full
implementation scope, design decisions, and consumer impact inventories.

**Three Phase 12 priorities:**

**Priority A — Growth pipeline step formalization (15 numbered steps, no root files).**
The existing flat-prefix `02-growth-model/` root files and `01-`–`07-` subdirs are replaced
with a clean 15-step numbered layout mirroring HMM's 16-step structure. The current `01-detection`
concept is atomized into 5 discrete engine sub-steps matching HMM's pre-analysis granularity:

| Step | Dir | HMM analog |
|------|-----|------------|
| 01 | `01-growth-track` | `05-medianSmoothed-RCN` |
| 02 | `02-calls` | `06-HMM` |
| 03 | `03-shape-filter` | `08-aboveBackground` |
| 04 | `04-origins` | `09-summitStates` / `10-summitBins` |
| 05 | `05-progression` | _(no analog)_ |
| 06 | `06-stage-medians` | _(internal intermediate)_ |
| 07 | `07-signal-tracks` | — |
| 08 | `08-summits` | — |
| 09 | `09-summit-refinement` | `13-summit-refinement` |
| 10 | `10-timing` | `15-timing` |
| 11 | `11-overlapResolution` | — |
| 12 | `12-gap-analysis` | — |
| 13 | `13-aps` | `14-aps` |
| 14 | `14-plots` | — |
| 15 | `15-notebook` | — |

`--resolve-overlaps` flag removed; overlap resolution is mandatory (no use case for skipping).
Naming convention: hyphens, short readable names; `11-overlapResolution` follows HMM camelCase style.
Engine refactoring required: `growth_model_engine.py` must write each step's outputs directly to its
numbered dir (not flat-prefix + post-hoc move). A `growth_steps` dict will live in `output_layout.py`,
parallel to HMM's `steps` dict.

**Priority B — Pipeline execution order (HMM → Growth → Per-Stage).**
Current execution order (Growth → Per-Stage → HMM) is inverted relative to the directory numbering
(`01-hmm`, `02-growth-model`, `03-per-stage-mean-shift`). All execution branches in `onionskin.py`
reordered to HMM first, then growth, then per-stage. Dependency analysis confirms no pipeline
cross-dependencies prevent this reorder. Priority B is a prerequisite for Priority C.

**Priority C — HMM smoothed RCN reuse feasibility audit.**
Scoped as investigation only — not a committed implementation. After Priority B, HMM completes
before growth starts, making it possible for growth to read from HMM's `05-medianSmoothed-RCN`
instead of renormalizing. Audit will assess normalization compatibility, smoothing window
differences (`--hmm-smooth-halfwidth` vs `--growth-rcn-smooth-bins`), and flexibility trade-offs
before deciding whether to implement.

**Priority F — HMM steps dict migration to `output_layout.py`.**
HMM currently builds its `steps` dict internally in `hmm_engine.py`. For architectural
consistency, both growth and HMM should source their step paths from `output_layout.py`.
This is a pure refactor; HMM step paths do not change.

**Consumer impact documented:** hardcoded path inventories for `scripts/summit_inspector.py`,
`scripts/aps_cluster_report.py`, `tests/test_pipeline.py`, and `onionskin_core/readme.py`
enumerated with per-site old→new path mappings. Additional scripts flagged for audit:
`aps_cluster_experiments.py`, `generate_hmm_v2_references.py`, `compare_puffstep_outputs.py`.

supplemental wrap-up audit. Read the live code (not just the audit reports) before deciding.

**Findings kept in active scope:**

  architectural loose end. Scope: rename the key, the `ensure_layout_dirs` parameter

  keyword-argument sites in `onionskin.py`, and the two private helpers

  directory names are already correct.** Output filenames

## [v0.11.08] — 2026-04-15 18:05 EST

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 11 wrap-up follow-up — final-overseer workflow refinement

### Final Overseer workflow now includes backlog-overlap review

Updated [multi-agent/workflows/spec_plan_three_role_audit_loop.md](/Users/johnurban/searchPaths/github/onionskin/multi-agent/workflows/spec_plan_three_role_audit_loop.md) so the Role 3 Final Overseer contract explicitly includes checking the active spec or plan against [multi-agent/KNOWN_ISSUES.md](/Users/johnurban/searchPaths/github/onionskin/multi-agent/KNOWN_ISSUES.md) and [multi-agent/BRAINSTORM.md](/Users/johnurban/searchPaths/github/onionskin/multi-agent/BRAINSTORM.md) when relevant. The goal is to report overlap, distinguish already-parked work from genuinely new findings, and surface items still in the spirit of the current phase or plan.

The same workflow file now also reflects this requirement in the Role 3 expected output, the common failure modes list, and Template D for the wrap-up audit prompt so the overlap check is part of the reusable operating contract rather than an ad hoc extra instruction.

## [v0.11.05] — 2026-04-15 16:46 EST

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 11 wrap-up follow-up — workflow refinement for final-overseer remediation

### Workflow updated for post-wrap-up remediation loop

Updated multi-agent/workflows/spec_plan_three_role_audit_loop.md so the reusable workflow now handles the case where the Final Overseer finds additional actionable work after the nominally final wrap-up audit. The workflow now explicitly routes such findings back through Role 1 for triage and scoped repair instructions, then to Role 2 for implementation, and back to Role 1 for final closeout, with the expectation that Role 3 normally does not re-enter unless the user explicitly asks.

The workflow file also now formalizes target-file editing conventions instead of leaving them implicit. It specifies that audit and implementation history should be appended rather than rewritten, that the authoritative execution instructions should live in the relevant target section, and that the wrap-up audit should be written at the bottom of the spec/plan under `## Wrap-up audit with Final Overseer` unless the target file already has an explicit wrap-up heading. Additional prompt templates were added for post-wrap-up triage, post-wrap-up implementation, and final closeout after remediation.

Updated multi-agent/project_context/HANDOFF.md and multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md to reflect the new state after Gemini's wrap-up audit: Phase 11 is no longer treated as a fully settled endpoint for workflow purposes, and the next action is for the Auditor to review the Final Overseer findings and decide what becomes actionable repair work.

Validation: documentation-only change; no code tests run.

### Reusable spec/plan three-role workflow added

Added [multi-agent/workflows/spec_plan_three_role_audit_loop.md](/Users/johnurban/searchPaths/github/onionskin/multi-agent/workflows/spec_plan_three_role_audit_loop.md), a reusable agent-agnostic workflow prompt for the audit / implement / re-audit / wrap-up pattern used to close Phase 11. The new file defines three functional roles in one place: Role 1 as auditor / instruction writer, Role 2 as implementer plus in-situ supplemental auditor, and Role 3 as final overseer / wrap-up auditor. It also explicitly supports 2-role mode by reusing Role 1 for the final wrap-up audit.

The workflow file is structured for reuse rather than copied directly from the raw Phase 11 prompts. It includes: purpose and usage constraints, required inputs, shared rules, role responsibilities, the standard execution loop, required file updates, closeout rules, common failure modes, and compact prompt templates with placeholders for `{TARGET_FILE}` and `{TARGET_SECTION}`. The workflow preserves the strongest operational lessons from the Phase 11 loop, including inspecting live code instead of trusting reports, keeping the audit trail in the target spec/plan file, and including `scripts/` plus `tests/` in audit surfaces when relevant.

**Priority 11.3 — Deep audit complete. 9 findings documented; Copilot implementation pending.**

Audit scope: `onionskin_core/engines/`, `onionskin_core/refinement.py`,
`onionskin_core/binning.py`, top-level `onionskin_core/single_engine.py`, `scripts/`,
`tests/`, `onionskin.py`.

Architecture finding: `engines/multistage_engine.py` and `engines/single_engine.py` are
self-contained engine modules loaded at runtime via `common.load_reference_multistage()` /
`common.load_reference_single()`. The top-level `onionskin_core/single_engine.py` (the
per-stage mean-shift controller, 956 lines) already imports from `refinement.py` — no
extraction needed there. `scripts/` and `tests/` have no algorithmic duplicates worth
extracting.

All 9 findings are mechanical deduplication with no logic changes:

- FIND-A (multistage engine, 4 items): `read_bedgraph`, `per_chrom_median`,
  `normalize_log2_chrom_median`, and `detect_bin_size` in `engines/multistage_engine.py`
  are all duplicates of — or subsumed by — functions in `refinement.py`. Replace with
  imports. The `normalize_log2_chrom_median` call site already selects 4 columns at line 358,
  so substituting `refinement.add_norm_log2` requires no further change. The multistage
  `detect_bin_size` (mode-of-sizes, handles DF+path) is the most capable version and should
  replace the naive first-row `refinement.py:detect_bin_size` as the canonical shared
  implementation.
- FIND-B (single engine, 4 items): same four functions in `engines/single_engine.py`.
  `detect_bin_size` there is path-only; will use the upgraded `refinement` version after
  FIND-A4. `add_norm_log2` has a different API (takes meds as arg); call site can be
  simplified from 2 lines to 1 using `refinement.add_norm_log2(df)`.
- FIND-C (doc-only, 1 item): `onionskin_core/dead_code.py` is an intentional PuffStep
  code reservoir that must be kept. The old module docstring said "can be deleted once no
  longer needed" — corrected to a note explaining the file is a preserved reference archive.
  No behavior change. Docstring fix applied directly by Claude; no Copilot action needed.

**Dependency order:** FIND-A4 (`refinement.py:detect_bin_size` upgrade) must complete
before FIND-B4 (single engine `detect_bin_size` removal). All other items are independent.

Full instructions with exact line numbers, before/after code snippets, and dependency
notes are in `PHASE11_SPEC.md` under `### 11.3 Audit findings — 2026-04-15`.

TASK.md and PHASE11_SPEC.md status headers updated.

## [v0.10.46] — 2026-04-15 13:43 EDT

### Priority 11.1 follow-up regression for OPEN-4

Added a focused regression to `tests/test_pipeline.py` for the remaining OPEN-4 confidence gap.
The new test runs a toy single-file `--onionskin` case and asserts that grouped shape-filter plot
artifacts are emitted under `01-prior/01-multistage-growth/06-plots/shape_filter/`, requiring at
least one `shape_*.png` and one `shape_*.pdf`. This directly guards the artifact that OPEN-4 had
been silently suppressing when `_phase2_postprocess` was still using stale flat-prefix fallback
paths.

One useful controller nuance surfaced while adding the test: the regression must request
`--pipelines per-stage`, not the default `all`, because after the OPEN-1 fix `all` correctly
includes `growth`, and single-file input is not eligible for growth. That is expected behavior,
not a new bug.

Validation: `make test` PASS.

PHASE11_SPEC.md now notes that this regression was added based on post-implementation discussion,
so Claude can re-audit 11.1 with both the OPEN-4 runtime fix and a direct regression guard in
place.

## [v0.10.45] — 2026-04-15 13:26 EDT

### Priority 11.1 follow-up implementation — OPEN-4

Implemented OPEN-4 from Claude's v0.10.44 re-audit. In `onionskin.py::_phase2_postprocess`, the
single-file shape-filter plot block was still using the flat grouping-root prefix for its fallback
`_single_calls.tsv` path and rejected-BED path. Those references were stale under the grouped
direct-write ownership model and failed silently inside the existing `try/except` block, so
single-file runs could skip shape-filter plot emission without surfacing an error.

The fix was the minimal grouped-path correction requested in the audit: define
`ms_prefix = _layout_multistage_prefix(layout)` inside the shape-filter plot block, then use
`{ms_prefix}_single_calls.tsv` and `{ms_prefix}_amplicons_actively_rejected.bed` for the
single-file fallback paths. No other plotting logic changed.

Validation: `make test` PASS.

PHASE11_SPEC.md now records OPEN-4 as implemented (`v0.10.45`) with a follow-up implementation
note for Claude. Priority 11.1 remains pending Claude re-audit for acceptance/closeout.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

## [v0.10.44] — 2026-04-15 EDT

### Priority 11.1 re-audit — OPEN-1/2/3 verified; OPEN-4 identified and documented

Claude re-audit of Copilot's v0.10.43 Priority 11.1 implementation pass. All three
previously documented open items are confirmed correctly implemented:

- OPEN-1 (`--pipelines all` excludes HMM): verified. `_pipelines_include()` and
  `_resolve_requested_effective_pipelines()` correctly expand `all` to all three pipelines.
  Makefile and `tests/run_summit_precision_test.sh` use `--pipelines all`. Regression test
  in `tests/test_pipeline.py` locks the expansion. `make test` PASS.
- OPEN-2 (single/single-stage flat-write-then-organize): verified. All four call sites in
  `onionskin.py` converted to `ms_prefix = _layout_multistage_prefix(layout)` direct
  writes. `organize_outputs()` no longer imported or called anywhere in `onionskin.py`.
- OPEN-3 (capability matrix not in PIPELINE_SPEC): verified. Table at line 140 of
  `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md` matches `autodetect.py` lines 64-69
  exactly; source-of-truth callout included.

New finding — OPEN-4 (pre-existing, exposed by OPEN-2): `onionskin.py::_phase2_postprocess`
constructs two shape-filter plot input paths using the flat grouping-root prefix
(`layout['out_prefix']`) instead of the grouped `ms_prefix`. After OPEN-2, those files
live at the grouped path, so both lookups fail silently inside `try/except Exception`
blocks. No crash, but shape filter plots are never emitted for single-file mode runs.
This bug predates OPEN-2 (before OPEN-2, `organize_outputs()` had already moved the files
to the grouped path before `_phase2_postprocess` ran, making the flat references equally
wrong). Copilot implementation instructions written into `PHASE11_SPEC.md` under OPEN-4.
Summary table updated: OPEN-1/2/3 marked verified, OPEN-4 marked pending Copilot fix.

Priority 11.1 remains open pending Copilot OPEN-4 fix.

## [v0.10.42] — 2026-04-15 EDT

### Phase 11 re-scope, pipeline ordering fix, Priority 11.1 deep audit

**Phase 11 re-scoped (user-directed):** PHASE11_SPEC.md narrowed to three priorities only
(11.1 pipeline-local ownership, 11.2 growth peer-pipeline cleanup, 11.3 shared module extraction).
All previous priorities 11.4–11.8 (parallel child pipeline, amplicon reliability, RCN floor, etc.)
were removed from the active spec. They are preserved in the deferred work queue in memory and in
KNOWN_ISSUES/BRAINSTORM as appropriate.

**Pipeline ordering conflict resolved:** PHASE11_SPEC.md had an internal inconsistency — the
carry-over decisions section stated `01-hmm/`, `02-growth-model/`, `03-per-stage-mean-shift/`
while Priority 11.2 step 6 stated the reverse for positions 2 and 3. Confirmed correct order:
`01-hmm/` (most trusted/oldest), `02-growth-model/` (second most developed), `03-per-stage-mean-shift/`
(least developed, lowest current priority). PHASE11_SPEC.md Priority 11.2 step 6 updated.
KNOWN_ISSUES and BRAINSTORM had no conflicting ordering — no changes needed there.

**Priority 11.1 deep audit — three open items found:**
11.1 was marked complete at v0.10.32 but the audit reveals it is NOT fully closed. Detailed
findings and implementation instructions written to PHASE11_SPEC.md under "11.1 Audit findings".

OPEN-1 (`--pipelines all` excludes HMM — Critical): `_pipelines_include()` returns False for
"hmm" when `--pipelines all` is given (only "growth" and "per-stage" are in the "all" expansion).
`_resolve_requested_effective_pipelines()` has the same gap. Core design rule #11 states
`--pipelines all` means all three pipelines. This was explicitly noted as an unresolved finding
in v0.10.00 but was never fixed. All `--pipelines all,hmm` usage in Makefile and test harnesses
is a workaround for this bug. Fix: add `"hmm"` to the `all` branch in both functions; then
collapse `--pipelines all,hmm` → `--pipelines all` everywhere.

OPEN-2 (Single-file and single-stage growth paths still flat-write-then-organize — Structural):
Multistage growth runs correctly use `_layout_multistage_prefix(layout)` and write directly to
the grouped path. But single-file, single-stage, and per-stage controller paths still call
`run_single_stage()` / `run_singlefile_caller()` with the flat grouping-root `out_prefix`, then
call `organize_outputs()` to relocate. Four call sites identified (at approximately onionskin.py
lines 2277, 3052, 3095, 3193, 3235). Fix: substitute `_layout_multistage_prefix(layout)` for
`out_prefix` at each site; remove the subsequent `organize_outputs()` calls.

OPEN-3 (Capability matrix not written — Documentation): The input-contract → eligible-pipelines
logic exists in `onionskin_core/autodetect.py` lines 64-69 but is not documented in any written
planning surface. PHASE11_SPEC required outcome includes an explicit written matrix. Fix: add
a 5-row capability matrix table to `multi-agent/full_instructions/PIPELINE_SPEC.md`.

Detailed implementation instructions (file paths, code snippets, exact changes) for all three
items written to PHASE11_SPEC.md under "Priority 11.1 — 11.1 Audit findings".

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 11 — Priority 11.1 audit; pipeline ordering fix

## [v0.10.41] — 2026-04-14 ~21:00 EDT

### KNOWN_ISSUES cleanup, HMM posterior plot pair verified, hw3tw1 summit validation, HMM step-13 BED output, BRAINSTORM HMM summit entries

**KNOWN_ISSUES / PHASE11_SPEC cleanup:**
Removed duplicate `--rcn-smooth-bins` rename entry from KNOWN_ISSUES (was covered by the
combined smoothing CLI cleanup entry). Updated the trimmed-mean smoothing entry to note that
full summit validation is complete (see below). Carry-over items in PHASE11_SPEC 11.3 updated:
summit-refinement BED item marked resolved.

**HMM posterior plot pair — confirmed working:**
Ran `make summit-smoke` (ds2_hires prior+posterior with `--pipelines all,hmm`). Found
`02-posterior/03-hmm/15-timing/hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` was located by the test harness and
evaluated as `ds2_hires_hmm_post` (score 0/10, ORC dist=+8,079 bp — same 5 kb resolution
limitation as the prior). The posterior HMM pipeline correctly emits step-15 outputs under
`02-posterior/03-hmm/`; the summit inspector HMM posterior plot pair infrastructure is
confirmed functional end-to-end. KNOWN_ISSUES entry updated. No regressions.

**hw3tw1 summit validation — confirmed safe:**
Ran ds2_hires pipeline with `--hmm-trim-halfwidth 1` and evaluated HMM summit scores.
Result: 0/10, final_origin_bp=33,478,092, ORC dist=+7,629 bp — identical to the hw3tw0
median baseline. The trim halfwidth does not affect HMM summit positions at 5 kb resolution
(as expected; the 5 kb bin grid is the limiting factor, not smoothing). hw3tw1 is confirmed
safe to adopt as the default without degrading existing summit scores. Closes out the hw3tw1
evaluation queue item from v0.10.40.

**HMM step-13 BED output (`all_trajectories.summit_refinement.bed`):**
Added BED4 output to `run_step13_summit_refinement()` in
`onionskin_core/hmm_summit_refinement.py`. Emits one row per trajectory using the combined
summit interval strategy (most synthesized estimate); rows include all trajectories (kept and
non-kept); `filter_keep` status encoded in the BED name field as `|keep=1` or `|keep=0` for
easy IGV/bedtools filtering. Module docstring and PIPELINE_SPEC Appendix B updated to record
the new output file. Validation: `make test` PASS; BED confirmed present in
`13-summit-refinement/all_trajectories.summit_refinement.bed` in a hw3tw1 test run.

**BRAINSTORM — two new HMM summit accuracy entries:**
Added "[2026-04-14] HMM summit estimation — use stage of maximum summit RCN, not last stage":
documents the empirical finding that stages 2-4 give better summit localization than later
stages (elongation interference shifts the peak laterally); establishes `peak_rcn_stage` as the
correct foundation for HMM summit position selection; cross-references the `dip_rate`/
`best_onset_stage` entries and the sliding-offset follow-up.
Added "[2026-04-14] HMM summit refinement — sliding-offset sub-bin localization": documents
that the sliding-offset refinement already implemented for the growth pipeline in
`onionskin_core/refinement.py` (v0.5.54–v0.5.56) has not been applied to HMM; two approaches
(sliding-offset without hires data; hires-based `--refine-summits`) described; both are
post-Priority-11.4 work items.
Both entries also added to PHASE11_SPEC 11.3 carry-over queue.

**Known Issue [1]** [2026-04-13] HMM trimmed-mean smoothing: --hmm-trim-halfwidth — RESOLVED v0.10.39/v0.10.41 - `--hmm-trim-halfwidth` implemented (default 0 = median). N > 0 = symmetric trimmed mean over
middle 2*N+1 sorted window values. N == smooth_halfwidth = plain mean. `_window_stat()` added
in `signal_utils.py`. Step-5 filename uses `trimSmoothedHW{N}TW{T}` suffix when T > 0.
See CHANGELOG v0.10.39. Evaluation completed v0.10.40 (5-variant harness on ds2+chr-II;
hw3tw1 halved plateau runs, 8/8 amplicons retained, summit intervals unchanged). HMM summit
score at 5000 bp confirmed identical between hw3tw0 and hw3tw1 (v0.10.41) — tw1 is safe to
adopt as default. Full `make summit` validation confirmed no regression.

**Known Issue [2]** [2026-04-13] Summit inspector HMM posterior plot pair - RESOLVED v0.10.39/v0.10.41 -  data availability confirmed. **Diagnosis:** The inspector already runs per-pipeline (growth, HMM, per-stage) and for each pipeline tries to load posterior data from `02-posterior/<pipeline-dir>/` (lines 1293-1308 of summit_inspector.py). For the growth pipeline, this works because posterior runs produce origins TSV + stage medians. For HMM, `_load_pipeline_bundle` requires `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` (step 15) AND step-4 stage median RCN bedGraphs to return `status: ready`. If the posterior HMM run completes through steps 4 and 15, the inspector will generate a prior+posterior pair automatically — no inspector code change needed. The gap is whether the posterior HMM run currently emits step-4 stage medians and step-15 `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` under `02-posterior/03-hmm/`. Ran a full prior+posterior run and confirmed presence of `02-posterior/03-hmm/04-*/` and `02-posterior/03-hmm/15-timing/hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` are emitted. **Exit condition:** HMM posterior produced step-4 and step-15 outputs; inspector generates a prior/posterior pair for HMM automatically.

## [v0.10.40] — 2026-04-14 ~17:00 EDT

### PHASE11_SPEC priorities 11.4/11.5, --growth-rcn-smooth-bins rename, hmm-trim-halfwidth evaluation harness + results

**PHASE11_SPEC updated — new priorities 11.4 and 11.5:**
Two HIGH-priority items promoted from KNOWN_ISSUES/BRAINSTORM into the active phase spec.
Priority 11.4 (HMM parallel child pipeline): per-sample `indiv_samples/` subdirs through HMM
steps 03–11; prerequisite for step-14 APS raw-file bug fix, SAPS, and amplicon reliability
scoring; step renumbering timing→16-timing, clustering→17-clustering on completion.
Priority 11.5 (amplicon reliability scoring + flat-sample detection): pre-APS evidence-scoring
pass on five axes (RCN increase, width, area, triangle BIC, parabola height); flat-sample
detection before APS clustering; `--aps-preamp-threshold` flag; prerequisite for continued
cluster tests and SAPS. Existing priorities 11.4–11.6 renumbered to 11.6–11.8.
Priority 11.3 carry-over queue updated: trimmed-mean smoothing marked resolved (v0.10.39);
`--hmm-bin-size` auto-detect marked resolved (v0.10.38); `--rcn-smooth-bins` rename noted done.
KNOWN_ISSUES updated: amplicon reliability scoring entry marked promoted to PHASE11_SPEC 11.5.

**`--rcn-smooth-bins` → `--growth-rcn-smooth-bins` rename:**
Flag renamed to carry an explicit growth-pipeline prefix consistent with the project naming
convention. Moved from the `ovr` (overview) argument group to the growth argument group (`ms`)
in `onionskin.py`. Help text updated to state explicitly that this flag applies to the
growth/single pipeline only and is not used by the HMM pipeline. Both `getattr(args,
"growth_rcn_smooth_bins", 3)` references and `args.growth_rcn_smooth_bins` reference updated.
KNOWN_ISSUES entry for this rename closed.
Validation: `make test` PASS.

**`--hmm-trim-halfwidth` evaluation harness (`make hmm-trim-eval` + `make hmm-trim-report`):**
Added five make targets (`hmm-trim-eval-hw3tw0/hw3tw1/hw3tw2/hw4tw1/hw4tw2`) running the HMM
pipeline on ds2+chr-II with different smoothing variants, plus a combined `hmm-trim-eval`
dependency target and a separate `hmm-trim-report` target that generates a structured markdown
comparison report without re-running pipelines. All five run dirs go to `dev/runs/hmm_trim_eval/`.
New evaluation script `tests/eval_hmm_trim_halfwidth.py` covers five checks: (1) plateau run
counts in step-5 smoothed bedGraphs at each by-eye amplicon window; (2) state-path boundary
changes vs baseline (N boundaries, N matched/lost/gained, mean/median displacement in kb) from
step-7 collapsed bedGraphs — informational, flags amplicons to inspect in IGV; (3) summit state
interval widths from step-15 TSV `summit_state_start_bp`/`summit_state_end_bp`; (4) amplicon
retention vs `amplicons.by-eye.bed`; (5) II/9A score/10 and II/2B proximity inline (same logic
as `eval_summit_precision_v2.py`). Script accepts N run directories as `label:dir` pairs; first
is the baseline.

**`make hmm-trim-eval` results — summary:**

Check 1 (plateau reduction): Confirmed effective. hw3tw0 (median baseline) produces ~124
plateau runs / 307 plateau bins at II-3 (II/9A), max run length 6. hw3tw1 (hw=3, tw=1):
~69 runs / 148 bins, max 3 — roughly halved. hw3tw2 (hw=3, tw=2): ~37 runs / 74 bins, max 2 —
~70% reduction. hw4tw1 and hw4tw2 also reduce plateaus but less effectively than the hw=3
variants at the same trim halfwidth, because the wider window (9-bin) reintroduces
same-valued bins more often. Pattern consistent across all 13 chr-II by-eye amplicons.

Check 2 (state-path boundaries): No boundary gains or losses in any comparison (N lost = 0,
N gained = 0 throughout). Boundary count identical to baseline in all variants. Mean
displacements: mostly 0–6 kb, a few amplicons with single-stage means of 7–12 kb (II-4 stage
3 shows the largest shift at mean ~12 kb / median 20 kb in hw3tw2 — flag for IGV inspection).
Overall the state-path topology is stable across all trimming variants.

Check 3 (summit state interval widths): No systematic narrowing. Widths are essentially
unchanged between runs (most amplicons within ±5 kb, some ±10 kb, no directional trend).
Global mean: hw3tw0 = 123,750 bp; hw3tw1 = 126,250; hw3tw2 = 127,500. The plateau reduction
in the smoothed signal does not propagate into narrower HMM state assignments — the broad
emission/transition structure that determines summit-state regions is unaffected by fine
plateau structure. This is the expected outcome.

Check 4 (amplicon retention): Perfect 8/8 on `hmm=yes` expected amplicons in all five runs.
None of the 5 low-confidence `hmm=no` loci appear in any run. No false positives introduced.

Check 5 (summit precision): All five runs score 0/10 at II/9A with origin at 33,478,092 bp
(+7.6 kb from ORC center, just outside Level 1 boundary at 33,475,026 bp). Identical scores
because state paths are stable (check 2) and the summit estimate is derived from the state
path center. The full `make summit` test suite (with hires manifests) would give higher scores
and more differentiation; this comparison is identical by design at 5 kb resolution.

**Verdict:** `--hmm-smooth-halfwidth 3 --hmm-trim-halfwidth 1` (hw3tw1) is the recommended
default-change candidate. It halves plateau runs with no amplicon loss, no boundary topology
changes, and minimal state-path displacement. Summit state intervals are unchanged. The main
benefit is cleaner visualized signal (step-5 bedGraphs) and more local structure preserved
in the smoothed track for any downstream analysis that reads it. Further validation with the
full `make summit` suite is warranted before adopting as the new default. hw3tw2 reduces
plateaus more aggressively but begins to show larger boundary displacements at some loci.

**Known Issue** [2026-04-19] `--rcn-smooth-bins` naming, placement, and pipeline scope — RESOLVED [v0.10.40] — 2026-04-14- **`--rcn-smooth-bins` rename:** Renamed to `--growth-rcn-smooth-bins`;
moved from `ovr` group to growth argument group in `onionskin.py`; help text now explicitly states
growth/single pipeline only. `make test` passes.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 11 — HMM completeness, planning surface (11.3/11.4/11.5)
