## [v0.10.39] — 2026-04-19 EDT

### --aps-singleton-guard on|off, HMM trimmed-mean smoothing, summit inspector polygon marker + HMM interval schema

**`--aps-singleton-guard on|off` (replaces `--no-aps-singleton-guard`):**
`--no-aps-singleton-guard` removed; replaced with `--aps-singleton-guard on|off` (choices,
default `None`). `None` = auto: guard off for `keep` mode, on for all other k modes (preserves
prior auto-behavior). `on` forces guard active regardless of k mode (new capability — previously
impossible under `keep`). `off` forces guard inactive (replaces `--no-aps-singleton-guard`).
`_aps_fix_singletons()` rewritten to check `args.aps_singleton_guard` before auto logic.

**HMM trimmed-mean smoothing (`--hmm-trim-halfwidth`):**
New `--hmm-trim-halfwidth INT` flag (default 0, must be ≤ `--hmm-smooth-halfwidth`). When 0
(default), behavior is unchanged — pure median smooth at step 5. When N > 0: window values are
sorted and the mean of the middle `2*N+1` values is taken (symmetric trimmed mean), reducing
flat-topped summit plateaus while still damping outlier bins. When `trim_halfwidth == smooth_halfwidth`,
equivalent to the plain window mean (maximum inclusion, no trimming). Implementation:
`_window_stat()` added to `signal_utils.py` (`_local_median_smooth()` updated to accept
`trim_halfwidth`); `local_median_smooth_bedgraph()` updated; `_step5_smooth()` and
`_step10_summit_bins()` in `hmm_engine.py` updated; step-5 output filename uses
`medSmoothedHW{N}` when `trim_halfwidth=0` and `trimSmoothedHW{N}TW{T}` when T > 0.
`run_hmm()` signature extended with `trim_halfwidth: int = 0`. Validation added in `run_hmm()`
that `0 <= trim_halfwidth <= smoothing_halfwidth`. `--hmm-smooth-halfwidth` help text updated to
describe both median and trimmed-mean modes.

**Summit inspector `--summit-marker` and HMM state interval schema:**
New `--summit-marker center|polygon|both` flag (default `both`). `both` = vertical line at
summit + shaded span across the summit interval (matches prior growth behavior where CI span was
already drawn). `center` = vline only. `polygon` = shaded span only, no vline. For growth, the
span is the bootstrap CI. For HMM, the span is the summit state interval bounds. To support HMM
polygon display, `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` schema extended with `summit_state_start_bp` and
`summit_state_end_bp` columns (`_compute_summit_estimates()` in `hmm_ported_analyses.py` updated;
TSV column list updated). `_load_hmm_calls()` in `summit_inspector.py` now reads these columns
and populates `origin_ci_low_bp` / `origin_ci_high_bp` from state interval bounds; older TSVs
without the columns fall back to ±2500 bp around the center. `_draw_rcn_panel()` updated with
`summit_marker` parameter; span label auto-switches between 'summit interval' and 'bootstrap CI'
based on span width.

**Validation:** `make test` PASS; `make toy` PASS.

**Known Issue [1]** [2026-04-18] Singleton guard redesign: --aps-singleton-guard on|off — RESOLVED v0.10.39 - `--no-aps-singleton-guard` removed; `--aps-singleton-guard on|off` (default `None` = auto)
added. `on` forces guard active regardless of k mode. `off` forces inactive.
`_aps_fix_singletons()` rewritten. See above.

**Known Issue [2]** [2026-04-13] Summit inspector summit marker: --summit-marker — RESOLVED v0.10.39 - `--summit-marker center|polygon|both` added (default `both`). HMM TSV gains
`summit_state_start_bp`/`end_bp` columns. `_load_hmm_calls()` reads interval bounds for polygon
display; older TSVs fall back ±2500 bp. See above.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 11 — HMM completeness, inspector enhancements

---

## [v0.10.38] — 2026-04-18 EDT

### Posterior manifest isolation, HMM step-14 APS flag passthrough, HMM flag renaming + synonyms, HMM help-string overhaul

**Posterior manifest isolation:**
`_run_posterior()` now builds `post_layout` and calls `ensure_layout_dirs()` before writing
any manifest files, so `posterior_manifest.tsv` and `posterior_hires_manifest_N.tsv` are
written to `02-posterior/01-multistage-growth/` instead of the run root. The HMM arm gets
its own copy at `02-posterior/03-hmm/posterior_manifest.tsv` (content identical for now —
both use the same APS-based stage assignment — but the separate file gives each pipeline a
clean isolation boundary for future per-pipeline divergence). Per-stage can wait (no APS
module yet). Test `test_posterior_force_manifest_with_hires` updated to assert the new
growth-arm location.

**HMM step-14 APS CLI flag passthrough:**
`run_step14_hmm_aps()` in `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py` now accepts `cluster_k`,
`feature`, and `fix_singletons` parameters (defaults: `"keep"`, `"summit"`, `False`) that
match the growth-pipeline defaults. `_resolve_cluster_k()` from `aps.py` is used to
translate `cluster_k` to a numeric k. The feature matrix column is resolved via a
`{"area": "area_excess", "summit": "summit_excess"}` map. Params threaded from
`onionskin.py` (`_run_hmm_engine`) → `run_hmm()` (`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`)
→ `run_step14_hmm_aps()`. HMM step-14 now uses `summit+keep` by default, matching the
growth pipeline.

**HMM flag renaming with PuffStep synonyms:**
Six CLI flags renamed for clarity — "special" → "background", "other" → "amp-step". The old
PuffStep names are retained as accepted synonyms so existing scripts continue to work:
- `--hmm-background-idx` (also: `--hmm-special-idx`)
- `--hmm-expected-background-length` (also: `--hmm-expected-special-length`)
- `--hmm-init-background` (also: `--hmm-init-special`)
- `--hmm-leave-background-state` (also: `--hmm-leave-special-state`)
- `--hmm-expected-amp-step-length` (also: `--hmm-expected-other-length`)
- `--hmm-leave-amp-step` (also: `--hmm-leave-other`)
All `args.*` references in `_run_hmm_engine()` updated to new names. Internal
`hmm_engine.py` / `hmm_core.py` parameter names left as-is (internal API). The prior help
text swap (background described as "amplified regions" and vice versa) was a porting error
from PuffStep — corrected.

**HMM help-string overhaul:**
All HMM argparse help strings substantially enriched based on PuffStep puffcn documentation.
Key improvements: bin-size/expected-length conversion formula (p = bin_size / length)
documented inline; `--hmm-leave-background-state` and `--hmm-leave-amp-step` document the
`> 1 → length-in-bins` interpretation; `--hmm-exp-decay` and `--hmm-exp-decay-scale`
explain the copy-number-distance decay model with a concrete example;
`--hmm-background-idx` explains it only affects initial/transition probability construction
(not decoding); emission-means and emission-sigmas document the 8-state CN model and
per-model behaviour; step-8 merge/filter pipeline documented sequentially across
`--hmm-thresh-state`, `--hmm-merge1`, `--hmm-min-width`, `--hmm-merge2`,
`--hmm-max-state-thresh`; training/convergence flags explain when and how re-estimation
occurs. All PuffStep port references confirmed.

**`--hmm-bin-size` auto-detection and abort-on-mismatch:**
`--hmm-bin-size` default changed from `5000` to `None`. `_run_hmm_engine()` now
auto-detects the bin size from the first sample in the active manifest using
`detect_major_bin_size()` from `onionskin_core/binning.py`. If `--hmm-bin-size` is
explicitly provided and disagrees with the auto-detected value, onionskin aborts with a
clear error message rather than silently using the wrong bin size. If auto-detection
cannot read any sample path, onionskin also aborts. `bin_size` in `run_hmm()` is now
always the auto-detected value. This retires the de-facto hardcoded 5000 bp default that
could silently mismatch user input data at other bin resolutions. The flag is retained
as an explicit override/validation path.

**Validation:** `make test` PASS; `make toy` PASS; `make posterior-layout-smoke` PASS.
Posterior manifest confirmed at `02-posterior/01-multistage-growth/posterior_manifest.tsv`
and `02-posterior/03-hmm/posterior_manifest.tsv`; no root-level leftover.

**Known Issue [1]** [2026-04-13] Posterior manifests need pipeline-specific isolation and naming — RESOLVED v0.10.38 - `posterior_manifest.tsv` and `posterior_hires_manifest_N.tsv` now written to
`02-posterior/01-multistage-growth/` instead of the run root. `post_layout` is built
before the manifest paths are assigned so the directory already exists. Test updated
in `test_pipeline.py`. See above.

**Known Issue [2]** ## [2026-04-18] HMM step-14 APS ignores user APS CLI flags — RESOLVED v0.10.38 - `run_step14_hmm_aps()` signature extended with `cluster_k`, `feature`, `fix_singletons`.
`_resolve_cluster_k()` imported from `aps.py` and used to compute k. Feature column
resolved from `{"area": "area_excess", "summit": "summit_excess"}`. Params threaded
from `onionskin.py` → `run_hmm()` → `run_step14_hmm_aps()`. See above.

**Known Issue [3]** [2026-04-18] HMM flag naming: "special/other" → "background/amp-step" — RESOLVED v0.10.38 - All six CLI flags renamed in `onionskin.py` argparse and all `args.*` references updated
in `_run_hmm_engine()`. Help text for `--hmm-expected-background-length` and
`--hmm-expected-amp-step-length` corrected (prior help texts were swapped). Internal
`hmm_engine.py` / `hmm_core.py` parameter names left unchanged (internal API; user-facing
surface is now clean). See above.

**Known Issue [4]** [2026-04-13] HMM smoothing CLI cleanup: — RESOLVED v0.10.38 - `--hmm-bin-size` vs `--rcn-smooth-bins` - `--hmm-bin-size` Default changed from hardcoded 5000 to `None`.
`_run_hmm_engine()` now auto-detects bin size from the first manifest sample via
`detect_major_bin_size()`. If `--hmm-bin-size` is explicitly provided and disagrees with
auto-detected value, onionskin aborts. The flag is retained as an explicit override/validation path.

## [v0.10.37] — 2026-04-18 ~00:00 EDT

### APS investigation conclusion: ds2+4-chr results, CLI defaults changed to summit+keep, 5 posterior QC checks implemented

#### Summary

The 4-chromosome (X, II, III, IV) experiment definitively resolved the APS feature selection
question. `log10area+keep`, which appeared tied with `summit+keep` on chr-II-only data, fails
catastrophically on the 4-chr dataset: it drops 14 of 20 chr IV amplicons and 4 of 8 chr II
amplicons due to a singleton stage created by log10 over-separation of SAG47. `summit+keep`
retains 15/20 chr IV amplicons and 7/8 chr II amplicons. The new default is `summit+keep` with
no singleton guard (guard is meaningless for `keep` mode). Five automated posterior QC checks
are now implemented and run automatically at the end of every `--posterior` run.

#### Code changes (this session)

- **Changed `--aps-feature` default: `area` → `summit`** (`onionskin.py`).
  Summit values cluster more tightly within stages than area values. Summit is naturally
  singleton-resistant: unlike log10area, it does not over-separate mid-range samples when the APS
  dynamic range widens (e.g., multi-chromosome experiments).

- **Changed `--aps-cluster-k` default: `elbow` → `keep`** (`onionskin.py`).
  `keep` preserves the full developmental gradient. `elbow` picks k=2 on virtually all
  developmental amplification data, collapsing stages and inverting the growth track.
  Updated help text describes the reasoning and disqualifies `elbow` as a default for this application.

- **Added `_aps_fix_singletons()` helper** (`onionskin.py`).
  Centralized logic: with `--aps-cluster-k keep`, the singleton guard is automatically OFF
  regardless of the `--no-aps-singleton-guard` flag. With other k methods, the guard is ON
  unless `--no-aps-singleton-guard` is explicitly passed. Rationale: singletons are valid and
  expected with `keep` (every prior stage is preserved exactly); the guard is only useful
  for elbow/N-cluster methods where the chosen k may accidentally produce a lone-member cluster.

- **Implemented 5 automated posterior QC checks: `_run_posterior_qc()`** (`onionskin.py`).
  Called automatically at the end of every `_run_posterior()` run. Emits
  `{prefix}_posterior_qc_summary.tsv` in the posterior multistage-growth directory.
  Warnings printed to stderr for any failing check.

  1. **Monotonicity audit** — counts stage-to-stage fold regressions (>5% drop in median_peak_fold
     per amplicon) in prior vs posterior stage_summary. WARN if posterior regression count
     increases.

  2. **Amplicon dropout** — for each prior amplicon with positive delta_bic, checks if any
     posterior amplicon overlaps it by ≥30% of the prior call's span. Reports: n_prior,
     n_posterior, retained, dropped, gained. WARN if any prior amplicon is absent.

  3. **Intra-amplicon M-shape check** — loads posterior growth track bedGraph; for each detected
     posterior amplicon, finds the bin with the global maximum value within the amplicon
     boundaries. Flags as M-shape risk if the argmax midpoint is displaced >max(15% of
     halfwidth, 50kb) from the called summit (`peak_base_bp`). WARN if any amplicon is flagged.

  4. **Stage-1 contamination** — loads posterior cluster map and prior APS table; checks if any
     posterior stage-1 sample has APS_area > 2× the global minimum APS. WARN if any stage-1
     sample is above the threshold (indicates the baseline stage may contain pre-amplified samples).

  5. **Improvement score** — summarizes monotonicity delta and amplicon delta (retained/dropped/
     gained) as a one-line IMPROVED / DEGRADED / NEUTRAL verdict. Always emitted.

  Graceful degradation: if required files are missing, affected checks are skipped with a log
  message rather than crashing.

  Validated against `dev/runs/aps_clust_ds2_II/summit_keep/` — correctly reports: check 1 OK
  (1→0 regressions), check 2 OK (7 retained, 0 dropped, 1 gained), check 3 WARN (4 amplicons
  with growth-track max displaced from summit, calibration may need tuning), check 4 OK
  (no stage-1 contamination), check 5 IMPROVED.

- **Promoted "Posterior quality checks" KNOWN_ISSUES entry** to implemented.
  The entry is resolved: all 5 checks from the design in KNOWN_ISSUES.md are now wired.
  Removed from KNOWN_ISSUES.md; recorded here.

- **`make test` PASS, `make toy` PASS** after all changes.

#### ds2 + 4-chromosome experiment findings

**Reproducing:** `make clust-ds2-4chr`, `make clust-report-ds2-4chr` (added to Makefile).
**Artifacts:** `dev/runs/aps_clust_ds2_4chr/`

**chr IV results (20 prior amps, 10 prior regs) — the key discriminator:**

| Experiment | k | amps | dropped | post_regs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| summit + keep | 5 | **15** | 5 | 0 |
| area + keep | 5 | 13 | 7 | 2 |
| shape + keep | 5 | 13 | 7 | 4 |
| log10area + elbow | 3 | 12 | 8 | 0 |
| log10area + keep | 5 | **6** | **14** | 1 |
| log2summit + keep | 5 | 6 | 14 | 1 |
| log2shape + keep | 5 | 6 | 14 | 1 |

**Root cause of log10area+keep failure confirmed from cluster maps:**
In 4-chr APS space, SAG47 (prior stage 4 rep2, APS=8.9M) ends up as a **singleton at posterior
stage 2** with log10area+keep. A single-replicate stage median = SAG47's raw RCN — too noisy
for marginal chr IV amplicons to pass the BIC threshold. Result: 14/20 chr IV amplicons dropped.

With summit+keep, SAG47 (cluster_raw=4) is grouped with SAG45 (9.7M) and SAG43 (10.8M) at
posterior stage 2 — 3 samples, stable median. Only 5 chr IV amplicons dropped (likely the most
marginal ones). Summit values do not over-separate mid-range samples.

**Chr reliability ordering (established during 4-chr analysis):**
`II > III > X > IV`
- Chr II: clearest signal, best-characterized
- Chr III: reliable secondary chromosome
- Chr X: noisier (X/X' animal heterogeneity in ds2)
- Chr IV: substantial false-positive risk among 20 prior calls

**Previous "best candidate" retraction:**
The v0.10.36 conclusion that `log10area+keep` and `summit+keep` were joint best candidates was
based only on chr-II single-chromosome data. The 4-chr test broke `log10area+keep`. The chr-II-only
analysis was correct within its scope but failed to expose the singleton fragility that emerges
when the APS dynamic range widens across multiple chromosomes.

#### Documentation updates

- **`DECISIONS.md`** — added full 4-chr results tables (II, III, X, IV), root-cause analysis
  of the log10 singleton failure, cluster map comparison confirming SAG47 singleton diagnosis,
  chr reliability ordering, explanation of why chr-II-only tests were insufficient, and the
  revised recommendation. Status updated from "INVESTIGATION IN PROGRESS" to
  "DECISION REACHED (v0.10.37)".

- **`KNOWN_ISSUES.md`** — removed the "Posterior quality checks not integrated" entry (now
  implemented and recorded here). Retained stage-1 anchoring design (not yet implemented).
  Updated APS clustering entry investigation progress note.

- **`TASK.md`** and **`HANDOFF.md`** — updated to reflect investigation complete, CLI defaults
  changed, QC implemented; next items are the remaining KNOWN_ISSUES.


**Known Issue [1]** [2026-04-17] Posterior APS clustering collapses developmental gradient — RESOLVED v0.10.37 - **Root cause:** APS `elbow` criterion selects k=2 on developmental amplification data, collapsing
the gradient and inverting the growth track. Fixed by changing CLI defaults to
`--aps-feature summit --aps-cluster-k keep` in `onionskin.py`. The `summit+keep` combination
avoids singleton stages and preserves the full developmental gradient. The singleton guard is
auto-disabled for `keep` mode. See `DECISIONS.md` for the full investigation record and the
4-chromosome experiment that broke `log10area+keep` as an alternative.

**Known Issue [2]** [2026-04-14] Posterior quality checks not integrated into the pipeline — RESOLVED v0.10.37 - All 5 checks implemented in `_run_posterior_qc()` (`onionskin.py`). Emits
`{prefix}_posterior_qc_summary.tsv` in the posterior multistage-growth directory after every
`--posterior` run. Warnings printed to stderr on WARN status. See above.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 11 known-issues — APS investigation conclusion, CLI defaults, posterior QC implementation

## [v0.10.36] — 2026-04-14 EDT

### APS clustering investigation: new features, experiment grid, and ds1+II / ds2+II findings

#### Code changes

- Implemented four new `--aps-feature` choices in `onionskin_core/aps.py` and `onionskin.py`:
  - `log10area` — log10 transform of the area_excess scalar (floor=1.0). Intuitive: one unit
    = one order of magnitude. Separates the low-APS pre-amplification zone from the dense
    amplified cluster even when those differ by 2–3 orders of magnitude.
  - `log2area` — log2 transform of the same scalar (available for completeness; log10 preferred
    for area because it gives order-of-magnitude semantics).
  - `log2summit` — log2(summit_excess + 1) transform of the per-amplicon summit RCN scalar.
  - `log2shape` — applies `log2(max(RCN, 1.0)) = log2(excess + 1)` per bin before constructing
    the shape vector, then clusters on those log-transformed per-bin profiles. This compresses
    the dynamic range of highly amplified late-stage samples without discarding their shape.
  - `_FEATURE_COL` dict updated; `build_aps_shape_matrix()` gains a `log_transform` parameter;
    scalar dispatch in `compute_and_write_aps()` handles the new transforms.
  - `onionskin.py --aps-feature` choices extended; help text describes the semantics of each.

- Added `scripts/aps_cluster_experiments.py` — runs the full 11-experiment APS clustering
  feature × k-selection grid for any dataset+chromosome combination. Experiments: baseline
  (area+elbow), area+keep, shape+elbow, shape+keep, summit+keep, width+keep, log10area+elbow,
  log10area+keep, log2summit+keep, log2shape+keep, log2shape+elbow. Skips existing runs;
  supports `--force` and `--dry-run`.

- Added `scripts/aps_cluster_report.py` — generates a tabular comparison of all experiments
  under an experiment root directory. Columns: k (posterior stages), amplicons detected on
  the target chromosome, amplicons from the prior that were dropped, posterior regressions
  (>5% per-step drop in median_peak_fold), stage-1 sample count and minimum APS (pre-amp
  proxy), and summit location at a reference locus.

- Added four `Makefile` targets:
  - `make clust-ds1-II` — run all 11 experiments on ds1+chr-II, then print the report
  - `make clust-ds2-II` — run all 11 experiments on ds2+chr-II, then print the report
  - `make clust-report-ds1-II` — reprint the ds1+chr-II table from existing runs
  - `make clust-report-ds2-II` — reprint the ds2+chr-II table from existing runs

- Added two new entries to `multi-agent/KNOWN_ISSUES.md`:
  - **Posterior quality checks not integrated into the pipeline** — five automated checks
    designed: (1) monotonicity audit vs prior, (2) prior-amplicon dropout detection,
    (3) intra-amplicon M-shape check (peak displacement from summit), (4) stage-1
    contamination check (APS threshold), (5) posterior improvement score. Design calls
    for a `posterior_qc_summary.tsv` emitted alongside existing posterior outputs.
  - **Stage-1 pre-amplification isolation: no mechanism to anchor non-amplified samples** —
    candidate design documented: `--aps-preamp-threshold <APS_value>` flag that forces all
    samples below the threshold into a fixed stage-1 group before clustering the rest.

- Updated `multi-agent/project_context/DECISIONS.md`:
  - Revised criterion-4 (M-shape) for the ds2+II investigation. The original check compared
    summit zone vs external flanks (±300kb), missing the intra-amplicon "telephone pole"
    pattern where shoulders WITHIN the amplicon are higher than the summit zone. A corrected
    profile analysis of II-3 (chr II, 33100k–33850k, 5kb bins) found:
    - `area+elbow`: confirmed M-shape. Summit zone 4.1–5.2; both shoulders higher (6.75–6.85).
    - `shape+keep` / `log10area+keep` / `log2shape+keep`: right-shifted peak ~200kb from summit
      (26.83 at 33570k vs summit zone max 21.68 at 33365k). Not a classic M-shape but summit
      is not the global maximum within the amplicon.
    - `log2summit+keep`: near-centered (14.61 at 33385k; right shoulder 15.50 at 33620k).
  - Added full ds1+II experiment table (see Findings section below).

#### Findings: ds2+II and ds1+II APS clustering experiment grid

**Dataset contexts:**

| | ds2 | ds1 |
|---|---|---|
| Structure | 5 stages × 2 reps = 10 samples | 9 stages × 2 reps = 18 samples |
| APS range | ~1.15M–7.5M (0.8 log10 units) | ~100K–5.57M (1.74 log10 units) |
| Prior disorder | Low (1 regression, 8 amps on chr II) | Severe (8 regressions, 3 amps on chr II) |
| Stage-1 biology | Already partly amplified (1.15M min APS) | True pre-amplification (100K min APS) |

**ds2+II experiment results** (prior: 8 amps, 1 regression):

| Experiment | k | amps | prior lost | post regs | s1_n | II-3 summit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| baseline (area+elbow) | 2 | 6 | II-6 | 0 | 7 | 33465kb |
| area+keep | 5 | 9 | none | 0 | 2 | 33465kb |
| shape+elbow | 2 | 9 | none | 0 | 8 | 33465kb |
| shape+keep | 5 | 9 | none | 5 | 2 | 33465kb |
| summit+keep | 5 | 9 | none | 0 | 2 | 33465kb |
| width+keep | 4 | 6 | II-6 | 0 | 2 | 33465kb |
| log10area+elbow | 3 | 6 | II-6 | 0 | 2 | 33465kb |
| log10area+keep | 5 | 9 | none | 5 | 2 | 33465kb |
| log2summit+keep | 5 | 7 | II-6 | 1 | 2 | 33465kb |
| log2shape+keep | 5 | 9 | none | 5 | 2 | 33465kb |
| log2shape+elbow | 2 | 9 | none | 0 | 8 | 33465kb |

**ds1+II experiment results** (prior: 3 amps, 8 regressions; II-2 and II-3 not in prior):

| Experiment | k | amps | prior lost | post regs | s1_n | II-3 summit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| baseline (area+elbow) | 3 | 3 | II-6 | 0 | 7 | 33460kb |
| area+keep | 9 | 3 | II-6 | 4 | 3 | 33461kb |
| shape+elbow | 2 | 2 | II-6 + II-2 | 0 | 7 | — |
| shape+keep | 9 | 3 | II-6 | 4 | 3 | 33461kb |
| summit+keep | 9 | 4 | none | 5 | 3 | 33461kb |
| width+keep | 9 | 4 | none | 5 | 3 | 33461kb |
| log10area+elbow | 2 | 5* | none | 0 | 4 | 33460kb |
| log10area+keep | 9 | 4 | none | 5 | 1 | 33456kb |
| log2summit+keep | 9 | 4 | none | 6 | 1 | 33454kb |
| log2shape+keep | 9 | 4 | none | 6 | 3 | 33461kb |
| log2shape+elbow | 2 | 5* | none | 0 | 4 | 33460kb |

*5 amps includes a novel II-4 call at ~41.6M not present in the prior.

**Key findings and interpretation:**

1. **Elbow criterion is unreliable on both datasets.** Picks k=2–3 on ds1 regardless of feature,
   collapsing 9 developmental stages into 2–3 groups. On ds2 it fares slightly better (k=3 for
   log10area+elbow) but still loses prior amplicons in several cases. Not viable as a default.

2. **Amplicon dropout is the clearest hard failure.** Methods that drop a prior-detected amplicon
   are disqualified. On both datasets: baseline, area+keep, shape+keep, and log2summit+keep all
   drop at least one prior amplicon (II-6 at ~47M on chr II). shape+elbow also drops II-2.

3. **Surviving candidates on both datasets** (no dropout, full k=n_stages resolution):

   | Method | ds2: k/amps/regs | ds1: k/amps/regs | Stage-1 isolation |
   |---|---|---|---|
   | summit+keep | 5/9/0 | 9/4/5 | 2–3 samples |
   | log10area+keep | 5/9/5 | 9/4/5 | 1 sample (purest) |
   | log2shape+keep | 5/9/5 | 9/4/6 | 2–3 samples |

4. **Regressions on ds1 are not disqualifying.** The prior has 8 regressions; keep methods
   produce 4–6. That is genuine improvement. The residual regressions arise because stages 5–9
   are crowded into a 3–5.6M APS range with no detectable biological ordering signal. The
   clustering is correctly identifying the ambiguity rather than inventing false order.

5. **Stage-1 isolation is cleanest with log10area+keep on ds1.** The 100K→194K gap (Δ=0.29
   log10 units) is sufficient in log10 space to separate the true pre-amplification sample
   (S38) from early-amplification samples. log2shape+keep groups the 3 lowest-APS samples
   together (more statistically stable but less pure). Both behaviors are defensible.

6. **II-3 summit location is consistent.** All methods that detect II-3 place it at
   33,454–33,465kb on both datasets. Summit localization is not a differentiator.

7. **ds1 weakly informs; ds2 more strongly informs.** ds1 has major known staging problems
   (many more replicates to add, stages 5–9 are biologically ambiguous in this version).
   ds2 is the stronger signal for now. Further ds1 improvements and full-genome tests are next.

8. **Current best candidate: `log10area+keep+no-guard`.** No dropout on either dataset,
   best stage-1 pre-amp isolation on ds1, consistent summit localization. `log2shape+keep+no-guard`
   is a close second, preferred if singleton stage-1 isolation proves too aggressive on other data.

**Artifacts preserved at:**
- `dev/runs/aps_clust_ds1_II/` (symlinks to `ds1_aps_*` individual runs)
- `dev/runs/aps_clust_ds2_II/` (symlinks to `ds2_aps_*` individual runs)
- `scripts/aps_cluster_experiments.py`, `scripts/aps_cluster_report.py`
- `multi-agent/project_context/DECISIONS.md` (full tables, revised M-shape criterion-4)
- `multi-agent/KNOWN_ISSUES.md` (posterior QC design + stage-1 anchoring design)

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 11 known-issues — APS clustering investigation (ds1+II, ds2+II); new features and experiment infrastructure

## [v0.10.35] — 2026-04-17 EDT

- Fixed posterior output prefix bug in `onionskin.py::_run_posterior()`: `build_output_layout`
  was called with `out_prefix=None`, causing all posterior output files to be named
  `onionskin_*` (the hardcoded default) regardless of the user's `--out-prefix` value.
  Changed to pass `getattr(args, "out_prefix", None)` so the posterior arm uses the same
  prefix as the prior arm.
- Added regression test `test_posterior_output_files_use_user_out_prefix` in
  `tests/test_pipeline.py` that runs a prior+posterior toy pipeline with `--out-prefix myproject`
  and asserts that posterior `02-posterior/01-multistage-growth/myproject_calls.tsv` exists
  and `onionskin_calls.tsv` does not.
- Diagnosed the "posterior growth looks identical to prior" KNOWN_ISSUE: the posterior
  computation is correct (stage summaries, calls, and growth tracks all differ from prior);
  the bug was purely the filename prefix, which made posterior files look like a default run.
  Removed this item as a computation concern; it is resolved by the prefix fix above.

**Known Issue** [2026-04-13] Posterior growth track appears identical to prior — RESOLVED v0.10.35 - Diagnosed 2026-04-17. Root cause was a filename prefix bug, not a computation error.
`_run_posterior()` called `build_output_layout(out_prefix=None)`, causing all posterior
files to be named `onionskin_*` regardless of `--out-prefix`. The posterior computation
itself was correct (stage summaries, calls, and growth tracks all genuinely differed from
prior). Fixed in `onionskin.py` and covered by regression test
`test_posterior_output_files_use_user_out_prefix` in `tests/test_pipeline.py`.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.92 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 11 known-issues — posterior prefix fix and regression test

## [v0.10.31] — 2026-04-13 15:28 EDT

- Continued Phase 11.1 ownership cleanup by removing the remaining multistage runtime dependence on `organize_outputs()`.
  - Prior and posterior multistage growth runs no longer call `organize_outputs()` after the engine returns, because the grouped multistage prefix is now the canonical write location rather than an intermediate flat prefix.
  - `onionskin_core/output_layout.py::organize_outputs()` is now explicitly documented and guarded as a legacy flat-prefix organizer rather than as the normal multistage path.
- Audited helper consumers for grouped-growth path assumptions and updated the remaining user-facing offender:
  - `scripts/summit_inspector.py` now prefers canonical grouped growth paths before older flat/root discovery patterns when locating `_origins.tsv` and `05-aps/genome_stage_medians/`.
  - Added regression coverage in `tests/test_pipeline.py` that locks in grouped multistage outputs and asserts that the growth controller no longer leaves root-level `_calls.tsv`, `_origins.tsv`, `_timing.tsv`, or recommendation BED leftovers behind.
- Updated `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` so it no longer describes multistage growth organization as a post-hoc relocation step.

## [v0.10.15] — 2026-04-12 16:10 EDT

- Added `--force-manifest` for manifest-backed runs as an explicit compatibility path for non-canonical stage labels.
- Strict manifest validation remains the default; non-canonical manifests still fail clearly, but the error now points users to `--force-manifest` when they intentionally want onionskin to remap stage labels into the runtime `1..N` contract.
- Forced-manifest runs now write a canonical runtime `01-prior/00-synthetic-manifest.tsv` plus `00-stage-translation.tsv`, and do the same for hires manifests via deterministic `00-synthetic-hires-manifest_{i}.tsv` and `00-stage-translation.hires_{i}.tsv` sidecars.
- Hires manifests are not blindly coerced to match the primary manifest: the force path first checks row count and basename order, then verifies that the hires stage-assignment pattern induces the same canonical grouping pattern as the primary before remapping.
- Posterior remains supported under `--force-manifest` because the translated manifest is then treated like any other canonical runtime manifest; posterior hires manifests continue to reuse the same sample-order contract.
- Updated the `--hires-manifest` help text to document the same-sample / same-order / matching-filename expectation across resolutions.
- Added focused regression coverage for strict failure without `--force-manifest`, successful forced remap with hires and posterior, and clean rejection of hires manifests whose pre-remap group assignments do not actually match the primary manifest.
- Validation: focused `tests/test_pipeline.py` force-manifest slice PASS (`4 passed, 22 deselected`), `make test` PASS (`37 passed`), `make single` PASS (`2 / 2`), and `make toy` PASS.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

## How to read this file

**Versioning:** Entries use `[v0.X.YY]` format.
- `0` = pre-release (bumps to `1` at first public release)
- `X` = major phase, aligned with ROADMAP Phase numbers (e.g., `v0.3.xx` = Phase 3 work, `v0.4.xx` = Phase 4 work). Bump `X` when beginning a new major ROADMAP phase.
- `YY` = zero-indexed iteration counter within a phase, incrementing by 1 per development session (first entry in a new phase = `v0.X.00`).

**Timestamps:** Entry headers include a full date+time stamp: `## [v0.X.YY] — YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM EST`. All times are Eastern Time (EST/EDT). Month-level resolution is too coarse when multiple sessions happen in one day. Some early entries use `~HH:MM` to indicate an approximate retroactive timestamp.

**Authorship:** Each entry includes an `**Authors:**` line immediately before the `**Roadmap:**` line, listing the human and agent(s) involved. Retroactive entries before v0.5.19 do not have authorship lines. See `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md` for the per-agent format spec.

**Cross-references:** Each entry includes a `**Roadmap:**` line pointing to the relevant ROADMAP priority or phase.

**Pre-convention entries** (`[Phase x.xx]` format, v0.3.29 and earlier) are historical — do not rename them.

---

## Development authorship overview

| Era | Period | Primary agents |
|-----|--------|----------------|
| ChatGPT era | project inception – 2026-03-21 | John M. Urban + ChatGPT (GPT-4o). Architectural design, APS conception, biological framing, early implementation. Code transferred manually to the repo. |
| Claude Code era | 2026-03-22 – present | John M. Urban + Claude Code (claude-sonnet-4-6, primary; claude-opus-4 used for deep reasoning at one point; claude-haiku-4 for lightweight checks). Direct file editing and shell execution. |
| Multi-agent era | 2026-03 – present (overlaps) | Claude Code + Codex + Gemini CLI + Gemini Code Assist + GitHub Copilot (agent mode) running concurrently. Authorship tracked per entry from v0.5.19 onward. |

Retroactive timestamp note: entries v0.4.01–v0.5.18 that were missing `HH:MM` or `EST`
have been corrected with approximate times (marked `~`) in v0.5.29. Exact times for those
sessions are unknown; the `~` prefix signals approximation.

## [v0.10.11] — 2026-04-12 09:12 EDT

- Landed the first concrete dependency-graph refactor step for Priority 10.2 by removing the growth engine's public `--skip-per-stage` flag and deleting its hidden internal per-stage side-pipeline.
- Pipeline composition is now controller-owned rather than growth-engine-owned; mixed growth+per-stage runs still work because `onionskin.py` already sequences those lanes explicitly.
- Updated the live Phase 10 execution plan to define the safe migration order: remove engine-owned side-pipeline control first, then lift shared stage-structured preparation above the engines, then formalize controller-side dependency nodes, and only then attach downstream cross-pipeline synthesis.
- Updated `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` so per-stage is described as an independent controller-owned lane rather than a sidecar launched by the growth engine.
- Added focused regression coverage proving the multistage engine help no longer exposes the legacy per-stage control flags, and aligned the two-stage per-stage tests with the already-landed stage-aware default semantics by adding explicit override coverage for the standalone stage-structured path.
- Validation: focused `tests/test_pipeline.py` selection PASS (`5 passed, 15 deselected`) and broader Phase 10.2 focused PASS (`13 passed, 8 deselected`).

## [v0.10.10] — 2026-04-12 08:55 EDT

- Landed the next Priority 10.2 normalization-default slice by giving `--growth-norm-mode`, `--per-stage-norm-mode`, and `--hmm-norm-mode` real stage-aware default behavior instead of using them only as `inherit` shims.
- Growth now defaults to `chrom-median`.
- HMM and per-stage now default to `ref-stage` when 2+ stages are present with the configured reference stage, and otherwise fall back to the shared global `--norm-mode`.
- The explicit `inherit` option remains available as a compatibility override when a pipeline should follow the shared global `--norm-mode` instead of its stage-aware default.
- Updated `onionskin.py -h` help text and focused tests to document and validate the new semantics.
- Updated the live Phase 10 planning surface (`PHASE10_SPEC`, `ROADMAP`, `BRAINSTORM`, `HANDOFF`, `TASK`) and repo memory to reflect the new default-resolution contract.
- Validation: focused `tests/test_pipeline.py` selections passed (`6 passed, 13 deselected`; `11 passed, 8 deselected`).

**Roadmap:** Phase 10, Priority 10.2 stage-aware pipeline normalization defaults

## [v0.10.09] — 2026-04-12 08:47 EDT

- Landed the next Priority 10.2 uncoupling / UX slice by removing the top-level controller's dependence on the multistage engine's hidden per-stage side-pipeline when both `growth` and `per-stage` are requested.
- Combined growth+per-stage runs now execute growth first with `--skip-per-stage`, then run per-stage independently through the standalone per-stage controller.
- This top-level uncoupling means divergent growth-vs-per-stage normalization requests can now run cleanly in the same combined run instead of being rejected only because the two lanes shared one controller path.
- Updated `onionskin.py -h` group labels away from the older `Single-sample specific` / `Multistage specific` wording toward the newer continuum framing: `Per-stage / single-stage detection` and `Growth-model stage-structured`.
- Updated the live Phase 10 planning surface (`PHASE10_SPEC`, `ROADMAP`, `BRAINSTORM`, `HANDOFF`, `TASK`) and repo memory to reflect the new controller uncoupling and CLI language.
- Validation: focused `tests/test_pipeline.py` selection covering the new help headings, uncoupled growth+per-stage run, explicit two-stage HMM no-ratio forcing, and standalone per-stage behavior; PASS (`14 passed, 3 deselected`).

## [v0.10.08] — 2026-04-12 08:36 EDT

- Landed the next Priority 10.2 normalization-contract slice by introducing pipeline-specific normalization overrides at the top-level CLI: `--growth-norm-mode`, `--per-stage-norm-mode`, and `--hmm-norm-mode`.
- These new flags currently default to `inherit`, preserving the legacy shared `--norm-mode` behavior unless the user explicitly requests a pipeline-specific override.
- The controller now resolves its normalization contract from the requested pipeline set rather than assuming the shared global `--norm-mode` always describes every pipeline equally well.
- The HMM engine now accepts an explicit preprocessing normalization mode, so a two-stage HMM run can request chromosome-median no-ratio preprocessing directly instead of being forced onto the ratio-backed branch only because multiple groups are present.
- Currently unsupported divergent growth-vs-per-stage normalization requests inside the same combined run now fail explicitly instead of being flattened silently.
- Updated the live Phase 10 planning surface (`PHASE10_SPEC`, `ROADMAP`, `BRAINSTORM`, `HANDOFF`, `TASK`) and repo memory to reflect the new normalization-contract split.
- Validation: focused `tests/test_pipeline.py` selection covering the new CLI flags, explicit HMM no-ratio forcing on two-stage input, explicit HMM ref-stage rejection on one-stage input, and divergent growth/per-stage normalization conflicts; PASS (`14 passed, 3 deselected`).

## [v0.10.07] — 2026-04-12 08:09 EDT

- Landed the first implementation pass for the revised Priority 10.2 fix-up work rather than continuing only at the controller-planning layer.
- The no-ratio HMM branch now runs a dedicated step 3 gap-removal pass before chromosome-median normalization instead of skipping step 3 entirely.
- The current no-ratio step-3 rule is conservative by design: a bin is removed only when it is zero-valued across all step-2 group-median bedGraphs, using the existing gap-mask logic as a shared assembly-gap detector rather than as a generic file-specific zero-bin filter.
- Manifest stages are now enforced as onionskin's canonical `1..N` developmental-order contract; non-canonical manifests now fail explicitly instead of being silently renumbered.

## [v0.9.25] — 2026-04-11 08:11 EST

**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4)

**Roadmap:** Phase 9 parity diagnosis / corrected v2 baseline generation

- **Corrected v2 reference baselines:**
  - Added `scripts/generate_hmm_v2_references.py` to deterministically rebuild `dev/puffstep/automate/5kb-v2/` and `dev/puffstep/automate/5kb-corechrom-v2/` from the current validated full-genome native HMM run.
  - Generated `5kb-v2` and `5kb-corechrom-v2` as corrected step-9/10 baselines that preserve steps 01–08 from the legacy reference while replacing HMM summit outputs for stages 2–5 with the current validated native outputs.
- **Parity diagnosis result:**
  - Verified that the current full-genome native HMM run (`dev/runs/puffstep-py-fullgenome/03-hmm`) matches `5kb-v2` exactly: `06-HMM` 4/4 pass, `07-collapsedHMM` 4/4 pass, `08-aboveBackground` 8/8 pass, `09-summitStates` 4/4 pass, `10-summitBins` 8/8 pass, overall 28 files matched.
  - This rules out a new native HMM math / transition-matrix drift in the full-genome parity path.
  - Verified that the current core-only parity run still fails against `5kb-corechrom-v2` across steps 06–10, confirming that the remaining 1–3 bin state/boundary differences are introduced by the newer core-only rerun workflow rather than by the corrected step-9/10 summit semantics.
- **Interpretation:**
  - The remaining core-only diffs are now classified as a workflow-definition issue: the current `make puffstep-py` recomputes the HMM on `--chromosomes X,II,III,IV` before step 1, while `5kb-corechrom` was derived by filtering a full-genome legacy reference after the fact.
  - Because chromosome filtering now happens before median normalization, union statistics, chromosomal RCN normalization, smoothing, and HMM calling, modest step-6 state-path shifts propagate forward into steps 07–10.
- **Audit trail:**
  - Added `multi-agent/audits/2026-04-11_corechrom_parity_diagnosis.md` documenting the corrected-v2 baseline generation, the full-genome pass against `5kb-v2`, the remaining core-only failures against `5kb-corechrom-v2`, and the conclusion that the open drift is methodological rather than a newly introduced HMM-core bug.

## [v0.9.00] — 2026-04-09 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 9.1 — HMM-derived replication metrics (nesting-level extraction)

- **`onionskin_core/hmm_metrics.py`** (new module): Phase 9.1 implementation.

  - `NestingLevel` dataclass: one nesting level within an amplicon's state path.
    Fields: `level_idx` (0 = innermost/highest state), `state_value`, `left_bp`,
    `right_bp`, `domain_length_bp`.

  - `AmpliconMetrics` dataclass: per-amplicon per-stage nesting metrics.
    Fields: `amplicon_id`, `chrom`, `amplicon_start/end`, `stage_label`, `n_levels`
    (complexity score), `levels` list, `summit_state_left/right_bp`, `summit_state_value`,
    `origin_bp` (center of summit state interval), `active_window_left/right_bp`.

  - `extract_nesting_levels` logic: for each unique state S in the amplicon (sorted
    descending), the level domain is the span from leftmost to rightmost bin with
    state ≥ S. Level 0 = innermost (highest state). Level N-1 = outermost (lowest
    non-background state, spanning the full amplicon). Background-transition = oldest
    forks.

  - `_build_summit_map()`: matches each step-9 summit row to its parent amplicon by
    containment. Fallback: if a summit is missing, uses the highest-state bin span.

  - `compute_amplicon_metrics_for_stage()`: public API for one stage. Reads step-8
    BED + bedGraph + step-9 summit BED; returns `List[AmpliconMetrics]`.

  - `run_step11_amplicon_metrics()`: step 11 entry point. Parallel per-stage
    processing via `ThreadPoolExecutor`. Writes one TSV per stage plus
    `all_stages.hmm_amplicon_metrics.tsv` (combined, sorted by stage then amplicon).
    Column count is consistent across all TSVs (padded with empty strings for
    amplicons with fewer levels than the maximum).

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**:
  - Step 11 (`11-amplicon-metrics/`) added to the pipeline directory dict.
  - `run_hmm()` calls `run_step11_amplicon_metrics()` after step 10. Wired as a
    local import to keep the top-level import footprint clean.
  - Module docstring and pipeline steps list updated to include step 11.

- **`multi-agent/full_instructions/PHASE9_SPEC.md`** (new file): Phase 9 implementation
  spec for agents. Covers all 6 sub-priorities: output layouts, data structures,
  function signatures, coding conventions, testing expectations, and what to update
  when each sub-priority completes. Registered in `CLAUDE.md` as a required read
  for Phase 9 work.

- **`multi-agent/full_instructions/PHASE9_SPEC.md`**, **`ROADMAP.md`**,
  **`multi-agent/BRAINSTORM.md`**: Phase 9 scope fully planned in this session.
  ROADMAP Phase 9 rewritten to reflect HMM-as-standalone-pipeline framing (6 priorities).
  BRAINSTORM gains a Phase 10 cross-pipeline synthesis section.

- **Output (step 11) validated manually**: `puffstep-py` re-run confirms correct TSV
  output. Example: `II:6065000-6335000` stage_2 → n_levels=2, level_0 state=3
  [6195000-6255000] (60 kb), level_1 state=2 [6065000-6335000] (270 kb),
  origin_bp=6225000 (center of summit state interval).

- **Pre-existing regression noted (NOT introduced here)**: `make puff-compare` step 9/10
  failures were confirmed pre-existing before Phase 9 — fresh `puffstep-py` runs have
  produced fewer step-9 lines than the reference since Phase 8. Previous PASS results
  were against a stale run directory. Tracked for separate investigation.

- **Validation**: `make test` PASS. `make puff-compare` step 9/10 failures confirmed
  pre-existing (not introduced by Phase 9.1).

## [v0.8.04] — 2026-04-09 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8 close-out — step 8 above-background filter tunability

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**: `_step8_above_background()` gains five
  optional parameters (all default to gold-standard pipeline behavior — no behavior change).

  - `thresh_state=1`: keep bins with state > this value. Port of PuffStep `--thresh_state`.
  - `merge1=0`: bridge gaps ≤ this many bp when first merging above-threshold bins into
    regions. Default 0 = touching/overlapping only (matches `mergeBed -i` with no `-d`).
    Port of PuffStep summits `--merge1`.
  - `min_width=0`: drop regions narrower than this many bp. Default 0 = no filter.
    Port of PuffStep summits `--minwidth`.
  - `merge2=0`: second merge pass (gap in bp) after width filter. Default 0 = skip.
    Port of PuffStep summits `--merge2`.
  - `max_state_thresh=0`: drop regions whose max state ≤ this value. Default 0 = no filter.
    Port of PuffStep summits `--max_state_thresh`.

  Added `_merge_with_gap()` helper: merge intervals within `gap` bp, identical to
  `merge_bed_intervals` when gap=0.

  `run_hmm()` gains the same five parameters; step 8 call site wired.

- **`onionskin.py`**: Five new CLI flags wired end-to-end:
  `--hmm-thresh-state`, `--hmm-merge1`, `--hmm-min-width`, `--hmm-merge2`,
  `--hmm-max-state-thresh`. All default to current behavior; opt-in only.

- **Design rationale recorded:** The gold-standard bash pipeline (`puffStepPipeline.parallel.sh`)
  intentionally does NOT use PuffStep's `find_candidate_regions()` multi-filter logic.
  Step 8 is simply `state > 1 | mergeBed`, which is what onionskin implemented by default.
  The new flags add the PuffStep filter options as opt-in for harder datasets, without
  changing any default behavior.

- **`multi-agent/BRAINSTORM.md`**: New section "HMM step 9 — summit state robustness"
  documenting the known weakness (spurious high-state summit if HMM misbehaves) and four
  potential future solutions: structural validation, cross-pipeline consensus, light
  state-path median smoothing (likely best), and central-window summit search.

- **Validation**: `make test` (PASS), `make puff-compare` (OVERALL PASS, 24 match, 4 skipped).

## [v0.8.03] — 2026-04-09 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8.4 — retire PuffStep runtime; Phase 8 CLI close-out

### Phase 8.4: PuffStep runtime dependency retired

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**: Removed all PuffStep runtime code.
  - Removed `_find_puffstep_root()` and `_puffstep_tools()`.
  - Removed `ONIONSKIN_HMM_STEP6_BACKEND` env-var check.
  - Removed `step6_backend` parameter from `run_hmm()` and `_step6_hmm()`.
  - Removed `tools: Dict[str, str]` parameter from `_step6_hmm()`.
  - Removed the entire `elif backend == "puffstep":` subprocess block from `_step6_hmm`.
  - Updated module docstring: no longer delegates to PuffStep at runtime.
  - `make puff-compare` OVERALL PASS (24 match, 4 skipped) confirmed after removal.

### New HMM CLI flags (full PuffStep CLI parity + one new)

- **`--hmm-exp-decay on|off`** (default: `on`):
  Apply exponential distance decay to transition probabilities. When `on` (default),
  transitions between states farther apart in copy-number space are penalised
  exponentially (scaled by `--hmm-exp-decay-scale`). When `off`, all non-self transitions
  have equal probability (uniform). Only applies when transition probs are derived from
  `--hmm-leave-special-state` / `--hmm-leave-other`; ignored when `--hmm-transprobs` is
  provided. Port of PuffStep `--exp_decay` flag.
  - `hmm_core.py`: Added `_get_transition_probs_uniform()` for the `off` path.
  - Removed the old `raise ValueError("exp_decay=True required")` guard.

- **`--hmm-transprobs MATRIX`** (default: None):
  Provide a full (nstates × nstates) transition matrix as semicolon-separated rows of
  comma-separated values. Example: `'0.99,0.01;0.01,0.99'`. When provided, overrides
  `--hmm-exp-decay`, `--hmm-exp-decay-scale`, `--hmm-leave-special-state`, and
  `--hmm-leave-other` entirely. Matrix is row-normalised before use. Port of PuffStep
  `--transprobs`. Wired: `onionskin.py` → `run_hmm` → `_step6_hmm` →
  `write_hmm_states_bedgraph` → `hmm_state_path_for_bedgraph`.

- **`--hmm-learn-pseudo FLOAT`** (default: 1e-300):
  Pseudocount added to transition and initial-state counts during Viterbi training
  re-estimation (default ~0; effectively prevents hard zeros in the transition matrix
  when a state transition is never observed in a given chromosome). Port of PuffStep
  `--learnpseudo`. Ignored by Baum-Welch training (which handles zeros via soft
  assignment naturally). Wired through the full stack to `_update_tprobs` and
  `_update_iprobs` in `hmm_core.py`.

- **Confirmed already present:** `--hmm-converge` was already implemented (v0.7.26).

### Threading throughout

All three new parameters (`exp_decay`, `learnpseudo`, `transition_probs`) wired through:
`onionskin.py` → `run_hmm()` → `_step6_hmm()` → `write_hmm_states_bedgraph()` →
`hmm_state_path_for_bedgraph()`.

- **Validation**: `make test` (PASS), `make puff-compare` (OVERALL PASS, 24 match, 4 skipped).

---

## [v0.8.02] — 2026-04-09 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8.3 close-out — final PuffStep audit; winsorize + chromosome-median primitive

- **`onionskin_core/signal_utils.py`**: Two additions from final PuffStep audit.

  - `winsorize_bedgraph(input, output, floor=0.00001, ceiling=0.99999)`:
    Port of PuffStep `CovBedClass.global_winsorize_counts()`. Computes floor/ceiling quantiles
    across all chromosomes combined, then clips values to that range. Useful for outlier removal
    before HMM on noisy data. `floor`/`ceiling` are quantile levels (not values).

  - `chromosome_median_normalize_bedgraph(input, output)`:
    Port of PuffStep `CovBedClass.chromosome_median_normalize_data()`. Single-sample primitive:
    divides each bin by the median of its chromosome. Fallback chain when median == 0: mean;
    when mean == 0: set all bins on that chromosome to 1.0.
    **Relationship to protocol 31:** `chromosome_ratio_normalize_bedgraph` (p31) does
    ratio-normalize (test/control) first, then this chromosome-median step. They share identical
    fallback logic — this exposes the chromosome-median step as a standalone single-sample function
    for cases where no control is present. The core logic was already embedded in p31; this just
    makes it independently callable.

- **Final PuffStep audit complete.** Comprehensive review of all 10 PuffStep files:
  `puffStep.py`, `CovBedClass.py`, `hmm_fxns.py`, `cn.py`, `puff.py`, `findsummits.py`,
  `normalize.py`, `utils.py`, and both utility scripts. Findings:
  - `puff.py`: exact duplicate of onionskin's `hmm_core.py` — nothing to port.
  - `hmm_fxns.py`: all HMM math fully native in onionskin — nothing to port.
  - `local_winsorize_counts`: stub with just `pass` in PuffStep — never implemented, skip.
  - `s50` (stage-at-50%-reads helper): low-priority; not relevant to current work.
  - `MultiCovBed` secondary class: legacy/experimental; not used in main pipeline.
  - All CLI flags and subcommands (`normalize`, `puffcn`, `summits`): fully accounted for.
  - p16 + p17: already recorded as will-not-port (DECISIONS.md).

- **Validation**: `make test` (PASS).

---

## [v0.8.01] — 2026-04-09 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8.3 — Gaussian kernel smoothing; close out all PuffStep signal protocols

- **`onionskin_core/signal_utils.py`**: `gaussian_smooth_bedgraph(input_path, output_path, bandwidth)`.
  Port of PuffStep `CovBedClass.ksmooth_counts()` (R `ksmooth(kernel="normal")`).
  - `bandwidth`: Gaussian σ in base pairs — same units as PuffStep's `--bandwidth` flag
    (default 10000 bp in PuffStep).
  - Converts to bin-index σ: `sigma = bandwidth / bin_size`, where `bin_size` is inferred
    from the first bin of each chromosome.
  - Uses `scipy.ndimage.gaussian_filter1d(mode="reflect")` — reflect boundary is a practical
    approximation of R's linear-extrapolation boundary behaviour.
  - Operates per-chromosome (same as all other signal_utils functions).
  - Unlocks PuffStep protocols 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 13, 14, 15, all of which are compositions of
    `gaussian_smooth_bedgraph` + existing primitives (`median_normalize_bedgraph`,
    `ratio_bedgraph`). No additional code needed for those protocols — they are fully
    composable at the call site.

- **PuffStep protocol coverage — final accounting:**
  - All portworthy protocols now implemented (Cat 1–4 complete).
  - **p16** (glocal median ratio norm): **explicitly skipped — will not be ported.**
    Reason: marked `INDEV` in PuffStep source; not production-ready.
  - **p17** (median norm + scale-to-target-coverage + ratio + re-median norm):
    **explicitly skipped — will not be ported.**
    Reason: obscure pipeline; `scale_data` primitive not needed elsewhere; not part of
    any standard workflow.
  - These decisions are recorded in ROADMAP.md and PUFFSTEP-INTEGRATION-PLAN.md.
    Do not revisit them without a concrete use case.

- **Validation**: `make test` (PASS). `make puff-compare` not re-run (no HMM logic changed).

---

## [v0.8.00] — 2026-04-08 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8.3 — signal utilities expansion and signal transforms module

- **`onionskin_core/signal_utils.py`**: Nine new normalization/smoothing functions ported from
  PuffStep `normalize.py` and `CovBedClass.py`. All operate on bedGraph files (path in, path out).
  - `_safe_local_median(x, play_it_safe, extreme)`: internal fallback helper. If median of window
    is 0, tries trimmed mean → mean → 1.0. Port of PuffStep `_safe_median_`.
  - `_local_window_apply(values, halfwidth, fn)`: shared window helper. Boundary convention matches
    PuffStep exactly: first `A` bins use `[0:A+halfwidth+1]`, interior uses symmetric window,
    last `L-Z-1` bins use `[Z-halfwidth:L]`.
  - `local_median_normalize_bedgraph(input, output, halfwidth)`: Protocol 30. Divides each bin by
    its local-window median (play_it_safe fallback enabled). DISTINCT from
    `local_median_smooth_bedgraph` (p32) which replaces values with the median rather than dividing.
  - `local_mean_smooth_bedgraph(input, output, halfwidth)`: Protocol 34. Replaces each bin with
    the local-window mean.
  - `local_trimmed_mean_smooth_bedgraph(input, output, halfwidth, extreme=0.25)`: Protocol 33.
    Replaces each bin with the trimmed window mean (excludes bottom and top `extreme` fraction).
  - `spmr_normalize_bedgraph(input, output, scale=1e6)`: Protocol 22. Divides all values by total
    sum × scale (signal per million reads by default). Port of PuffStep `spmr_normalize`.
  - `robust_z_normalize_bedgraph(input, output)`: Protocol 18. `(x - median) / MAD`. Falls back to
    std if MAD == 0. Port of PuffStep `robust_z_score_normalize`.
  - `rank_normalize_bedgraph(input, output)`: Protocol 19. Replaces values with fractional rank
    (average method for ties). Port of PuffStep `rank_normalize`.
  - `rank_standardize_bedgraph(input, output)`: Protocol 23. `(rank - midpoint) / midpoint`;
    centered at 0, approximately bounded ±1. Port of PuffStep `rank_standardize`.

- **`onionskin_core/signal_transforms.py`** (new file): Signal transformation utilities for
  biological signal analysis. Distinct from normalization (which corrects technical scale) — these
  convert coverage into a different biological representation.
  - Self-contained: includes its own `_load_rows`, `_write_rows`, `_by_chrom` helpers to avoid
    circular imports with `signal_utils`.
  - `compute_skew_bedgraph(input, output)`: Replication Fork Direction (RFD).
    `(V[i]-V[i-1])/(V[i]+V[i-1]) * 100`. Port of PuffStep `CovBedClass.computeSkew()`.
    First bin per chromosome = 0. Division-by-zero safe. Used in OK-seq/SNS-seq/EdU-seq analyses.
  - `compute_percent_change_bedgraph(input, output)`: `(V[i]-V[i-1])/V[i-1] * 100`. Port of
    PuffStep `CovBedClass.computePercentChange()`. First bin per chromosome = 0.
  - `compute_skew_change_bedgraph(input, output)`: Derivative of RFD / 200. Port of PuffStep
    `CovBedClass.computeSkewChange()`. Positive peaks → replication origins; negative peaks →
    termination zones. Division by 200 normalizes for ×100 in skew and ÷2 in derivative.
  - `log2_bedgraph(input, output, pseudocount=0.0)`: Log₂-transform.
  - `log10_bedgraph(input, output, pseudocount=0.0)`: Log₁₀-transform.
  - `ratio_bedgraph(test, control, output, pseudocount=0.01)`: `(test+pseudo)/(control+pseudo)`.
    Port of PuffStep `normalize_to_other`.
  - `subtract_bedgraph(test, control, output)`: `test - control`. Port of `subtract_other`.
  - `pct_diff_bedgraph(test, control, output, pseudocount=0.0)`: `100*(test-control)/control`.
    Asymmetric; treats control as reference. Port of `pct_diff_from_other`.
  - `pct_skew_bedgraph(test, control, output, pseudocount=0.0)`:
    `100*(test-control)/(|test|+|control|)`. Symmetric, bounded ±100. Port of `pct_skew_given_other`.

- **Deferred**: Kernel smoothing (PuffStep protocols 2/3/4/5/6/13/14/15 use R's `ksmooth` via
  `rpy2` — would need `scipy.ndimage.gaussian_filter1d` as substitute). Glocal median ratio
  normalization (protocol 16, marked `INDEV` in PuffStep source). These can be added later.

- **Validation**: `make test` (PASS), `make puff-compare` (24 match, 4 skipped — OVERALL PASS).

---

## [v0.7.27] — 2026-04-09 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8 (new) — true Baum-Welch (soft EM) training mode

- **`onionskin_core/hmm_core.py`**: Baum-Welch (soft EM) training, new beyond PuffStep.
  - `_forward_loglik()`: forward pass returning both scaled fwd matrix and log P(obs|model),
    used for convergence tracking in BW (sum of log scale factors = log-likelihood exactly).
  - `_bw_estep_one()`: E-step for one chromosome — computes gamma (nstates×T posterior
    state occupancy) and xi_sum (nstates×nstates accumulated transition expectations)
    from scaled fwd × bwd. Normalizes per time step.
  - `_bw_mstep_transitions()`: M-step transition update from accumulated xi sums.
  - `_bw_mstep_initial()`: M-step initial state update from gamma at t=0.
  - `_bw_mstep_emissions()`: M-step emission update using gamma-weighted sufficient
    statistics. Normal: weighted mean + variance. Gamma: method-of-moments
    (shape=mean²/var, scale=var/mean). Exponential/Poisson: weighted mean.
    Geometric: p=1/weighted_mean. `constrain=True` leaves params unchanged.
  - `_baum_welch_train()`: full soft-EM loop — accumulates E-step stats across all
    chromosomes, runs M-step, checks convergence on total log-likelihood, returns
    updated (eprobs, tprobs, iprobs). Caller does a final decode pass.
  - `hmm_state_path_for_bedgraph` gains `training: str` param (`"viterbi"` default,
    `"baum_welch"` for soft EM). When `training="baum_welch"` and `iters>1`, runs BW
    to convergence then does a final Viterbi/posterior decode.
  - `write_hmm_states_bedgraph` gains same param.

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**: `training` param wired through
  `run_hmm()` → `_step6_hmm()` → `write_hmm_states_bedgraph`.

- **`onionskin.py`**: `--hmm-training viterbi|baum_welch` added. Default: `viterbi`
  (PuffStep-compatible). `baum_welch` is the new experimental mode.

- **Validation**: `make test` (4 passed), `make puff-compare` (24 match, 4 skipped — OVERALL PASS).
  Smoke test: both training modes run cleanly with iters=3.

---

## [v0.7.26] — 2026-04-09 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8.1c — iterative parameter re-estimation (Viterbi training) native

- **`onionskin_core/hmm_core.py`**: Viterbi training loop and supporting helpers.
  - `_log10_prob_path()`: log10 probability of a decoded state path; port of PuffStep
    `log10_prob_state_path()`. Used for convergence check between iterations.
  - `_update_eprobs()`: re-estimate emission parameters from hard-assigned state path;
    port of PuffStep `learn_eprobs()`. Updates mean/sigma (normal/gamma), mean (exponential/
    poisson), or p=1/mean (geometric). `constrain=True` leaves params unchanged.
  - `_update_tprobs()`: count transitions in decoded path, add pseudocount, row-normalize;
    port of PuffStep `learn_tprobs()`.
  - `_update_iprobs()`: count initial states across chromosomes; port of PuffStep `learn_iprobs()`.
  - `hmm_state_path_for_bedgraph` gains `iters`, `converge`, `constrain_emit`, `emitpseudo`.
    When `iters > 1`: decode → log10prob → convergence check → update params → repeat.
    **Note:** this is Viterbi training (hard EM), matching PuffStep's `do_hmm_iter_steps()`.
    PuffStep also did not implement true Baum-Welch (soft EM with γ/ξ); that remains a
    future addition (Phase 9 or later).
  - `write_hmm_states_bedgraph` gains same params.

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**: all Tier 3 options now native. Removed the
  `tier3_nondefault_unimplemented` block entirely. `iters/converge/constrain_emit/emitpseudo`
  wired through to `write_hmm_states_bedgraph`.

- **Validation**: `make test` (4 passed), `make puff-compare` (24 match, 4 skipped — OVERALL PASS).

---

## [v0.7.25] — 2026-04-09 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8.1b — k-means emission initialization native

- **`onionskin_core/hmm_core.py`**: added `_kmeans_init_emissions(data, nstates)`.
  Uses `scipy.cluster.vq.kmeans`/`vq` to cluster all bedGraph values into `nstates` groups.
  Returns (means, sigmas) sorted ascending by mean; sigma floor = 1e-6. Port of PuffStep
  `get_state_emissions_from_kmeans()` (R/rpy2 removed in PuffStep; re-implemented in numpy/scipy).
  `hmm_state_path_for_bedgraph` gains `kmeans: Optional[int]`; if set, concatenates all
  chromosome data, runs k-means, and overrides `emission_means`/`emission_sigmas`.
  `write_hmm_states_bedgraph` gains same `kmeans` param.
- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**: `kmeans` wired through to native
  `write_hmm_states_bedgraph` call. Removed `kmeans is not None` from
  `tier3_nondefault_unimplemented` block. Remaining blocked: iters/converge/constrain_emit/emitpseudo.
- **Validation**: `make test` (4 passed), `make puff-compare` (24 match, 4 skipped — OVERALL PASS).
  Smoke test: k-means with 3 clusters correctly derives means [1.0, 4.0, 8.0] from synthetic data.

## [v0.7.23] — 2026-04-08 21:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 8.1 — native alternative emission models for step-6 HMM

- **`onionskin_core/hmm_core.py`**: generalized emission model support.
  - Added `SUPPORTED_EMODELS = {"normal", "exponential", "poisson", "geometric", "gamma"}`.
  - New `_log_emit_seq(data, eprobs, emodel)` — vectorized log-emission matrix (nstates × T)
    dispatching to the correct scipy distribution. Parameter semantics by model:
    `eprobs[0]` = means/lambda/p/shape; `eprobs[1]` = sigma/scale (unused for exp/poisson/geom).
  - Generalized `_viterbi`, `_forward`, `_backward`, `_posterior_path` — accept `emodel` arg;
    replace the former `_viterbi_normal`, `_forward_normal`, `_backward_normal`, `_posterior_path_normal`.
  - Minor: precompute `log_trans = np.log(transitions)` outside the Viterbi inner loop.
  - `hmm_state_path_for_bedgraph` and `write_hmm_states_bedgraph` accept `emodel` param
    (default `"normal"` — fully backward-compatible).

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**: wired `emodel` through to native backend
  `write_hmm_states_bedgraph` call. Removed emodel from the "Tier 3 native block" — it is
  now native. Remaining blocked options (kmeans, iters, converge, constrain_emit, emitpseudo)
  still require PuffStep fallback until Phase 8.2.

- **`onionskin.py`**: updated `--hmm-emodel` help text to reflect native support for all
  5 models; removed "Native backend currently supports `normal` only" caveat. Dropped
  `discrete` from the list (requires table-format params, deferred).

- **Validation**: `make test` (4 passed), `make puff-compare` (24 match, 4 skipped — OVERALL PASS).
  Smoke test: all 5 emission models (normal, exponential, poisson, geometric, gamma) run without error.

## [v0.7.22] — 2026-04-08 20:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 7.4.5 — eliminate `pybedtools` from steps 9/10; fix protocol-31 sort

- **`onionskin_core/hmm_summits.py`** (new): pure-Python port of PuffStep's
  `findsummits.summits()` + `max_subinterval()` + `CovBedClass.collapsed_bdg()`.
  No `pybedtools`, no `bedtools` binary. Key semantics preserved exactly:
  - Single-bin contig edge case: `start=end=end[0]` (zero-width sentinel, skipped in intersect).
  - Middle-most max-score bin selection: `mid_mi = mi[(nmax+1)//2 - 1]`.
  - Two-pointer sweep intersection per chromosome.

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**: `_step9_summit_states()` and
  `_step10_summit_bins()` updated to import `extract_summits` from `hmm_summits`
  instead of `extract_summits_like_puffstep` from `signal_utils`.

- **`onionskin_core/signal_utils.py`**: removed `extract_summits_like_puffstep()` and its
  orphaned pybedtools body. All `pybedtools`/`StringIO`/`BedTool` references eliminated.
  Added explicit `out.sort(key=lambda r: (r[0], r[1]))` in
  `chromosome_ratio_normalize_bedgraph()` before write (protocol-31 sort correctness).

- **Validation**: `make puff-compare` → 24 match, 4 skipped — OVERALL PASS.
  `make test` → 4 passed. No behavioral regression.

## [v0.7.21] — 2026-04-08 ~18:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 7 review + scope expansion — Phases 8 and 9 defined; dependency policy established

- **`multi-agent/plans/PUFFSTEP-INTEGRATION-PLAN.md`**: Substantially rewritten following
  cross-agent review of Copilot's v0.7.06–v0.7.20 work.
  - Vision statement added: onionskin will fully replace PuffStep; `--pipelines hmm` should
    be able to do anything PuffStep's CLI can do.
  - **Phase 8** added: full PuffStep feature parity — all emission models (exponential,
    Poisson, Gamma, etc.), Baum-Welch re-estimation, posterior decoding, additional
    normalization protocols; retire `ONIONSKIN_HMM_STEP6_BACKEND` and all PuffStep runtime
    dependency when complete.
  - **Phase 9** added: HMM synthesis with onionskin modules — APS, timing, fork asymmetry,
    `--hmm-optimize` adaptive parameter selection, all-stage HMM, unified results.
  - Tier 3 decision documented: native implementation is Phase 8 (not a fallback-only
    stable contract). PuffStep fallback is temporary scaffolding, not a permanent option.
  - Known Phase 7.4.5 issues documented: `pybedtools` in steps 9/10 (must replace with
    pure-Python port from `PuffStep/puffStep_core/findsummits.py`); missing explicit sort
    in `chromosome_ratio_normalize_bedgraph()`.
  - **Dependency policy** added: onionskin must not depend on external binaries.
    `numpy`, `pandas`, `scipy`, `matplotlib` are permitted; `pybedtools`, `pysam`, and
    any library that shells out to `bedtools`, `samtools`, `tabix`, etc. are not permitted.
    Rationale: onionskin is a pure-Python package. PuffStep's own source is pure Python
    and is the correct porting reference.

- **`ROADMAP.md`**: Phase 7 marked substantially complete. Phase 8 (full feature parity)
  and Phase 9 (HMM synthesis) added as explicit phases. Priority 7.4.4 Tier 3 decision
  updated: native implementation is Phase 8 work. Priority 7.4.5 updated with specific
  cleanup targets.

- **`multi-agent/project_context/TASK.md`** and **`HANDOFF.md`**: Updated to reflect
  Phase 7.4.5 as next action (pybedtools replacement first, then protocol-31 sort fix),
  and to document the Phase 8/9 trajectory.

- No runtime or algorithm behavior changed in this entry.

## [v0.7.05] — 2026-04-08 14:30 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 7.3 — Port steps 7 and 8 to native Python (first two migrations)

- **`onionskin_core/signal_utils.py`**: Added two BED/bedGraph utility functions:
  - `collapse_bedgraph_runs(path, tolerance=0.0)` — port of
    `PuffStep/utils/collapseBedGraphRuns.py`; collapses consecutive bedGraph
    bins sharing the same col-4 value into a single interval. Returns a list of
    `(chrom, start, end, val)` tuples. Coordinates need not be contiguous; only
    consecutive rows (in sorted input order) with the same value are collapsed.
  - `merge_bed_intervals(intervals)` — port of `mergeBed` default behaviour; merges
    overlapping or adjacent `(chrom, start, end)` intervals. Sorts input, then merges
    any pair where `start <= prev_end` on the same chromosome.

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`**: Step 7 and step 8 now use native Python;
  no external tool calls remain in either step.
  - Step 7 (`_step7_collapse`): removed subprocess call to `collapseBedGraphRuns.py` +
    `sort`; now calls `collapse_bedgraph_runs()` and sorts in Python. `tools` parameter
    removed from function signature and call site. `"collapse"` key removed from
    `_puffstep_tools()`.
  - Step 8 (`_step8_above_background`): removed remaining subprocess calls (`sort` and
    `mergeBed`). Filter, sort, bedGraph write, and BED merge are all now pure Python
    using `merge_bed_intervals()`.

- Validated: `make puff-compare` — **24 files match, 4 skipped — OVERALL PASS**.
  Steps 07 and 08 in gold-standard comparison continue to pass after both migrations.

## [v0.7.03] — 2026-04-08 12:30 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 7.2 — `make puff-compare` + `scripts/compare_puffstep_outputs.py`

- **`scripts/compare_puffstep_outputs.py`** (new): Reusable validation script comparing
  HMM pipeline outputs (steps 06–10) from any run directory against the gold-standard
  reference (`dev/puffstep/automate/5kb/`). Comparison logic per step:
  - Steps 06–09: exact bin/interval/state match after sort by (chrom, start)
  - Step 10 smoothed (`*medSmoothed*`): exact interval match
  - Step 10 raw: exact cols 1-5, float tolerance (1e-6) on RCN col 6
  - Empty reference files skipped with SKIP (known gold-standard gap)
  - Files missing from run reported as MISSING (causes failure)
  - Files extra in run reported as EXTRA (informational; `--strict-extra` to fail)
  Prints per-file PASS/FAIL lines and a summary table. Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = any fail.

- **`Makefile`**: Added `make puff-compare` target. Runs
  `scripts/compare_puffstep_outputs.py` against `dev/puffstep/automate/5kb/` vs
  `dev/runs/puffstep-py/03-hmm` by default. Override with `make puff-compare RUN=<dir>`.

- Validated: `make puff-compare` reports **24 files match, 4 skipped** (empty raw
  summit bins in gold standard) — OVERALL PASS on steps 06–10.

## [v0.7.02] — 2026-04-08 11:30 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 7.1 — `--pipelines` composable flag; remove `--skip-per-stage`, `--run-hmm`, `--hmm-only`, `--skip-hmm`

- **`onionskin.py`**: Replaced four pipeline-control flags (`--skip-per-stage`, `--run-hmm`,
  `--hmm-only`, `--skip-hmm`) with a single composable `--pipelines LIST` flag.
  Accepts a comma-separated list of pipeline names (`growth`, `per-stage`, `hmm`) or
  `all`. Default is `all` (growth + per-stage; hmm is opt-in until Phase 7 is complete).
  `--pipelines hmm` short-circuits the main onionskin pipeline entirely and runs only
  the HMM engine. `--pipelines growth` skips per-stage mean-shift. Any list including
  `hmm` runs the HMM engine after other selected pipelines. Added `_pipelines_include()`
  helper used by `_build_ms_argv`, `_run_hmm_engine`, and the `main()` short-circuit.

- **`Makefile`**: Updated `make puffstep-py` to use `--pipelines hmm` instead of `--run-hmm`.
  Wall time now 25.9s — identical to the bash pipeline — confirming no overhead when
  the HMM engine runs standalone.

- `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`, `make twin`, `make puffstep-py` — all pass.
  `make puffstep-py` output: 100% exact match vs gold standard on steps 06–10.

---

## [v0.7.01] — 2026-04-08 10:30 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Phase 7.1 — Python HMM engine orchestration wrapper + `make puffstep-py`

- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py`** (new): Python orchestration wrapper implementing
  the full 10-step HMM pipeline. In Phase 7.1, each step delegates to PuffStep CLI tools
  and external utilities via subprocess; porting to native onionskin_core implementations
  begins in Phase 7.3. Steps 1/4/5/6/9/10 call `puffStep.py`; Steps 2/3/7 call PuffStep
  utility scripts; Step 8 uses Python I/O + `mergeBed`. All steps parallelize via
  `ThreadPoolExecutor`. Outputs land in `<hmm_out_dir>/01-mednorm/` … `10-summitBins/`.

- **`onionskin.py`**: Added HMM engine parameter flags and `_run_hmm_engine()` helper
  (pipeline selection flags superseded by `--pipelines` in v0.7.02). Key parameter flags:
  `--hmm-bin-size` (5000), `--hmm-smooth-halfwidth` (3), `--hmm-emission-means`,
  `--hmm-emission-sigmas`, `--hmm-expected-special-length` (1e6),
  `--hmm-expected-other-length` (75000), `--hmm-pseudo` (0). HMM output directory is
  `<out-dir>/03-hmm/`. Wired into `onionskin_core.engines.hmm_engine.run_hmm`.

- **`Makefile`**: Added `make puffstep-py` target — runs `onionskin.py` with `--run-hmm`
  on the full-genome DS2 5 kb manifest; outputs to `dev/runs/puffstep-py/`. Wall time:
  ~90 seconds total, but this includes the full multistage pipeline (~65s) running before
  the HMM engine. The HMM engine itself runs in ~25s — identical to the bash pipeline —
  confirming no meaningful subprocess overhead in the Python wrapper.

- **Validation** (`make puff-compare` manual): compared `dev/runs/puffstep-py/03-hmm/`
  steps 06–10 against gold standard (`dev/puffstep/automate/5kb/`). **100% exact match**
  on all steps and all 4 groups (06 HMM state paths, 07 collapsed, 08 above-background,
  09 summit states, 10 smoothed summit bins). Phase 7.1 acceptance criterion met.

- `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`, `make twin` — all pass.

## [v0.7.00] — 2026-04-08 09:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.87 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Phase 7.0 — engine rename housekeeping + `make puffstep-II` baseline

Phase 7 begins. Version bump to v0.7.xx reflects Phase 7 work.

- **`onionskin_core/engines/`**: Renamed `multistage.py` → `multistage_engine.py` and
  `single.py` → `single_engine.py` for consistency with the `*_engine.py` naming convention.
  Updated all import references in `common.py` and `dead_code.py`. No behavioral changes.

- **`Makefile`**: Added `make puffstep-II` target. Runs the PuffStep bash pipeline
  (`PuffStep/utils/puffStepPipeline.parallel.sh`) on the full-genome DS2 5 kb dataset
  (`dev/datasets/full_genome/batch/manifest.batch-5kb.fofn`). Outputs land in
  `dev/runs/puffstep-II/`. Wall time: ~26 seconds (heavily parallelized). PATH is
  prepended with `CONDA_BIN` (`/Users/johnurban/software/conda/anaconda3/bin`) so that
  `python3` resolves to the conda base environment (which has pandas); the Homebrew
  `python3` in default PATH does not. This is a known fragility of Phase 7.0 only —
  the Python engine (Phase 7.1+) will use whatever Python is running onionskin and
  requires no PATH manipulation.

- **Validation**: Compared `dev/runs/puffstep-II/` steps 06–10 against the gold-standard
  reference at `dev/puffstep/automate/5kb/`:
  - Steps 06 (HMM state paths), 07 (collapsed), 08 (above-background), 09 (summit state
    regions): **exact match** on all 4 groups, all files.
  - Step 10 smoothed summit bins: **exact match** on all 4 groups.
  - Step 10 raw summit bins: gold-standard files are intentionally empty (known gap in
    original run); new run correctly populates them. This is the one known divergence —
    the new run is more correct.

- **`multi-agent/plans/PUFFSTEP-INTEGRATION-PLAN.md`**: Created. Captures integration
  architecture, guiding principles, phased implementation plan (7.0–7.5), make target
  summary, and resolved open items (engine name: `hmm_engine.py`; manifest: full-genome

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Phase 5 close-out (5.13); Phase 6 close-out; ROADMAP restructuring

- **`onionskin.py`**: Added `_apply_multistage_shape_filter()` — shape filter diagnostic for multistage calls (Priority 5.13). Runs after every multistage engine completion. Always writes `_shape_scores_multistage.tsv` (per-call `delta_bic` and `would_reject` columns) and reports to stderr. When `--shape-filter-multistage` is set, filters `_calls.tsv` in place, rewrites `_unified_onionskin_calls.bed`, and writes `_amplicons_actively_rejected_multistage.bed`.
