## [v0.5.63] — 2026-04-07 ~15:30 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Phase 6.5.E.2 — Hires summit refinement for per-stage mean-shift; parallelization

- **`onionskin_core/single_engine.py`**: Refactored `run_per_stage_mean_shift` for parallelization and hires refinement.
  - Extracted per-stage loop body into `_per_stage_one()` — a self-contained worker function with no shared mutable state, safe to run concurrently.
  - `run_per_stage_mean_shift` now dispatches all stages via `ThreadPoolExecutor`. Results collected in stage order; unified outputs computed after all stages complete.
  - Added `hires_track_sets` parameter: `Optional[List[Tuple[sample_tracks_dict, hires_bin_size]]]`. When provided, each stage's passing calls are refined at each hires resolution inside `_per_stage_one` (Phase E.2).
  - Added `max_workers` parameter (default 0 = all CPUs).
  - Phase E.2 hires outputs per stage per resolution:
    - `{prefix}_stageN_aggregate.hires_{bin}.bedGraph` — hires fold track for IGV
    - `{prefix}_stageN_summits.hires_{bin}.bed` — summits refined at hires bin size via `stage2_refine`
  - Added `as_completed` to `concurrent.futures` imports.

- **`onionskin_core/engines/multistage.py`**: Updated `run_per_stage_mean_shift` call to pass `hires_track_sets` (built from `hires_sets` already in memory) and `max_workers` (from `args.threads`).

## [v0.5.62] — 2026-04-07 ~14:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Phase 6.5.E — Per-stage mean-shift outputs

- **`onionskin_core/single_engine.py`**: Added `run_per_stage_mean_shift()` — new function implementing the per-stage single-mode detection pipeline.
  - For each unique stage, computes the per-stage median log2 track across all replicates, runs `stage1_mean_shift` + `stage2_score` per chromosome, applies exact and peak-proximity dedup, then applies `shape_filter_calls`.
  - Writes per-stage outputs to `02-per-stage-mean-shift/`:
    - `{prefix}_stageN_aggregate.bedGraph` — per-stage aggregate track in fold space
    - `{prefix}_stageN_calls.tsv/.bed` — passing calls after shape filter
    - `{prefix}_stageN_summits.bed` — summit positions for IGV
    - `{prefix}_stageN_putative_collapsed_repeats.tsv/.bed` — shape-filter rejects
  - After all stages: `_unify_stage_calls()` merges overlapping intervals across stages → `{prefix}_unified_stage_calls.tsv/.bed` (with `stages_present` column) and `{prefix}_unified_collapsed_repeats.tsv/.bed`.
  - Added `stage1_mean_shift` to imports from `detection`; added `stage2_score` to imports from `refinement`.

- **`onionskin_core/engines/multistage.py`**: Wired `run_per_stage_mean_shift` into `main()`.
  - Imports `run_per_stage_mean_shift` from `onionskin_core.single_engine`.
  - Derives `02-per-stage-mean-shift/` path from `os.path.dirname(args.out_prefix)`.
  - Calls after all growth-pipeline outputs are written; uses `base_tracks` (already in memory).

## [v0.5.57] — 2026-04-07 ~20:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Phase 6.5.A — Code Unification Refactor, Phase A: break circular import chain

- **Broke the circular import chain between `refinement.py`, `common.py`, and `engines/multistage.py`**

  Root cause: `common.py` imported both engine modules at module load time. Since `refinement.py`
  imports `common.py`, `engines/multistage.py` could not import `refinement.py` without triggering
  a circular import. Workaround during v0.5.53–v0.5.54 was deferred (inside-function) imports in
  `multistage.py`. This fix eliminates the root cause.

  - **`onionskin_core/common.py`**: Removed unused `from onionskin_core.engines.multistage import main as _multistage_main` import. Converted `load_reference_single()` and `load_reference_multistage()` to lazy imports (deferred inside function bodies) rather than module-level imports. `common.py` now has zero module-level imports from either engine. `smooth_rcn_median` and `genomic_sort_key` remain here; all callers unchanged.

  - **`onionskin_core/engines/multistage.py`**: Promoted deferred `from onionskin_core.refinement import refine_origin_sliding_offset` calls (lines 838 and 863, previously needed as workarounds) to a single clean module-level import. Removed both inline deferred import lines.

- **Architecture note**: The lazy-import pattern in `common.py` is the permanent design going forward. Any new engine module added to the dispatch layer should follow the same pattern (defer import to function call time, not module load time).

- **Plan written**: `multi-agent/plans/CODE-UNIFICATION-REFACTOR.md` — phased plan for full engine unification (Phases A–E), with enough detail for any agent to continue without re-studying the codebase.

- **ROADMAP updated**: Phase 6.5 (Code Unification Refactor) added as active pre-HMM phase.

- **BRAINSTORM updated**: `[2026-04-07] Multi-engine integration and HMM meta-analysis vision` — describes the strategic goal of a three-approach meta-analysis (multistage gradient + single-mode per stage + HMM) after full unification.

- `make test`, `make toy`, `make single` — all pass.

## [v0.5.56] — 2026-04-07 ~18:30 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Priority 5.11 — Phase 3 (port to single-file/single-stage)

- **Sliding-offset estimator ported to single-file engine** (`single_engine.py`, `refinement.py`)

  `sliding_offset_bp` and `sliding_offset_hires_bp` now appear in `_origins.tsv` and
  `_metrics.tsv` from the single-file engine, alongside the existing `summit_parabola_bp`
  column. The hires column is populated when `--hires-manifest` is supplied; otherwise `None`.

  **Architecture:** `sliding_offset_profile()` and `refine_origin_sliding_offset()` moved
  to `onionskin_core/refinement.py` as shared public utilities (no common/engines dependencies).
  The multistage engine uses deferred imports (`from onionskin_core.refinement import ...`
  inside the function) to avoid the circular import chain: common → multistage → refinement
  → common. Single-engine imports both functions at module level (no circular risk there).

  For single-file mode, the profile for each call is a single smoothed log2-RCN array (one
  per call, no stage structure); the weight vector is `[1.0]`. The same `refine_origin_sliding_offset`
  function handles this transparently — it is designed to accept any list of profiles.

  Tests pass: `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`. Verified `sliding_offset_bp` and
  `sliding_offset_hires_bp` columns present in fresh single-engine `_origins.tsv`.

  **5.11 now fully implemented** across all engines. Remaining: close the ROADMAP entry
  after empirical benchmark comparison on DS1/DS2.

## [v0.5.55] — 2026-04-07 ~18:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Priority 5.11 — Phase 2 (hires-informed sliding-offset)

- **Hires sliding-offset estimator (`multistage.py`)** — `sliding_offset_hires_bp` column in
  `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv` and `{call_id}:aggregate:sliding_offset_hires` BED entry
  in `_summit_estimates.bed` (present only when `--hires-manifest` is supplied).

  When a hires manifest is provided, the sliding-offset sweep also runs on the finest hires
  tracks (e.g., 500 bp bins). The profile-building and smoothing use the hires bin size, but
  the offset sweep still targets the base-resolution bin width — we are still looking for the
  best 5 kb window, just informed by exact 500 bp measurements rather than interpolated ones.
  This is exact (not approximate): the coverage-weighted mean of hires bins within each shifted
  5 kb window is computed directly from measured values.

  Both estimators are always produced independently:
  - `sliding_offset_bp` — coarse interpolation, always present
  - `sliding_offset_hires_bp` — hires-exact, present only with `--hires-manifest`

  Both compete as independent candidates in `eval_summit_precision.py`. After benchmarking
  against II/9A and II/2B ground truth, you can quantify how much the hires data improves
  (or does not improve) over coarse interpolation.

  **Implementation:** `refine_origin_for_call` gains `hires_tracks` and `hires_bin_size`
  optional parameters. Per-stage hires profiles are built using `stage_median_track` on the
  hires tracks, then `_refine_origin_sliding_offset` is called with those profiles and
  `bin_width=base_bin`. The finest hires set is passed to the base-resolution call only.
  Tests pass: `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`. Verified column present and empty
  (correctly) when no hires manifest; BED entries absent when no hires data.

---

## [v0.5.54] — 2026-04-07 ~17:30 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Priority 5.11 — Phase 1 (coarse interpolation, multistage)

- **Sliding-offset summit estimator (`multistage.py`)** — new `sliding_offset_bp` column in
  `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv` and `{call_id}:aggregate:sliding_offset` BED entry in
  `_summit_estimates.bed`.

  **What it does:** Fixed 5 kb bin partitions are arbitrary — the true origin may fall anywhere
  within a bin. The sliding-offset method tries all partition offsets δ ∈ {0, 100, ..., 4900 bp}
  (100 bp step), re-weights each stage's smoothed profile to the shifted partition via
  coverage-weighted interpolation, computes a stage-weighted aggregate, and takes the argmax.
  The best offset is the one where the aggregate peak value is highest — a bin perfectly centered
  on the true origin captures more signal than a miscentered bin mixing in shoulders.

  **New functions:**
  - `_sliding_offset_profile(coord, profile, bin_width, offset)`: re-weights one smoothed profile
    to a shifted partition using coverage-weighted averaging of overlapping fixed bins. No raw
    read access required — works entirely from the existing bin table.
  - `_refine_origin_sliding_offset(smoothed_profiles, coord, bin_width, stage_weights, peak0, step=100)`:
    sweeps offsets, aggregates across stages, returns best-centered bin center. General-purpose:
    designed for portability to single-file and single-stage modes (future work).

  **Broader framing:** This is a pre-processing / re-binning step, not parabola-specific.
  `sliding_offset_bp` is a new estimator candidate that participates in `eval_summit_precision.py`
  benchmarking alongside `argmax_mean_bp` and `parabola_mean_bp`.

  **Tests pass:** `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`. Verified `sliding_offset_bp` present in
  `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv` and `sliding_offset` BED entries in `_summit_estimates.bed`;
  values within expected range of existing estimators on toy chr II amplicons.

  **Remaining (5.11 open):** Port to single-file/single-stage engines; Phase 2 hires-informed mode.

## [v0.5.52] — 2026-04-07 ~16:00 EDT

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Priority 5.5 — cluster-k selection methods

- **`--aps-cluster-k` now accepts named modes in addition to integers** (`aps.py`, `onionskin.py`)

  Three k-selection methods are now available via `--aps-cluster-k`:

  - `'elbow'` (default): Ward linkage acceleration criterion — second differences of merge
    distances; existing behavior since v0.4.12. No new dependencies.
  - `'sweep'`: silhouette score sweep over k=2..n_stages; picks k with highest average
    silhouette score in Euclidean space. Requires scikit-learn (`pip install scikit-learn`).
    Verbose output logs the score at each k candidate.
  - `'keep'`: use the number of unique input stages as k exactly (matches prior grouping).
  - Integer string (e.g., `'3'`): use exactly that many clusters.
  - Legacy `0` still accepted as an alias for `'elbow'`.

  **Implementation:** New `_resolve_cluster_k(cluster_k, n_stages)` helper converts the
  string/int argument to internal sentinels: 0 (elbow), -1 (sweep), N (explicit).
  New `_optimal_k_silhouette(data, Z, max_k)` function runs the sweep on the already-computed
  Ward linkage matrix (no redundant linkage computation). `hierarchical_cluster` gains k=-1
  handling alongside existing k=0 (elbow). `compute_and_write_aps` signature updated:
  `cluster_k: Union[int, str] = 'elbow'`. Tests pass (`make test`, `make toy`, `make single`).

## [v0.5.49] — 2026-04-07 ~10:00 EDT

- **APS oscillation annotation and global degradation detection (`aps.py`)**

  Implements two new outputs from the probabilistic oscillation test (Priority 5.2):

  **1. Per-amplicon oscillation annotation** — new columns in
  `onionskin_aps_locus_contributions.tsv`:
  - `max_rcn_stage`: post-onset stage with the highest summit-window median RCN
  - `regression_stage`: stage s+1 of the first confirmed dip at or after `max_rcn_stage`
    (NaN if no regression detected after the peak)
  - `oscillating_transitions`: comma-separated confirmed dip transitions, e.g. `"5→6,8→9"`
  - `is_oscillator`: True if the locus has ≥1 confirmed dip not coinciding with a
    global degradation stage (sensitive flag — intended to surface loci for per-sample
    inspection, not to imply the amplicon is spurious)
  - `locus_weight`: always 1.0; oscillation informs annotation, not APS weighting

  **2. Global degradation stage report** — new file
  `aps/aps_stage_regression_report.tsv`:
  One row per stage transition with columns: `transition`, `s`, `s1`,
  `n_loci_assessed`, `n_loci_with_dip`, `frac_loci_with_dip`,
  `is_global_degradation_stage`.  A transition is flagged as global degradation when
  ≥50% of assessed loci show a confirmed probabilistic dip simultaneously — consistent
  with late-stage nuclease degradation (salivary gland breakdown) rather than
  individual locus oscillation.

  **Biological framing:** Amplification is monotonically non-decreasing within a single
  cell.  Apparent stage-to-stage drops in pooled data reflect sample-level phenomena:
  underestimated within-stage variance, amplicons that respond differently across
  individual animals (e.g. stress-responsive loci), or global nuclease degradation.
  The oscillation/degradation tests distinguish these cases and flag them for further
  inspection rather than discounting the amplicons.

  **`_compute_locus_diagnostics` refactored** to return `(diag_df, transition_ps)`;
  `_compute_global_degradation` added; `_build_stage_stats` extracted (used by both
  `write_stage_median_tracks` and the oscillation test without duplicate computation).

  **`--aps-weight-loci`** docstring updated: noted as experimental and not recommended;
  currently has no numerical effect since `locus_weight` is always 1.0.

  DS1 chr II spot-check: no global degradation stage detected (max fraction 43% at
  7→8); most amplicons flagged as oscillators (sensitive by design); `regression_stage`
  correctly NaN for `II:54.4-56.1` whose only confirmed dip precedes its peak.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.2 — APS weighting strategies

---

## [v0.5.48] — 2026-04-07 ~08:00 EDT

- **Probabilistic oscillation test implemented (`aps.py`)**

  `_compute_locus_diagnostics` extended with Gaussian overlap test for stage-to-stage
  drops.  For each post-onset transition (s → s+1):

      P(RCN_s > RCN_{s+1}) = Φ((μ_s − μ_{s+1}) / √(σ_s² + σ_{s+1}²))

  where σ = MAD × 1.4826 (Gaussian approximation of Laplace scale; CLT justifies
  Gaussian model for replicate averages).  New columns: `n_real_dips`, `n_transitions`.
  N=1 stages use per-locus pooled σ or a global default (median of all N>1 stage MADs).
  Summit window uses `_SUMMIT_WINDOW_BINS=1` (3-bin window) for robustness.

  Calibration results on DS1 chr II: confirmed dips are statistically real (replicates
  agree well within stages; large between-stage swings at II:21.4-23.5 and II:40.7-42.5
  reflect genuine biology, not noise).  Formula does not feed into locus_weight yet —
  locus_weight reverted to always 1.0.  See v0.5.49 for the oscillation annotation output.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.2 — APS weighting strategies

---

## [v0.5.47] — 2026-04-01 ~20:30 EDT

- **Per-stage MAD tracks implemented (`aps.py`)**

  `write_stage_median_tracks()` extended to write four bedGraph files per stage
  instead of two:

  - `stageN.RCN.bedGraph` (existing)
  - `stageN.log2RCN.bedGraph` (existing)
  - `stageN.MAD_RCN.bedGraph` (new) — median absolute deviation of replicate RCN values per bin
  - `stageN.MAD_log2RCN.bedGraph` (new) — MAD of replicate log2RCN values per bin

  Implementation reuses the replicate stacks already assembled for median computation.
  `N=1` replicates → MAD = 0.0 safely (median of zero deviations).  Verified on DS1
  chr II: MAD values are sensible (near-zero in stable regions, elevated at amplicon
  edges where replicates diverge).

  This is the prerequisite for the probabilistic oscillation test (Priority 5.2 next slice).

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.2 — APS weighting strategies

---

## [v0.5.46] — 2026-04-01 ~19:30 EDT

- **APS locus_weight formula reverted to 1.0 placeholder (`aps.py`)**

  The absolute-threshold product formula implemented in v0.5.45 was tested against
  DS1 chr II (9 stages, 2 replicates per stage) and found to produce wrong results
  on all four ambiguous loci — all of which were confirmed real by IGV inspection
  and independent evidence (HMM detection, fork-front dynamics, twin-peak training
  ground truth).  Formula reverted; `locus_weight = 1.0` again.

  **What went wrong:**

  Two compounding errors:

  1. *Absolute thresholds conflate strength with credibility.*  The formula
     (`post_support` = fraction of stages with `peak_rcn >= 2.0`; `dip_rate` =
     fraction with `peak_rcn < 1.5`) assigned `locus_weight = 0.0` to `II:45.8-46.6`
     because it never reaches RCN 2.0.  But this is a genuine low-signal amplicon —
     possibly amplified in a subset of cells, broad (865 kb), triangle-shaped, with
     fork-front dynamics visible across stages and confirmed by HMM in DS2.  Discounting
     it would defeat one of onionskin's founding goals: detecting weak, broad, low-level
     amplicons blended with background.  `area_excess` already naturally down-weights
     weak loci relative to strong ones; a credibility weight that *also* penalizes
     low RCN double-penalizes real biology.

  2. *Point estimates with N=2 per stage misfire.*  With only 2 replicates per stage,
     per-stage mean `peak_rcn` has high sampling variance.  Stage-to-stage oscillations
     in the metrics (`II:54.4-56.1`, `II:21.4-23.5`, `II:40.7-42.5`) are dominated by
     within-stage replicate noise, not true biological variation.  Any formula using
     point estimates to detect dips will produce spurious oscillations at typical
     real-world sample sizes.

  **What a correct formula requires:**

  - *Per-stage variance* (MAD or stdev per bin) — not yet written as output.  This is
    the prerequisite for any probabilistic oscillation test.
  - *Probabilistic dip detection*: only flag a stage drop as a true dip when
    P(true dip | within-stage variance) exceeds a high threshold (e.g. 0.8–0.9).
  - *Relative thresholds* — evaluated against each locus's own signal, not fixed RCN
    cutoffs that penalize weak-but-real amplicons.

  **Prior groupings must work independently:**
  The formula must be robust on prior groupings, not just posterior.  Users may reject
  posterior groupings; prior analyses must stand on their own.  Posterior groupings
  help by pooling more replicates per group (reducing variance), but they are not a
  prerequisite for prior-mode analysis.

  **Growth track — partially circular for credibility:**
  The growth track is the primary detection signal (amplicons are found because their
  growth track z-score is high), so using it as a credibility signal is partially
  circular.  The fork-front bilateral shape in the growth track is more specific and
  carries independent information, but using it requires care.  Not pursued yet.

  **Calibration amplicons documented** (DS1 chr II — any future formula must give
  these weight ≈ 1.0):
  - `chrII:45755000-46620000` — genuine weak amplicon confirmed by IGV + DS2 HMM
  - `chrII:54380000-55152500` — real
  - `chrII:21440000-22737500` — real, first peak of twin-peak training pair
  - `chrII:40660000-42530000` — real, high per-stage variance from small N

  **Next implementation slice:** add per-stage MAD tracks (per-stage spread of
  replicate RCN per bin) — the prerequisite for a variance-aware oscillation test.
  See BRAINSTORM.md [2026-04-01] and ROADMAP Priority 5.2 for full design rationale.

  `post_support` and `dip_rate` columns retained in output (their definitions using
  absolute thresholds are noted as imperfect proxies).  All other output unchanged.
  All tests pass: `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`.

- **BRAINSTORM.md [2026-04-01] substantially expanded**

  Added: why the formula failed, prior-vs-posterior grouping principle, growth track
  circularity discussion, per-stage variance as prerequisite, probabilistic oscillation
  test design, relative-threshold requirement, four calibration amplicons with IGV
  coordinates and notes.

- **ROADMAP Priority 5.2 updated**

  Revised implementation status to reflect revert; added key design constraints
  (no absolute-RCN penalty, prior-grouping robustness, variance-awareness);
  documented calibration amplicons; identified next slice as per-stage MAD tracks.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.2 — APS weighting strategies

---

## [v0.5.45] — 2026-04-01 ~18:30 EDT

- **APS locus weighting — Priority 5.2 formula implemented (`aps.py`)**

  Closed out Priority 5.2: `locus_weight` is now a real computed value, not a
  placeholder.  Verified on DS1 chr II (9 stages, 18 samples) — the first real dataset
  with enough stages and variance to expose flickery loci.

  **Formula (parameter-free product form):**
  ```
  locus_weight = post_support × (1 − dip_rate)
  ```
  Both factors are in [0,1]; their product is in [0,1].  No sigmoid calibration
  parameters needed.  Interpretable: reliable loci get 1.0; consistently absent or
  always-dipping loci get 0.0; partial reliability gives intermediate values.

  **`post_support` — finalized definition:**
  Fraction of post-onset stages where mean `peak_rcn >= 2.0` (amplification detection
  threshold).  Measures how consistently the locus is detected above background after
  it turns on.  Conditioned on post-onset stages only — late-onset loci are not
  penalized for being absent before onset.  Previously a placeholder (mean peak_rcn
  across all stages); now correctly defined and onset-conditioned.

  **`dip_rate` — finalized definition:**
  Fraction of post-onset stages where mean `peak_rcn < 1.5` (onset-span threshold —
  near-background).  Captures loci that drop all the way back to background after onset,
  whether due to flickering detection or late-stage nuclease degradation.  Uses a lower
  threshold than `post_support` (1.5 vs 2.0) so the two metrics are independent: a
  locus hovering at 1.6–1.9 reduces `post_support` without raising `dip_rate`.
  Previously computed over all stages (pre-onset low values incorrectly counted as
  dips); now restricted to post-onset stages only.

  **Call_id format mismatch fix:**
  The timing TSV uses exact-bp call_ids (`II:45755000-46620000`) while the resolved BED
  uses Mb-rounded call_ids (`II:45.8-46.6`).  A coordinate-based fallback lookup
  (`timing_onset_coord_map`) is now built alongside `timing_onset_map` in
  `compute_and_write_aps()` and passed to `_compute_locus_diagnostics()`, mirroring
  the same pattern used for `summit_bp_map` / `_summit_coord_map`.  Without this fix,
  onset lookups silently fell back to the `peak_rcn >= 2.0` heuristic for mismatched
  loci, which gave wrong onset stages.

  **Verified on DS1 chr II (9 stages):**
  - `II:45.8-46.6` (onset=6, never exceeds 2.0 post-onset): weight=0.00 ✓ — genuinely
    marginal, barely above background in all stages
  - `II:54.4-56.1` (drops below 2.0 in 2 of 7 post-onset stages): weight=0.61 ✓
  - `II:21.4-23.5` (one stage at 1.97, just below threshold): weight=0.83 ✓
  - `II:32.6-34.7`, `II:5.2-7.4`, `II:40.7-42.5`, `II:47.0-48.2` (all consistently
    above 2.0 post-onset): weight=1.00 ✓
  - APS impact: 4–17% reduction in early/low-stage samples (more area from marginal
    loci relative to total); smaller reduction in high-amplitude stages — correct.

  **BRAINSTORM.md [2026-04-01] updated:** marked `post_support`, `dip_rate`, and
  `locus_weight` formula as resolved; open questions that remain noted.

  **ROADMAP Priority 5.2 updated:** "Remaining work" section revised — items 1–3
  (finalize post_support, fix dip_rate, add peak_rcn_stage) closed; items 4–5
  (calibration on more data, --prior-weight) remain open future work.

  All tests pass: `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.2 — APS weighting strategies

---

## [v0.5.44] — 2026-04-01 ~17:00 EDT

- **APS locus weighting machinery — Priority 5.2 second slice (`aps.py`, `onionskin.py`)**

  Implemented the full weighting infrastructure for `--aps-weight-loci`.  The formula
  (sigmoid of diagnostic inputs) is not yet implemented — `locus_weight` remains 1.0 —
  but all machinery is in place and the weighted output columns are production-ready.

  **What was built:**

  - `--aps-weight-loci` CLI flag added (off by default).  When active:
    - `weighted_area_excess = locus_weight × area_excess` added to
      `onionskin_aps_locus_contributions.tsv`
    - `APS_area_weighted = sum(weighted_area_excess per sample)` added to
      `onionskin_aps.tsv`
    - When `--aps-feature area`, the clustering matrix uses `weighted_area_excess`
      instead of `area_excess` — so unreliable loci influence posterior grouping less.
    - No effect on `summit`, `width`, or `shape` clustering features.
    - When `locus_weight = 1.0` (current), `APS_area_weighted == APS_area` exactly.

  - `weight_loci: bool` parameter added to `compute_and_write_aps()`.

  - `--aps-weight-loci` wired through `onionskin.py` call site.

  **`_compute_locus_diagnostics()` corrected and documented (`aps.py`):**

  The initial implementation (v0.5.43) had two fundamental signal errors discovered
  in design review (see BRAINSTORM.md [2026-04-01] "APS locus diagnostics"):

  1. `best_onset_stage` was computed from `area_excess` using an argmax-of-mean-shift
     heuristic.  This is WRONG.  `area_excess` grows with fork travel distance and
     compounds across stages — the biggest area jump is almost always the last stage
     regardless of when the origin actually fired.  The v0.5.43 implementation would
     assign `best_onset_stage = last_stage` for nearly every locus.
     **Fix:** now pulls `onset_stage` from the timing module's output TSV (which uses
     RCN-threshold + shape-evidence on summit RCN — the correct signal).  Falls back to
     first stage where mean `peak_rcn >= 2.0` when timing TSV is absent.  Timing onset
     map is built inside `compute_and_write_aps()` and passed to the diagnostic function.

  2. `post_support` was `mean(area_excess) / locus_width_bp`.  Area-based, same problem.
     **Partial fix:** changed to mean `peak_rcn` across all stages as a rough proxy.
     This is still a PLACEHOLDER — it is not conditioned on onset and does not
     cleanly measure "consistency after onset."  Marked as such in code and ROADMAP.
     Do not rely on current `post_support` values for biological interpretation.

  3. `dip_rate` was `fraction of stages where area_excess < 10% of mean post-onset area_excess`.
     Area never dips even during late-stage nuclease degradation (area keeps growing as
     forks travel further), so this metric would always return 0.0 on real data.
     **Fix:** changed to `fraction of stages where mean peak_rcn < 50% of max(peak_rcn)`.
     Uses summit RCN — the correct signal.  The 50% threshold is arbitrary and will need
     calibration.  Also: currently computed over ALL stages, not post-onset only (pre-onset
     low values would count as dips for late-onset loci).  This is a known limitation
     documented in the code docstring and ROADMAP.

  **Why area_excess is the wrong signal for onset/consistency:**
  `area_excess = sum((RCN-1) × bin_width)` integrates signal over the whole locus width.
  As replication forks travel outward from the origin, they add amplified sequence further
  from the center each stage.  Even after the origin stops firing new initiation rounds,
  existing forks keep traveling and adding to total area.  Area grows monotonically (or
  near-monotonically) across all stages for any real amplicon.  Summit RCN (`peak_rcn`),
  by contrast, reflects the RCN directly at the replication origin and rises when new
  initiation rounds begin, plateaus when they stop, and may decline in very late stages
  when nucleases begin degrading the salivary gland genome.

  **Late-stage nuclease degradation (DS1, 9 stages):**
  In datasets with many developmental stages, the latest stages may show declining summit
  RCN because salivary gland cells begin to be broken down.  Nonspecific nucleases
  uniformly sample the genome; amplified regions have more copies and lose more absolute
  DNA, which can reduce apparent RCN at the origin.  This means the stage of maximum
  summit RCN is not necessarily the last stage.  `dip_rate` (using `peak_rcn`) is designed
  to detect this, but its definition is not yet fully correct (see above).

  **`compute_and_write_aps()` now passes `timing_onset_map` to diagnostics.**
  The timing TSV is already loaded at this point (for `compute_amplicon_importance()`);
  the onset map is built as a side-product without additional I/O.

- **BRAINSTORM.md — APS locus diagnostics design discussion documented**

  Added detailed entry [2026-04-01] capturing the full design discussion: biological
  intent of each diagnostic column, why `area_excess` is wrong for onset/dip detection,
  the nuclease degradation concern for late-stage datasets, the `post_support` definition
  gap, the open question about pre-onset dip counting, and the future `--prior-weight`
  idea.  This is the authoritative record of design intent for future implementers.

- **ROADMAP Priority 5.2 substantially rewritten**

  Added three "Important caution" subsections: late-onset loci (don't penalize absence
  before onset), `area_excess` vs `summit_rcn` (wrong signal for onset/dip detection),
  and late-stage nuclease degradation.  Rewrote the weighting design section to reflect
  current implementation state.  Replaced the algorithm description (which used
  `area_excess` throughout — wrong) with accurate per-column documentation of current
  implementation, known limitations, and remaining work.

  All tests pass: `make test`, `make toy`, `make single`.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.2 — APS weighting strategies

## [v0.5.43] — 2026-04-01 ~16:00 EDT

- **APS locus diagnostics — Priority 5.2 first slice (`aps.py`)**

  Added `_compute_locus_diagnostics()` function to `aps.py`. Called post-hoc after
  `compute_aps_tables()` returns in `compute_and_write_aps()`, merged onto `contrib_df`
  before writing `onionskin_aps_locus_contributions.tsv`. Adds four new columns:

  - `best_onset_stage` — stage that maximises `mean(area_excess for t≥s) − mean(area_excess for t<s)`;
    correctly handles late-onset loci (no penalty for being absent before onset)
  - `post_support` — mean `area_excess` per bp in post-onset stages (locus-width-normalised
    so large and small loci are on the same scale)
  - `dip_rate` — fraction of post-onset stages where mean `area_excess` falls below 10%
    of mean post-onset `area_excess`; high values indicate a flickery / inconsistent locus
  - `locus_weight` — 1.0 placeholder; actual reliability weighting is future work (Priority 5.2)

  These diagnostics expose the inputs needed to evaluate and eventually implement the
  reliability-weighted APS described in ROADMAP Priority 5.2. No APS computation changes;
  all existing outputs are unchanged. `make test`, `make toy`, `make single` all pass.

- **ROADMAP Priority 5.2 — purpose and design clarified**

  Rewrote the Objectives and Scientific reason sections to document that `locus_weight`
  is a **credibility discount**, not a size/amplitude adjustment (size and amplitude are
  already encoded in `area_excess`). Added framing as a "posterior APS" — using the full
  stage dataset to assess each locus's trustworthiness, then folding that back into the
  APS sum. Corrected the algorithm notation to use `area_excess` throughout (replacing
  vague `p_{tℓ}` probability notation). Noted that `locus_weight` does not yet exist in
  code and that the first implementation slice is diagnostics-only.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.85 (claude-sonnet-4-6)

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.2 — APS weighting strategies

- **`PIPELINE_SPEC.md` audit**: Audited specification against codebase. Added 8 missing CLI flags to Appendix A (Defaults) to sync with `onionskin.py`: `--timing-onset-quantile`, `--timing-exclude-loci`, `--aps-feature`, `--aps-shape-no-normalize`, `--threads`, `--verbose`, `--debug`. 
  - Prompt given to GCA chat:
  ```
  Can you run the audit of PIPELINE_SPEC.md using the instructions from pipeline_spec_audit_prompt.md and the context of all the following files: @onionskin_core/aps_plots.py @onionskin_core/binning.py @onionskin_core/autodetect.py @onionskin_core/dead_code.py @onionskin_core/detection.py @onionskin_core/summit_plots.py @onionskin_core/shape_filter_plots.py @onionskin_core/readme.py @onionskin_core/log.py @onionskin_core/aps.py @onionskin_core/single_engine.py @onionskin_core/io.py @onionskin_core/init.py @onionskin_core/refinement.py @onionskin_core/rcn_io.py @onionskin_core/multistage_engine.py @onionskin_core/timing.py @onionskin_core/common.py @onionskin_core/engines/multistage.py @onionskin_core/engines/single.py @onionskin_core/engines/init.py @onionskin_core/profile_plots.py @onionskin_core/output_layout.py @onionskin_core/overlap.py @onionskin_core/overlap_plots.py @onionskin_core/posterior.py @onionskin_core/qc_plots.py @onionskin_core/notebooks.py @onionskin_core/modeling.py @onionskin_core/summaries.py
  ```

  - However, those pasted in as text, not as properly `@`-linked files.
  - Thus, all but one of those files were linked, and I am not sure why.
  - With the audit results, GCA replied: "Although you provided a list of files with @, only onionskin.py and the documentation files were fully injected into the chat context window by the IDE. However, because onionskin.py contains build_parser() — which definitively declares every CLI parameter, default, and type — I was able to thoroughly audit the PIPELINE_SPEC.md Appendix A and Phase 2 guard logic against the absolute ground truth of the CLI parser!"
- **`AUDIT_HISTORY.md`**: Logged the formal `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` audit.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Gemini Code Assist (model auto-selected)

**Roadmap:** Phase 5 — documentation and multi-agent infrastructure

      candidate, no-split regression amplicons, and summit precision training data summary

      stages, DS2 = batch 5 stages), three compounding factors that make DS1 harder (single-
      animal variance, plateau/decline stages, chrom-median compression), current test
      expectations (DS1 9/9, DS2 9/9 with `step` default), diagnostic guidance, future

    timestamps: added `HH:MM` to v0.5.04–v0.5.08 (had `EST` but no time), v0.5.09–v0.5.17
    (had date only), v0.5.19–v0.5.28 (had date+EST but no time); added `EST` to v0.4.03–
    v0.4.13 (had `HH:MM` but no timezone); replaced `2026-03` stubs in v0.4.01–v0.4.02 with

## [v0.5.23] — 2026-03-29 ~18:30 EST

- **`PIPELINE_SPEC.md` spec sync (Phase 5.22 codebase audit).** Full audit of all 19 source files against the spec; 23 findings fixed:
  - **Header:** Updated to Phase 5.22, date 2026-03-29.
  - **Directory layout:** `prior/` → `01-prior/`; added `plots/` and `notebook/` to tree.
  - **Pipeline 1 Step 1/2 code locations:** `engines/single.py` → `refinement.py` (where `read_bedgraph`, `detect_bin_size`, `add_norm_log2` actually live).
  - **Pipeline 1 Step 6 `--strict-bic`:** Added description of strict mode ($k=0$ flat, $k=1$ triangle); per-call penalty = $\ln(n_\text{bins})$; threshold context.
  - **Pipeline 1 Step 6 rejected BED:** Added `_amplicons_actively_rejected.bed` output description.
  - **Pipeline 1 Step 6b (new):** Added peak-proximity deduplication step (`apply_peak_proximity_dedup_tsv`), which was undocumented for Pipeline 1.
  - **Pipeline 2 Step 6-7 note:** Corrected — `_single_calls.tsv` IS overwritten after shape filtering (not "never updated").
  - **Pipeline 2 Step 9:** Added `_amplicons_actively_rejected.bed` and `--strict-bic` support note.
  - **Pipeline 3 Step 9:** Rewrote bootstrap description — per-stage argmax aggregation (weighted mean + median), separate CIs for each. Old combined-profile formula removed.
  - **Pipeline 3 Step 11 `_origins.tsv` schema:** Added 9 missing columns: `final_origin_bp_median`, `origin_ci_low/high_bp_median`, `summit_estimator_used`, `argmax_mean_bp`, `argmax_mean_ci_low/high_bp`, `parabola_mean_bp`, `parabola_median_bp`, `n_parabola_valid`. Updated `final_origin_bp` description (parabola fallback logic).
  - **Pipeline 3 Step 13:** Added 4 summit estimate output files (`_stage_summit_estimates.tsv`, `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv`, `_all_summit_estimates.tsv`, `_summit_estimates.bed`).
  - **Phase 2 flowchart P3 condition:** "manifest present" → "gap-mask bedGraph source available (manifest or explicit `gap_mask_bg`)".
  - **Phase 2 error handling note:** Added all independent try/except blocks (profile plots, shape filter plots, summit plots, QC plots, notebooks, amplicon recommendations).
  - **Phase 2 new steps P5/P6/P7:** Added diagnostic plots subsection, amplicon recommendation finalization (`_amplicons_recommended_to_keep.bed`, `_amplicons_recommended_for_exclusion.bed`), and README step.
  - **Phase 2 Step P4a:** Fixed `_compute_chrom_medians()` note — substitutes 1.0 for exactly 0.0 or non-finite only; negative medians pass through.
  - **Phase 2 Step P3:** Updated condition description to match code logic.
  - **Posterior run section (new):** Added full section documenting `--posterior`, `_write_run_meta`, `_check_prior_complete`, `build_posterior_manifest`, and `02-posterior/` directory layout.
  - **Appendix A:** `--aps-cluster-k` default 3 → 0 (auto); added `--strict-bic`, `--no-aps-singleton-guard`, `--posterior`; added new numeric params table for `--dedup-peak-dist-kb`, `--rcn-smooth-bins`, `--aps-focus-loci`; fixed missing `--` prefixes on all multistage-specific flags.
  - **Appendix B:** Pipeline 1/2 — corrected `_single_calls.tsv` description; added `_amplicons_actively_rejected.bed`; Pipeline 3 — added 4 summit estimate files; Phase 2 — added `plots/`, `notebook/`, `_amplicons_recommended_to_keep/for_exclusion.bed`, gap report files.

## [v0.5.22] — 2026-03-29 ~17:00 EST

- **`--strict-bic` tests added and validated.**
  - `tests/test_strict_bic.sh`: runs default and `--strict-bic` mode on toy_file, toy_stage,
    and ds2_stage5 (DS2 stage 5 two-rep manifest, chr II); asserts strict ⊆ default via
    `compare_strict_bic.py --check-subset`.
  - `tests/compare_strict_bic.py`: per-call score comparison table showing default score,
    strict score, delta (expected: −log(n_bins)), pass/fail at threshold, and status.
  - `Makefile`: added `strict-bic` target (`bash tests/test_strict_bic.sh`).
  - **Baseline result:** all 3 cases pass; delta is consistently −5.7 to −6.0 (n ≈ 400 bins,
    log(400) ≈ 6.0 ✓); no calls change status at threshold=50 on either toy or DS2 real data.
    At the current threshold the strict penalty has no practical effect — both modes accept
    the same calls. Threshold re-calibration would be required before enabling strict mode
    in production.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code ~2.1.81 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Priority 5.13

---

## [v0.5.21] — 2026-03-29 ~16:00 EST

- **`--strict-bic` flag added to shape filter.** By default the shape filter uses k=1 for
  both the flat and triangle BIC models; the penalty terms cancel and the score is a pure
  log-ratio of SSEs (likelihood-ratio test). The flat model actually has k=0 (baseline is
  fixed externally, not fitted), so the default mode under-penalises the flat model by
  `log(n)`. With `--strict-bic`, k=0 is used for flat and k=1 for triangle, correctly
  requiring the triangle model to overcome the `log(n)` complexity penalty.
  - Default behavior (`--strict-bic` absent) is unchanged — no effect on existing runs.
  - `--strict-bic` is threaded through `_dBIC_flat_vs_tri`, `_shape_filter_calls`,
    `apply_shape_filter_tsv`, and `run_single_stage`; wired from argparse via `_refine_kwargs`
    and both `apply_shape_filter_tsv` call sites.
  - Scores under strict mode will be systematically lower than default; threshold 50 may
    need re-tuning before strict mode is used in production. Intended for future calibration.

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code ~2.1.81 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Priority 5.13 (shape filter exploration — strict BIC groundwork)

---

## [v0.5.20] — 2026-03-29 ~15:00 EST

- **Single-file shape filter: rejected calls now written to `_amplicons_actively_rejected.bed`
  in `run_single_stage` code path.**  Previously, shape filter rejections in `run_single_stage`
  (manifest single-stage and `--onionskin` multi-file modes) were silently dropped with only a
  count to stderr.  Only the `apply_shape_filter_tsv` code path (direct single-file and
  manifest single-file via `run_singlefile_caller`) was writing the rejected BED.
  - `_shape_filter_calls` now returns `(kept_df, full_scored_df)` instead of just `kept_df`.
    `full_scored_df` contains `shape_score_raw` for **all** rows including rejected ones,
    enabling callers to record rejections with accurate scores.  This also fixes a latent bug
    in `apply_shape_filter_tsv` where rejected rows were written with `nan` scores (the score
    lookup was built from kept rows only).
  - Added `_append_shape_rejected_bed(df_scored, kept_ids, min_score, rejected_bed_path)` —
    shared helper used by both `apply_shape_filter_tsv` and `run_single_stage` to write the
    6-column rejected BED (chrom, start, end, name, score, reason).
  - `run_single_stage` gains `rejected_bed_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter.  When set,
    rejected calls are appended to that path after shape filtering.
  - Both `run_single_stage` call sites in `onionskin.py` now pass
    `rejected_bed_path=out_prefix + "_amplicons_actively_rejected.bed"`.
  - No change to call results — only the rejected BED output is new.
- **ROADMAP: Priority 5.13 added** — multistage shape filter exploration (deferred; design
  questions documented; verbose-only first pass planned).

**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code ~2.1.81 (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Roadmap:** Priority 4.9 (actively rejected BED — single-file gap closed); Priority 5.13 (new)

## [v0.5.18] — 2026-03-28 23:55 EST

- **Summit eval v2 — 10-level evidence hierarchy.** Replaced the 5-level hard/soft scoring system with a v2 framework informed by 2D gels, NSA qPCR, and ORC binding data.

## [v0.5.17] — 2026-03-27 ~23:00 EST

- **Refinement smoothing: retained `smooth_mean`; added `primary_bin_size` parameter for future use.** Tested replacing `smooth_mean` with `rolling_median` (primary-bin-cap rule) in `refine_origin_for_call`. Results were neutral-to-worse: `rolling_median` produces step-like plateau artifacts that degrade parabola fitting (ds2_1000bp ORC-Core dist 1,340→1,868 bp; ds2_500bp L4 miss 220→710 bp; pass/fail count unchanged). Reverted to `smooth_mean` as default.

  - Rolling_median approach deferred to ROADMAP (Priority 5.9.1).

## [v0.5.16] — 2026-03-27 ~22:00 EST

- **Posterior hires manifests — correct implementation.** Hires manifests contain the **same samples in the same order** as the primary manifest (only the bin-size directory differs). The APS-cluster-based stage permutation computed for the primary manifest applies identically to all hires manifests. Previously `skip_hires=True` was hard-coded in `_run_posterior()`, preventing hires data from being used in the posterior phase.
  - **`posterior.py` `build_posterior_manifest()`:** Extended to accept `hires_manifest_paths` and `hires_out_paths`. For each hires manifest, applies the same `sample_id → posterior_stage` mapping and writes a posterior hires manifest (with hires file paths, posterior stage order). Returns `(primary_out_path, [hires_out_path, ...])`.
  - **`onionskin.py` `_run_posterior()`:** Removed `skip_hires=True`. Now parses `args.hires_manifest`, computes posterior hires manifest output paths, calls `build_posterior_manifest()` with hires params, and passes posterior hires manifests to the engine via `hires_manifest_override`.
  - **`onionskin.py` `_build_ms_argv()`:** Replaced `skip_hires: bool` parameter with `hires_manifest_override: Optional[str]`, allowing the caller to supply a different hires manifest string (or `""` to suppress hires, or `None` to use `args.hires_manifest` unchanged).
- **Hires manifest QC — basename validation:** `multistage.py` now validates that each hires manifest has the same sample filenames in the same order as the base manifest (only directory may differ). Raises a clear `ValueError` listing up to 5 mismatched positions.
- **Renamed `_post_base` → `_post`** throughout test suite and report. The `_post_base` suffix was premature — it implied posterior always used base resolution. Now `ds2_hires_post` uses posterior hires manifests (hires data at posterior stage order); `ds2_5000bp_post` uses base only (no hires in that prior run). All 10 comparison pairs in `consolidate_summit_reports.py` updated accordingly.

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.9.8 (posterior hires — completes the posterior pipeline)

## [v0.5.15] — 2026-03-27 ~21:00 EST

- **Summit test suite: complete comparison matrix.** The test suite now explicitly answers four questions about summit accuracy:
  1. *Does hires refinement help over plain 5kb?* → `ds2_hires` vs `ds2_5000bp` (both prior)
  2. *Does APS-informed posterior grouping help at 5kb?* → `ds2_5000bp_post_base` vs `ds2_5000bp`
  3. *Posterior from hires-APS clusters vs 5kb-APS clusters?* → `ds2_hires_post_base` vs `ds2_5000bp_post_base`
  4. *Prior hires vs best posterior base?* → `ds2_hires` vs `ds2_hires_post_base` / `ds2_5000bp_post_base`
- **Added `_run_pipeline_bg_posterior`** — like `_run_pipeline_bg` but adds `--compute-aps --posterior`, writing prior and posterior sentinel files. `ds2_5000bp` and `ds1_5000bp` now use it (10 parallel pipeline runs total, up from 8).
- **Renamed `_posterior` → `_post_base`** in all posterior labels (e.g. `ds2_hires_post_base`). Makes explicit that posterior runs always use base (5kb) resolution — posterior manifests have reassigned stages that are incompatible with the original hires manifests (`skip_hires=True`).
- **Explicit comparison section in consolidated report.** `consolidate_summit_reports.py` now emits a "Summit Accuracy Comparisons" table at the end, pairing 10 known run combinations, showing best-estimator ORC-Core distance for each side, which is better, and by how many bp. Section is omitted if no pairable runs are present (e.g. smoke mode with only `ds2_hires`).

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.9.8 (extended)

## [v0.5.14] — 2026-03-27 ~20:00 EST

- **Parallel hires + base track loading (multistage engine):** All hires manifests are now parsed and validated upfront (fast, text-only), then base tracks and all hires tracks are loaded simultaneously using a `ThreadPoolExecutor`. Previously base loaded first, then hires loaded one at a time. Expected speedup: ~N× where N = number of hires manifests + 1 (base), bounded by storage I/O bandwidth. With two hires manifests (the common case), peak wall-clock for loading drops from 3× the largest-manifest load time to 1×.
- **Parallel hires manifest loading (single engine):** Multiple hires manifests now load concurrently via `ThreadPoolExecutor` instead of serially. Same speedup profile as multistage for the hires-only step. Base fold track still loads first (separate step).

**Roadmap:** performance — no specific ROADMAP entry; enables faster `make summit` runs and real-time use with hires data.

## [v0.5.07] — 2026-03-27 ~14:00 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.9, Priority 5.10 — Per-stage parabola summit estimates and new summit output files.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/engines/multistage.py`

- Changed `stage_weight_mode` default in `refine_origin_for_call` from `"replicate"` to `"equal"`. All stage-weight-based aggregation now defaults to equal weighting per stage rather than per replicate.
- Added per-stage parabola vertex computation in `refine_origin_for_call`: after computing argmax per stage, fits a downward-opening parabola to the smoothed log2 profile within `max(window_kb * 500, 5000)` bp of `peak0`. Records per-stage vertex if valid (downward-opening, vertex within fit window). Returns `stage_peaks_data` list plus `parabola_mean_bp`, `parabola_median_bp`, and `n_parabola_valid` in the return dict.
- `_process_one_call` now extracts `stage_peaks_data` from `final_origin`, builds `stage_summit_rows` (one dict per stage), and adds `parabola_mean_bp`, `parabola_median_bp`, `n_parabola_valid` to the `row` dict. Return signature changed from `(row, prog_df)` to `(row, prog_df, stage_summit_rows)`.
- Results collection loop updated to unpack the new 3-tuple and accumulate `all_stage_summit_rows`.

### Added — four new summit output files (multistage runs)

- **`_stage_summit_estimates.tsv`**: one row per (amplicon × stage); columns `call_id`, `chrom`, `stage`, `argmax_bp`, `argmax_bin`, `parabola_vertex_bp`, `parabola_vertex_bin`.
- **`_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv`**: one row per amplicon; argmax mean/median with CIs, parabola mean/median, `n_parabola_valid`, `n_stages`.
- **`_all_summit_estimates.tsv`**: wide format — aggregate columns plus per-stage `stage_N_argmax_bp` and `stage_N_parabola_vertex_bp` columns pivoted for notebook use.
- **`_summit_estimates.bed`**: BED6 with all estimator positions (per-stage argmax + bin, per-stage parabola + bin, aggregate argmax mean/median with CIs, aggregate parabola mean/median). Score column = stage number for per-stage rows, 0 for aggregate. Sorted by chrom/start. Designed for IGV side-by-side comparison.

### Added — ROADMAP.md

- Priority 5.10: full schema documentation for all four summit output files, design notes, and IGV usage guidance.

## [v0.5.06] — 2026-03-27 ~12:30 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.8 — Summit diagnostic plots updated for per-stage summits.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`

- `plot_summit_fit` gains `origin_bp_median: Optional[int]` parameter.
- Per-stage summit positions are computed from each stage's already-smoothed RCN profile (argmax of `smooth_rcn_median` output) and drawn as thin dashed vertical lines in stage color. The stage whose summit is closest to the median estimate is drawn thicker and annotated `(= median)` in the legend.
- Median summit drawn as dark-red dash-dot line (`#c0392b`).
- Weighted-mean summit line label updated to `weighted mean summit = N bp` for clarity.
- Bootstrap CI shading label updated to `bootstrap CI (weighted mean)`.
- Legend restructured: stage RCN curve swatches → per-stage summit vlines → CI / mean / median.
- `write_summit_plots` passes `final_origin_bp_median` from TSV to each task (graceful fallback to `None` if column absent, e.g. old TSVs).

### Changed — `scripts/summit_inspector.py`

- `_compute_panel_data` computes `stage_summits: Dict[int, int]` (per-stage argmax from displayed smoothed RCN) and reads `origin_bp_median` from `call_row` (falls back to `None`). Both added to returned panel data dict.
- `_draw_rcn_panel` applies the same per-stage summit vline logic as `summit_plots.py` above: thin dashed stage-color lines, thicker + `(= median)` annotation for the closest-to-median stage, separate dark-red dash-dot median line, solid weighted-mean line.

---

## [v0.5.05] — 2026-03-27 ~11:00 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.8 — Summit estimation: per-stage argmax aggregation.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/engines/multistage.py`

- **`refine_origin_for_call`: per-stage argmax aggregation (replaces combined-profile argmax)**
  Previously: all stage-median log2 profiles were weighted-combined into one profile, then a single argmax was taken. With replicate-count weighting (default), this was approximately the argmax of the grand mean of all samples — invariant to stage grouping. Prior and posterior runs therefore always produced identical `final_origin_bp`.
  Now: each stage-median profile is smoothed independently, its argmax is found, and the per-stage peak positions are aggregated by weighted mean (`final_origin_bp`) and unweighted median (`final_origin_bp_median`). Both are written to `_origins.tsv`. Stage grouping changes now move the estimated summit position.
  The bootstrap is updated in parallel: each replicate computes per-stage medians, per-stage argmaxes, and aggregates by weighted mean and median independently. Separate CIs are reported for each.
  `stage_weight_mode` still controls the weights used for the weighted-mean aggregation; `equal` (all stages weight 1) is recommended when group sizes differ, `replicate` gives more weight to larger stages.

- **New `_origins.tsv` columns**: `final_origin_bp_median`, `origin_ci_low_bp_median`, `origin_ci_high_bp_median` — the median-aggregated summit estimate alongside the existing weighted-mean columns. Allows direct comparison of both aggregation strategies.

---

## [v0.5.04] — 2026-03-27 ~10:00 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.1 — Posterior run bug fixes; Priority 5.7 added to ROADMAP.

### Fixed — `onionskin_core/posterior.py`

- **Posterior run missing plots — root cause (Bug 1)**: `build_posterior_manifest` wrote the TSV with column order `stage\tfile\tsample_id`. `io.read_manifest` detects header layout by first-column name, then applies **hardcoded positional** parsing: the `stage_first` branch assumes `parts[0]=stage, parts[1]=sample_id, parts[2]=path` — the opposite of what was written. This caused `compute_sample_rcn_tracks` to attempt opening the sample_id string as a file path, raising `FileNotFoundError` on every sample. Since `stage_medians/` remained empty, no profile plots were generated; since `posterior/plots/aps/` is created later in the same function, the entire APS plots subdirectory was absent. Only `overlap_diagnostics/` (separate try/except) and `summit_fits/` (reads `_origins.tsv` directly) survived. Fix: write manifest as `file\tstage\tsample_id` (file-first) so `read_manifest` detects `file_first` and correctly uses `parts[0]=path, parts[1]=stage, parts[2]=sid`.

### Fixed — `onionskin.py`

- **Posterior hires manifest stage mismatch (defensive fix)**: `_run_posterior` was passing `--hires-manifest` to the posterior engine. The engine validates that hires manifest stages match base manifest stages row-for-row; the posterior manifest carries APS-cluster-derived stage assignments that differ from the prior, so this validation raises `ValueError`. Fix: `_build_ms_argv` gains `skip_hires: bool = False`; `_run_posterior` calls it with `skip_hires=True`. The hires manifest is not appropriate for posterior runs regardless.
- **Posterior engine exceptions now handled**: `run_multistage` in `_run_posterior` is wrapped in `try/except` so engine errors are logged to stderr and processing continues through `organize_outputs` and `_phase2_postprocess`.

### Changed — `scripts/summit_inspector.py`

- **Summit inspector same-position note**: When both panels show the same summit position, the figure title reads `summit shift: 0 bp (expected — same genomic signal)`. **Note:** this framing is under review — if stage regrouping changes the per-stage weighted RCN signal, the summit estimation should be sensitive to it. See Bug 2 investigation.

### Added — `ROADMAP.md`

- **Priority 5.7**: `--no-aps-singleton-guard` CLI flag — lets users disable the singleton guard when forcing a specific `--aps-cluster-k` (e.g., k=5 to reproduce prior grouping) without the guard collapsing clusters. Enables side-by-side comparison of auto-k vs forced-k posterior runs.

---

## [v0.5.03] — 2026-03-27 02:00 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.1 — Summit inspector prior+posterior stacked view.

### Changed — `scripts/summit_inspector.py`

- Auto-detects a `posterior/` directory alongside the prior run (sibling of `prior/` or child of the top-level run dir). When found, renders a **stacked** layout: prior RCN panel, posterior RCN panel, shared annotation tracks — all sharing the x-axis. Summit shift (`+N bp` or `-N bp`) reported in the figure title.
- New `_find_posterior_dir(run_dir)`: handles both `prior/`-as-run-dir and parent-as-run-dir.
- New `_match_call_by_proximity(df, chrom, origin_bp, max_dist_bp)`: finds the posterior call whose origin is nearest to the prior origin. Falls back gracefully (prior-only, with a message) if no match within `zoom_kb` distance.
- New `_compute_panel_data(call_row, rcn_bg, zoom_kb, fit_kb)`: extracted from the old monolithic `plot_amplicon_zoom` — computes per-stage smoothed RCN, weighted-mean, and parabola fit for one panel. Called separately for prior and posterior.
- New `_draw_rcn_panel(ax, pd_, label, summit_color, ref_origin_bp, ...)`: draws one RCN panel into a pre-created axes. Prior panel uses steelblue; posterior panel uses green (`#2ca02c`). Prior summit position shown as a dashed steelblue reference line in the posterior panel so any shift is immediately visible.
- `_draw_track_panel`: extracted from the old inline annotation track drawing. When dual mode is active, draws both summit lines (steelblue=prior, green=posterior) in the track panel.
- `plot_amplicon_zoom`: accepts `post_call_row` and `post_rcn_bg` optional kwargs. When provided, builds a 3-row gridspec (prior, posterior, tracks); otherwise renders the original single-panel layout unchanged.
- `--no-posterior` flag added to suppress auto-detection.
- Default output filename gains `_prior_posterior` suffix when dual mode is active.

---

## [v0.5.02] — 2026-03-27 01:45 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.1 — Posterior re-run auto-detection.

### Added — `onionskin.py`

- `_write_run_meta(layout, manifest_path)`: writes `prior/.run_meta.json` at the end of a successful prior multistage run. Records `out_prefix`, absolute `manifest` path, and `version`. Enables posterior-only re-runs to find and validate the prior without re-running detection.
- `_check_prior_complete(out_dir, manifest_path)`: validates a prior run in four steps — (1) `.run_meta.json` exists and is parseable, (2) manifest path matches the current invocation, (3) recorded `out_prefix` has files on disk, (4) required APS cluster files (`aps_clusters_raw.tsv`, `aps_order_area.tsv`) are present. Returns a reconstructed layout dict on success, `None` with an informative `stderr` message on any failure. Returns `None` silently (no message) when no prior run exists at all, so a first-time run is undisturbed.
- Multistage path in `main`: if `--posterior` is set and `_check_prior_complete` succeeds, the prior run is skipped entirely and `_run_posterior` is called directly with the validated layout. This lets users add `--posterior` to an already-completed command and get only the posterior outputs without re-running detection.
- `import json` moved to module-level imports.

### Behavior

```
# First run (no --posterior): runs prior, writes .run_meta.json
onionskin --manifest m.tsv --out-dir results --compute-aps [flags...]

# Re-run adding --posterior: detects prior is done, skips it, runs posterior only
onionskin --manifest m.tsv --out-dir results --compute-aps --posterior [same flags...]
```

If the manifest path changes, or APS files are missing, `_check_prior_complete` returns `None` with a clear explanation and the full prior+posterior run proceeds normally.

---

## [v0.5.01] — 2026-03-27 01:15 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 5.1 — Posterior reruns implemented.

### Added — `onionskin_core/posterior.py` (new module)

- `build_posterior_manifest(aps_dir, prior_manifest_path, out_path)`: reads `aps_clusters_raw.tsv` and `aps_order_area.tsv` from an APS output directory, orders clusters by mean `APS_area` (lowest = earliest = stage 1), maps each sample to its posterior stage, and writes a standard onionskin manifest TSV at `out_path`. Also writes `<out_path_stem>_cluster_map.tsv` alongside for auditing (`sample_id, prior_stage, cluster_raw, posterior_stage, APS_area`). Raises `FileNotFoundError` if APS outputs are absent and `ValueError` if sample_ids cannot be matched to bedGraph paths.

### Added — `onionskin.py`

- `--posterior` flag: after the prior multistage run and APS clustering complete, builds a posterior manifest (via `build_posterior_manifest`) and re-runs the full pipeline on it. Posterior outputs land in `<out-dir>/posterior/` alongside `<out-dir>/prior/`. APS clustering re-runs on the posterior manifest — expected to re-affirm the grouping. Requires `--compute-aps`. Prints a clear error if APS files are absent.
- `_run_posterior(prior_layout, args)`: internal helper that orchestrates posterior manifest build, multistage engine re-run, output organization, and phase-2 post-processing for the posterior grouping.
- `_build_ms_argv`: added `manifest_override` parameter so the posterior run can substitute a different manifest path while inheriting all other detection/engine parameters from `args`.

---

Phase 4 (output completeness and correctness) is closed as substantially complete. All core priorities are done or closed. Three incremental items carried forward as Priority 5.0 (keep/exclude refinements, timing fold-change unified summit, `--refine-summits`). No code changes in this entry — version bump marks the phase transition and ROADMAP update only.

## [v0.4.31] — 2026-03-27 00:30 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 4.12 — APS clustering auto-k selection and singleton guard.

### Added — `onionskin_core/aps.py`

- `_optimal_k_from_linkage(Z, max_k)`: selects the optimal cluster count using the acceleration criterion (elbow on second differences of Ward merge distances). Evaluates k from 2 to `max_k` and picks the k where the gap between consecutive top-level merge distances is largest. Requires no additional imports beyond existing scipy usage.
- `_fix_singletons(clusters, mat)`: post-processing reassignment of singleton clusters. For each sample in a cluster of size 1, computes Euclidean distance to all non-singleton cluster centroids in APS feature space and reassigns to the nearest. Logs each reassignment at verbose level. No-ops if all clusters are singletons (cannot merge into anything) or if no singletons exist.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/aps.py`

- `hierarchical_cluster`: now accepts `k=0` for auto-selection (previously only accepted explicit k) and `max_k` for bounding the auto-k search. Auto-k uses `_optimal_k_from_linkage`; both auto and explicit k paths apply `_fix_singletons` afterward. `k` is clamped to `min(k, n_samples)` to avoid requesting more clusters than samples.
- `compute_and_write_aps`: auto-k resolution now computes `_cluster_max_k = max(2, n_stages)` and passes it to `hierarchical_cluster(max_k=_cluster_max_k)` rather than pre-resolving to a flat k. This lets the elbow criterion operate within the stage-count bound rather than being forced to exactly `n_stages` clusters.

---

## [v0.4.30] — 2026-03-26 23:59 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 4.7.2 — APS dendrogram leaf color bug fixed.

### Fixed — `onionskin_core/aps_plots.py`

- `plot_dendrogram`: `scipy.cluster.hierarchy.dendrogram(Z, labels=X)` expects `X[i]` to be the label for the i-th *original observation* (row index in the data matrix), not the i-th leaf in display order. Previously, `leaf_labels` (in display order) was passed directly as `labels=`, causing all sample IDs to be placed at the wrong leaf positions. Cluster colors assigned via `ax.get_xticklabels()` therefore looked up the wrong sample → wrong cluster → wrong color, producing visually incoherent dendrograms where the same cluster color appeared in disconnected subtrees. Fix: invert the `leaves_list(Z)` permutation to reconstruct an `orig_labels` array in original-observation order before calling `dendrogram()`.

---

## [v0.4.29] — 2026-03-26 23:45 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 4.7.2 — APS heatmap column/row ordering corrected to genomic position.

### Added — `onionskin_core/common.py`

- `genomic_sort_key(chrom, start)`: natural-sort tuple for genomic coordinates. Splits chrom on digit runs so that I < II < III, chr1 < chr2 < chr10, 2L < 3L, etc. Used as a sort key for any list of amplicons that needs chromosomal left-to-right ordering.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/aps.py`

- `build_aps_matrix`: columns (amplicons) are now sorted by `(chrom, start)` genomic position instead of lexicographic call_id string. The previous lexicographic sort misorders short-form labels like `II:5.2-7.4` after `II:32.7-34.6` because `'3' < '5'` as strings. All heatmaps derived from this matrix — raw, z-scored, dendrogram-ordered, prior-ordered, per-amplicon-ordered — now have correct left-to-right column ordering.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/aps_plots.py`

- Growth curve per-amplicon loop: amplicons now iterated in genomic order, not lexicographic.
- Stage progression heatmap (4.7.3): rows (amplicons) now sorted by genomic position, not call_id string.

---

## [v0.4.28] — 2026-03-26 23:30 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 4.8 — Parallelization of per-amplicon plot rendering.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`, `shape_filter_plots.py`, `profile_plots.py`

- All three `write_*` functions now accept `max_workers: int = 0` and dispatch per-amplicon matplotlib renders to a `ProcessPoolExecutor`.
- Each module adds a top-level `_render_one_*` worker function (picklable; no lambda/closure).
- Sequential fallback when `max_workers=1` or only one amplicon.
- `write_profile_plots`: per-amplicon profiles (4.7.4) are parallelized; chromosome overviews (4.7.9) remain sequential.
- `write_overlap_diagnostic_plots`: not parallelized — uses pre-loaded DataFrames in `bg_cache`; deferred.

### Changed — `onionskin.py`

- `max_workers=int(getattr(args, "threads", 0))` threaded to `_write_profiles`, `_write_shape`, and `_write_summit` calls.

### Impact

On a typical 14-amplicon dataset: ~14 summit plots + ~14 shape plots + ~14 profile plots were rendered sequentially (~40–60 s total at ~1–2 s/plot). With all CPUs these complete in ~5–10 s. Combined with existing APS plot parallelism, the full Phase 2 plot stage is now fully parallelized.

---

## [v0.4.27] — 2026-03-26 23:00 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 4.11 — APS_summit uses refined origin position from `_origins.tsv`.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/aps.py`

- **`_locus_metrics`**: accepts `summit_bp: Optional[int]`. When provided, `summit_excess` = smoothed RCN at the bin containing `summit_bp` (median of bins at that position; nearest-bin fallback if boundary falls between bins), minus 1. When `None`, falls back to `max(RCN) - 1` over the whole window (previous behavior). The `peak_rcn` field is unchanged — it still reports the whole-window max for reference.
- **`compute_aps_tables`**: accepts `summit_bp_map: Optional[Dict[str, int]]` (call_id → summit_bp) and `summit_coord_map: Optional[Dict[Tuple[str,int,int], int]]` ((chrom, start, end) → summit_bp). Per-locus lookup tries call_id first, then coordinate key, then falls back to max(RCN). The coord fallback is needed because `_origins.tsv` uses exact-bp call_ids (`II:810000-1890000`) while the resolved BED column 4 may use Mb-rounded labels (`II:0.8-1.9`).
- **`compute_and_write_aps`**: accepts `origins_tsv: Optional[str]`. Reads `_origins.tsv`, normalises column aliases (single-mode vs multistage), builds both lookup dicts, logs whether summit positions were loaded or fallback is active.

### Changed — `onionskin.py`

- `origins_tsv=f"{layout['out_prefix']}_origins.tsv"` threaded to `compute_and_write_aps`.

### Why this matters

`APS_summit` previously measured `max(RCN)` anywhere in the amplicon window. For a broad asymmetric amplicon or one with a noisy shoulder, the max bin can be far from the true origin. Now `APS_summit` uses the bin at `final_origin_bp` from `_origins.tsv` — the same position the refinement module carefully computed. APS_summit ordering and importance ranking now reflect amplitude *at the origin*, not amplitude *somewhere in the amplicon*.

---

## [v0.4.26] — 2026-03-26 22:30 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 4.6 / 4.7.6 — ±3-bin running median smooth for summit refinement and APS computation.

### Added — `onionskin_core/common.py`

- `smooth_rcn_median(y, n_side=3)`: running median smooth via `scipy.ndimage.median_filter` with `mode='nearest'` edge extension. n=3 → 7-bin window → ±15 kb at 5 kb bins. Creates a new array; does not modify input in place.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/refinement.py`

- `refine_summit_parabola()` now accepts `smooth_bins: int = 3`; applies `smooth_rcn_median` to all resolution levels before parabola fitting. Suppresses outlier bins that would otherwise bias the fitted vertex position.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/single_engine.py`

- `run_single_stage()` accepts `rcn_smooth_bins: int = 3` and threads it to `refine_summit_parabola()`.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/aps.py`

- `compute_aps_tables()` and `compute_and_write_aps()` accept `smooth_bins: int = 3`; smoothed copies of sample tracks created once per sample before APS integration loop. APS_area and APS_summit now computed on smoothed RCN. APS_summit (max(RCN)−1 within amplicon) is maximally sensitive to single outlier bins, making smoothing especially impactful there.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`

- Per-stage RCN traces now smoothed with `smooth_rcn_median` before plotting, so plots show what the algorithm uses rather than raw noisy bins.

### Added — `onionskin.py`

- `--rcn-smooth-bins INT` CLI argument (default 3) in the Advanced/refinement group. Threaded to `_refine_kwargs()` and to `compute_and_write_aps()`. Set 0 to disable.

### Design note

Raw RCN is preserved for shape filter (rectangle detection) and twin-peak valley detection where sensitivity to sharp features matters. Smoothed RCN is used for summit parabola fitting and APS integration where outlier bins cause bias. The two paths coexist; `smooth_rcn_median` is applied on-the-fly and never written to disk.

---

## [v0.4.25] — 2026-03-26 21:00 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 4.7.6 — Summit diagnostic plots improvements + new summit inspector script.

### Changed — `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`

- **Colormap**: switched from viridis to plasma (0.05–0.85 range); avoids bright-yellow end that is unreadable on white background
- **Legend**: per-stage lines now appear in legend as color swatches labeled `stage N`; multi-column layout when many stages
- **Line labels**: "weighted avg" → "stage-weighted mean (display only)" to make clear this is a visualization artifact not used by the pipeline; parabola vertex dotted line now labeled "parabola vertex (viz) = N bp" and included in legend
- **CI annotation**: when `ci_low == ci_high` (zero-width bootstrap CI, common with single-replicate-per-stage runs), text now explicitly says "point estimate only — single replicate per stage" instead of displaying a zero-width range
- **CI span**: `axvspan` only drawn when `ci_high > ci_low`; avoids invisible zero-width artifact

### Added — `scripts/summit_inspector.py`

Generalized deep-zoom summit diagnostic script for manual review and annotation:

- `--run-dir` + `--call-id` or `--region chrom:start-end` to select amplicon
- Auto-discovers `_origins.tsv` and `stage_medians/` from `--run-dir`
- `--annot-bed`: optional annotation BED file parsed into a **genome-browser-style track panel** below the RCN curves
- `--zoom-kb`: configurable half-width (default 100 kb)
- `--output`: custom output base path; defaults to `<run-dir>/plots/summit_fits/inspector_<call_id>_zoom<N>kb`
- Outputs both PDF and PNG

**Track panel layout** (genome-browser style, below RCN panel, shared x-axis):
- Rows ordered top→bottom: HMM summit bin → HMM summit-state → HMM breadth → Level 6 → Level 5 → … → Level 1 → Molecular features
- HMM rows colored by stage (plasma colormap, matching RCN traces)
- Level 1–6 rows colored red→orange→amber→yellow-green→green→blue (outermost to tightest)
- Molecular features drawn as labeled rectangles (minimum display width enforced so sub-kb features are visible)
- Features narrower than 0.3% of view width expanded to that minimum
- Vertical summit and parabola-vertex reference lines extend through track panel
- Empty rows (categories with no records in view) auto-suppressed

- For dev/regression: `python scripts/summit_inspector.py --run-dir dev/runs/<run>/prior --region II:33000000-34000000 --annot-bed tests/summit_training_data/01-II9A-evidence-based-confinement-boundaries-and-rules.bed --zoom-kb 50`

#### Files changed

- `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`
- `scripts/summit_inspector.py` (new)

## [v0.4.19] — 2026-03-26 18:05 EST

**Roadmap:** Priority 4.7.5 — Shape filter diagnostic plots; Priority 4.7.6 — Summit diagnostic plots.

### Added — shape filter diagnostic plots (`plots/shape_filter/`)

New module `onionskin_core/shape_filter_plots.py` generates one figure per assessed
amplicon (both PASS and FAIL) showing per-stage RCN profiles with the triangle fit
overlay and dBIC verdict annotation.

#### Figure layout — `shape_{call_id}.pdf/.png`

Single panel:
- Per-stage RCN profiles (thin, viridis colored); last-stage profile thicker/opaque
- Fitted asymmetric triangle overlay (orange dashed), with amplitude h annotation
- Call start/end vertical dashed lines; summit vertical line
- Verdict annotation: `PASS` (green) or `FAIL` (red) with `dBIC = X.X (threshold = 50)`

Works in **multistage** and **single-stage** modes. In single-stage mode, the
normalized RCN bedGraph (from `summary_bedgraphs/`) is seeded into `stage_medians/`
automatically so the function can find a profile. In `--onionskin` single-file mode,
the raw input bedGraph is used as the RCN proxy.

Additionally, `run_single_stage` now writes back the shape-filtered df_calls
(including `shape_score_raw`) to `_single_calls.tsv`, so the column is available
to downstream tools in all single-mode runs.

#### Files changed

- `onionskin_core/shape_filter_plots.py`: new module; handles both multistage
  (`call_id`, `peak_base_bp`) and single-mode (`chrom:start-end` id, `peak`) column names
- `onionskin_core/single_engine.py`: write-back df_calls to `_single_calls.tsv`
  after shape filter so `shape_score_raw` persists in the output
- `onionskin.py`: fallback `_calls.tsv → _single_calls.tsv` for shape filter plots;
  stage_medians seeding from summary_bedgraphs or raw input bedgraph in single mode

### Added — summit diagnostic plots (`plots/summit_fits/`)

New module `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py` generates one figure per amplicon in
`_origins.tsv` showing per-stage RCN profiles, weighted-average combined profile,
fitted parabola curve, and bootstrap CI band.

#### Figure layout — `summit_{call_id}.pdf/.png`

Single panel:
- Per-stage RCN traces (thin, viridis colored), zoomed ±display_window around summit
- Weighted-average combined log2 profile (thick gray; weight = stage number)
- Fitted parabola curve in linear RCN (dashed orange); shown only when fit is valid
  (downward-opening, vertex within fit window)
- Vertical line at `final_origin_bp` (blue solid)
- CI shading (`origin_ci_low_bp → origin_ci_high_bp`, steelblue alpha=0.10)
- Annotations: CI bounds and width; "parabola fit invalid" note when fit fails

Works with base-resolution data only (no hires manifest required). Handles both
multistage origins (`final_origin_bp`, `origin_ci_low_bp/high_bp`) and single-mode
origins (`origin_bp`, `origin_ci_lo/hi`) by normalising column names before reading.

#### Files changed

- `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`: new module; entry point `write_summit_plots`
- `onionskin.py`: wired in `_phase2_postprocess`; column-name normalisation for
  single-mode origins TSV
- `onionskin_core/summit_plots.py`: column aliases added (`origin_bp → final_origin_bp`,
  `origin_ci_lo/hi → origin_ci_low/high_bp`, `amplicon_id → call_id`)
